law_title: قانون آب و نحوه ملی شدن آن (Water Law and its Nationalization)
justification: Nationalized all surface and groundwater resources and gave the state (later Ministry of Energy/regional water authorities) exclusive licensing power over wells, canals and irrigation, reshaping how agricultural water is allocated and metered nationwide.
caveats: Long-run trends in irrigated land share, freshwater withdrawal and per-capita renewable water are driven far more by population growth, dam-building programs, drought cycles and agricultural policy than by this single statute; WDI series for Iran begin only in the 1960s-70s so pre/post comparison is limited, and many other laws/five-year plans layered on top of this one over 50+ years.

law_title: قانون تقسیم عرصه و اعیان باغات مشمول قوانین و مقررات اصلاحات ارضی بین مالکان و زارعان مربوط (1972 orchard-land division law)
justification: A narrow single-article statute mandating division of orchard/garden land and associated structures between landlords and sharecropping farmers who had been excluded from the main 1962-63 land-reform rounds, part of mopping-up the earlier land redistribution.
caveats: Extremely narrow instrument (orchards/gardens only, one article) with no dedicated chart in our index; any effect on agricultural land or output statistics is swamped by the much larger 1962-63 Land Reform Law and other structural changes. Recorded at very low confidence mainly as background context.

law_title: قانون اصلاحات ارضی (1963 Land Reform Law)
justification: Landmark law capping landlord holdings and redistributing land to peasant cultivators, fundamentally restructuring farm-size distribution, tenancy and rural labor relations; widely credited with accelerating rural-to-urban migration and altering agricultural productivity patterns through the 1960s-70s.
caveats: WDI agricultural/land series for Iran mostly start in the 1960s so there is no clean pre-reform baseline; subsequent oil-boom industrialization, the White Revolution's other components (literacy corps, profit-sharing), and 1970s urban-migration pull factors are strong confounders. WID top income/wealth shares for Iran going back this far are model-based estimates, not survey data, so any link there is speculative and kept at very low confidence.

law_title: قانون اصلاحات ارضی (1963 Land Reform Law)
justification: Land redistribution away from large landlords is one hypothesized structural driver of a long-run decline in top income/wealth concentration in WID's modeled historical series for Iran.
caveats: WID's pre-1980s Iran figures are back-cast/imputed from limited data, not direct surveys; the 1979 Revolution, nationalizations, war economy and repeated currency crises are far larger and more proximate drivers of top-share swings than this 1963 law. Confidence kept minimal.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1348 کل کشور (FY1348/1969 national budget law)
justification: Ordinary annual appropriations act setting FY1348 government spending and revenue; no dedicated chart in our index covers government budget totals for this period (our budget-totals series starts in 1992 and pre-revolution fiscal charts stop by the early 1960s).
caveats: No held chart with matching year coverage exists, so this is recorded as a non-mapping outcome rather than a real correlation.

law_title: آیین‌نامه چگونگی ایجاد مناطق آزاد تجاری - صنعتی (2014 bylaw on establishing free trade-industrial zones)
justification: Sets the administrative criteria and approval procedure for creating new free trade-industrial zones (customs and tax incentives meant to attract investment and boost trade), which over time can expand the base of firms benefiting from FTZ status.
caveats: This is a procedural/criteria bylaw, not itself a tariff or customs-rate change, so its direct measurable effect on aggregate FDI or trade flows is diffuse. Iran's FDI and trade series in this period (2014-2020s) are dominated by sanctions relief (JCPOA 2015-16) and sanctions snapback (2018), which vastly outweigh this administrative instrument.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1309 کل مملکتی (FY1309/1930 national budget law)
justification: Ordinary annual appropriations act for FY1309; no chart in our index has data coverage reaching back to 1930.
caveats: No held chart with matching year coverage exists; recorded as a non-mapping outcome.

law_title: اصلاح تصمیم‌نامه اجرایی ماده (7) قانون هدفمند کردن یارانه‌ها (2011 amendment to Article 7 Targeted Subsidies implementing decision)
justification: On inspection this is a narrow technical amendment reassigning which body sets private-sector medical service tariffs under the health-insurance implementing rules of Article 7 of the Targeted Subsidies Law; it does not itself change subsidy payout amounts or energy/bread prices.
caveats: The seed description characterizes this as directly shaping subsidy payouts, but the actual text is a narrow administrative/medical-tariff procedure change with no plausible mechanism onto any chart in our index (no health-expenditure or health-insurance chart is held).

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی تبصره (1) بند (ج) ماده (3) قانون اصل 44 (2009 Article 44 implementing bylaw on outsourcing state-enterprise services)
justification: On inspection this bylaw only authorizes state enterprises and ministries to purchase financial/technical/engineering/management services from the private sector for eleven listed state-run activity groups; Article 5 explicitly keeps investment, ownership and management of those activities with the state, so it is a procurement rule, not an ownership-transfer/divestiture instrument.
caveats: The seed description frames this as a core privatization/divestiture instrument, but the text itself preserves state ownership and only outsources ancillary services; no plausible direct link to any privatization-relevant chart in our index (we hold no state-enterprise-count or divestiture-proceeds series).

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1335 کل کشور (FY1335/1956 national budget law)
justification: The FY1335 budget's revenue schedule includes a specific line item for the state's share of oil revenue (سهمیه دولت از درآمد نفت), placing this annual appropriations act within the same 1955/56-1962/63 window covered by our oil-revenue-allocation chart.
caveats: The actual formula splitting oil income between the ordinary budget, NIOC, the Plan Organization and the oil consortium was set primarily by separate agreements/plan legislation, not by this single annual budget act; this budget law only reflects and appropriates the government's already-determined share, so the causal weight resting on this specific statute is limited.

law_title: قانون بودجه مملکتی سنه 1303 شمسی (FY1303/1925 national budget law)
justification: Ordinary annual appropriations act for FY1303 (1925), the first budget of the new Pahlavi era; no chart in our index has data coverage reaching this far back.
caveats: No held chart with matching year coverage exists; recorded as a non-mapping outcome.

law_title: بخشنامه وزارت نیرو در خصوص تعرفه‌های برق و شرایط عمومی آنها (2025 Ministry of Energy circular on electricity tariffs)
justification: Comprehensive new electricity tariff schedule effective Khordad 1404 (~May 2025), raising per-kWh rates and restructuring block pricing for households, agriculture and industry, which mechanically feeds into the housing/utilities component of consumer inflation.
caveats: Electricity is only one component of Iran's CPI basket; the 2025 inflation figure is dominated by rial depreciation, money-supply growth and sanctions-driven import costs, so the tariff circular is a contributing but not primary driver of the aggregate inflation number.

law_title: بخشنامه وزارت نیرو در خصوص تعرفه‌های برق و شرایط عمومی آنها (2025 Ministry of Energy circular on electricity tariffs, duplicate filing)
justification: Identical circular to law_id 6d47ff0f4f0e (same document, filed separately from an administrative court docket): comprehensive electricity tariff schedule effective Khordad 1404 raising per-kWh rates for households, agriculture and industry, feeding into utilities inflation.
caveats: Electricity is only one component of Iran's CPI basket; aggregate 2025 inflation is dominated by currency depreciation and monetary factors, not this tariff change alone. This record is a duplicate of the correlation filed under law_id 6d47ff0f4f0e since both law_ids point to the same underlying instrument.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي ماده 7 قانون اصلاح موادي از قانون برنامه چهارم توسعه ... و اجراي سياستهاي كلي اصل چهل و چهارم قانون اساسي
lag_description: 1-3 year lag, cumulative over the 2009-2015 period as the licensing regime was rolled out
justification: This bylaw caps business-license issuance at 10 days for a response and 1 month for final issuance, bans ad hoc/unauthorized permit requirements by state bodies, and mandates a public 'Investment Guide Book' of licensing requirements -- a deregulation instrument intended to ease private economic-activity formation nationally under the Article 44 privatization/liberalization program.
caveats: New-business formation in Iran is driven far more by macro conditions (sanctions cycles, currency devaluation, oil-revenue swings, inflation) than by licensing procedure reform; World Bank business-registration data for Iran is sparse and enforcement of this bylaw was uneven, so any observed movement in the chart is only weakly attributable to this specific instrument.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي تبصره 5 اصلاحي بند الف ماده 3 قانون اجراي سياست هاي كلي اصل 44 قانون اساسي (شهرك‌هاي صنعتي)
justification: This bylaw restructures provincial industrial-park companies as 'developmental' state subsidiaries with streamlined authority to license, land-allocate and co-invest in private industrial parks/small-industry zones, intended to ease private-sector business establishment and investment as part of the Article 44 privatization/deregulation package.
caveats: Business registration and industrial investment in Iran in this period were dominated by sanctions intensification (2012-2015) and the JCPOA-era rebound, which swamp any effect from this narrow administrative bylaw; the link to national business-formation statistics is indirect and low-confidence.

law_title: آئین نامه اجرائی فصل دوم - قانون توزیع عادلانه آب
lag_description: gradual, effects unfold over years to decades as well-drilling permits and water allocations were issued under the regime
justification: Implements Chapter 2 (and the well/qanat permitting regime of Chapter 1) of the 1968 Fair Water Distribution Law, governing licensing of groundwater extraction (chah/qanat permits) and allocation of surface and underground water -- the central input-governance instrument for irrigated agriculture in post-revolution Iran.
caveats: Iran's irrigated-land and water-stress indicators are driven jointly by dam construction, population growth, drought cycles, and decades of weakly-enforced permitting (widely documented over-extraction of groundwater); this bylaw is one governance input among many and its enforcement was inconsistent, so causal attribution to any single chart movement is weak.

law_title: قانون تعاونی نمودن تولید و یکپارچه شدن اراضی در حوزه عمل شرکتهای تعاونی روستائی
lag_description: gradual, multi-year rollout via rural cooperative programs
justification: Authorizes the Ministry of Agriculture to consolidate fragmented farm plots of rural-cooperative members into unified holdings via new irrigation networks and land-leveling, aiming to raise water/land-use efficiency and output per capita in cooperative-served villages, while preserving individual ownership.
caveats: Land consolidation under this law was implemented only in limited pilot areas and progressed slowly; national agricultural production trends in this period were dominated by the broader 1962 land reform, Green Revolution input adoption (fertilizer, mechanization), oil-boom-driven rural-urban migration, and weather variability, so this specific consolidation program's marginal effect on national output series is speculative.

law_title: قانون اصلاحات ارضی (Land Reform Law)
lag_description: multi-year to decade-long, effects unfold through the 1960s-70s as redistribution rounds proceeded
justification: Foundational land-reform statute capping individual landholding at 400 ha irrigated/800 ha unirrigated, redistributing surplus land to tenant farmers via 10-year installment purchase, and creating the High Land Reform Council/provincial commissions -- the landmark structural change to Iran's agricultural land-tenure system that preceded the 1962 White Revolution land reform decrees.
caveats: Economic historians (e.g., Karshenas, Katouzian) dispute whether land reform raised or depressed short-run agricultural productivity -- some landlords disinvested from irrigation/mechanization while some new smallholders lacked capital and cooperative support. Output and land-use series in this era are also driven by Green Revolution input diffusion, oil-boom labor migration out of agriculture, weather, and later the 1979 revolution and Iran-Iraq war, making clean attribution to this one law impossible.

law_title: قانون بازار اوراق بهادار جمهوری اسلامی ایران (Securities Market Law)
lag_description: 1-5 year lag, with market-depth growth continuing for a decade or more afterward
justification: Establishes Iran's modern capital-markets regulator (Securities and Exchange Organization) and the Supreme Council of the Bourse, replacing the old 1966 stock-exchange law and creating the legal foundation (listing rules, broker-dealer licensing, self-regulatory exchange structure) for the subsequent expansion of the Tehran Stock Exchange in listings, market capitalization, and trading volume.
caveats: TSE market-cap and turnover in Iran are heavily driven by sanctions cycles, currency depreciation (which pushes retail money into equities as an inflation hedge), privatization share offloading via Article 44, and periodic speculative bubbles (e.g., 2020) -- the 2005 law is a necessary institutional precondition but far from the sole driver of subsequent market-size swings.

law_title: قانون مبارزه با پولشویی (Anti-Money Laundering Law)
lag_description: gradual, multi-year as KYC/customer-due-diligence rules were rolled out across banks
justification: Imposes customer identification (KYC), suspicious-transaction reporting, and record-keeping obligations on banks and financial institutions, which plausibly nudged some previously informal cash holdings/transactions into formally documented bank accounts over time.
caveats: Bank-account ownership and branch/ATM growth in Iran are driven far more by financial-inclusion pushes, mobile/electronic banking rollout, urbanization, and basic banking-sector expansion than by AML compliance specifically; conversely, the law's compliance costs and FATF-related correspondent-banking frictions may have had an offsetting effect on international financial integration. This is a weak, largely speculative link.

law_title: قانون اجازه مبادله و اجرای سه فقره قرارداد نفت (Law authorizing three oil contracts, incl. 1971 Tehran Agreement)
justification: Authorizes execution of the 1971 Tehran Agreement and related participation/sales contracts between Iran and the international oil consortium, which raised posted prices and Iran's per-barrel revenue share -- a direct, documented instrument driving the sharp increase in Iranian oil-rent income in the early 1970s.
caveats: Oil rents and production in this era were also shaped by the broader 1973 OPEC embargo/price shock, Iran's own OPEC production-quota decisions, and Consortium investment behavior; production volume in particular reflects field capacity/OPEC coordination more than pricing terms alone, so the link to production volume is weaker than to oil rents/revenue.

law_title: قانون قرارداد بین کشورهای منطقه خلیج‌فارس و شرکتهای نفتی در مورد تعیین بهای نفت خام (1972 Geneva Agreement)
justification: Ratifies the January 1972 Geneva Agreement between Persian Gulf oil-producing states and the international oil companies, adjusting posted crude prices upward to compensate for US dollar devaluation -- a direct pricing instrument that raised Iran's per-barrel oil revenue.
caveats: This was a currency-adjustment top-up on top of the 1971 Tehran Agreement, part of a rapid sequence of price renegotiations culminating in the 1973-74 embargo-driven price quadrupling; isolating this specific agreement's marginal contribution to oil rents from the broader 1971-74 price trajectory is difficult.

law_title: قانون راجع بواگذاری معاملات ارزی به بانک ملی ایران (Law transferring FX transactions to Bank Melli)
lag_description: immediate imposition of exchange controls, with cumulative divergence effects over subsequent decades
justification: Centralizes all foreign-exchange buying/selling/transfer at Bank Melli Iran, mandates surrender of export FX proceeds to the bank, restricts FX dealing to licensed banks under Bank Melli's directives, and abolishes the prior Exchange Commission -- the foundational exchange-control law establishing the state-managed official FX regime that underlies Iran's long-running official-vs-parallel exchange rate gap.
caveats: The official/parallel FX gap chart spans from 1937 (pre-dating this law) through 2026 and is driven far more by later episodes (1979 revolution capital controls, 1990s multiple-rate unification attempts, 2010s sanctions-driven rial collapse) than by this single 1958 centralization law; this law is best read as establishing the institutional architecture (state FX monopoly) that made a persistent official-parallel gap possible, not as directly causing any specific later widening.

law_title: قانون مالیات بر ارزش افزوده (VAT Law)
justification: Imposes a unified 9% VAT on goods and services with quarterly filing and an electronic invoicing/mo'addian system (Arts. 4-8), directly constituting Iran's tax-on-goods-and-services revenue stream and updating the earlier 2008 VAT act.
caveats: Oil revenue and exchange-rate valuation dominate swings in total government revenue; the WDI Government Finance series held here are lagged/sparse and may not yet cover fiscal years after 2021.

law_title: قانون مالیات بر ارزش افزوده (VAT Law)
justification: A national VAT is passed through to retail prices to some degree; the 2021 law re-legislated the VAT regime at a rate (9%) close to its predecessor's, so it is at most one of several price-level drivers.
caveats: Inflation in Iran over this period is overwhelmingly driven by exchange-rate depreciation and money-supply growth under sanctions, not VAT changes; a single-digit VAT rate has only a marginal, hard-to-isolate effect on aggregate CPI/WPI.

law_title: قانون مالیاتهای مستقیم (Direct Taxation Law)
lag_description: gradual, multi-year (foundational code, amended repeatedly since 1988)
justification: Foundational national tax code establishing the income, corporate-profit and property tax bases and rates that remain (with amendments) the legal basis of Iran's direct-tax revenue.
caveats: The law has been amended dozens of times since 1988 (rates, brackets, exemptions), so any single-year revenue movement reflects later amendments, enforcement/compliance changes, inflation bracket-creep and oil-revenue substitution as much as the original 1988 text.

law_title: قانون مالیاتهای مستقیم (Direct Taxation Law, duplicate copy)
lag_description: gradual, multi-year (foundational code, amended repeatedly since 1988)
justification: Duplicate text of the foundational Direct Taxation Law establishing the income, corporate-profit and property tax bases and rates that remain the legal basis of Iran's direct-tax revenue.
caveats: Amended dozens of times since 1988; any observed revenue movement reflects later amendments and enforcement/oil-revenue substitution as much as this original text. Same underlying instrument as law_id 0bbd4860ceaa.

law_title: قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Export/Import Regulations Law)
lag_description: annual (tariff/licensing schedules revised yearly under this law's authority)
justification: Master framework law establishing Iran's foreign-trade regime: import/export licensing, prohibited/restricted-goods classification, and the mechanism by which an annually revised tariff book and import/export regulation are issued (Arts. 1-3), governing virtually all merchandise trade since 1993.
caveats: Actual trade volumes and composition are driven at least as much by sanctions regimes, oil-export revenue, and the exchange-rate/import-financing system as by this framework law; the annually revised tariff schedules it authorizes are not separately captured in this database.

law_title: قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Export/Import Regulations Law, duplicate copy)
lag_description: annual (tariff/licensing schedules revised yearly under this law's authority)
justification: Duplicate text of the master Export/Import Regulations Law establishing Iran's licensing, prohibited/restricted-goods lists and annual tariff-book mechanism governing merchandise trade since 1993.
caveats: Same instrument as law_id 781e8c3880d3; trade volumes are driven at least as much by sanctions, oil revenue and FX policy as by this framework law.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال ۱۴۰۴ کل کشور - بخش اول (FY1404/2025 Annual Budget Law, Part 1)
justification: Sets the customs-valuation exchange rate off the official Gold and FX Exchange Center rate and mandates a 1404 reform of the preferential ('tarjihi') FX rate paired with a compensating subsidy for lower-income households, directly targeting Iran's official/preferential exchange-rate structure; it is also itself the law setting overall general-government resource and expenditure ceilings for the year.
caveats: The held FX chart's 2025 values will reflect sanctions, oil-export access and central-bank market intervention far more than this single provision, and the held annual-budget-totals chart currently runs only to 2022 (SH1401) so may not yet include FY1404 figures; oil/gas-condensate export revenue-sharing formulas set elsewhere in this law drive the largest swings in budget totals.

law_title: قانون تضمین خرید محصولات اساسی کشاورزی (Guaranteed Purchase Price Law for Basic Agricultural Products)
justification: Obliges the government to announce and guarantee minimum purchase prices annually for a named list of staple crops (wheat, rice, barley, maize, sugar beet, cotton, oilseeds, tea, potato, onion, pulses, raisins/grapes, dates, citrus, apples, pomegranates, figs, livestock/dairy products, silk cocoons) and to buy at that floor - a direct, named government price-support instrument.
caveats: World/regional commodity prices, input-cost inflation, exchange-rate depreciation, drought and sanctions on agrochemical/machinery imports are major independent drivers of the same producer-price series; FAOSTAT producer prices are not identical to the official guaranteed procurement price (only the dedicated wheat chart tracks that price directly), and enforcement/funding of the guarantee has varied by year.

law_title: قانون چگونگی اداره مناطق آزاد تجاری-صنعتی جمهوری اسلامی ایران (Free Trade-Industrial Zones Administration Law)
justification: Establishes the legal and administrative framework (zone high council and zone organizations) for Iran's free trade-industrial zones (e.g., Kish, Qeshm, Chabahar), which are Iran's principal vehicles for attracting recorded foreign investment and duty-free trade.
caveats: Provincial FDI data also reflects sanctions relief/re-imposition cycles, currency instability, and zone-specific administrative changes made long after 1993; no chart in this database isolates free-zone activity specifically, so this is a diffuse institutional link rather than a measured effect.

law_title: قانون تشویق صادرات و تولید (Law Encouraging Exports and Production)
justification: Early Pahlavi-era export-promotion law exempting exports from internal levies and machinery-import duties and creating the chamber-of-commerce export/import licensing ('kart-e bazargani') system that still underlies Iran's export administration.
caveats: Most substantive provisions have since been repealed or superseded by later export/import and customs legislation (several articles are explicitly annotated as repealed/منسوخه in the source text); no chart tracks export-institution history specifically, and the law predates almost all held trade-volume series.

law_title: قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Export/Import Regulations Law, 1987 version)
lag_description: annual (tariff schedules revised yearly), superseded by the 1993 law
justification: War-economy-era predecessor to the 1993 Export/Import Regulations Law, setting the same style of annually-revised licensing/tariff framework for trade during the final years of the Iran-Iraq war.
caveats: Superseded by the 1993 consolidation (law_id 781e8c3880d3); Iran-Iraq war destruction, the 1986 oil-price collapse and wartime FX rationing (see the 1985 FX budget law) are the dominant drivers of trade volumes in this period, not this regulatory text itself.

law_title: قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات سال ۱۳۶۵ (Export/Import Regulations Law, 1986 version)
lag_description: annual (tariff schedules revised yearly), superseded by later versions
justification: Earlier war-economy predecessor to the 1993 Export/Import Regulations Law, setting an annually-revised licensing/tariff framework during the height of the Iran-Iraq war and the 1986 oil-price collapse.
caveats: Superseded by later versions (1987, 1993); Iran-Iraq war destruction, the 1986 oil-price collapse and wartime FX rationing are the dominant drivers of trade volumes in this period, not this regulatory text itself.

law_title: قانون بودجه ارزی سال ۱۳۶۴ کل کشور (FX Budget Law, FY1364/1985)
lag_description: same year, effects persisting through the multi-year war economy
justification: Wartime law creating a committee-run rationed/multiple exchange-rate allocation system for $15bn in FX receipts, prioritizing defense, then basic goods, industry and other sectors - a direct institutional driver of Iran's chronic official-vs-parallel exchange-rate gap.
caveats: The Iran-Iraq war itself, the 1986 oil-price collapse, and later sanctions episodes are large independent drivers of the same official/parallel gap in subsequent years; this law governs only FY1364 (1985/86) and similar annual FX-budget laws were passed for other years.

law_title: قانون بودجه ارزی سال ۱۳۶۴ کل کشور (FX Budget Law, FY1364/1985)
justification: Rations scarce foreign exchange to import categories by administrative priority (defense first, then basic goods, industry, private-sector inputs), directly constraining import volumes and composition during the war economy.
caveats: Iran-Iraq war logistics/blockade and the 1986 oil-price collapse independently suppressed the FX receipts being rationed in the first place, so import declines in this period cannot be attributed to the allocation mechanism alone.

law_title: قانون تشکیل وزارت اصلاحات ارضی و تعاون روستائی (Law Establishing the Ministry of Land Reform and Rural Cooperation)
justification: Creates the ministry tasked with executing the Shah's land-reform program (redistributing land from large landlords, forming rural cooperatives and agricultural joint-stock companies), the administrative vehicle for one of the White Revolution's central planks; over the following decade this reshaped farm structure and could plausibly show up in aggregate crop/livestock output and yield trends.
caveats: This 1967 law only creates the implementing ministry — the substantive land redistribution had already begun under the 1962 Land Reform Law, which is not among the mapped instruments. Iran's agricultural output in this era was also driven by Green Revolution input adoption (fertilizer, irrigation expansion), weather variability, and rural-to-urban migration during the oil boom, all of which confound any land-reform effect. Economic historians dispute whether land reform raised or depressed farm productivity in the short run.

law_title: قانون اصلاح قانون تضمین خرید محصولات اساسی کشاورزی (Amendment extending the Guaranteed Agricultural Purchase-Price Law to raisins, dates, dried apricots, citrus, apples, pomegranates, figs, animal products and silk cocoon)
justification: Directly adds these specific crops/products to the state's guaranteed floor-purchase-price list under the 1989 Basic Agricultural Products Purchase-Guarantee Law, meaning the government sets and defends a minimum farm-gate price for each, which typically puts upward/stabilizing pressure on producer prices relative to a free market.
caveats: FAOSTAT producer-price series reflect market outcomes shaped by many factors besides the guarantee (weather/harvest size, input costs, exchange-rate pass-through, informal-market dynamics, general inflation); the guaranteed price is a floor, not always the binding market price, so correlation with observed price levels is only partial evidence of the law's effect.

law_title: تصویب آیین‌نامه‌هایی در اجرای سیاست‌های کلی اصل ۴۴ قانون اساسی (Implementing bylaws for the Article 44 privatization program: preferential-share transfers to managers/workers, Justice Shares distribution, road/railway private-sector share)
justification: Operationalizes the headline Article 44 divestiture program — rules for transferring 'Justice Shares' (سهام عدالت) to citizens, preferential shares to employees/managers of privatized firms, and the state/private split in road and rail activity — a core instrument of Iran's 2000s-2010s privatization push.
caveats: We hold no chart tracking Iran's privatization/state-enterprise-divestiture volume, capital-market ownership structure, or Justice Shares specifically, so no chart can be tied to this instrument with confidence; any observed effect would run diffusely through capital-market and industry charts that are also driven by sanctions, oil prices, and macro policy over the same period. No record is returned for lack of a matching chart.

law_title: قانون تعدیل و تثبیت اجاره‌بها (National Rent Adjustment and Stabilization Law — rent freeze/cap tied to March 1972 index)
justification: Directly bars rent-increase lawsuits and caps allowable rent at a March-1972 CPI-indexed base, a national rent-price-control instrument intended to suppress housing rental inflation during the pre-revolution boom.
caveats: We hold no chart with an Iranian rent-price index or housing-cost series for the 1970s (the only Housing-category chart is a five-point census of housing units, not price data), so no chart can be mapped to this law despite the mechanism being a textbook direct price control. No record is returned for lack of a matching chart.

law_title: لایحه قانونی راجع به نحوه بهره‌برداری از معادن کشور (Post-revolution law on mine exploitation — state takeover/administration of forfeited mining licenses via a special prosecutorial task force)
justification: Establishes a state task force to seize and directly operate mines whose licenses are revoked, part of the broader nationalization/state-control wave over extractive industries in the immediate post-revolutionary period, when mining output fell sharply amid the revolution, war and capital flight.
caveats: Mining production in 1979-1981 collapsed for many overlapping reasons — the revolution itself, the outbreak of the Iran-Iraq War, sanctions, capital flight, and loss of technical/foreign staff — so attributing the decline specifically to this administrative mine-seizure mechanism (rather than the general post-revolutionary state-control push) is speculative.

law_title: تصمیم قانونی دائر بملی شدن صنعت نفت (Parliamentary decision confirming/extending study of the nationalization of the oil industry)
lag_description: immediate collapse 1951-53, reversal after 1954 Consortium Agreement
justification: This is the legislative act ratifying the Oil Commission's nationalization decision (the seed of the March 1951 Oil Nationalization Law), triggering the AIOC/British boycott and blockade of Iranian oil exports that collapsed production for roughly three years until the 1954 Consortium Agreement restored output under new revenue-sharing terms.
caveats: The 1953 coup, the Anglo-American embargo, and Cold War geopolitics are deeply entangled with this event and are arguably more proximate causes of the specific production collapse and its 1954 reversal than this particular procedural parliamentary act; disentangling this law from the broader Mossadegh-era nationalization crisis is not possible from the text alone.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال ۱۳۴۵ (National Annual Budget Law for FY1345/1966)
justification: Sets the national government's spending and revenue plan for FY1966, a routine annual fiscal instrument rather than a structural policy change.
caveats: We hold no chart with government budget totals, revenue or expenditure data covering the mid-1960s (the closest chart, iranplanbudgetorg__annual_budget_law_totals, only starts in 1992; Pahlavi-era Government Finance charts cover only 1955-63 oil-revenue allocation, which predates this budget year). No record is returned for lack of a matching chart.

law_title: قانون اصلاح قانون برنامه سوم توسعه و قانون بودجه سال ۱۳۷۹ (Amendment creating/formalizing the Oil Stabilization Fund — Foreign Exchange Reserve Account for crude-export revenue — plus FY1379 budget line adjustments)
justification: Article 60 of the Third Development Plan (as amended here) mandates that crude-oil export revenue above the plan's forecast be held in a new 'Foreign Exchange Reserve Account' at the Central Bank rather than spent immediately, and restricts drawing on it to cover only oil-revenue shortfalls (not non-oil deficits) — the founding instrument of what later became Iran's National Development Fund, intended to reduce fiscal reliance on volatile oil income and government borrowing from the central bank.
caveats: Total central-bank reserves and government debt to the CBI are driven far more by oil-price cycles, sanctions, and monetary policy than by this single accounting mechanism; the Fund's actual insulating effect was frequently overridden by later laws permitting withdrawals for general budget support, so any link to aggregate reserves/debt trends is indirect and weak.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال ۱۳۴۴ (National Annual Budget Law for FY1344/1965)
justification: Sets the national government's spending and revenue plan for FY1965, a routine annual fiscal instrument.
caveats: No chart in the index provides government budget totals or fiscal aggregates for the mid-1960s. No record is returned for lack of a matching chart.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی شرایط و تضمین خرید برق (Executive bylaw setting terms and guaranteed feed-in tariffs for electricity purchase from independent power producers, incl. renewable/waste-heat incentive payments)
justification: Establishes Iran's wholesale electricity market rules and guaranteed purchase tariffs for private/independent power producers, with explicit extra incentive payments (Article 10) for renewable-energy and waste-heat-recovery plants, an instrument specifically designed to grow private and renewable generation capacity.
caveats: Renewable electricity's share of Iran's generation mix remained very small for years after this bylaw and was also shaped by subsequent feed-in-tariff laws, global renewable-technology cost declines, sanctions on equipment imports, and cheap domestic fossil-fuel/electricity pricing that undercut renewables' competitiveness — so this single 2005 bylaw is at most one contributing factor among several.

law_title: قانون جلوگیری از افزایش قیمتها تا پایان برنامه سوم توسعه (Law Halting Price Increases Through the End of the Third Development Plan)
justification: A direct, single-article national price-control instrument: it retroactively cancels all state-sector price/tariff increases made outside the Third Plan's own rules and redirects budget savings toward subsidizing basic goods, an explicit anti-inflation measure named in the text as being 'in execution of inflation-control policies'.
caveats: Iran's CPI/WPI trajectory in the early 2000s was also driven by monetary expansion (liquidity growth), the managed exchange-rate regime, oil-revenue inflows, and structural subsidy costs; a freeze on formal state-sector prices does not necessarily control the broader consumer basket, and the law itself expired with the Third Plan (~2004), limiting any lasting effect.

law_title: اصلاح ماده ۵ آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده ۱۰ قانون هدفمندکردن یارانه‌ها (Amendment renaming provincial-committee official titles in the Article 10 subsidy-targeting fund bylaw)
justification: This specific 2025 amendment only renames which provincial official chairs the local subsidy-fund committee (from the governor's planning deputy to the head of the provincial Management and Planning Organization); it does not change any subsidy amount, energy price, or transfer payment.
caveats: The parent instrument (Article 10 of the 2010 Targeted Subsidies Law, which allocates subsidy-reform savings to production/infrastructure support) is a major economic policy, but this particular amendment is a bureaucratic title change with no direct price or fiscal mechanism, and none of the fuel-price, subsidy-transfer, or CPI charts we hold extend to 2025 in a way that could evidence its effect. No record is returned for lack of both a substantive mechanism and a matching chart.

law_title: اعطاي امتياز نفت قسمتي از شرق وشمال شرق به شركت نفت آمريكا و ايران (Amiranian Oil concession, eastern/NE Iran)
justification: Granted the American-Iranian Oil Company (Amiranian/Seaboard) an exploration and production concession over eastern/northeastern Iran, outside AIOC's southern concession area.
caveats: This concession never produced commercially significant oil -- the company drilled exploratory wells in the following years but found no exploitable deposits and effectively abandoned the area by the early 1940s. National oil production and export figures for this period (e.g., owid__oil_production_volume) are driven almost entirely by AIOC's southern fields, not this concession. No chart in the index isolates eastern/NE Iran output, so there is no chart this specific instrument can be credibly tied to; recording a link to national oil totals would misattribute AIOC-driven growth to this law.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1370 كل كشور (National Annual Budget Law, FY1370)
justification: The annual budget law is the primary legal instrument fixing central-government expenditure and revenue totals for FY1370, the first post-Iran-Iraq-War / First Five-Year Development Plan budget; WDI's Iran government-finance (GC.*) series begins exactly in 1972 and covers 1991, and SIPRI-based military expenditure share data begins in 1990, both spanning this fiscal year.
caveats: WDI general-government-finance figures reflect executed/reported outturn, not the enacted law's line items, and can diverge substantially from the appropriated budget due to inflation, exchange-rate distortions (multiple official rates in this era), and post-war reconstruction spending pressures. Reconstruction needs and oil-revenue recovery after the 1988 ceasefire are independent drivers of the same expenditure/revenue growth.

law_title: قانون اصلاح موادي از قانون مالياتهاي مستقيم (Amendment of the Direct Taxes Law)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag (tax-code changes affect the following fiscal years' collections)
justification: A comprehensive overhaul of Iran's Direct Taxes Law (revised inheritance-tax brackets, repeal/consolidation of dozens of articles, and later a unified flat corporate/legal-entity tax rate). This is a documented, named tax-policy instrument directly acting on income/profit tax administration and rates, which is exactly what the WDI GC.TAX.YPKG (taxes on income, profits & capital gains) and GC.TAX.TOTL (total tax revenue) series measure; IRN data for these series runs through the relevant post-2001 years.
caveats: Tax revenue in nominal/real terms is also driven by oil-price cycles, exchange-rate movements (rial depreciation raises nominal LCU-denominated revenue), inflation, and enforcement/compliance capacity independent of the statutory rate changes; the WDI series is an aggregate outturn measure, not a rate series, so it conflates rate design with base growth and collection efficiency.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1336 كل كشور (National Annual Budget Law, FY1336)
justification: The annual budget law for FY1336 (1957/58) set the government's appropriation of anticipated oil revenue between the general Treasury budget, NIOC, and the Plan Organization -- the exact split shown in these two Pahlavi-era oil-revenue-allocation charts, whose windows (1955/56-1962/63 and 1957-1959) directly overlap this fiscal year.
caveats: The actual allocation split was also shaped by separate Plan Organization legislation (the Second Development Plan) and by AIOC/Consortium production and price decisions outside the budget law's control; this is a plausible but not exclusive channel, and confidence is limited because only the law's structural table of contents (not line-by-line appropriations) was available to read.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1357 كل كشور (National Annual Budget Law, FY1357)
lag_description: same fiscal year, with mid-year revisions
justification: This is the base annual budget law for FY1357 (the last full pre-revolution fiscal year); the chart 'Mid-Year Supplementary Budget Additions' explicitly tracks FY1357 add-ons made against this baseline, and WDI's IRN government-finance series (which starts in 1972) also spans 1978.
caveats: 1978 was a year of acute political upheaval (mass strikes, oil-worker walkouts, capital flight) that disrupted both revenue collection and planned spending well beyond what the budget law itself dictated; supplementary additions reflect political/fiscal improvisation as much as the original law's design.

law_title: قانون قرارداد شركت نفت خليج فارس (Gulf Oil Company [Persian Gulf offshore] Contract Law)
lag_description: gradual, 3-10 year lag (offshore field development and first production)
justification: Ratifies a joint-structure petroleum contract (NIOC partnering with a foreign operator under the post-1957 Petroleum Act model) for offshore Persian Gulf exploration and production, adding a new production stream alongside the Consortium's onshore fields, contributing to Iran's rising national oil output and fuel-export share through the late 1960s-70s.
caveats: National oil production and fuel-export-share growth in this period was overwhelmingly driven by the Consortium's (former AIOC) onshore fields and by rising world oil demand/OPEC-era pricing; this single offshore joint-venture contract was one of several new contracts signed in the 1960s (Iran also signed with ENI/AGIP, Pan American, etc.) and its own contribution cannot be isolated in an aggregate national series.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي قانون تنظيم بازار غيرمتشكل پولي (Executive Bylaw, Law on Regulating the Informal Money Market)
justification: Requires any person or entity conducting deposit-taking or lending ('banking operations') to obtain a Central Bank license, directing police to shut down unlicensed operators; this formalization mechanism plausibly shifted some previously unmeasured informal-sector credit/deposits into the officially measured banking system, affecting recorded money-supply and private-credit aggregates.
caveats: Iran's shadow/informal credit market (gray-market money lenders, unlicensed 'credit institutions') persisted and grew substantially after this bylaw despite it, particularly the unlicensed-institution crisis of the 2010s; measured M2/private-credit growth in this period is dominated by CBI monetary-base creation, sanctions-driven dollarization, and licensed non-bank credit institution expansion rather than enforcement against informal lenders.

law_title: تصویبنامه اصلاح آیین‌نامه نحوه واگذاری سهام ترجیحی (Amendment to the Preferential-Share Transfer Bylaw, Article 44 policy)
justification: A narrow technical amendment extending eligibility for discounted 'preferential shares' to employees of subsidiaries of privatized firms and setting a 10-year installment-payment schedule; part of the broader Article 44 privatization apparatus that moved large state enterprises onto the Tehran Stock Exchange and is a plausible minor contributor to listed market capitalization in the early 2010s.
caveats: This specific amendment only adjusts payment terms and eligibility for one privatization mechanism among many; Iran's stock-market capitalization in this period was overwhelmingly driven by the broader Article 44 divestment wave, subsidy-reform-driven inflation, rial depreciation, and sanctions-driven capital flight into equities as an inflation hedge -- this procedural bylaw's own effect is not separable from those larger forces, hence very low confidence.

law_title: لايحه بودجه سال 1360 (National Budget Bill, FY1360)
justification: The government budget bill for FY1360 set planned central-government spending and revenue for the second full year of the Iran-Iraq War; WDI's Iran government-finance series (starting 1972) spans this year, showing sharply rising nominal expenditure consistent with wartime budget growth.
caveats: This is recorded as a 'bill' (لايحه) in the source archive, so its exact enactment/amendment path is not confirmed from the text alone. Far more of the expenditure/revenue movement in FY1360 is attributable to the Iran-Iraq War itself (military mobilization, war damage, oil-export disruption from Kharg Island attacks) than to the budget law's own design; the war is the dominant confound for nearly every fiscal aggregate in this year.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1374 كل كشور (National Budget Law FY1374/1995-96)
justification: The FY1374 budget law is one of the annual instruments directly aggregated into this chart's revenue/expenditure totals series (which spans 1992-2022); the law's approved figures for that year are the data point itself, not merely a correlate.
caveats: This is a definitional/compilation relationship rather than an external policy shock; the chart is literally built from the sequence of annual budget laws including this one.

law_title: قانون اصلاح قانون انحصار دخانيات (Tobacco State Monopoly Law Amendment, 1932)
lag_description: same year, effects persisting through the chart's 1890-1995 span
justification: The law converts the earlier Regie-style tobacco concession into a fully state-run purchasing/production monopoly (Dokhaniyat), fixing mandatory government purchase prices by tobacco type/region and import duties on cigarette paper -- exactly the institutional transition this chart tracks.
caveats: The chart is sparse (irregular snapshot years) so the precise magnitude of the 1932 amendment's effect on workforce/output cannot be cleanly isolated from later amendments to the same monopoly law.

law_title: قانون اصلاح قانون انحصار دخانيات (Tobacco State Monopoly Law Amendment, 1932)
lag_description: multi-decade lag (law predates chart window by ~20 years)
justification: The 1954-59 cigarette production data reflects the state monopoly production system whose legal foundation (mandatory state purchase of leaf tobacco, state control of manufacturing equipment) was set by this 1932 amendment.
caveats: By the 1950s the monopoly had been through several further amendments and reorganizations; production levels in this window are more directly driven by demand growth, urbanization, and later regulations than by the 1932 text specifically.

law_title: قانون بودجه كل سال 1313 مملكتي (National Budget Law FY1313/1934-35)
justification: No chart in the index covers government revenue/expenditure totals, oil-revenue allocation, or customs receipts for the 1930s; the earliest comparable series (oil revenue allocation, banking statistics) begin in 1955.
caveats: No credible chart match exists in our current chart index for this early Reza Shah-era budget law; omitted as a real correlation record per instructions (no chart_ids/categories set).

law_title: قانون ساختار نظام جامع رفاه و تأمين اجتماعي (Comprehensive Welfare & Social Security System Structure Law, 2004)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag (institutional restructuring takes time to show in coverage/payout data)
justification: The law establishes a unified national welfare/social-security governance structure (High Council, coordination among insurance funds and relief institutions) intended to expand and standardize coverage; the pensioner-count and benefits-paid series for the Social Security Organization in the years immediately following (2004-2006) is a plausible downstream indicator.
caveats: Growth in SSO pensioners/benefits over this period is also driven by workforce aging, wage/pension indexation rules, and unrelated SSO administrative expansion, so the specific contribution of this 2004 structural law cannot be isolated from the trend.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1338 كل كشور (National Budget Law FY1338/1959-60)
justification: Article 1 of this budget law explicitly re-sets the statutory division of national oil revenue for 1338 among the Plan Organization, the National Iranian Oil Company, and the Treasury (amending the prior 1336 allocation law) -- the exact subject this chart tracks.
caveats: The chart's stated window (1955/56-1962/63) may reflect a running allocation formula rather than year-by-year re-legislation, so the 1338 law's marginal effect on the specific FY1338 data point versus the underlying multi-year formula is hard to separate.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1364 كل كشور (National Budget Law FY1364/1985-86, wartime budget)
lag_description: same fiscal year, cumulative over the war years
justification: The law mandates large central-bank cash advances (تنخواه) to procure subsidized basic goods (wheat, sugar, fertilizer, cotton, rice, etc.) and directly earmarks budget lines for war expenditure and defense procurement financed through the banking system, explicitly referencing outstanding central bank claims on government agencies from prior years that must be settled -- a direct wartime deficit-monetization mechanism.
caveats: Government debt to the central bank over 1978-2016 is driven by many other factors (oil price collapse in the mid-1980s, sanctions-constrained hard-currency revenue, hyperinflation-era financing needs in other years); this single annual budget law is one of many years' worth of similar wartime budgets contributing to the same trend, not a unique shock.

law_title: قانون تشكيل شركت سهامي معادن مس سرچشمه كرمان (Sarcheshmeh Kerman Copper Mines Company Establishment Law, 1972)
lag_description: gradual, 1-5 year lag (mine/smelter construction and ramp-up)
justification: The law creates the state-owned company mandated to develop and operate the Sarcheshmeh deposit, Iran's largest copper reserve; the chart's mine-output, smelter-output and refined-copper series for the 1970s reflects the direct output of this newly chartered entity as it brought the mine and smelter online.
caveats: Production ramp-up also depended on foreign technical/financing partnerships and global copper prices in the 1970s, and the chart itself has gaps (1966/67, 1971/72) that complicate isolating the exact post-1972 jump.

law_title: آيين‌نامه اجرايي ماده 7 قانون هدفمند كردن يارانه‌ها (Executive Bylaw for Article 7 of the Subsidy Targeting Law, 2010)
lag_description: same fiscal year onset (late 2010), effects unfolding over 2011-2017
justification: This bylaw operationalizes the resource-allocation side (cash/in-kind transfers, discriminatory utility pricing, welfare programs funded from subsidy savings) of Iran's landmark December 2010 subsidy reform, whose companion price-setting provisions sharply raised energy/fuel prices; the domestic oil-product consumption series and the CPI series for 2010-2012 are the standard indicators cited for this reform's impact.
caveats: This specific bylaw governs downstream distribution of subsidy savings (welfare/cash-transfer programs), not the fuel/electricity price-setting itself, which is set by companion instruments under the same parent law -- the causal chain to consumption/price charts runs through the broader reform package, not this bylaw alone. The 2010-2012 period also saw intensifying international sanctions and currency depreciation, which independently drove inflation and consumption patterns.

law_title: آيين‌نامه اجرايي ماده 7 قانون هدفمند كردن يارانه‌ها (Executive Bylaw for Article 7 of the Subsidy Targeting Law, 2010)
justification: Article 7 directs a share of subsidy savings to welfare programs (Imam Khomeini Relief Committee, Welfare Organization, housing/marriage/nutrition assistance for low-income households), which could plausibly have supported poverty reduction visible in the 2011-2020 provincial poverty comparison.
caveats: Highly multi-causal over this decade: sanctions, currency collapse (2012, 2018), and the universal cash subsidy (a much larger and separate cash-transfer channel than the Article 7 welfare allocations) all interact with poverty trends; this bylaw's specific marginal contribution cannot be isolated.

law_title: لايحه قانون ملي شدن موسسات بيمه و موسسات اعتباري (Nationalization of Banks and Insurance Institutions Law, 1979)
lag_description: immediate for ownership status; persists for decades
justification: This 1979 decree nationalized all private banks and insurance companies; the state-dominated ownership structure of the Iranian insurance market visible in the 2006-2013 chart (State vs. Private companies) is a direct, multi-decade legacy of this nationalization, only beginning to erode with post-2000 private insurer licensing.
caveats: By 2006-2013 partial privatization/re-licensing of private insurers had already begun, so the chart shows the tail end of the nationalization's effect being gradually reversed, not its peak.

law_title: لايحه قانون ملي شدن موسسات بيمه و موسسات اعتباري (Nationalization of Banks and Insurance Institutions Law, 1979)
justification: Nationalization consolidated all deposit-taking institutions under state ownership and control, plausibly contributing to structural shifts in deposit-money-bank asset shares and bank-deposits-to-GDP ratios visible in these long-run (1961-2016) series around the revolution.
caveats: The 1979-1981 period also includes the revolution itself, capital flight, the start of the Iran-Iraq war, and general macroeconomic disruption, all of which independently affected banking-sector aggregates; the nationalization's isolated contribution to these ratios is not cleanly identifiable.

law_title: طرح قانوني دائر بطرز اجراي اصل ملي شدن صنعت نفت در سراسر كشور (Oil Nationalization Implementation Bill, 1951)
justification: This bill implemented the nationalization of the Anglo-Iranian Oil Company's operations; the subsequent British-led boycott and export blockade caused Iranian oil production to collapse from 1951 through 1953, recovering only after the 1954 Consortium Agreement -- one of the best-documented policy-driven production shocks in Iranian economic history.
caveats: The 1953 coup and subsequent consortium negotiations, not the nationalization bill alone, determined the timing of the recovery; the production collapse is a joint product of the nationalization decision and the international response to it.

law_title: طرح قانوني دائر بطرز اجراي اصل ملي شدن صنعت نفت در سراسر كشور (Oil Nationalization Implementation Bill, 1951)
justification: Nationalization directly targeted foreign (British) control of the oil industry's workforce and management, and the chart's nationality breakdown for oil-industry employment (1939-1960) directly captures the resulting shift in the British-vs-Iranian employment composition through the nationalization crisis and its 1954 resolution.
caveats: Employment figures also reflect the general contraction of oil operations during the 1951-53 boycott (fewer total jobs of any nationality), not only compositional shifts.

law_title: طرح قانوني دائر بطرز اجراي اصل ملي شدن صنعت نفت در سراسر كشور (Oil Nationalization Implementation Bill, 1951)
justification: The revenue-sharing structure among the Plan Organization, NIOC, and Treasury visible from 1955/56 onward exists in the institutional form it does because the 1954 Consortium Agreement -- the eventual resolution of the nationalization crisis -- restored oil exports and created NIOC's role as the counterparty for revenue allocation.
caveats: The chart's window begins after the crisis was already resolved, so it mainly reflects the post-settlement institutional baseline rather than the nationalization event itself.

law_title: قانون زمين شهري (Urban Land Law, 1987)
justification: The law gave government authority to appropriate and redistribute idle/vacant urban land for housing development, restricting private land dealing; the 1996 census point in this chart (9 years after enactment) is the first census to reflect any such supply-side effect on housing-unit formation.
caveats: Housing-unit growth between the 1986 and 1996 census points is dominated by population growth, post-war (Iran-Iraq war) urban reconstruction and rural-to-urban migration, and the effect of urban land restriction on private construction is contested in the literature (critics argue it suppressed private supply and pushed prices up rather than expanding housing units); no land-price chart exists in our index to test that channel directly.

law_title: قانون تقسیم و فروش املاک مورد اجاره بزارعین مستاجر (Law on Division and Sale of Tenant-Leased Farmland, 1969)
justification: This is the final-stage land reform law forcing landlords to sell tenant-leased farmland to the cultivating tenant farmers, restructuring rural land ownership; wheat production (Iran's principal staple crop, grown mostly on the smallholdings affected) is the standard proxy economists use to assess the reform's productivity effects.
caveats: The land-reform literature on Iran is split on whether fragmentation into smallholdings raised or depressed near-term productivity; weather variability, irrigation investment, guaranteed-price policy, and the broader White Revolution mechanization push are all confounding drivers of wheat output in this period, so this link is deliberately low-confidence.

law_title: قانون تقسیم و فروش املاک مورد اجاره بزارعین مستاجر (Law on Division and Sale of Tenant-Leased Farmland, 1969)
justification: Post-land-reform smallholder farmers, newly holding formal title, were targeted by parallel government programs (extension services, farm corporations, credit) encouraging modern input adoption including chemical fertilizer, consistent with the era's rising fertilizer-consumption trend.
caveats: Fertilizer consumption growth in 1970s Iran is primarily attributed to the broader Green Revolution input-subsidy and extension push rather than the ownership-transfer law specifically; this is a background/contextual link, not a direct instrument.

law_title: آيين‌نامه اجرايي تشخيص، انطباق و طبقه‌بندي فعاليت‌ها و بنگاه‌هاي اقتصادي (اصل 44)
lag_description: same year to a few years after each transfer decree
justification: This bylaw is the operative implementing regulation for Article 44 privatization: it classifies every state enterprise into Group 1 (permanent state monopoly), Group 2, or Group 3 (transferable) via cabinet-approved annexes, and subsequent cabinet decrees moved specific firms between annexes to enable sale. The 2012 privatization of Iran's state tobacco monopoly (referenced directly in the chart title) occurred under this classification/transfer mechanism.
caveats: The chart is a single 2018 snapshot documenting the post-privatization market, so it cannot show a time-series break; other liberalization measures and market entrants also shaped post-privatization consumption/import patterns.

law_title: آيين‌نامه اجرايي تشخيص، انطباق و طبقه‌بندي فعاليت‌ها و بنگاه‌هاي اقتصادي (اصل 44)
lag_description: 1-5 year lag as state shares were transferred via IPOs/OTC block sales
justification: The bylaw's annexes placed insurance companies and many industrial/mining firms in transferable groups, and Article 44 policy mandated using the Tehran Stock Exchange as the primary privatization channel; large blocks of state-owned insurers and industrial holdings were floated or sold via TSE/OTC in the following years, mechanically raising both the share of private ownership in sectors like insurance and listed market capitalization.
caveats: Market capitalization is also driven by liquidity conditions, inflation-driven equity flight, sanctions-era capital controls, and IPO cycles unrelated to Article 44; the insurance-ownership chart reflects only 1385-1391 and cannot be attributed solely to this one bylaw among several Article 44 implementing instruments.

law_title: قانون اصلاح موادي از قانون مالياتهاي مستقيم مصوب 3 اسفند 1366
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-3 years as new provisions phased in (1370/1371)
justification: This is a direct, article-by-article overhaul of Iran's Direct Taxation Law (corporate income tax, exemptions for religious/charitable income, withholding provisions), passed 1371 SH (1992) with staggered effective dates (1368-1371). Changes to statutory rates, brackets, and exemptions mechanically move aggregate tax revenue.
caveats: Direction is ambiguous because the amendment both broadened some exemptions and tightened others; overall tax revenue as % of GDP in this period was also driven by oil-revenue substitution, inflation (bracket creep), and post-war reconstruction spending needs, and WDI government-finance coverage for Iran in the early 1990s is sparse.

law_title: اصلاح سود بازرگاني و زيرنويس تعرفه‌ها (آيين‌نامه اجرايي مقررات صادرات و واردات)
justification: This 2002 cabinet decree directly amends 'sud-e bazargani' (commercial-profit surcharge, a component of Iran's effective import duty) and tariff sub-heading conditions across numerous HS chapters (toys, chapter 84 machinery items, vehicle-related lines), which is exactly what WDI's applied tariff-rate indicators measure.
caveats: This is one of many annual tariff-schedule amendments issued under the Export/Import Regulations Law; WDI's simple/weighted-mean tariff series aggregate thousands of lines, so a single sub-heading amendment moves the average only marginally, and other simultaneous decrees in the same period also changed rates.

law_title: اصلاح سود بازرگاني و زيرنويس تعرفه‌ها (آيين‌نامه اجرايي مقررات صادرات و واردات)
lag_description: 1-5 year lag as tariff protection encouraged local assembly
justification: The amendment explicitly adds an import restriction condition to all tariff lines covered by Iran's automobile law ('qanun-e khodro'), reinforcing tariff protection for domestic vehicle assemblers (Iran Khodro, Saipa) active in this period.
caveats: Domestic vehicle production in the 2000s was driven mainly by joint-venture licensing deals (Peugeot, Renault, Kia) and subsidized credit, not primarily this tariff sub-heading amendment; correlation with output growth is weak evidence of this specific decree's causal weight.

law_title: قانون مواد الحاقي به قانون تقسيم و فروش املاك مورد اجاره مزارعين مستاجر
justification: This is a supplementary law extending the Shah's land-reform program (White Revolution), converting tenant farmers on leased land into owner-operators, which economic literature links to changed investment incentives and cropping patterns in Iranian agriculture through the 1970s.
caveats: Cereal production/yield in this era were driven at least as much by irrigation dam construction (e.g. Dez, Karkheh), fertilizer/mechanization subsidies under the Fifth Plan, and rainfall variability; land reform's productivity effect is contested in the literature (some studies find fragmentation hurt yields short-run).

law_title: قانون مواد الحاقي به قانون تقسيم و فروش املاك مورد اجاره مزارعين مستاجر
justification: Redistributive land reform is a textbook driver of reduced rural income inequality in agrarian economies.
caveats: WDI Gini coefficient data for Iran does not begin until well after this 1971 law and the broader 1960s land-reform program; no credible direct link can be drawn from available data points to this specific supplementary act.

law_title: قانون عمليات بانكي بدون ربا (بهره)
lag_description: same fiscal year, effects persist as standing institutional framework
justification: This law abolished interest-based banking nationwide, replacing lending/deposit interest with mandated 'profit rates' on Islamic contracts (mozarebeh, joalah, etc.) set administratively rather than by market clearing, which is the direct institutional determinant of what WDI records as Iran's real interest rate.
caveats: Administratively set profit rates plus high, volatile inflation make the 'real interest rate' series noisy and driven mainly by CPI swings (sanctions, currency crises) rather than by this 1983 framework law itself, which has not changed the core mechanism since.

law_title: قانون توسعه صنعت گاز
justification: This law created the legal and institutional mandate (National Iranian Gas Company build-out, pipeline network, domestic gas substitution for oil) underpinning Iran's subsequent multi-decade expansion of natural gas production, transmission, and domestic distribution captured in the Energy category's gas charts.
caveats: This is a broad enabling framework, not a single measurable action; actual gas sector growth reflects decades of separate investment decisions, the 1979 revolution and 1980-88 war disruption, sanctions on LNG/pipeline technology, and OPEC/gas-market prices, so attributing specific-year movements to this 1972 law is not defensible.

law_title: قانون نفت
lag_description: standing institutional background, not a discrete shock
justification: The Petroleum Law is the foundational statute asserting state (Ministry of Oil/NIOC) sovereign ownership over all petroleum resources and defining upstream/downstream operations and contract structures; it sets the legal envelope within which Iran's oil production and export volumes are managed, but it is a framework/definitional law repeatedly amended (most recently 2011), not a production-quota or pricing instrument.
caveats: Actual oil production and export volumes are overwhelmingly driven by OPEC quotas, sanctions, war damage (1980-88), and field investment/technology decisions rather than by this framework law itself.

law_title: خلاصه برنامه پنجم عمراني كشور
justification: The Fifth Development Plan set formal targets for income distribution, industrial capacity utilization, and regional balance for 1973-1978, and its budget was sharply revised upward mid-course to absorb the 1973-74 oil price windfall, shaping the pattern of investment and consumption growth recorded in national accounts for that period.
caveats: The dominant driver of Iran's 1973-1978 macroeconomic boom (and subsequent overheating/inflation) was the quadrupling of oil prices after the 1973 OPEC embargo, not the plan document itself, which was substantially rewritten in response to that windfall; WDI series coverage for Iran in this exact period is also thin for several indicators.

law_title: قانون اصلاح تعرفه گمركي
lag_description: standing framework, superseded by many later amendments
justification: This 1958 law established the modern customs tariff schedule structure (chapters, duty classes, commercial-profit surcharge mechanism) that all subsequent Iranian tariff law, including the 1985 Export/Import Regulations Law and later amendments, built upon.
caveats: The specific 1958 rates have been superseded many times over (including by law 23c49d04deb8 in this batch); WDI's applied/MFN tariff-rate series for Iran begin decades after 1958, so no direct data linkage is possible, only institutional lineage.

law_title: قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات سال 1364
justification: This 1985 law (originally a one-year measure, repeatedly re-enacted) created the standing post-revolution framework requiring a 'karte bazargani' (trade license) for commercial import/export, cabinet-set annual permitted/banned goods lists, and the licensing bureaucracy that has governed Iran's formal trade volumes and composition ever since.
caveats: Trade volumes in this era were dominated by the 1980-88 Iran-Iraq war, multiple exchange-rate regimes/rationing, and later international sanctions; the regulatory licensing framework is a necessary administrative condition for trade but not the proximate driver of volume swings.

law_title: قانون مبارزه با قاچاق كالا و ارز
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as enforcement bodies were operationalized
justification: This law criminalizes unauthorized cross-border goods and currency movement and creates dedicated enforcement/adjudication bodies (Article 1 definitions, discovery bonuses to informants under Article 77), aiming to narrow the gap between official and black-market channels for both goods and foreign exchange.
caveats: The official-vs-parallel FX gap in Iran is overwhelmingly driven by sanctions intensity and macro expectations (devaluation fears, inflation) rather than by anti-smuggling enforcement; a stricter smuggling law passed exactly as sanctions escalated in 2012-13 makes it hard to separate the law's effect from the sanctions shock itself, which likely dominates.

law_title: قانون احكام دائمي برنامه‌هاي توسعه كشور
justification: This omnibus permanent-provisions law (73 articles) codifies a 'managed float' exchange-rate regime, central bank governance rules, National Development Fund oil-revenue-savings mandates (min. 30% of oil export proceeds), and dozens of standing fiscal/banking/customs rules that replaced the need for repeated multi-year development-plan legislation.
caveats: This is an extremely broad, multi-topic codification law covering nearly every economic domain at once; it is not a single-mechanism instrument, so any one chart's movement is very unlikely to be attributable mainly to it rather than to the specific sectoral rules, sanctions, or macro shocks operating alongside it. Confidence is kept low deliberately given this breadth.

law_title: قانون برنامه عمرانی چهارم کشور (4th National Development Plan)
justification: Article 1/5 commits 480 billion rials (80% of oil revenue plus foreign borrowing) to a 5-year industrial/infrastructure investment program for exactly FY1347-1351; the electric-power-generation-by-use chart covers precisely these plan years and reflects the plan's electrification build-out.
caveats: Iran's late-1960s/early-1970s growth was also driven by rising world oil prices, the broader White Revolution reforms, and private-sector investment independent of the plan; GDP growth and monetary aggregates have many simultaneous drivers, so isolating the plan's specific contribution is not possible from the data alone.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده 6 قانون هدفمند کردن یارانه‌ها (Executive bylaw, Art.6 Subsidy Targeting Law - industrial bread financing)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as industrial bakery capacity is built out
justification: Article 4 uses subsidy-targeting proceeds (Art. 8(f) of the Subsidy Targeting Law) to subsidize bank-loan interest down to 7% for industrial bakeries, and Article 5 funds compensation for traditional bakeries closing — an implementing instrument of the broader subsidy-reform program that also reset bread/wheat pricing.
caveats: This bylaw itself does not set the wheat procurement price (a separate GIEWS/Ministry of Agriculture mechanism); the guaranteed price series is more directly moved by input costs, general inflation, and exchange-rate depreciation than by this narrow industrial-capacity subsidy.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون حمایت از شرکت‌های دانش‌بنیان (Knowledge-Based Companies Support Law, implementing bylaw)
lag_description: gradual, 1-3 year lag building to multi-year effect
justification: Defines the legal category 'knowledge-based company' (Article 2) and operationalizes the Innovation and Prosperity Fund (ماده 5 قانون) that provides risk capital, subsidized credit and commercialization support (Article 3) — a direct legal/financial instrument aimed at raising R&D spending, researcher counts and patenting.
caveats: University expansion, sanctions-driven import substitution, and broader tech-sector trends also pushed R&D and patenting activity; Iran's high-tech exports and tech manufacturing value-added are additionally constrained by sanctions on hardware/components, making those specific series weaker, more multi-causal indicators of this law's effect.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون حمایت از شرکت‌های دانش‌بنیان (Knowledge-Based Companies Support Law, implementing bylaw)
justification: The same fund/incentive structure is intended to grow medium/high-tech manufacturing and exports over time by commercializing domestic innovations, per the bylaw's Article 3 mandate on commercialization policy.
caveats: International sanctions on technology imports/exports are a much larger and more direct constraint on Iran's high-tech trade and manufacturing figures than this domestic support scheme; confidence is kept low because the mechanism is indirect and multi-causal.

law_title: قانون حفاظت و توسعه صنایع ایران (Law on Protection and Development of Iranian Industries)
lag_description: same fiscal year onset, effects unfolding over the following decade
justification: Article 1 nationalizes steel, copper, aluminum, shipbuilding and auto/aircraft assembly outright, and places large industrial/mining firms whose owners fled or profited illegitimately under state ownership and management — a direct, named structural transformation of the manufacturing/industrial sector immediately after the 1979 revolution.
caveats: The 1979 revolution itself (capital flight, expropriation fears, managerial exodus), the 1979-81 hostage-crisis sanctions, and the 1980 outbreak of the Iran-Iraq War are simultaneous and likely larger shocks to industrial output in this exact period; this nationalization law is one of several concurrent causes of the era's industrial-value-added collapse, not the sole driver.

law_title: قانون برنامه و بودجه کشور (Planning and Budget Organization Law)
justification: Article 1 defines the foundational vocabulary and institutional architecture (Plan & Budget Organization, five-year development plans, annual plans, current vs. development-budget credits) used to construct every subsequent Iranian national budget and five-year plan, making it the procedural backbone of the government-finance data series rather than a lever on any specific fiscal aggregate.
caveats: As a purely definitional/administrative statute it does not itself raise or lower spending, revenue, or debt; actual movements in government-finance charts over the following decades are driven by annual budget laws, oil-revenue swings, war, and sanctions, not by this framework law.

law_title: لایحه قانونی اداره امور بانک‌ها (Law on the Administration of Banks' Affairs — bank nationalization)
lag_description: same fiscal year onset, effects persisting over subsequent decades
justification: Article 1 places all commercial and specialized banks — state-owned and newly nationalized alike — under a single unified administrative structure (Article 2's General Assembly/Supreme Council/Board hierarchy), ending private bank ownership nationwide and shifting credit-allocation decisions to the state, a direct structural cause of the subsequent fall in market-driven private-sector bank credit.
caveats: The 1983 Usury-Free Banking Law, the 1980-88 war economy, and later international sanctions on Iran's banking system independently reshaped private-credit dynamics after this starting condition; this law establishes state control but subsequent credit trends reflect many later policy layers.

law_title: لایحه قانونی اداره امور بانک‌ها (Law on the Administration of Banks' Affairs — bank nationalization)
lag_description: gradual, over the following decades (chart span 1978-2016 begins the same year)
justification: A fully state-administered banking system, with the central bank and nationalized banks under unified government control, made it structurally easier for subsequent governments to finance deficits by borrowing from the banking system/central bank; the chart's start year (1978) sits immediately adjacent to this law's 1979 enactment.
caveats: War financing (1980-88), oil-revenue shortfalls, and later sanctions-driven fiscal stress are the more proximate and larger drivers of government borrowing from the central bank across this 1978-2016 span; bank nationalization is a background enabling condition, not a direct trigger of any single year's borrowing spike.

law_title: قانون محاسبات عمومی کشور (Public Accounting Law)
justification: Article 1 defines the three-part structure of Iran's national budget (general government budget, dedicated-revenue agencies, state-enterprise/bank budgets) and governs treasury accounting/reporting procedures that underlie how subsequent fiscal statistics are compiled.
caveats: This is a pure accounting/procedural statute; it does not itself change any spending, revenue, debt or tax level, so any correlation with government-finance chart movements would be coincidental at best. Recorded as low-confidence context only.

law_title: قانون محاسبات عمومی کشور (Public Accounting Law, duplicate filing)
justification: Same instrument and mechanism as law_id 3a193b0c0416 (identical title/year, duplicate database entry): defines national budget structure and treasury accounting procedures underlying subsequent fiscal reporting.
caveats: Same as the paired entry — a procedural/accounting law with no direct causal lever on fiscal aggregates; any chart correlation would be coincidental.

law_title: قانون تأسیس بانک توسعه کشاورزی ایران (Law Establishing the Agricultural Development Bank of Iran)
lag_description: gradual, 1-3 year lag building over subsequent decades
justification: Establishes a dedicated farm-credit institution to channel subsidized loans to agriculture, one of several inputs (alongside irrigation and land reform) that supported the sector's mechanization and output growth from the late 1960s onward.
caveats: The concurrent White Revolution land reform (from 1962), dam/irrigation construction, and fertilizer subsidies are more direct and better-documented drivers of the FAO agriculture-production series than the mere establishment of a credit institution; no farm-credit-specific chart exists in the index to isolate this law's effect, so the link to the broad Agriculture Production category is necessarily diffuse and low-confidence.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1369 کل کشور (National Budget Law, FY1369/1990-91)
justification: The single-article law fixes total FY1369 government revenue and expenditure at 13,156,288,651,000 rials, split across the general budget, state-enterprise/bank budgets, and dedicated-revenue agencies; the chart directly tracks mid-year supplementary additions made to this exact FY1369 budget.
caveats: The underlying size and composition of the FY1369 budget mainly reflects immediate post-Iran-Iraq-War reconstruction needs and the start of the First Development Plan (1989-93), not an independent effect of the annual budget law's drafting mechanics — the law records the fiscal stance chosen by policymakers rather than causing it.

law_title: قانون اجازه تعیین میزان حقوق گمرکی و سود بازرگانی کالاهای وارداتی توسط دولت (Law authorizing government-set customs duties/commercial profit on imports)
lag_description: same fiscal year (law was valid through mid-1994 per its own sunset clause)
