justification: The single-article law directly authorizes the executive to restructure import tariffs — replacing fixed customs duties with commercial-profit charges valued at the floating exchange rate, and to raise/reprioritize duties so as to protect domestic production while curbing luxury and non-essential imports (Article + Notes 1-3) — a direct lever on import-tax revenue and import composition.
caveats: The early-1990s multiple-exchange-rate system and post-war reconstruction import surge are simultaneous confounders of import-tax revenue and trade-tax shares in this period; the law was explicitly temporary (through 15 Tir 1373), so its effect on longer-run trend charts is limited to a narrow window.

law_title: قانون اجازه مبادله و اجراء قرارداد پیمانکاری اکتشاف و تولید نفت با اراپ (Law authorizing the NIOC-ERAP oil exploration/production contract)
lag_description: multi-year (exploration-to-production ramp-up typical of upstream E&P contracts)
justification: The single-article law ratifies a 44-article NIOC/ERAP (French state oil consortium) exploration and production service contract under the framework of the 1957 Petroleum Act, adding a new non-Consortium upstream producer that incrementally contributed to Iran's aggregate physical oil-production volume from the late 1960s.
caveats: The dominant driver of Iran's oil production in this era was the 1954 Consortium Agreement's operations, which vastly outweighed this single service contract; OPEC quota decisions, global oil demand, the 1973 oil shock, and the 1979 revolution are far larger determinants of the long-run production-volume series than this one contract.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1373 كل كشور (Annual National Budget Law, FY1373/1994)
justification: This is the enacted annual budget law itself, setting the statutory revenue and expenditure totals for FY1373; the chart 'Annual Budget Law Totals' (1992-2022) is directly compiled from these enacted budget figures.
caveats: Direction is ambiguous because a budget law sets planned totals which can move up or down year to year relative to the prior year depending on oil revenue forecasts, inflation, and policy priorities; actual execution may diverge from the enacted law due to oil price shocks, sanctions, or supplementary budgets.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1371 كل كشور (Annual National Budget Law, FY1371/1992)
justification: This is the enacted annual budget law itself, setting the statutory revenue and expenditure totals for FY1371 (the first year in the chart's 1992-2022 series); the chart is directly compiled from these enacted budget figures.
caveats: Direction is ambiguous; totals reflect nominal enacted figures which can be pushed up by inflation/currency depreciation independent of real policy stance. Actual outturns can diverge from the law due to oil price swings, war-reconstruction needs, or mid-year supplementary budgets.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1378 كل كشور (Annual National Budget Law, FY1378/1999)
justification: This is the enacted annual budget law itself, setting the statutory revenue and expenditure totals for FY1378; the chart 'Annual Budget Law Totals' (1992-2022) is directly compiled from these enacted budget figures.
caveats: Direction is ambiguous; nominal totals are influenced heavily by inflation and rial depreciation as well as the 1998 oil price collapse that squeezed that year's actual budget, independent of the law's stated intent.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1377 كل كشور (Annual National Budget Law, FY1377/1998) [duplicate filing]
justification: This is the enacted annual budget law itself, setting the statutory revenue and expenditure totals for FY1377; the chart 'Annual Budget Law Totals' (1992-2022) is directly compiled from these enacted budget figures.
caveats: Direction is ambiguous; nominal totals reflect inflation-driven growth as much as policy choices. This record is a duplicate filing of the same budget law as another law_id in this batch and should not be double-counted as an independent policy event.

law_title: قانون تنظيم بخشي از مقررات مالي دولت (State Financial Regulations Law)
lag_description: gradual, multi-year (procedural framework in force from 2002 onward)
justification: This omnibus law is a procedural/administrative framework governing how state agencies collect and dispose of revenue, set fees, and manage extra-budgetary funds (spanning dozens of unrelated sectoral provisions), which structurally shapes subsequent annual budget composition and government-finance reporting rather than moving any single fiscal aggregate directly.
caveats: This is a broad framework law touching many unrelated administrative domains simultaneously (agriculture, education, health, employment, etc.); it is not a targeted fiscal instrument, so any observed movement in government-finance aggregates after 2002 is much more plausibly driven by oil revenue, inflation, sanctions, or subsequent annual budget laws than by this law specifically.

law_title: قانون تنظيم بخشي از مقررات مالي دولت (State Financial Regulations Law) [duplicate filing]
lag_description: gradual, multi-year (procedural framework in force from 2002 onward)
justification: This omnibus law is a procedural/administrative framework governing how state agencies collect and dispose of revenue, set fees, and manage extra-budgetary funds, which structurally shapes subsequent annual budget composition and government-finance reporting rather than moving any single fiscal aggregate directly.
caveats: Duplicate filing of the same law as another law_id in this batch (76703fdbc108); do not double-count. It is a broad framework law, not a targeted fiscal instrument, so observed movement in government-finance aggregates is more plausibly driven by oil revenue, inflation, sanctions, or annual budget laws.

law_title: اجازه ساختن و نگهداري لوله حمل نفت به شركت لوله نفت ايران (Authorization to Build and Maintain an Oil Transport Pipeline, Iran Oil Pipeline Company)
justification: Authorizes and grants a concession for construction/maintenance of a domestic oil-transport pipeline by an Iranian pipeline company, which is minor domestic transport infrastructure that could marginally support later oil throughput and distribution capacity.
caveats: No chart in the index covers Iranian oil production or infrastructure for the 1930s-40s period at this granularity; the dominant driver of Iran's oil production and export volumes in this era was the Anglo-Iranian Oil Company's concession operations, not this narrow domestic pipeline authorization, so no chart_id is recorded despite the plausible mechanism.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي شيوه هاي قيمت گذاري بنگاه ها (Executive Bylaw on Pricing Methods for State Enterprises Subject to Privatization)
justification: Establishes the methodology for valuing and pricing state-owned enterprises being divested under the Principle 44 privatization program; standardized valuation rules could have influenced the pace and pricing of enterprise listings/block sales that feed into Tehran Stock Exchange capitalization and listed-company counts.
caveats: This is a technical valuation-methodology bylaw, not a driver of aggregate market movements by itself; Iran's stock market capitalization and listing counts from 2009 onward were dominated by much larger factors -- rial devaluation, inflation-driven equity flight, sanctions, and broad Principle 44 divestment waves -- making this at most a minor contributing/enabling factor. No specific chart_id assigned since the link to any single held chart is too diffuse to name confidently.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی نحوه طبقه‌بندی بنگاه‌ها، تایید صورتهای مالی و بازاریابی بنگاه‌های مشمول واگذاری
lag_description: gradual, multi-year (bylaw governs an ongoing administrative process from 2009 onward)
justification: This 2009 cabinet bylaw operationalizes the Article 44 divestiture program by standardizing how state enterprises are classified, their financial statements certified, and their shares marketed/advertised before sale to the Tehran Stock Exchange or via auction. As one of the administrative gears of the broader privatization machinery, it plausibly contributed at the margin to the flow of state-owned shares onto the public market.
caveats: This is a narrow procedural/administrative regulation, not a price- or quantity-setting instrument. The dominant drivers of listed-company counts and market capitalization are the parent Article 44 law itself, macro liquidity conditions, sanctions, and stock-market cycles -- this bylaw's independent effect is not separately identifiable.

law_title: قانون الحاق برخی مواد به قانون تنظیم بخشی از مقررات مالی دولت (۲)
lag_description: same fiscal year onward, effects compounding over subsequent years
justification: This is a large omnibus fiscal-management law (86 articles) restructuring multiple government-finance mechanisms simultaneously: an added annual VAT surtax for health financing, a new formula for settling accounts between the Treasury and the Oil Ministry over crude/gas-condensate export revenue, National Development Fund allocation rules, and mandated restructuring of public pension funds. Because it touches tax revenue, oil-revenue distribution, and public debt/pension mechanics at once, it is a genuine contributing driver of several government-finance aggregates rather than a single-measure instrument.
caveats: The law bundles many distinct provisions with offsetting fiscal directions (new revenue sources vs. new earmarked expenditures), so no single directional effect on any one aggregate can be asserted with confidence. Oil-price swings, sanctions, and annual budget laws passed in the same years are much larger drivers of these same series.

law_title: قانون توسعه حمل و نقل عمومی و مدیریت مصرف سوخت
lag_description: gradual over 3-5 years (law explicitly phases targets through 1390/2011)
justification: The 2007 Public Transport Development and Fuel Consumption Management Law directly mandates smart fuel cards, gasoline/diesel rationing, CNG conversion incentives, tariff exemptions for importing low-consumption/hybrid/rail vehicles, and expansion of rail and public-transit modal share (with explicit numeric targets in the law's own tables), all aimed at curbing growth in gasoline/diesel consumption and boosting rail's share of freight and passenger transport.
caveats: This law's effects are entangled with the much larger 2010 targeted subsidy reform (Yaraneha) which raised fuel prices nationally and is the dominant driver of consumption trends after 2010; oil-price shocks, sanctions on vehicle/parts imports, and urbanization also move these series independently of this law.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال ۱۳۸۳ کل کشور
justification: This is the annual national budget law for FY1383; the 'Annual Budget Law Totals' series records exactly the aggregate revenue/expenditure figure this law itself legislated for that fiscal year, making it a direct documentary source for that data point.
caveats: Year-over-year nominal growth in the totals series mostly reflects high domestic inflation and oil-revenue swings rather than any real fiscal expansion specific to this law; the chart is a record of the law's content, not an independent measurement of its economic effect.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال ۱۳۸۴ کل کشور
justification: This is the annual national budget law for FY1384; the 'Annual Budget Law Totals' series records exactly the aggregate revenue/expenditure figure this law itself legislated for that fiscal year, making it a direct documentary source for that data point.
caveats: Year-over-year nominal growth in the totals series mostly reflects high domestic inflation and oil-revenue swings (this was also a high oil-price year) rather than any real fiscal expansion specific to this law; the chart is a record of the law's content, not an independent measurement of its economic effect.

law_title: ابطال بخشنامه شماره ۲۰۰/۹۵/۲۵ سازمان امور مالیاتی کشور (رأی هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری)
justification: The Administrative Justice Court annulled a 2016 Tax Administration circular that had taxed the currency-translation gains ('tasir-e arz') of non-oil exporters, including handwoven-carpet exporters. The ruling's own record cites the carpet exporters' union complaint that the circular was causing a 'general recession' in handwoven carpet exports; annulling it removed one tax drag on export proceeds.
caveats: Iran's handwoven carpet export decline over recent decades is driven mainly by structural forces -- competition from machine-made and Chinese/Indian carpets, sanctions on banking/payment channels, and shrinking traditional export markets -- so this single tax-circular annulment is at most a minor, temporary relief rather than a primary driver of the export-value series.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ساماندهی تولید، فرآوری و بازرگانی چای
lag_description: 1-3 year lag (agricultural regulatory changes take several growing seasons to show in production/trade data)
justification: This 2006 bylaw reorganizes domestic tea-leaf production, processing standards, and trade/marketing arrangements, intending to modernize an industry long dependent on guaranteed government purchase prices; such regulatory reorganization of the value chain can plausibly affect leaf output, processed output quality, and trade volumes over subsequent years.
caveats: Iranian tea production and trade are more strongly driven by the annually-set guaranteed purchase price, weather/drought conditions, farmer income incentives, and global tea prices; this organizational bylaw is a secondary, indirect lever rather than a price- or output-setting instrument.

law_title: رأی شماره ۵۲۶ هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری - واگذاری سهام ترجیحی پالایشگاه نفت شیراز
justification: This ruling resolves a narrow intra-court jurisdictional conflict over a single refinery's preferential-share transfer dispute during privatization; it is a dispute-resolution/procedural instrument, not a measure that sets prices, output, or aggregate financial flows.
caveats: No chart in the index tracks refinery-specific ownership disputes or privatization transaction values; any link to broader oil/refinery production or privatization series would be speculative, so no record is warranted for this law.

law_title: قانون ملی شدن بانکها (Bank Nationalization Law)
lag_description: immediate legal effect, economic consequences over following years
justification: The 1979 Revolutionary Council decree declared all banks state property overnight and vested management appointment power in the government, fundamentally restructuring the banking system from private/mixed ownership to full state control. This is a documented, direct instrument acting squarely on the banking/monetary system.
caveats: Confidence capped below 5 because the decree text itself is only two short articles with no granular targets (no explicit credit or deposit growth target), so the mapped charts capture downstream effects that are also driven by the broader 1979 revolution, capital flight, war economy (1980-88), and later banking reforms (e.g. post-2000 private bank re-licensing) rather than this decree alone. Direction is ambiguous: nationalization stabilized deposits short-term (state guarantee) but is widely blamed for credit misallocation and slower private-sector credit growth for decades.

law_title: لایحه قانونی اصلاح قانون اصلاحات ارضی (Amendment to the Land Reform Law)
justification: This 1962 cabinet decree is a technical/procedural amendment to the original Land Reform Law (Dey 1340) — it adds notes on registration-office procedure, dispute referral to land-reform officials, and mortgage-formality mechanics for implementing land transfers to peasants. It is part of the machinery of the Shah's land-redistribution program, which is broadly credited/blamed in the literature for shifts in Iranian agricultural output and land tenure through the 1960s-70s.
caveats: The amendment itself sets no production target and is purely procedural; we hold no land-ownership/tenure-distribution chart to link directly. Any movement in the agriculture production charts is heavily confounded by simultaneous factors: Green Revolution input adoption (fertilizer, irrigation), 1960s-70s oil-boom-driven rural-urban migration, weather/drought cycles, and the dozens of other land-reform implementing decrees issued 1962-1971. Treat as low-confidence background context, not a direct driver of any single chart.

law_title: رأی شماره ۲۳۲۵ هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری - ابطال بخشنامه تعیین حداقل مزد کارگران
justification: This Administrative Justice Court ruling annuls, on jurisdictional grounds, a legal-affairs circular from the Vice-Presidency that had interpreted how the Supreme Labor Council's annual minimum-wage decisions interact with contract renewals and social-security premium calculations. It does not itself set or change the minimum wage — that power remains with the Supreme Labor Council (Shora-ye Aali-e Kar) — it merely voids one executive-branch interpretive memo for exceeding that office's authority.
caveats: We hold no minimum-wage-level or real-wage chart in the index at all, so there is no chart this could plausibly move even if it were substantively significant. Returning no chart mapping for this law is the correct outcome; listed here only to document that it was reviewed and found to have no traceable chart-level effect.

law_title: رأی شماره‌های ۱۲۶۴ و ۱۳۱۷ هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری - هزینه جابجایی صندوق بازنشستگی مخابرات
justification: This ruling resolves a conflict among lower-court decisions about who pays the pension-fund-transfer cost differential for Iran Telecom employees following the company's 2009 Article-44 privatization — it holds the government (not the privatized company) liable for that specific transition cost. It is a narrow HR/pension-liability dispute, not a decision affecting telecom market structure, investment, or output.
caveats: No telecom market/output/investment chart in our index would plausibly move from a pension-liability allocation ruling issued nine years after the actual privatization transaction. We hold no pension-liability chart either. No credible mechanism connects this ruling to any chart we hold.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال ۱۳۲۷ مجلس شورای ملی
justification: On inspection, this is NOT the national state budget — it is the internal operating-expense appropriation for the Majlis (parliament) itself: staff/representative allowances, the parliamentary print shop, and the official gazette office. It has no bearing on national fiscal policy.
caveats: This is a bookkeeping correction to the batch's 'why' field, which mischaracterized it as 'the national annual budget law.' The sums involved (~31.7 million rials for a full year of legislature operations) are immaterial to any macroeconomic series we track. No chart mapping applies.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال ۱۳۲۳ مجلس شورای ملی
justification: Same category of instrument as 614df3a33bd4: this is the Majlis's own internal operating-budget appropriation (representative allowances, staff salaries, printing office), not the national government budget, despite the batch label calling it a national budget law.
caveats: Immaterial administrative appropriation; no plausible link to any macro/fiscal chart we hold. No chart mapping applies.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال ۱۳۴۹ کل کشور (National Budget Law, FY1349/1970)
lag_description: same fiscal year, with monetary effects lagging into the following year
justification: Unlike the other 'budget' laws in this batch, this one is genuinely the whole-country national budget ('کل کشور'), setting appropriations across ministries (War Ministry armaments/procurement, Information Ministry radio revenue, gendarmerie, treasury-bond financing of the deficit per Note 24). Any deficit financed through treasury bonds/central-bank credit would show up in the government-debt-to-banking-system and monetary aggregates for that fiscal year, which is the tail year of two Pahlavi-era monetary charts we hold.
caveats: The extracted law text is fragmentary (only the numbered notes/footnotes survived scraping, not the full revenue/expenditure schedule tables), so the actual size and direction of the fiscal deficit for FY1349 cannot be confirmed from this source alone. Monetary aggregates in 1969-70 were also driven by oil-revenue inflows, credit policy, and private-sector deposit growth independent of this specific budget law. This is a generic annual appropriations act, not a targeted policy instrument, so treat as low-confidence background contribution only.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال ۱۳۳۳ مجلس سنا
justification: This is the Senate's (Majlis-e Sanā) own internal operating-expense appropriation (representative pay, staff salaries), not a national budget law. It has no macroeconomic scope.
caveats: The batch's 'why' field mischaracterized this as 'National annual budget law (Senate) for FY1333' — it is the Senate's own housekeeping budget, comparable in scope to 614df3a33bd4 and cf556748d470. No chart mapping applies.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال ۱۳۲۸ مجلس شورای ملی
justification: Same category as the other Majlis budget laws in this batch: internal parliamentary operating appropriation, not a national fiscal instrument.
caveats: Mischaracterized by the batch as a national budget law; it is legislature housekeeping only. No chart mapping applies.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال ۱۳۳۹ مجلس شورای ملی
justification: Same category as the other Majlis budget laws in this batch: an internal appropriation for the parliament's own staff/operating costs (this instance explicitly amends a retiree cost-of-living clause), not the national government budget.
caveats: Mischaracterized by the batch as a national budget law; it is legislature housekeeping only. No chart mapping applies.

law_title: قانون اصلاح موادی از قانون برنامه سوم توسعه ... (Amendment to Third Development Plan Law)
justification: This is a two-article technical amendment to the Third Five-Year Plan: Article 1 fixes a phrase-ordering typo in an earlier amendment about asset sale-price determination; Article 2 delegates water tariff, connection-fee, and wastewater-disposal-charge setting to the Ministry of Energy's proposal plus Economic Council approval, within the price ceilings already set by Article 5 of the Plan Law.
caveats: We hold no water-tariff, utility-price, or water-services CPI-component chart in the index, so there is no chart this narrow tariff-delegation instrument could plausibly move. No chart mapping applies.

law_title: در اجرای تبصره ۳ ماده ۲ قانون اصلاحات ارضی (Implementing Regulation, Land Reform Law Art. 2 Note 3)
justification: This 1962 cabinet regulation sets the procedure for partitioning the state's share out of commonly-owned (mashaa) estates before land titles are transferred to peasant farmers under Article 2 of the Land Reform Law — i.e., it operationalizes part of the actual land-redistribution mechanics of the White Revolution land reform.
caveats: This is implementation procedure (survey/partition/dispute-arbitration mechanics), not the substantive redistribution decision itself, and we hold no land-tenure/ownership-distribution chart to link directly. Any observed shift in agricultural production series over the following decade is heavily confounded by Green Revolution input adoption, oil-boom-era rural-urban migration, weather, and the many other land-reform implementing decrees issued across 1962-1971. Treat as low-confidence background context only, shared with law 2a81fd112c0a.

law_title: قانون موافقتنامه تجارت ترجیحی بین کشورهای عضو دی-هشت (D-8 Preferential Trade Agreement)
lag_description: gradual, phased over 4-8 years post-ratification; exact entry-into-force date left undetermined by the text itself
justification: Ratifies the D-8 Preferential Trade Agreement (Bangladesh, Egypt, Iran, Indonesia, Malaysia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Turkey), committing Iran to phased tariff cuts (rates above 25%/15%/10% bands compressed to those ceilings) and a freeze/removal of new non-tariff and para-tariff barriers on covered goods traded among members -- a direct trade-liberalization instrument.
caveats: D-8 partners are a small share of Iran's total trade; Iran's aggregate export/import volumes are dominated by oil prices, sanctions cycles and the exchange rate, so any D-8-specific effect is unlikely to be visible in national trade aggregates. No bilateral Iran-D8 trade chart exists in our index to isolate this from other drivers.

law_title: دستورالعمل بسته پرداخت کمک جبرانی و حمایت غذایی از گروه‌های کم‌درآمد جامعه (Compensatory Cash & Food-Support Payment Package)
justification: Directive of the Council of Ministers establishing a direct cash 'compensatory aid' (2,000,000 rial) plus a household-size-tiered food-support cash transfer, funded by the exchange-rate margin on petrochemical/steel/refinery feedstock and channeled through the Targeted Subsidies Organization to low-income civil servants, pensioners, Imam Khomeini Relief Committee/Behzisti beneficiaries and informal workers -- a targeted social-transfer instrument expanding safety-net coverage and transfer spending.
caveats: This was one of several overlapping ad hoc compensatory packages issued around the 2018 rial devaluation and reimposition of US sanctions; national poverty/inequality and social-protection indicators in this period are driven at least as much by the inflation/devaluation shock itself as by this specific one-off directive, so isolating its effect from the data we hold is not possible.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی توزیع سهام عدالت (Justice Shares Distribution Bylaw)
lag_description: 1-10 year lag; distribution and free-trading of Justice Shares was repeatedly delayed/frozen and largely completed only after 2016-2020 reforms
justification: Implementing bylaw for the Article 44 privatization program's 'Justice Shares' scheme, transferring state-enterprise equity to provincial investment cooperatives for eventual allocation to lower-income deciles -- a direct instrument expanding the shareholder base and free float of Tehran Stock Exchange-listed companies.
caveats: TSE market capitalization, turnover and listed-company counts in this period were driven far more by oil-revenue cycles, rial depreciation (which inflates nominal rial-denominated market cap), sanctions, and the 2020 retail-investor boom than by this single distribution program; Justice Shares themselves were frozen and non-tradable for years, muting the intended liquidity effect.

law_title: آیین‌نامه نحوه واگذاری سهام ترجیحی (Preferential Shares Transfer Bylaw)
lag_description: multi-year, ongoing as each state enterprise is privatized
justification: Companion Article 44 privatization bylaw authorizing discounted installment sale of up to 5% of a divested enterprise's shares to its own managers/employees/retirees (and a further 5% to outside qualified managers), incrementally widening private/insider ownership of formerly state-owned, TSE-listed firms.
caveats: Affects only a capped 5-10% slice of shares per privatized firm and is spread across many individual privatization transactions over years; TSE-wide capitalization and turnover indicators are dominated by macro factors (oil revenue, exchange rate, sanctions, investor sentiment) rather than this narrow allocation mechanism.

law_title: الحاق ماده (11) مکرر به آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Addition of Article 11-bis to the Import/Export Regulations Bylaw)
lag_description: not applicable / no measurable lag expected
justification: A narrow procedural 'grandfathering' rule: exporters who already hold a valid letter of credit, a customs-declared/cotage-numbered shipment, or a valid export contract before an export-rule change (new duties, currency-surrender requirements, subsidy-status changes, etc.) are shielded from the new rules for that shipment. It changes legal certainty for exporters mid-transition, not the level of any tariff, price or trade flow itself.
caveats: This is an administrative-certainty provision, not a rate or quantity instrument; any effect on aggregate export volumes would be far too small and diffuse to be distinguishable from oil-price, sanctions, and exchange-rate driven swings in the trade data we hold. Included only as a very-low-confidence background note.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1368 کل کشور (Annual National Budget Law, FY1368)
lag_description: same fiscal year (1989/1368), effects on debt stock cumulative over subsequent years
justification: Routine annual appropriations act for the first postwar fiscal year (Iran-Iraq War ceasefire mid-1988), setting treasury working-capital limits (تنخواه‌گردان خزانه), sectoral credit allocations via Bank Markazi, and reconstruction/defense-related revenue provisions typical of the era's deficit-financed budgets, which fed into central-bank financing of government deficits.
caveats: This is a routine, un-exceptional annual budget rather than a distinct reform; our chart index has no dataset with 1368-specific granularity to isolate its effect, and government debt/monetary trends of this period are dominated by postwar reconstruction costs, oil-revenue swings, and cumulative effects of many successive annual budgets rather than this single year's law.

law_title: قانون تاسیس شرکت سهامی شیلات جنوب ایران (South Iran Fisheries Co. Establishment Act)
lag_description: gradual, over the company's 1971-2005 operating life
justification: Created a state-backed joint-stock fishing company with exclusive licensing authority over Persian Gulf/Sea of Oman fishing grounds, customs exemptions on vessels and processing equipment, and borrowing authority (up to 1 billion rial) to expand the fleet and canning/cold-storage capacity in southern Iran.
caveats: The company was dissolved in 1384 (2005) with functions folded into the national Fisheries Organization (Shilat); total fisheries production also reflects Caspian catch, aquaculture growth, and stock/technology changes unrelated to this single southern-region company; no chart isolates Persian Gulf catch specifically, so correlation with national totals is weak.

law_title: قانون اصلاح پاره‌ای از مواد و الحاق مواد جدید بقانون تاسیس صندوق توسعه کشاورزی ایران (Amendment to Agricultural Development Fund of Iran founding law)
lag_description: 1-5 year lag; cumulative over the fund's operating life
justification: Expanded the Agricultural Development Fund's capital, gave it bond-issuance authority (up to 5 billion rial in development bonds) and broadened its lending mandate to finance private investment in commercial farming, land reclamation, and agro-processing/export capacity.
caveats: The fund's capital was small relative to the whole agricultural sector; output growth in this era was driven far more by the White Revolution land reform, irrigation investment, weather, and world commodity prices than by this credit fund alone; no series isolates fund-financed output.

law_title: قانون صندوق ضمانت صادرات ایران (Export Guarantee Fund of Iran Act)
justification: Established a state export-credit insurance company (predecessor of today's EGFI) to insure non-oil exporters against commercial/political payment risk and guarantee export financing, aiming to lower the risk premium on non-oil exports such as carpets and other manufactures.
caveats: Iran's export mix has been overwhelmingly driven by oil revenue and exchange-rate policy rather than export-credit insurance; carpet and non-oil export volumes also move with global demand, sanctions, and domestic price competitiveness; the fund's initial guarantee capacity (200 million rial) was modest relative to national trade flows.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1372 کل کشور (National Budget Law, FY1372/1993)
justification: This is the enacted national budget law for FY1372, directly fixing that year's approved government revenue and expenditure totals — the same underlying figures compiled in the 'Annual Budget Law Totals' chart.
caveats: Actual executed spending and revenue routinely diverge from the approved law due to mid-year supplementary budgets, inflation, and oil-revenue shortfalls; the chart may reflect approved rather than realized figures, so it should not be read as a precise outturn.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1362 کل کشور (National Budget Law, FY1362/1983)
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-2 year lag
justification: Sets wartime government spending (defense mobilization, subsidized imports) for FY1362 against oil-export revenue constrained by the Iran-Iraq war; deficits in this era were routinely financed via direct government borrowing from Bank Markazi (the central bank), which this chart tracks.
caveats: War intensity, the 1980s oil price collapse, and sanctions-driven revenue shortfalls are more proximate drivers of central-bank lending to government than the annual budget law's stated totals alone; no line-item breakdown isolates this specific law's contribution to CBI credit.

law_title: قانون اصلاح بعضی از مقررات قانون بودجه سال 1359 (Amendment to certain provisions of the FY1359 Budget Law)
justification: Omnibus amendment to the first post-revolution full-year budget law (covering the opening months of the Iran-Iraq war), touching customs administration, agriculture, defense-personnel and trade provisions; like other wartime budgets its net financing needs would have fed into central-bank credit to government.
caveats: This is a broad procedural amendment spanning dozens of unrelated provisions; attributing a specific quantitative effect on CBI lending to this amendment alone — versus the base 1359 budget law or the war itself — is speculative.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1363 کل کشور (National Budget Law, FY1363/1984)
justification: Approved wartime government spending for FY1363 against oil-revenue-constrained receipts during a peak year of the Iran-Iraq war, financed in part via central-bank credit to government.
caveats: War intensity, oil price swings, and sanctions are larger drivers of central-bank lending than the budget law's approved framework alone; realized financing mix is not separately documented.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1367 کل کشور (National Budget Law, FY1367/1988)
justification: FY1367 budget covers the final and costliest year of the Iran-Iraq war (including the 'War of the Cities' and closing offensives), a period of peak deficit financing via the central bank.
caveats: War intensity, oil price collapse, and sanctions are more proximate drivers than the budget law's approved totals; realized CBI financing for this specific year is not separately broken out.

law_title: لایحه بودجه سال 1365 کل کشور (Budget Bill, FY1365/1986)
justification: Government budget bill for FY1365, submitted during the height of wartime deficit spending; if enacted close to this form, its approved spending would have added to reliance on central-bank financing of the deficit.
caveats: Archived as a 'لایحه' (government bill/proposal) rather than confirmed final enacted text, so provisions may differ from what the Majlis ultimately passed; same war/oil-price/sanctions caveats as the other wartime budget laws apply.

law_title: دستورالعمل تبصره 2 ماده 134 قانون برنامه پنجم توسعه (Energy Service Company / ESCO contracting directive under Article 134 note 2 of the 5th Development Plan)
justification: Set up a savings-based performance-contracting framework letting government agencies hire private energy-service companies to cut energy use in public buildings, repaid only from realized energy savings and partly funded from the Targeted Subsidies Reform pool — a genuine but very small-scale energy-efficiency mechanism.
caveats: Scope is limited to public-sector buildings and financed from a modest earmarked pool; national energy intensity is dominated by industrial structure, heavily subsidized fuel/electricity prices, and vehicle fleet efficiency, none of which this directive touches; effect on any economy-wide chart is speculative.

law_title: لايحه قانوني اصلاح قانون ملي شدن بانكها مصوب 1358/3/17 شوراي انقلاب اسلامي
lag_description: immediate legal effect, economic effects (if any) diffuse over following years
justification: This single-article amendment merely exempts the banks nationalized under the June 1979 nationalization decree from Article 116 of the Commercial Code (a corporate-governance/liability provision); it does not itself set lending policy, reserve ratios, or deposit terms.
caveats: The actual driver of Iran's post-1979 banking structural shift is the original nationalization law (not in this batch), plus the 1979-81 revolutionary upheaval, war economy, and later Islamic banking law of 1983. This amendment's own contribution to monetary aggregates is not separable from those larger events.

law_title: قانون ممنوعيت دولت از مذاكره و عقد قرارداد راجع بامتياز نفت با خارجيها
lag_description: multi-year lag; legal/political precursor to the 1951 oil nationalization crisis and 1954 Consortium Agreement
justification: This 1944 law barred the Prime Minister and cabinet from negotiating or signing new oil concessions with foreign parties without parliamentary knowledge, a landmark restriction on executive oil diplomacy that fed into the political momentum culminating in Mossadegh's 1951 nationalization and the 1951-54 Anglo-Iranian oil dispute, which sharply disrupted Iranian oil output.
caveats: The law itself is a procedural/legal prohibition, not a production or pricing instrument; the actual production disruption came from the later 1951-53 nationalization crisis and international boycott, driven by many additional political and international factors (AIOC negotiations, Cold War politics, the 1953 coup) not captured by this law alone.

law_title: قانون راجع باكتشاف و بهره برداري از منابع طبيعي فلات قاره ايران
lag_description: gradual, over subsequent decades as offshore exploration/production expanded
justification: This 1955 law asserted Iranian sovereignty over continental-shelf resources and authorized the state to create institutions for offshore exploration/exploitation, laying the legal foundation for the offshore oil concessions and NIOC joint-structure agreements (from 1957 onward) that eventually brought new offshore fields into production.
caveats: The law is a jurisdictional/legal claim, not an operational concession or production instrument; actual offshore output growth over subsequent decades reflects specific concession agreements, technology, and global oil-market conditions far more than this foundational statute.

law_title: قانون الغاي قرارداد نفت با كنسرسيوم مصوب 1333 و اجازه اجراي قرارداد فروش و خريد نفت بين دولت شاهنشاهي ايران و شركتهاي خارجي
lag_description: same-to-next fiscal year through the late 1970s (until the 1979 revolution)
justification: This law formally annulled the 1954 Oil Consortium Agreement and authorized a new Sale and Purchase Agreement giving NIOC full operational control of Iranian oil fields (the 1973 oil takeover), while the former consortium members retained long-term crude purchase rights; this consolidated Iran's control over production decisions and oil-revenue capture just as the 1973 oil-price shock hit.
caveats: Iran's 1973-77 production and revenue surge is heavily driven by the concurrent OPEC-led 1973 oil price quadrupling, not solely by this ownership/control change; disentangling the effect of operational control from the price shock is not possible from aggregate volume/revenue data alone.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي ماده (8) قانون هدفمند كردن يارانه ها
justification: This bylaw operationalizes Article 8 of the 2010 Targeted Subsidies Law, channeling the funds freed up by energy-price liberalization into interest-subsidized loans and outright grants ('managed funds') to industry, mining, agriculture, transport, bread production, and non-oil exporters via commercial banks, directly increasing recorded government subsidy/transfer expenditure.
caveats: This bylaw governs allocation mechanics only; it does not itself set the higher energy/bread prices that generated the freed-up resources (that is a different article of the parent law, not in this batch). Aggregate subsidy-and-transfer spending is also affected by inflation indexing, cash-transfer program costs, and later revisions to the subsidy reform.

law_title: قانون اصلاح موادي از قانون برنامه سوم توسعه اقتصادي، اجتماعي و فرهنگي جمهوري اسلامي ايران و چگونگي برقراري و وصول عوارض و ساير وجوه از توليدكنندگان كالا، ارائه دهندگان خدمات و كالاهاي وارداتي
lag_description: 2002/03 through 2008 (superseded by the VAT Law of 2008)
justification: Known as the 'Fee Consolidation Law' (قانون تجميع عوارض), this law consolidated scattered customs duties, import registration fees, and municipal levies into a unified import duty set at 4% of customs value plus a separately-set commercial profit rate, and imposed unified excise-style taxes/duties on gasoline (30%), cigarettes, soft drinks, telecom, electricity/gas/water, hotels, and vehicle transfers, directly restructuring how import duties and domestic consumption levies were assessed and collected nationally.
caveats: Actual applied tariff/commercial-profit rates were set annually by a separate cabinet-approved schedule, not fixed in this law itself, so year-to-year tariff levels reflect those decrees more than this framework law; the law was itself repealed by the 2008 VAT law, limiting its effect window to about six years.

law_title: قانون تنظيم بخشي از مقررات تسهيل نوسازي صنايع كشور و اصلاح ماده (113) قانون برنامه سوم توسعه اقتصادي، اجتماعي و فرهنگي جمهوري اسلامي ايران
lag_description: multi-year, effects accruing over the following decade
justification: This broad industrial-modernization law package authorized bank financing for contractors bidding on industrial projects, tax deductions for capital increases, duty-free import of production machinery/spare parts, energy-savings subsidies to industrial plants, early-retirement/severance financing for surplus industrial workers, and participation bonds (up to 50% of project investment) for listed companies to fund renovation, aimed at raising industrial productivity and competitiveness.
caveats: This is one of many industrial-policy instruments enacted over the 2000s-2010s (alongside privatization, subsidy reform, and sanctions-driven import substitution); manufacturing value-added and capital-market growth reflect oil-revenue cycles, sanctions, and exchange-rate swings far more than this single facilitation law, so any contribution is diffuse and not separately identifiable in the aggregate data.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1386 كل كشور
justification: This is the full national annual budget law for FY1386, setting the government's approved total revenue and expenditure allocations for that year, the direct legal source of the FY1386 data point in the annual budget-totals series.
caveats: The chart is a mechanical record of enacted budget totals across many years, so this record is close to definitional for the FY1386 data point rather than an independent causal claim about behavior; actual budget execution (vs. the enacted law) can and often does diverge due to oil-revenue shortfalls, supplementary budgets, and inflation.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1379 كل كشور (Annual National Budget Law, FY1379)
justification: This is the enacted national appropriations act for FY1379; the annual_budget_law_totals chart is built directly from the totals enacted in Iran's yearly budget laws, so the 1379 data point is definitionally sourced from this instrument rather than merely correlated with it.
caveats: The chart records legislated appropriations, not actual execution/spending, which frequently diverges due to oil-revenue shortfalls, sanctions, or mid-year supplementary budgets. Nominal totals are also driven heavily by inflation and exchange-rate depreciation rather than real fiscal expansion.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1385 كل كشور (Annual National Budget Law, FY1385)
justification: Enacted FY1385 national budget law; both charts are direct compilations of enacted annual budget totals/resource-use breakdowns, and 1385 is the first year covered by the government_budget_summary series, making this law the source instrument for that chart's starting data point.
caveats: Reflects legislated appropriations, not execution. FY1385 was also a period of rapidly rising oil revenue, which inflated budget totals independent of any change in fiscal policy stance embodied in this specific law.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1376 كل كشور (Annual National Budget Law, FY1376)
justification: Enacted FY1376 national budget law; the annual_budget_law_totals chart (covering 1992-2022) is compiled directly from such annual budget acts, so the 1376 data point derives from this instrument.
caveats: Records legislated totals, not actual execution. 1997 fell during a period of low oil prices, so year-over-year changes in the chart may reflect revenue constraints rather than discretionary policy choices in this particular law.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده (۱۴) قانون ساماندهی و حمایت از تولید و عرضه مسکن (Implementing Bylaw, Art. 14 of the Housing Production & Supply Organization Law)
justification: The bylaw authorizes mortgage-backed securities (اوراق رهنی مسکن), leasing-company bond issuance for housing, special deposit certificates for mass-housing builders, and government-backed credit guarantees for developers, all of which channel additional bank/non-bank credit toward the private sector via the housing pipeline.
caveats: Domestic credit to the private sector (% of GDP) is overwhelmingly driven by monetary policy, inflation, sanctions-driven credit contraction, and directed-lending mandates unrelated to housing; this is a narrow implementing regulation for a single article of an existing law, so any marginal contribution is small and not separately identifiable in the aggregate series.

law_title: رأی شماره ۱۲۹۲ هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری - ابطال بند ۲ تصویب‌نامه افزایش بهای گاز مصرفی (Administrative Court ruling annulling a cabinet decree provision raising residential gas prices for above-baseline consumption)
justification: The ruling voids a November 2020 cabinet decree clause that would have applied a 15% price surcharge to a household's entire gas bill (not just excess usage) once consumption exceeded a historical baseline; annulling it removes one narrow escalation mechanism from the state-set residential natural-gas tariff, a component of the CPI basket.
caveats: This is an extremely narrow, single-clause annulment layered on top of the much larger Targeted Subsidies Act pricing regime, which remains in force and dominates gas/energy price movements. Its effect on aggregate CPI is likely undetectable given the many other larger drivers of Iranian inflation (money supply growth, exchange-rate pass-through, sanctions). The Iran-specific COICOP-group CPI chart in this dataset (iran_sci1399__cpi_by_coicop_group_1390_1399) only runs through SH1399 (~2020/21) and does not cover the 1400 (2021) ruling date, so it was not used.

law_title: قانون الحاق موادی به قانون تنظیم بخشی از مقررات مالی دولت (۱) (Law Adding Provisions to the State Financial Regulations Law (1))
justification: This is a broad omnibus fiscal-management law amending state financial regulations: it governs government/state-company budget execution, forex-debt bookkeeping at the central bank, letters-of-credit financing for public works, agricultural credit terms, and treasury guarantees for BOT infrastructure projects, touching many items tracked under Government Finance (expenditure composition, subsidies/transfers, government debt).
caveats: As an omnibus law with dozens of largely technical, unrelated provisions (customs anti-dumping powers, wheat-milling liberalization, land-registry fees, worker training funds), no single Government Finance metric can be cleanly attributed to it; broader fiscal trends are driven far more by oil revenue, sanctions, and successive budget laws than by this particular amending statute.

law_title: قانون الحاق موادی به قانون تنظیم بخشی از مقررات مالی دولت (۱) (Law Adding Provisions to the State Financial Regulations Law (1))
justification: Article 13 authorizes the government to levy compensatory ('mabeh-tafavot') duties on foreign goods (rice, wheat, barley, palm oil, sugar were later applied under this article) priced abnormally low, and to cut commercial-profit tariffs on competing imports to protect consumers - a direct, if narrow, tariff-setting mechanism.
caveats: The applied-tariff-rate WDI series is an economy-wide average; this law only authorizes case-by-case compensatory duties on specific commodities decided later by the Economic Council, so the aggregate tariff series will reflect many other trade-policy changes (WTO accession attempts, sanctions-driven import substitution, general tariff schedule revisions) far more than this enabling clause.

law_title: قانون الحاق موادي به قانون تنظيم بخشي از مقررات مالي دولت (1) (duplicate entry of the same State Financial Regulations amendment law)
justification: Duplicate record of law_id 88b5593dfcb8 (same omnibus fiscal-management statute); see that record for the underlying mechanism.
caveats: Same caveats as the primary entry: omnibus law with many unrelated technical provisions, effect on any single Government Finance metric is not separately identifiable from oil-revenue and sanctions-driven fiscal trends.

law_title: قانون الحاق موادي به قانون تنظيم بخشي از مقررات مالي دولت (1) (duplicate entry of the same State Financial Regulations amendment law)
justification: Duplicate record of law_id 88b5593dfcb8; Article 13's compensatory-duty and tariff-reduction authority is the same mechanism described in that entry.
caveats: Same caveats as the primary entry: tariff series reflect economy-wide averages driven by many policies beyond this narrow enabling clause.

law_title: دستورالعمل حقوق ورودی گوشی تلفن همراه خارجی (Customs Directive on Import Duties for Foreign Mobile Phones, replacing circular No. 1901562/1403)
justification: Iran Customs directive setting a tiered import-duty and commercial-profit schedule for foreign mobile handsets (15% duty for €600-1000 units, 30% above €1000, plus VAT and surcharges), implementing FY1404 budget-law tariff bands; higher effective landed cost on premium phones could plausibly dampen import volumes/value within the ICT-goods import category and slow growth in cellular subscriptions at the margin.
caveats: This is a narrow, highly technical customs circular mostly restating rates already legislated in the FY1404 budget law rather than an independent policy shift. Mobile subscriptions and ICT goods imports in Iran are far more strongly driven by sanctions, currency depreciation, gray-market/parallel-import channels, and general consumer demand. Given the 2025 effective date, current WDI series likely have not yet caught up with this period, so any observed movement in the charted data predates the directive.

law_title: دستورالعمل حقوق ورودی گوشی تلفن همراه خارجی (duplicate entry of the same mobile-phone customs directive)
justification: Duplicate of law_id eb3e4a0e58f9 (identical customs directive text); see that record for the mechanism.
caveats: Same caveats as the primary entry: narrow technical circular, effect swamped by sanctions/currency/gray-market factors, and likely not yet reflected in available series given the 2025 effective date.

law_title: قانون حمل و نقل و عبور كالاهاي خارجي از قلمرو جمهوري اسلامي ايران (Law on Transport and Transit of Foreign Goods through the Territory of the Islamic Republic of Iran)
justification: Establishes Iran's foundational legal regime for foreign goods transiting its territory: transit cargo is exempted from customs duties/commercial profit and only pays operational/handling fees, banking/credit support is mandated for Iranian international transport firms, and simplified TIR/container procedures are set up - the framework underlying Iran's transit-trade and transport-services export revenue.
caveats: Iran's transit and transport-services trade is shaped far more by regional geopolitics (Afghanistan conflict, Central Asian route competition, sanctions on shipping/insurance, relations with neighbors) and infrastructure investment than by this 1996 framework law alone; the law is a background legal enabler rather than a discrete shock, making its isolated contribution to the chart hard to distinguish from these larger forces.

law_title: قانون حمل و نقل و عبور كالاهاي خارجي از قلمرو جمهوري اسلامي ايران (Law on Transport and Transit of Foreign Goods through the Territory of the Islamic Republic of Iran)
justification: This law and its 1996-era implementing customs-clearance procedures for transit cargo form part of the longstanding legal-institutional backdrop against which Iran's later-measured customs clearance efficiency (part of the World Bank Logistics Performance Index) is assessed.
caveats: The LPI series only begins in the mid-2000s, nearly a decade after this law's enactment, and captures customs efficiency for ordinary trade as well as transit; day-to-day clearance performance is driven mostly by staffing, digitization, and sanctions-related banking frictions rather than this framework statute, so treat this as background context only, not a causal driver of period-to-period changes.

law_title: قانون تسهيل برقي كردن چاههاي كشاورزي (Law Facilitating the Electrification of Agricultural Wells)
lag_description: gradual, over a decade or more
justification: Mandates 24/7 power supply to agricultural well pumps, exempts agricultural wells from paying their share of power-generation/transmission investment costs, and directs National Iranian Oil Company oil-revenue transfers to subsidize the electrification rollout - lowering the marginal cost of groundwater pumping. Cheap, reliable electricity for well pumps is widely cited in the literature on Iran's water crisis as a key enabler of the explosive growth in unregulated/semi-regulated agricultural groundwater extraction from the 1990s-2010s.
caveats: Iran's rising freshwater withdrawal and water stress are driven by many compounding factors beyond this one law - successive droughts, population and irrigated-area growth, dam construction, inefficient flood-irrigation practices, weak enforcement of well permits, and other input subsidies (cheap diesel, guaranteed crop prices). This law is one piece of a broader subsidy regime, so its standalone quantitative contribution to national water-withdrawal figures cannot be isolated from aggregate WDI series.

law_title: قانون تسهيل برقي كردن چاههاي كشاورزي (Law Facilitating the Electrification of Agricultural Wells)
justification: By mandating utilities to prioritize and fully fund connection of agricultural wells to the grid (in place of diesel generators), the law incrementally extended rural electrical infrastructure into farming areas.
caveats: Iran's rural household electrification rate was already high and rising steadily before 2001 due to broader rural-development programs; this law targets farm well connections specifically rather than general rural household access, so its distinct contribution to the aggregate rural-electricity-access indicator is likely small and difficult to separate from the pre-existing trend.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1340 كل كشور (Annual National Budget Law, FY1340/1961-62)
law_date: FY1340 SH (1961/62); law text enacted 1343/07/28 (Oct 1964) — a retroactive/delayed enactment
lag_description: same fiscal year for the appropriations covered, but this particular law's text was not formally enacted until roughly three years after the fiscal year it covers
justification: As the general annual appropriations act for FY1340, this law set the Treasury's share of the state budget for that year, which is one of the four allocation buckets (Budget / NIOC / Plan Organization / B.P.C.) tracked in the oil-revenue-allocation chart for exactly this period.
caveats: The actual split of oil income between these four recipients was governed primarily by the Consortium Agreement and the Plan Organization's own budget law, not by this general appropriations act, so this law is at most one contributing input to the recorded split. The three-year enactment lag also means de facto spending for FY1340 likely proceeded under provisional authorizations before this law was finalized, weakening any same-year causal reading.

law_title: بودجه سال 1352 كل كشور (Annual National Budget Law, FY1352/1973-74)
justification: This is the enacted appropriations act whose enacted revenue and expenditure totals for FY1352 are the direct source data feeding this database's Pahlavi-era extension of the WDI government expenditure/revenue series for that year (confirmed present in the underlying majlis_budget_law_series data for fiscal year 1352).
caveats: These are enacted-law (ex-ante) figures, not audited outturns, so the recorded value reflects what was budgeted rather than what was actually spent/collected; actual execution could diverge, especially amid the 1973 oil-price boom that also drove large supplementary/mid-year budget revisions not captured by this base law alone.

law_title: قانون بودجه سال 1387 كل كشور (Annual National Budget Law, FY1387/2008-09)
justification: This annual budget act is the direct legal instrument whose enacted resource/expenditure totals constitute the FY1387 data point in both the 'Annual Budget Law Totals' series (built explicitly from these laws) and the SCI government-budget resources-and-uses summary for FY1385-1400.
caveats: Enacted totals can differ from actual budget execution/outturns (revisions, supplementary budgets, oil-revenue shortfalls were common in this period), so the chart value reflects the legislated plan for that year, not necessarily realized spending.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی شیوه‌های قیمت‌گذاری سهام، تخفیفها و چگونگی پرداخت قیمت توسط خریداران (Executive bylaw on share-pricing methods, discounts and payment terms for privatization sales)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as priced blocks are subsequently floated
justification: Sets the formal methodology (book value, earnings multiples, discounts) the Privatization Organization must use to price state-owned shares — including Justice Shares — before they can be sold or listed, a necessary precondition for the Article 44 divestment program that expanded Tehran Stock Exchange listings and market capitalization through the mid-to-late 2000s.
caveats: Market capitalization and listed-company counts are driven by many factors besides this pricing bylaw — inflation-driven equity rallies, interest-rate policy, sanctions-driven capital flight into equities, and the separate offering/listing bylaws — so this is one procedural input among several, not a standalone driver of the aggregate market trend.

law_title: آئین نامه شرایط عرضه سهام شرکت‌های مشمول واگذاری در بورس یا بازار خارج از بورس (Bylaw on conditions for offering privatization-eligible shares on the exchange or OTC market)
law_date: 1386/09/12 (Dec 2007), Supreme Council of the Stock Exchange
lag_description: 1-2 year lag as mandated floats are executed
justification: Mandates concrete IPO mechanics for state divestitures under the Article 44 policy — a minimum 5% retail tranche followed by at least 20% of shares within six months, plus mandatory disclosure of continuing state privileges/pricing arrangements — directly shaping the pace and depth of new listings and free-float on the Tehran Stock Exchange during the late-2000s privatization wave.
caveats: TSE market capitalization and listed-company counts over this period were also strongly shaped by high domestic inflation pushing savings into equities, sanctions limiting alternative investment channels, and macro liquidity growth, so this bylaw is a contributing procedural driver rather than the sole cause of index/listing growth.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده (۱۷) قانون اصلاح الگوی مصرف انرژی (Executive bylaw implementing Article 17 of the Energy Consumption Pattern Reform Law — ESCO contracts)
lag_description: gradual, over several years as ESCO contracts scale up
justification: Establishes state-subsidized insurance and loan-interest support for energy-service companies that sign performance contracts to cut clients' energy use, one of the implementing mechanisms of Iran's 2010 Energy Consumption Pattern Reform Law aimed at lowering national energy intensity.
caveats: National energy-intensity trends in Iran are dominated by much larger forces — the 2010 and subsequent rounds of energy-price/subsidy reform, industrial structure, and sanctions-driven economic contraction — so this narrow ESCO-subsidy scheme is at most a small contributing lever, not a primary driver of the aggregate energy-intensity or per-capita energy-use series.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قیمت‌گذاری سهام متعلق به دولت و شرکت‌های دولتی، تخفیف‌ها و چگونگی پرداخت قیمت توسط خریداران (Executive bylaw on pricing of state/state-company shares for divestment, discounts and buyer payment terms)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as priced shares are subsequently floated
justification: Successor/companion bylaw to the 2005 pricing methodology, formally governing how state and state-company shares (including Justice Shares) are valued and discounted before divestment, again a precondition step feeding the flow of new state-owned listings onto the Tehran Stock Exchange in the late-2000s privatization wave.
caveats: As with the related 2005 and 2007 pricing/offering bylaws, aggregate market-capitalization and listed-company trends reflect many concurrent macro and market forces (inflation, liquidity, sanctions), so this procedural pricing rule is only one contributing input.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي تبصره ماده 14 قانون هواي پاك (Clean Air Law Art.14 implementing bylaw — relocation of polluting industrial units)
lag_description: gradual over a decade (relocation/compliance is multi-year and enforcement was uneven)
justification: The bylaw creates a working group empowered to order polluting industrial/mining/service units to relocate out of populated areas, with subsidized-loan incentives and a fine for non-compliance, directly targeting industrial point-source pollution.
caveats: PM2.5 exposure and air-pollution mortality in Iran are driven mainly by vehicle fleet age/fuel quality, urban topography (esp. Tehran/Ahvaz), sanctions-limited catalytic-converter and fuel-standard technology, and weather/dust events, not just this bylaw. Implementation of forced relocation was historically slow and inconsistently enforced, so the marginal effect on national PM2.5 series is likely small and hard to isolate.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي افزايش ثروت خانوارها ... توزيع سهام عدالت (Justice Shares distribution — central/provincial headquarters bylaw)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag for initial rollout; effects on measured wealth shares likely materializing over a decade as shares vested and became tradable (mid-2010s)
justification: This bylaw establishes the central and provincial Justice Shares Headquarters that administered the transfer of state-company equity to the two lowest income deciles nationwide, an explicit government instrument to raise low-income household wealth and reduce top-heavy wealth/income concentration.
caveats: Justice Shares distribution stretched over many years, was frequently frozen or delayed, and share values were highly volatile with the stock market and rial devaluation, diluting any wealth-equalizing effect. Iran's top wealth/income shares are also driven by inflation, sanctions-driven asset reallocation, and informal-economy dynamics that swamp this single administrative bylaw's contribution; WID series are themselves estimates with wide uncertainty for Iran.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي گسترش بنگاههاي كوچك اقتصادي زودبازده وكارآفرين (Quick-return small-enterprise credit expansion bylaw)
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-3 year lag (bank credit quotas set annually 1384-1388 / 2005-2009)
justification: The bylaw mandates banks direct a rising share (20% to 50% over 1384-1388) of credit resources to subsidized loans for small enterprises (<50 employees) explicitly to boost employment and entrepreneurship, with interest-subsidy funding earmarked in annual budgets.
caveats: This was one of several concurrent employment/credit programs of the mid-2000s oil-boom era; private credit-to-GDP and self-employment rates are also driven by oil-revenue-fueled liquidity expansion, inflation, and broader macro cycles. The program is widely reported to have produced high loan-default and low project-completion rates, muting its real economic effect relative to its nominal credit allocation.

law_title: آيين نامه چگونگي قراردادهاي بيع متقابل غير نفتي (Non-oil buy-back/counter-trade investment contracts bylaw)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag (contract negotiation and project execution cycles)
justification: The bylaw sets the legal/procedural framework (guarantees under the Foreign Investment Law, tax and customs facilitation, repayment via export of produced goods) enabling non-oil buy-back financing arrangements, a channel intended to attract foreign capital into non-oil production without conventional FDI equity structures.
caveats: Buy-back financing is a debt-like, repayment-in-kind structure that may not even register as conventional FDI in balance-of-payments statistics; actual non-oil FDI inflows to Iran remained small and were dominated by sanctions status, oil price cycles, and broader investment climate rather than this procedural bylaw specifically.

law_title: قانون حفاظت و بهره برداری از منابع آبزی جمهوری اسلامی ایران (Law on Conservation and Exploitation of Aquatic Resources of the Islamic Republic of Iran)
lag_description: gradual over a decade+ (framework law predates chart data by 15-30 years)
justification: This 1995 framework law establishes Shilat (Iran Fisheries Organization) as the sole licensing/regulatory authority over all aquatic-resource exploitation, aquaculture (تکثیر و پرورش آبزیان), fishing-vessel permits, and export of fishery products (Arts. 3, 4, 6-9), which is the legal basis under which sturgeon aquaculture is licensed and caviar production/export is centrally recorded and controlled to this day.
caveats: This is a foundational regulatory/licensing framework, not a specific production quota or price instrument, and the observed 2010-2024 production/export levels are driven far more by wild sturgeon stock collapse (Caspian overfishing, pollution, dam construction), CITES export quotas, aquaculture technology adoption, and sanctions-driven export substitution than by this 1995 statute itself. Treat as background legal context enabling the data series to exist, not a direct causal driver of the trend.

law_title: قانون الحاق دولت جمهوری اسلامی ایران به موافقتنامه نظام جهانی ترجیحات بازرگانی بین کشورهای در حال توسعه (GSTP)
lag_description: no consistent lag; GSTP tariff concessions were negotiated and implemented piecemeal over subsequent decades
justification: Accession to the Global System of Trade Preferences (GSTP) commits Iran to preferential (lower) tariff treatment on a negotiated product list for imports from other developing-country GSTP members, a mechanism that in principle nudges down average applied tariffs on that slice of trade.
caveats: GSTP concessions cover only a narrow product list traded with a subset of developing countries and were implemented slowly and partially; Iran's average applied tariff rate is overwhelmingly shaped by domestic industrial-protection policy and periodic tariff-schedule reforms unrelated to GSTP. No discernible GSTP-attributable movement should be expected in the aggregate tariff-rate series.

law_title: قانون ساماندهی مبادلات مرزی
justification: The 2005 Border Trade Organization Law formalizes duty-exempt or reduced-duty import quotas for border residents and cooperatives operating through designated border markets (bazarcheh), with the total value capped at 3% of national imports matched against 3% of non-oil exports, directly shaping the volume and customs-duty yield of this defined trade channel.
caveats: The law legally caps this channel at a small, fixed share of total trade, so it cannot explain movements in aggregate merchandise trade or total customs revenue. Post-2012 growth in informal/border trade with neighboring countries was driven far more by sanctions-evasion incentives and multiple-exchange-rate arbitrage than by this administrative statute; unlicensed smuggling and under/over-invoicing outside the legal border-trade system are not captured by it either.

law_title: قانون اجازه تحصیل چهل و دو میلیون دلار وام از بانک بین‌المللی ترمیم و توسعه جهت ساختمان سد دز
lag_description: 3-8 year lag (dam built 1960-1963, powerhouse units phased in through the late 1960s)
justification: This law authorized a $42m World Bank loan to build the multi-purpose Dez Dam, including a hydroelectric powerhouse (four 65MW units) and transmission lines to Khuzestan cities, and created the Khuzestan Water and Power Authority — a direct, financed addition to national hydroelectric generating capacity.
caveats: Dez was one of several dams built in this era (Karaj/Amir-Kabir preceded it, Sefid Rud and others followed under the same Second/Third development plans), so national hydroelectric-share and generation-by-use series reflect the combined dam-building and thermal-capacity program, not this single loan alone. Attribution to one law is imprecise.

law_title: قانون تشکیل شرکتهای سهامی زراعی
lag_description: gradual, multi-year (program rolled out village-by-village through the 1970s-80s)
justification: This law consolidated fragmented post-land-reform smallholdings into joint-stock farming companies (sahami zarai) with pooled land, state capital, subsidized credit, and mechanization, explicitly aiming to raise per-farmer income and cropped-area productivity.
caveats: The sahami zarai program covered only a minority of Iran's arable land and is widely regarded by agricultural historians as underperforming its stated goals. National crop-production series are dominated by rainfall/drought variability, the broader 1962-71 land reform, oil-boom-era input subsidies, and post-1979 agricultural policy shifts, none of which are attributable to this specific institutional program.

law_title: قانون توسعه صنعت ایرانگردی و جهانگردی
justification: This law created the Supreme Council of Tourism, mandated industrial-rate bank financing and discounted land grants for tourism facilities, gave customs facilitation for inbound tourists, and centralized licensing of travel/tourism operators under the Ministry of Culture — the core post-war legal scaffolding for rebuilding Iran's tourism sector after the Iran-Iraq war.
caveats: International arrivals and receipts are heavily driven by sanctions status, visa policy, exchange-rate swings, safety perceptions, and diplomatic relations (e.g., the JCPOA-era 2015-2018 tourism uptick and subsequent sanctions-driven decline dwarf any effect traceable to this 1991 institutional law). Domestic and religious-pilgrimage tourism, not captured well by these indicators, followed a largely separate trajectory.

law_title: دستورالعمل تبادل اطلاعات مودیان مالیاتی موضوع ماده (120) قانون برنامه پنجم توسعه
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as inter-agency data-sharing infrastructure was built out
justification: This directive obliges all executive agencies -banks, customs, licensing bodies, insurers, property/company registries- to feed transaction, trade, and cash-flow data into the Tax Organization's database, directly strengthening third-party information used to assess income, corporate, and transaction taxes and curb evasion.
caveats: Tax revenue and its GDP share are dominated by oil-price cycles, rial devaluation/inflation (which mechanically inflates nominal tax receipts), the 2008 VAT law, and sanctions-driven contraction of the formal economy; this administrative data-sharing directive is one incremental compliance tool among many, and its isolated effect cannot be separated out in aggregate tax-revenue series.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی روش‌های واگذاری
lag_description: same year to multi-year (state enterprises were listed/sold in tranches through the 2010s)
justification: This executive bylaw operationalizes the Article 44 divestment law's specific transfer methods (stock-exchange listing or block sale of state shares, IPOs, restructure-then-sell, lease-to-own, management contracts, mergers), directly governing how and when large state and quasi-state enterprises entered the Tehran Stock Exchange.
caveats: TSE market capitalization and listed-company counts also move sharply with inflation, rial devaluation, sanctions-driven capital flight from bank deposits into equities (notably 2019-2020), and monetary expansion; the privatization program's specific contribution cannot be isolated from these larger macro-financial forces. Much of the 2000s-2010s 'privatization' was also widely criticized as transfers to quasi-state entities (khodemani/'nimeh dolati' bonyads and pension funds) rather than genuine market divestment, muddying the causal link to a market-based capitalization measure.

law_title: اصلاح آیین‌نامه اجرایی گسترش بنگاه‌های کوچک اقتصادی زودبازده و کارآفرین
justification: This amendment expanded the 'quick-return small enterprise' (zoodbazde) subsidized bank-credit scheme -adding a liquidity-shortfall carve-out, interest-rate subsidy contingent on realized job creation, and an 18,000-billion-rial bank funding mandate- administered through provincial employment task forces as a direct labor-market credit intervention aimed at cutting unemployment.
caveats: The zoodbazde credit program is widely documented (including in Central Bank and IMF-adjacent reviews) as generating substantial non-performing loans with limited durable job creation. National unemployment trends are far more strongly driven by the demographic entry of the 1980s baby-boom cohort into the labor force, oil-revenue-driven business cycles, and international sanctions than by this single credit scheme.

law_title: آیین‌نامه نحوه تأسیس و اداره مؤسسات اعتباری غیردولتی
lag_description: 1-3 year lag, with later contractionary effects once stricter licensing enforcement began (2017-2019 unlicensed-institution crackdown)
justification: This bylaw set licensing, minimum-capital, ownership-concentration, and governance requirements for establishing non-state banks and non-bank credit institutions, formalizing (and, through stricter vetting, later constraining) a segment of the credit system that had grown rapidly through unlicensed/unregulated 'credit institutions' (moassesat-e etebari) in the early 2010s.
caveats: Private-sector credit as a share of GDP is dominated by inflation (which mechanically inflates nominal credit while real credit access can stagnate), central-bank monetary-base growth, and directed-lending mandates. This bylaw's main documented effect was on the unlicensed-institution crisis specifically (many were later merged into state banks in 2018-2019 after depositor runs), a dynamic not well captured by aggregate private-credit-to-GDP or branch-count series.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون توسعه حمل‌ونقل عمومی و مدیریت مصرف سوخت
lag_description: gradual, multi-year (program ran 2008-2017 before most operational articles were repealed)
justification: This 2008 bylaw created a fuel-management task force (Setad) and tasked oil, industry and transport ministries with refinery upgrade programs, dual-fuel/CNG vehicle production support, and public-transit investment, all aimed at curbing gasoline/gasoil consumption growth.
caveats: Most operational articles were administrative deadlines for ministries to draft sub-programs (many later repealed in 2017), not self-executing price or quantity controls; actual domestic fuel consumption is far more strongly driven by the heavily subsidized retail price of gasoline/gasoil, population and vehicle-fleet growth, and the much larger 2010 targeted-subsidies price reform.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون توسعه حمل‌ونقل عمومی و مدیریت مصرف سوخت
justification: Article 17 directed the Ministry of Industries to produce a vehicle-production plan emphasizing dual-fuel (gasoline/CNG), low-consumption and hybrid/electric vehicles, which would have shaped the composition (though not necessarily the total level) of domestic vehicle output.
caveats: Overall national vehicle production is dominated by industrial-policy cycles, sanctions, and exchange-rate/import-parts availability shocks; this bylaw's effect on the composition of output cannot be isolated from those larger forces in aggregate production data.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی صرفه‌جویی مصرف انرژی در ساختمان‌ها
lag_description: gradual over a decade (compliance deadline set for 1399/2020-21)
justification: This 2017 bylaw, issued under Article 18 of the Energy Consumption Pattern Reform Law, required government buildings to undergo energy audits and retrofit toward 'green building' standards, with energy-carrier tariff penalties for non-compliant agencies.
caveats: The mandate applies only to public-sector buildings, a small share of the national building stock; residential and commercial energy use (the bulk of consumption) is governed by separate, far more powerful drivers -- heavily subsidized electricity/gas prices, population and housing-stock growth, and the private construction sector's own weak enforcement of building energy codes. Any national-level effect is speculative.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده (24) قانون رفع موانع تولید رقابت‌پذیر و ارتقای نظام مالی کشور
justification: This bylaw operationalized the divestment of state capital-asset projects via public auction, channeled the proceeds into recapitalizing state-owned banks, and mandated that those banks lend at least three times the recapitalized amount to qualified buyers of the divested projects -- a direct, legally mandated credit-expansion instrument.
caveats: Aggregate private-sector credit and private-credit-to-GDP ratios in Iran are dominated by monetary policy, high inflation, sanctions-driven balance-sheet stress in state banks, and directed-lending mandates from many other laws; this single instrument's incremental contribution cannot be isolated in the aggregate series.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده (37) قانون رفع موانع تولید رقابت‌پذیر و ارتقای نظام مالی کشور
lag_description: 1-3 year lag, cumulative over the 5-year non-adjustment window the bylaw itself set for export duties
justification: The bylaw bans export duties on essentially all goods except raw materials, subsidized goods and low-value-added goods, imposes export tariffs specifically on those categories (e.g. ores/raw minerals), and recycles the proceeds into export incentives (cash awards, subsidized-credit for value chains, marketing support) for higher-value-added exports -- a direct instrument to shift export composition away from raw-material exports toward processed/manufactured goods.
caveats: Iran's export composition is overwhelmingly driven by oil/gas export volumes and prices and by sanctions cutting off access to international markets for manufactured goods; this domestic tariff-and-incentive instrument is a second-order lever compared to those macro forces, and implementation (the list of taxed HS codes) depended on further Economic Council approvals not captured in the bylaw text itself.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده (52) قانون اصلاح الگوی مصرف انرژی
