justification: This bylaw promoted combined heat-and-power (CHP/cogeneration) technology deployment, feed-in purchase of surplus electricity by the state utility, and desalination co-location, financed via the Article 12 savings-based investment mechanism, intended to raise national energy-use efficiency.
caveats: This is a narrow, technology-specific promotion instrument (cogeneration adoption) whose national-level effect on aggregate energy intensity or per-capita energy use is minor relative to price subsidies, industrial structure, and the sanctions-driven investment climate; any link to an aggregate energy-use chart is speculative.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده (12) قانون رفع موانع تولید رقابت‌پذیر و ارتقای نظام مالی کشور
justification: This bylaw created a general savings-based investment/performance-contracting mechanism (used heavily in oil, gas and power projects) letting private investors finance efficiency and loss-reduction projects and be repaid from the resulting fuel/energy savings or extra revenue -- an ESCO-style financing tool intended to reduce fuel and energy waste in state enterprises.
caveats: This is a purely financial/contracting mechanism, not itself a price or quantity control; its incremental effect on national oil-product consumption is impossible to separate from subsidized pricing, sanctions-driven refinery investment constraints, and demand growth, and take-up under the mechanism is not documented at a scale likely to move aggregate consumption.

law_title: آیین‌نامه از رده خارج کردن خودروهای فرسوده
justification: This bylaw created a nationwide vehicle-scrappage scheme: cash grants and dedicated central-bank credit lines for owners scrapping old vehicles and buying new ones, a mandate that automakers surrender scrap certificates equal to at least 30% of monthly output, and preferential import tariffs for importers presenting scrap certificates -- a direct demand-side stimulus for new vehicle purchases/production.
caveats: The scrappage scheme coincided with the 2008-09 global financial crisis (many countries ran similar cash-for-clunkers programs) and with Iran's own credit-expansion and subsidy-reform cycles of that period; national vehicle production is also driven by sanctions, currency devaluation, and domestic-content/localization policy, so the scrappage program's isolated contribution to the production series is uncertain.

law_title: آیین‌نامه تأسیس صندوق‌های غیر دولتی توسعه صادرات و نحوه حمایت از آنها
justification: This bylaw let private-sector export trade associations establish non-government export-development funds with a 5-year tax exemption and 50% government cost-sharing on marketing/exhibition expenses, a soft institution-building instrument to support non-oil export growth.
caveats: Participation was voluntary and fund capitalization modest; non-oil export performance over this period was dominated by oil-price cycles, exchange-rate policy, and (from the mid-2000s) escalating international sanctions, so any contribution from this specific support mechanism is likely small and cannot be isolated in aggregate export data.

law_title: قانون تشکیل شرکتهای سهامی زراعی
lag_description: gradual over a decade or more (companies formed progressively through the 1970s)
justification: This land-reform-era law let the Ministry of Land Reform and Rural Cooperation consolidate the small, fragmented plots created by the 1962-63 land reform into joint-stock 'agricultural companies' (Sherkat Sahami Zar'ei), intended to enable mechanization, larger-scale cultivation, and higher farm incomes and cereal yields.
caveats: The agricultural joint-stock company program is historically regarded as only partially successful and was implemented in a limited number of regions; Iran's cereal/wheat production and yield trends over the 1960s-70s were also driven by irrigation investment (dam building), fertilizer/input subsidies, weather variability, and the broader White Revolution land-reform package, making the isolated effect of this specific corporate-consolidation instrument hard to identify in aggregate production series.

law_title: آيين‌نامه اجرايي قانون احداث پروژه‌هاي عمراني بخش راه و ترابري از طريق مشاركت بانك‌ها و ساير منابع مالي و پولي كشور
lag_description: gradual, as individual BOT/lease-to-own transport projects are structured and come online over several years
justification: This bylaw (repeatedly amended 1394-1402) is the operating executive regulation that lets the Ministry of Roads finance, build and operate road/rail infrastructure via bank-and-financial-market-funded build-operate-transfer or build-lease-transfer company structures rather than budget appropriations -- it is literally the legal mechanism WDI's private-participation-in-transport-infrastructure series is designed to track.
caveats: Whether any given year's PPI/PPP investment figure moves depends far more on bank liquidity, sanctions-driven capital scarcity, and specific project pipelines than on the bylaw's mere existence; the regulation only enables the channel, it does not guarantee deal flow.

law_title: تصويب‌نامه تعيين مجموع حقوق گمركي و سود بازرگاني كالاهاي مشمول رديف‌هاي تعرفه (40 رديف)
lag_description: same fiscal year for the specific tariff lines affected
justification: This is a routine cabinet decree resetting the combined customs-duty-plus-commercial-profit rate on 40 specific HS tariff codes under the standing import/export regulations bylaw -- a small piece of the constant stream of tariff-schedule maintenance that shapes landed import costs.
caveats: Only 40 narrowly defined tariff lines are touched (goods not identified in the excerpt read), so any effect is invisible in aggregate merchandise-import series, which are driven far more by the exchange rate, sanctions, and oil-revenue-funded import demand than by any single tariff decree of this kind.

law_title: آيين‌نامه وصول مطالبات سررسيد گذشته، معوق و مشكوك‌الوصول مؤسسات اعتباري (ريالي و ارزي)
lag_description: multi-year, as classification/collection practices reshape bank balance sheets
justification: This system-wide bylaw standardizes how all rial and forex credit institutions classify and pursue overdue, doubtful and non-performing loans, which affects loan-loss provisioning, willingness to extend new credit, and the reported quality/volume of bank credit to the private sector.
caveats: Aggregate private-sector credit-to-GDP is dominated by CBI monetary policy, directed/subsidized lending mandates, inflation and sanctions-driven balance-sheet effects; a single NPL-collection procedure bylaw is one contributing administrative factor among many and its net direction (tighter collection could either free up new lending capacity or make banks more cautious) is not determinable from the text alone.

law_title: آيين‌نامه اجرايي قانون نحوه اعمال تعزيرات حكومتي راجع به قاچاق كالا و ارز
lag_description: variable, dependent on enforcement intensity over time
justification: This is the standing executive bylaw operationalizing penalties, confiscation and adjudication procedures for goods and currency smuggling; stricter enforcement of currency-smuggling penalties is a plausible contributing factor in the size of the official-vs-parallel exchange-rate gap, and goods-smuggling enforcement affects how much cross-border trade shows up in official merchandise trade statistics versus informal channels.
caveats: The official/parallel FX gap and measured trade volumes are overwhelmingly driven by sanctions, multiple-exchange-rate policy, and macro conditions rather than enforcement of this decades-old bylaw; enforcement intensity itself is not documented in available data, so this link is largely speculative background context rather than a traceable causal driver.

law_title: رأی شماره 487 هيأت عمومي ديوان عدالت اداري - ابطال مصوبه دبيرخانه كارگروه تنظيم بازار (كودهاي شيميايي)
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-3 year lag as petrochemical fertilizer pricing shifts from the administered/negotiated 2014 price back toward the Targeted-Subsidies-Law bourse-based pricing mechanism
justification: The court struck down a Market Regulation Working Group directive that had forced petrochemical companies to sell urea/phosphate/potash fertilizer to farmers at a below-market price negotiated with the Agricultural Support Services Company, ruling the mandate exceeded the working group's authority and must instead follow the Targeted Subsidies Law's stock-exchange (bourse) pricing rule -- effectively removing a below-market price floor for domestic fertilizer purchased by farmers, which could raise input costs and dampen fertilizer application rates.
caveats: Fertilizer consumption per hectare in FAOSTAT/WDI data is also driven by import costs, exchange-rate devaluation, sanctions on fertilizer trade, drought-driven planting decisions, and separate government subsidy programs; this single administrative-law ruling affecting one 2014 pricing directive is only one contributing factor and its practical enforcement/duration is not verifiable from the ruling text alone.

law_title: قانون تغییر اعتبارات فصول و بندهای قانون برنامه عمرانی چهارم کشور
justification: A single-article law shuffling the internal chapter/clause allocations of the 4th Development Plan's budget (568bn rials across agriculture, oil/gas, water, power, roads, education, etc.) among sectors already appropriated; it reallocates, not adds, funding.
caveats: This is a technical intra-plan budget reallocation from over 50 years ago with no chart in the index tracking sub-sectoral development-plan appropriations at this granularity; any visible movement in broad indicators of that era is overwhelmingly driven by oil revenue and world prices, not this reallocation. No credible chart-level mechanism identified.

law_title: قانون توسعه موسسات برق غیر دولتی
justification: Mandates private/non-government electricity utilities to submit and execute capacity-expansion programs meeting per-capita wattage targets (80-200W depending on city size, +10%/year growth), with government empowered to nationalize non-compliant utilities; this is a direct instrument to expand electricity generation capacity.
caveats: Electricity generation growth in this era was driven mainly by government/Ministry of Water and Power investment (state utility build-out) funded by oil revenue, not solely this law targeting the smaller non-government utility segment; hard to isolate this law's specific contribution.

law_title: قانون موافقتنامه تشویق و حمایت متقابل ا ... جمهوری اسلامی ایران و دولت جمهوری اتریش
justification: Ratifies a bilateral investment treaty guaranteeing Austrian investors protection against expropriation, free repatriation of capital/profits, and dispute arbitration, intended to encourage bilateral FDI flows.
caveats: Austria is a minor source of FDI into Iran; this is one of dozens of similar BITs Iran signed in the 2000s, and aggregate FDI trends are dominated by sanctions regimes, oil price cycles, and overall investment climate rather than any single bilateral treaty.

law_title: آئین‌نامه شماره 70 درخصوص شرایط عمومی بیمه‌نامه نوسانات نرخ ارز واحدهای تولیدی - صادراتی
justification: Establishes the general terms for an optional insurance product that lets exporting/producing firms hedge against exchange-rate fluctuation risk on commercial contracts; it is a risk-mitigation instrument for individual firms, not a policy lever that itself moves the exchange rate or trade aggregates.
caveats: As a voluntary micro-level insurance product with unclear uptake, it has no plausible measurable footprint on any aggregate exchange-rate or trade chart we hold; any co-movement with the 2012 rial crisis (sanctions-driven) would be coincidental, not caused by this bylaw.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی اعطای تسهیلات بانکی در سال 1387
justification: Sets binding annual quotas (تسهیلات تکلیفی) requiring banks to extend a fixed pool of directed credit to state enterprises, agriculture procurement, and the cooperative/private sector for FY1387, a recurring annual mechanism used across many years to channel bank lending by government priority.
caveats: This is one single fiscal year's directed-credit decree among an annually repeated series of similar decrees; aggregate private-sector credit trends are driven far more by monetary policy, inflation, and sanctions-era liquidity conditions than by this one year's quota-setting.

law_title: اتخاذ تصمیماتی در خصوص چگونگی پرداخت جوایز و مشوق‌های صادراتی
justification: Sets the mechanics for non-oil export cash awards (scaled to value-added share and export competitiveness), transport subsidies for perishable exports, packaging subsidies, and market-development grants, directly intended to boost non-oil export volumes and values for the 1385 fiscal year.
caveats: This is a single-year, narrowly-scoped incentive decree (with several amendments/annulments by administrative courts later); non-oil export trends over the 2000s were far more strongly shaped by the exchange rate, oil-revenue-driven overvaluation, and sanctions than by this award scheme.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قیمت‌گذاری سهام دولت در شرکت‌ها جهت پرداخت دیون به صندوق‌های بازنشستگی و واگذاری به کارکنان برای اصلاح ساختار نیروی انسانی و بازخریدی آنان
justification: A purely technical share-valuation methodology (book value adjustments, P/E-style formulas, bourse-listing price rules) for pricing state-owned company shares that are transferred to pension funds as debt settlement or sold to redundant workers as part of workforce restructuring buyouts.
caveats: This is an internal accounting/valuation procedure, not a policy that itself drives privatization volume, listed-company counts, or market capitalization; any co-movement with capital-market growth in the early-2000s Article 44 privatization push is better attributed to the broader privatization law and macro liquidity conditions.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده (44) قانون رفع موانع تولید رقابت‌پذیر و ارتقای نظام مالی کشور
justification: Despite the law's title referencing production-barrier removal, this specific implementing bylaw for Article 44 establishes non-governmental research-and-technology funds with government co-financing (up to 49% of capital), aimed directly at expanding R&D financing infrastructure for applied/developmental technology.
caveats: R&D expenditure as a share of GDP is affected by overall GDP growth, university/government research budgets, and sanctions-driven access to technology; this single co-financing mechanism is one contributor among many and its actual funding scale relative to national R&D spending is likely small.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی مواد (5) و (6) قانون مبارزه با قاچاق کالا و ارز
justification: Establishes nationwide smuggling-detection and trade-monitoring systems (comprehensive trade system, single-window customs platform) integrating banking and tax data to curb goods and currency smuggling, which could plausibly narrow the informal/parallel FX market and shift some smuggled trade into officially recorded channels.
caveats: 2016-2018 coincides with the JCPOA's collapse and re-imposition of US sanctions (2018), which overwhelmingly widened the official-parallel exchange rate gap and drove currency smuggling regardless of this bylaw; isolating this law's effect from the sanctions shock is not credible with confidence.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون تشکیل و اداره مناطق ویژه اقتصادی جمهوری اسلامی ایران
justification: Sets the governance, customs, and administrative framework for Iran's Special Economic Zones (SEZs), which offer streamlined customs procedures and investment incentives intended to attract manufacturing/export-oriented investment and boost non-oil exports processed through the zones.
caveats: SEZs represent a small share of Iran's total trade and investment; national FDI and export trends are dominated by sanctions, oil revenue, and exchange-rate policy rather than SEZ administrative arrangements, and no chart isolates SEZ-specific activity.

law_title: آیین‌نامه پیشگیری از انباشت مطالبات غیرجاری بانکی
justification: Issued by the anti-corruption coordination headquarters, this bylaw sets procedural rules for banks to prevent build-up of non-performing loans (early-warning criteria, restructuring/write-off controls), which could tighten new lending standards and affect loan-to-deposit dynamics.
caveats: No chart in the index directly tracks NPL ratios; the closest proxy (provincial loan-to-deposit ratio, 2019-2023) is affected far more by the 2018-2020 sanctions-driven liquidity crunch, inflation, and currency depreciation than by this prudential bylaw specifically.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون تشویق و حمایت سرمایه‌گذاری خارجی
justification: Implements the landmark 2002 Foreign Investment Promotion and Protection Act (FIPPA), Iran's modern FDI legal framework replacing the 1955 law, guaranteeing capital/profit repatriation, expropriation protection, and creating the OIETAI licensing body -- a direct, purpose-built instrument to increase inbound FDI.
caveats: Iran's FDI inflows over the 2000s-2010s were episodic and heavily shaped by sanctions cycles (tightening 2006-2015, relief 2016-2018, re-imposition 2018-), oil price swings, and political relations far more than by this legal framework alone; FIPPA created the legal possibility for FDI growth but could not overcome sanctions-driven investor risk aversion in most years.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی تبصره (۲) ماده (۷) قانون حمایت از احیاء، بهسازی و نوسازی بافت‌های فرسوده و ناکارآمد شهری
justification: The bylaw authorizes new housing-finance instruments (mortgage bonds, housing savings funds, worn-out-fabric investment funds) to channel private capital into urban renewal, but it creates institutional plumbing rather than a price or quantity outcome we track.
caveats: No chart in the index measures housing prices, housing investment, or mortgage-bond issuance for the relevant post-2014 period; the only housing chart we hold is a pre-2016 census tenure series. No credible chart mapping — omitted.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده (۶) قانون الحاق موادی به قانون تنظیم بخشی از مقررات مالی دولت
lag_description: gradual, multi-year (large infrastructure/BOT projects take years to develop)
justification: The bylaw lets the Ministry of Economic Affairs and Finance and Central Bank guarantee repayment obligations of Iranian state companies under BOT, buyback, and civil-partnership contracts with foreign investors, reducing sovereign/counterparty risk and making state-sector FDI deals more bankable.
caveats: FDI inflows into Iran are dominated by sanctions status, oil prices, and broader political relations, which swamp any effect from this narrow guarantee mechanism; the instrument mainly covers project-specific state-company deals, not economy-wide investment climate.

law_title: بخشنامه سازمان توسعه تجارت ایران درخصوص ابلاغ اصلاحات تعرفه‌ای سال ۱۴۰۴ و اصلاحات طبقات سود بازرگانی متناسب با تغییرات نرخ ارز مبنای محاسبه حقوق ورودی
justification: This circular directly recalibrates the FY1404 tariff schedule and commercial-profit brackets to the new customs base exchange rate, mechanically changing the rial value of import duties assessed on the same physical import volumes.
caveats: Effect is a technical/administrative pass-through of an underlying FX-rate change (itself driven by broader currency depreciation/sanctions dynamics), not an independent tariff-policy decision; too recent for chart data to reflect it yet, and WDI series lag several years.

law_title: رأی شماره ۲۵۵۲ هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری - ابطال بخشنامه مالیاتی درباره مالیات بر افزایش ارزش سهام (سرمایه‌گذاری‌های جاری) شرکت‌های بورسی
justification: The ruling struck down a National Tax Administration circular that had let tax officials levy income tax on unrealized (mark-to-market) increases in the value of listed shares held by firms as current investments; removing this double-taxation risk lowers the effective tax burden on holding equities and could support market capitalization and trading activity.
caveats: Iran's stock market (TEDPIX/market cap) in this period was driven far more by inflation expectations, exchange-rate depreciation, and the 1399-1400 bubble-and-crash cycle than by this narrow administrative tax dispute; the ruling addressed unrealized/mark-to-market treatment for corporate holders specifically, not the broader retail market.

law_title: قانون اصلاح قانون راجع به تأسیس شرکت شهرکهای صنعتی ایران
justification: The amendment restructures governance and financing of the Iran Industrial Estates Company (adds ministry oversight, mandates land-value-based capital recovery, treats inter-company transfers as grants, and directs the company to expand the number of industrial parks), strengthening the institutional vehicle that supplies serviced industrial land nationwide.
caveats: Industrial/manufacturing value added is driven far more by sanctions, oil revenue cycles, exchange-rate policy, and energy subsidies than by industrial-park administration; this is one of many industrial-estate bylaws over the decades and its marginal contribution cannot be isolated.

law_title: قانون اجازه تبدیل به احسن و واگذاری دهات و مزارع موقوفه عام به زارعین صاحب نسق
justification: Part of the Shah-era land-reform program, the law compels the Ministry of Cooperatives and Rural Affairs to purchase waqf farm villages and redistribute them to tenant farmers (nasaq holders), a land-tenure change historically debated as a driver of agricultural productivity and output.
caveats: This is one narrow slice (waqf endowment land only) of a much larger multi-decade land-reform program; agricultural production/land series in our index are diffuse WDI aggregates whose movements are dominated by weather, oil-boom-era investment, and later the 1979 revolution and war, making any isolated effect from this specific law speculative. No specific chart id included given the weakness of the link; recorded as a category-level, low-confidence background factor only.

law_title: قانون متمم قانون بودجه سال 1384 کل کشور
justification: This mid-year supplementary budget law directly added roughly 46,000+70,000 billion rials to FY1384 expenditure/revenue lines (personnel costs, subsidies, provincial deficits), which by definition raises the total annual budget-law figure for that year.
caveats: Effect is small relative to the full annual budget and is a routine technical top-up rather than a policy shift; other supplementary and main budget laws for the same year also move this aggregate, so isolating this one instrument's contribution is not possible from the chart alone.

law_title: قانون تشکیل مرکز توسعه صادرات ایران
lag_description: gradual, multi-year (institution-building lag before export-promotion programs mature)
justification: Establishes Iran's Export Development Center, funded via customs surcharges on machinery imports, to run export subsidies/prizes and marketing/promotion for Iranian manufactured exports, a direct instrument aimed at raising non-oil export volumes.
caveats: Aggregate export series in our index are dominated by oil exports, which this law does not target; non-oil export growth over subsequent decades also reflects industrialization policy, exchange-rate regime, and global demand, so the marginal effect of this one institution cannot be isolated.

law_title: قانون جامع کنترل و مبارزه ملی با دخانیات
justification: Article 8 mandates an annual 10% tax-driven price increase on tobacco products, combined with an advertising ban (Art. 3), mandatory graphic health warnings covering 50% of packaging (Art. 5), and government-only import licensing (Art. 4) -- a direct, intentional package of demand-reduction instruments.
caveats: Global secular decline in smoking prevalence, rising incomes, other health-education campaigns, and enforcement gaps (well-documented smuggling of untaxed cigarettes in Iran) all also move tobacco-use prevalence, so the law's isolated effect is hard to separate from these broader trends.

law_title: قانون جامع کنترل و مبارزه ملی با دخانیات
justification: By suppressing demand and centralizing import authority, the law could indirectly dampen domestic tobacco-leaf production and shift the import-dependency profile of the market documented in the 2018 snapshot.
caveats: Domestic tobacco leaf production and the 2012 privatization of the state tobacco monopoly (Tobacco Company of Iran) are driven mainly by agricultural policy and industrial restructuring, not this health law; the causal chain from consumption-control rules to farm-level production is indirect and weak.

law_title: قانون معافیت از پرداخت سهم بیمه کارفرمایانی که حداکثر پنج نفر کارگر دارند
lag_description: gradual, multi-decade (structural incentive in force continuously)
justification: Exempts employers with up to 5 workers from the employer's share of social-insurance premiums, a standing labor-cost subsidy that lowers the cost of staying small/informal and can encourage self-employment and micro-firm employment relative to larger formal payrolls.
caveats: Iran's high shares of self-employment and vulnerable employment reflect many structural factors (economic structure, sanctions-driven SME dominance, weak enforcement of labor law, informal sector norms); this single long-standing exemption is one of several policies pulling in the same direction and cannot be isolated in the aggregate WDI series.

law_title: قانون مربوط باجازه ساختمان سدهای درودزن و زرینه‌رود
lag_description: gradual over a decade (construction + reservoir commissioning lag)
justification: Authorizes government financing (up to $45m in foreign credit) for the Dorudzan (Fars) and Zarrinehrud (Azerbaijan) dams for irrigation water and electricity generation, part of Iran's late-1950s/1960s national dam-building program that expanded irrigated land and hydroelectric capacity.
caveats: These two dams are only a fraction of Iran's multi-dam construction program (Dez, Karaj, Safid Rud, Karun cascade, etc.) that ran over subsequent decades; national aggregate irrigated-land and hydroelectric-share series reflect the cumulative effect of dozens of such projects plus broader agricultural and energy policy, so this single law's marginal contribution cannot be isolated.

law_title: قانون گسترش کشاورزی در قطبهای کشاورزی
lag_description: gradual over a decade or more (irrigation networks take years to build)
justification: Law directs the Ministry of Agriculture to build comprehensive irrigation/drainage infrastructure and designates ~20 named 'agricultural poles' (Qazvin, Sefid-Rud, Dez, Varamin, Zayandeh-Rud, Moghan, Khuzestan schemes, etc.) for intensified farming and livestock, directly targeting irrigated land expansion and crop yields.
caveats: Effects are diffuse and multi-decade; land reform, oil-revenue-financed dam building, weather/drought cycles, and the 1979 revolution's later reorganization of these same lands (see law fdff449d7e22) all confound attribution of any national-level irrigated-land or crop-yield trend to this single 1975 law.

law_title: قانون تأسیس شرکت سهامی خاص توسعه نیشکر و صنایع جانبی آن
lag_description: 1-5 year lag (state company had to be founded, capitalized, and build production units before output shows up)
justification: Single-article law authorizes the Ministry of Agriculture to found a dedicated state sugarcane development company (funded via the First Development Plan's Note 29(h) credits) specifically to build sugarcane cultivation and processing capacity, directly targeting national sugarcane/sugar output.
caveats: Sugar output also depends on world sugar prices, water availability in Khuzestan, and later privatization of the resulting production units (per Tabsare 4), so growth in sugar production cannot be attributed solely to this founding law.
chart_ids: faostat__Raw cane or beet sugar (centrifugal only)__production

law_title: قانون افزایش اعتبارات برنامه سوم
lag_description: same-to-following fiscal years (1964-1968, remainder of the Third Development Plan)
justification: Raises Third Development Plan appropriations from 145bn to 200bn rials, with the increase spread across agriculture/irrigation, industry/mining, power/fuel, communications, health, education, and urban development chapters -- a broad increase in state development spending that would show up, if at all, in aggregate public investment/consumption measures.
caveats: This is a routine mid-plan credit top-up, not a targeted instrument on any single measure; Iran's 1960s growth was overwhelmingly driven by rising oil revenue and land reform, and WDI-style national accounts series for Iran in this era are thin, so any correlation with capital formation is weak and largely coincidental with the broader oil-financed development boom.

law_title: لایحه قانونی راجع به معافیت ماشین‌آلات تولیدی ... از پرداخت حقوق گمرکی و سود بازرگانی
lag_description: gradual, ongoing (law remained in force for decades, amended 2011, only overridden by the 2008 VAT law)
justification: Exempts production machinery imported by licensed industrial/mining production units from customs duty and commercial-profit tax, directly lowering the cost of importing capital machinery and thus incentivizing machinery imports and industrial capital deepening.
caveats: Effect is one of many trade-policy instruments (later general tariff schedules, VAT law, sanctions-driven import restrictions) shaping machinery imports over 40+ years; the exemption's scope (only 'licensed' units, subject to ministry discretion) limits its economy-wide bite, and sanctions/exchange-rate shocks are likely larger drivers of import volume swings.

law_title: قانون عدم الزام سپردن وثیقه ملکی به بانکها ... اشتغال بیشتر در طرحهای تولیدی و صادراتی
lag_description: 1-3 year lag (credit access easing feeds through to investment/hiring with a delay)
justification: Requires state banks and institutions to accept a wide menu of alternative collateral (project liens, agricultural documents, chain guarantees, export-credit insurance, etc.) instead of mandatory real-estate collateral for production and export project loans, explicitly to ease investment financing and boost employment, particularly for rural/agricultural and export-oriented borrowers.
caveats: Credit expansion and employment are driven by many macro factors (oil-price cycle, exchange rate, overall monetary policy, sanctions); this law only removes one specific lending friction and its aggregate effect on national credit-to-GDP or unemployment is likely small relative to those forces.

law_title: لایحه قانونی مالکیت و بهره‌برداری از اراضی واقع در آبخور سد دز و الغاء قراردادهای کشت و صنعت
lag_description: same-to-following years, disruption; multi-year for any recovery
justification: Revolutionary Council decree voids all existing agribusiness ('kesht-o-sanat') contracts and land-tenure arrangements in the Dez dam catchment (Khuzestan), transferring the Pahlavi-era corporate agribusiness lands (sugarcane, cotton) to the Ministry of Agriculture for reallocation to smallholders -- a major regional land-tenure shock in a key sugarcane/cotton growing area.
caveats: Any national production dip or shift around 1979-1980 is heavily confounded by the wider Revolution, the onset of the Iran-Iraq War in the same Khuzestan region in 1980, and general agricultural disruption of that period, making it hard to isolate this specific land-tenure law's effect from war/revolution effects.

law_title: قانون راجع به ساختمان و بهره‌برداری سد و تأسیسات لار و لتیان
lag_description: several-year lag (dam/hydro construction takes years; Latian completed ~1967, Lar ~1980)
justification: Authorizes and funds construction/operation of the Lar and Latian dams and associated hydroelectric/water-supply installations serving Tehran, a direct instrument increasing national hydroelectric generation capacity and water/power access for the capital region.
caveats: National hydroelectric share and electricity-access figures reflect many other dam and thermal-power projects built over the same decades (Dez, Karaj, Sefid-Rud, etc.), so isolating this specific pair of dams' contribution to the aggregate series is not possible from the chart alone.

law_title: قانون تأسیس بیمه مرکزی ایران و بیمه‌گری
lag_description: immediate (institution created by the law) with effects compounding over subsequent decades
justification: This law is the founding statute of Bimeh Markazi Iran (Central Insurance of Iran / the state reinsurer and industry regulator); the chart tracking Bimeh Markazi Iran's own financial highlights is a direct record of the entity this law creates.
caveats: The specific 2007-2013 financial figures reflect decades of subsequent regulatory and market changes (post-revolution nationalization of insurers, 2000s market liberalization) rather than the 1971 founding law itself, so the law explains the institution's existence, not its later financial performance level.

law_title: قانون تشکیل بانک تعاون کشاورزی ایران
lag_description: gradual over 1970s (credit institution build-out and loan disbursement take years to affect output)
justification: Establishes a dedicated agricultural cooperative bank to channel credit to farmer cooperatives formed after the White Revolution land reforms, a plausible contributor to 1970s growth in agricultural investment and output via expanded rural credit access.
caveats: No chart in the index tracks this bank specifically or agricultural credit volumes directly; any link to aggregate crop production is indirect and shares the stage with irrigation investment, guaranteed-price policy, and oil-revenue-financed input subsidies of the same era.

law_title: آيين نامه نحوه تأمين برق و سوخت صنايع (Bylaw on Electricity/Fuel Supply Arrangements for Industries)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag (plant construction and grid-connection take time after guarantees are issued)
justification: Article 9 of the bylaw guarantees fuel supply and priority support to non-governmental (private) power plants built to serve industrial energy demand, and obliges Tavanir/oil-and-power ministries to treat private generators on equal footing with state plants for fuel delivery and subsidy allocation — a direct administrative lever encouraging private capital into electricity generation.
caveats: WDI 'Investment in energy with private participation' is a global-methodology aggregate that does not isolate this specific bylaw's effect; Iran's broader privatization drive (Article 44 policy), international sanctions restricting foreign capital and technology, and swings in global energy investment appetite are far larger drivers of any movement in this series than this single administrative bylaw.

law_title: ضوابط سياستي-نظارتي شبكه بانكي كشور در سال 1388 (Annual Banking-Network Policy/Supervisory Directives, FY1388/2009)
justification: This is the Money and Credit Council's binding annual banking-system directive for FY1388, explicitly fixing deposit legal-reserve ratios (e.g. cutting sight-deposit reserves from 20% to 17%), ceiling deposit and facility profit rates, mandating a minimum 25% of bank facilities to agriculture/water and target shares to industry (35%), housing (20%) and exports (8%), and setting the interbank overdraft penalty rate at 34% — directly shaping that year's credit expansion, deposit growth and effective lending rates.
caveats: This is one of many nearly-identical annual directives Iran issues every year (the same instrument recurs with updated numbers each fiscal year), so isolating FY1388's specific incremental effect from the general policy regime is not possible from the chart alone; aggregate credit and rate outcomes were also driven by oil-revenue-financed fiscal expansion, high inflation that year, and the broader banking-sector distress that would later surface as non-performing loans.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Implementing Bylaw of the Export/Import Regulations Act)
law_date: 1988 (1367), continuously amended through the 1990s and later re-affirmed
lag_description: gradual over a decade (foundational, standing regulation rather than a one-time shock)
justification: This bylaw establishes the core administrative architecture of Iran's entire post-revolution foreign-trade regime: annual permitted-goods lists, the currency-conversion ('پیمان ارزی') obligation tying import rights to surrendered export earnings, export-price-setting commissions, tariff discount/rebate schedules, and border-trade cooperative quotas. As the operating machinery for essentially all merchandise trade, it forms the background regulatory context for the entire trade-data time series rather than moving any single measure.
caveats: Because this is a standing framework regulation amended dozens of times rather than a discrete policy shock, it cannot be credited with causing any specific year-to-year movement in trade charts; oil-export revenue swings, sanctions regimes, and exchange-rate policy are the dominant drivers of Iran's trade volumes and composition throughout this period. Flagging as background context, not a causal driver of any particular data point.

law_title: دستورالعمل حقوق ورودي گوشي تلفن همراه (Directive Setting Customs Duty on Imported Mobile Phones)
justification: Sets the exact tiered customs-duty schedule (roughly 4-15%+ depending on declared euro-denominated price band and import channel — commercial, passenger or postal) for all imported mobile handsets, directly determining the landed cost and therefore volume/value of this specific ICT import category.
caveats: ICT goods imports in WDI cover laptops, computers and telecom equipment well beyond phones, so this narrow directive is only one contributor to that aggregate; overall ICT import value is far more sensitive to the rial exchange rate, sanctions on international payment/logistics channels, and household purchasing power. Given the directive's 2024-2025 date, WDI's typical reporting lag may not yet reflect any effect at all.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي قانون جلوگيري از خردشدن اراضي كشاورزي (Implementing Bylaw, Farmland-Fragmentation Prevention Act)
justification: Bans subdividing farmland below fixed technical/economic minimum parcel sizes and directs subsidized credit, irrigation-network investment and mechanization support toward farmers who consolidate ('یکپارچه‌سازی') fragmented holdings into larger, more efficient units — a textbook land-consolidation mechanism aimed at raising per-hectare productivity.
caveats: Enforcement of Iranian land-fragmentation rules has historically been weak against inheritance-driven subdivision and informal land transactions; cereal yield in Iran is much more strongly and immediately driven by rainfall/drought cycles, water-table depletion, and input-subsidy policy than by this consolidation bylaw.

law_title: آيين نامه صندوق اشتغال، ازدواج و مسكن مهر امام رضا (ع) (Imam Reza Mehr Employment, Marriage and Housing Fund Bylaw)
justification: Establishes a qard al-hasaneh (interest-free) credit fund providing small loans for youth start-up employment, marriage expenses and housing, intended to marginally ease labor-market entry and household formation, particularly in underserved regions.
caveats: This is one small charitable/quasi-governmental micro-credit fund among many overlapping state employment and marriage-support schemes; national labor-force participation and unemployment trends are overwhelmingly driven by the youth demographic bulge, sanctions-driven recessions, and overall GDP growth rather than this fund. No marriage- or housing-specific chart in the database has data overlapping the fund's active years (existing marriage/housing charts end in 2006, the year before this fund was created), so any link is necessarily indirect and speculative.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي ماده 61 قانون اصلاح الگوي مصرف انرژي (Implementing Bylaw, Article 61 of the Energy Consumption Pattern Reform Act — renewable/private power feed-in tariff)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag (project development and grid connection take time after a tariff guarantee)
justification: Sets a formal guaranteed long-term feed-in-tariff formula (base rate = energy-conversion value + avoided-pollution/water savings + avoided-fuel value, indexed to the euro exchange rate and retail price index) that non-governmental renewable and clean power producers receive for grid-connected output — a direct, purpose-built price instrument to induce private investment in power generation.
caveats: Renewable generation remained a small share of Iran's power mix throughout this period because heavily subsidized fossil generation (natural gas) undercut the economics of the guaranteed tariff, international sanctions blocked access to foreign renewable technology and project financing, and rial depreciation eroded the local value of the euro-indexed tariff over time. WDI's aggregate series cannot isolate this bylaw's specific contribution from these larger countervailing forces.

law_title: آيين نامه اعطاي اعتبارات صادراتي ويژه افغانستان (Special Export Credit Facility for Afghanistan)
justification: Earmarks a modest special export-credit line — five designated state banks (Melli, Mellat, Tejarat, Refah, Sepah), each authorized up to $10 million per year for five years — specifically to finance Iranian exporters' and buyers' trade with Afghanistan, including post-conflict reconstruction-related goods.
caveats: The facility's maximum scale (roughly $50 million/year across all five banks, and not even a binding obligation — the text notes it does not constitute a mandatory facility for the banking network) is negligible relative to Iran's total merchandise exports and the broader South Asia regional-export-share series, which is dominated by trade with Pakistan and India and by macro factors such as sanctions and oil-linked import capacity, not this narrow bilateral credit line. This link is speculative and included for completeness, not because a measurable effect is expected.

law_title: Implementing Bylaws on Imported vs. Domestically-Made Road-Building Machinery/Vehicles
justification: Implements a sliding customs-duty discount formula (tied to each vehicle or component's domestic-content share, measured via the 'IPI' local-value-added list, and to export performance) that rewards manufacturers for localizing parts production of vehicles and road-building machinery — part of the early-1990s import-substitution push behind Iran's post-war automotive industry buildup.
caveats: Iran's vehicle-production growth in this era was driven mainly by broader state industrial policy, foreign joint-venture/licensing deals (e.g., with Peugeot, Kia), and post-Iran-Iraq-War reconstruction demand; this specific customs-discount formula is one administrative lever among several, several of its provisions were later repealed in 2024, and it also covers non-automotive machinery (road-building equipment) not captured by the vehicle-production chart at all.

law_title: Support Scheme for Large Centralized Carpet-Weaving Complexes
justification: Provides a bank-loan interest-rate subsidy (up to 4 percentage points, budgeted annually by the Management and Planning Organization) plus formal licensing, health-insurance coverage for weavers, and quality/traceability certification requirements for large centralized hand-weaving workshops, intended to formalize and modernize production and support the export competitiveness of Iranian handwoven carpets.
caveats: The carpet export chart is itself flagged as 'sparse' in the post-1994 era; Iran's handwoven carpet exports fell sharply over this period mainly due to competition from machine-made and Indian/Pakistani/Afghan hand-made rivals, US/EU sanctions disrupting payment channels for Iranian goods, and a shrinking domestic pool of artisan weavers — structural pressures this relatively small support scheme was unlikely to offset.

law_title: قانون سازمان برق ایران (Iran Electricity Organization Law)
justification: Created the national state electricity organization (precursor to Tavanir/Ministry of Energy structure) with authority to build, operate and regulate generation/distribution nationwide, directly driving the buildout of Iran's grid and generation capacity documented in the 1968-72 power-generation chart and long-run electrification/consumption trends.
caveats: Electrification and consumption growth over subsequent decades also reflect oil-revenue-funded infrastructure spending, population/urban growth, and later post-revolution and post-war reconstruction programs, not this law alone.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون ارتقاء کیفی تولید خودرو و سایر تولیدات صنعتی داخلی (Auto/industrial quality upgrade bylaw)
justification: Mandated compulsory standards for domestic and imported vehicles/parts, banned import/registration of non-compliant vehicles, and directed the Ministry to propose protective tariffs for domestic producers — a direct industrial-policy lever on the domestic auto sector captured in the national vehicle production series.
caveats: Iranian vehicle production in this period was more heavily driven by sanctions on imported parts, currency devaluation, and periodic import bans/tariff changes than by this quality-standard bylaw specifically; production could rise (import substitution) or fall (compliance costs, parts shortages) depending on enforcement.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده 33 قانون افزایش بهره‌وری بخش کشاورزی (Guaranteed agricultural price / commodity-exchange implementing bylaw)
justification: Establishes the mechanism for state-guaranteed minimum purchase prices (قیمت تضمینی) for farm products traded on the commodity exchange, with government paying producers the gap between guaranteed price and exchange price — a direct administrative floor under producer prices for staple crops.
caveats: FAOSTAT producer prices for Iran are dominated by general inflation, exchange-rate depreciation, and input-cost shocks (fertilizer, fuel); the guaranteed-price mechanism is one policy layer among many and was inconsistently funded/enforced across years.

law_title: ضوابط سیاستی-نظارتی شبکه بانکی کشور (Annual banking-network policy/supervisory directive)
justification: Sets mandatory sectoral credit-allocation quotas across banks (agriculture 25%, industry/mining 33%, housing 20%, exports 7%) and administered subsidized lending rates (10% productive, 12% general), directly shaping the volume and price of bank credit to the private sector.
caveats: Aggregate private-sector credit and real interest rates in Iran are driven far more by CBI monetary policy, inflation, and government borrowing needs than by this one annual directive, which is reissued/revised most years with shifting quotas.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون جلوگیری از خرد شدن اراضی کشاورزی (Bylaw preventing agricultural land fragmentation)
justification: Bars notaries and banks from registering subdivisions/loans on farm parcels below the technically-viable minimum size, aiming to consolidate holdings into larger, more mechanizable units that the law's drafters expected to raise yields.
caveats: Cereal yield and crop production in Iran are dominated by rainfall/drought cycles, irrigation investment, and input (fertilizer, seed) access — this land-tenure bylaw is a minor, weakly-enforced contributor at best and its effect is not separable from these larger drivers.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون حمایت صنعتی و جلوگیری از تعطیل کارخانه‌های کشور (Bylaw to prevent factory closures via bank-debt restructuring)
justification: Set up inter-agency committees and procedures for restructuring troubled manufacturers' bank debts to keep factories operating, issued amid the 2018 re-imposition of US sanctions — a defensive/relief instrument aimed at cushioning manufacturing value added.
caveats: Manufacturing value added in 2018-2019 collapsed primarily because of the sanctions shock and rial devaluation; this bylaw is a mitigating response measure, not an independent driver, and its practical enforcement/coverage is unclear.

law_title: ضوابط فعالیت شرکت‌های تأمین سرمایه (Investment/underwriting company operating rules)
justification: Established the regulatory framework for Iran's first investment-banking/underwriting firms, an institutional building block for corporate bond and equity issuance that fed into subsequent Tehran Stock Exchange growth in listings and trading value.
caveats: TSE market capitalization and trading volume in the following decade were driven mainly by the Article 44 privatization program, subsidy-cash injections into households buying shares, and sanctions-era capital flight into equities as an inflation hedge — this regulation is one minor institutional precondition among much larger drivers.

law_title: آئین‌نامه شماره 60 درخصوص سرمایه‌گذاری مؤسسات بیمه (Insurance company investment regulation)
justification: Sets permitted asset classes and allocation ceilings (deposits, bonds, equities, real estate) for insurers' investment portfolios, directly shaping the investment-income component of insurer financial statements from the mid-2000s onward.
caveats: Insurer financial performance (including Bimeh Markazi's) is driven mainly by underwriting/reinsurance cycles, inflation, and the oil-linked business cycle; this asset-allocation rule affects only the investment-income line and its practical bite depended on how binding the ceilings were relative to insurers' actual portfolios.

law_title: بخشنامه گمرک در خصوص اصلاحات تعرفه ایی سال ۱۴۰۴ (Customs circular on 1404 tariff schedule amendments)
justification: Implements cabinet resolution amending 240 tariff lines (raising/lowering business-profit duty, splitting/merging/creating tariff codes) and explicitly lowers business-profit duty on codes whose import-duty base FX rate rose under the 1404 budget law, directly changing effective import tariffs and customs revenue on the affected product lines.
caveats: Only 240 of thousands of HS codes are touched, so aggregate WDI tariff-rate indices (simple/weighted mean across all products) will show only a small, diluted shift; the same-year budget law's FX-rate change for duty calculation is itself a bigger driver of import costs than this circular. WDI/Iran series also typically lag official reporting by 1-2+ years.

law_title: بخشنامه گمرک در خصوص اصلاحات تعرفه ایی سال ۱۴۰۴ (duplicate filing, Customs circular on 1404 tariff schedule amendments)
justification: Identical instrument to law_id 32e3705e4dc9 (duplicate filing across two court-decision archives) — same 240-tariff-line amendment tied to the FX rate change in the 1404 budget law.
caveats: Same as the duplicate record: narrow scope relative to full tariff schedule, and the concurrent budget-law FX change is a larger confound. Treat as one policy event, not two independent shocks.

law_title: رأی ۹۱۵-۹۱۶ هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری - ابطال بند بخشنامه گمرک درباره ممنوعیت واردات برنج پس از ۳۱/۴/۱۳۹۵ (Court ruling voiding customs circular excluding rice from Article-11 import relief)
justification: Struck down (retroactive to the 2015 circular's issuance) a customs rule that had barred already-ordered/paid rice shipments from post-deadline clearance exceptions, restoring importers' ability to clear pending rice cargo under Article 11 relief.
caveats: This is a narrow individual-dispute ruling protecting vested rights of importers who had already completed paperwork before a 2016 deadline, not a general liberalization of rice trade policy; national rice import/production/price series are dominated by domestic harvest weather, FX rate, and the government's seasonal import-ban calendar rather than this one ruling.

law_title: رأی ۱۵۲ هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری - ابطال بخشنامه ارزش‌گذاری گمرک ایران مبتنی بر متال بولتن (Court ruling voiding customs bulletin valuing metals/goods by Metal Bulletin index)
justification: Voided (from date of issuance) Iran Customs' practice of using the London-based Metal Bulletin and similar foreign price indices to set customs value for metals, cars, phones and other exchange-listed goods, because it bypassed the statutory valuation hierarchy (invoice price, then comparable transactions, then price lists) in the Customs Affairs Law — this changes how declared import values (and hence duties collected) are computed for a wide range of commodity imports.
caveats: Effect is on valuation methodology/procedure, not tariff rates themselves, and customs likely reverted to a compliant version of the same market-index approach under the amended 2011 Customs Law's own valuation methods soon after; aggregate customs-duty and metals-import series are driven far more by world commodity prices, sanctions and the rial exchange rate than by this procedural ruling.

law_title: رأی ۵۳۵-۵۳۶ هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری - ابطال مالیات سود تفاوت تسعیر ارز حاصل صادرات (Court ruling voiding tax on FX-translation profit from export proceeds)
justification: Struck down the tax administration's 2014 circular that had taxed exporters' FX-translation gains on export earnings, confirming the statutory exemption (Direct Taxes Act Art. 141, Removing Obstacles to Competitive Production Act Art. 36, Permanent Development Plans Act Art. 45) that keeps export currency-translation profit tax-free — this lowers the effective tax burden on exporters, particularly during periods of rial depreciation when translation gains are large.
caveats: Sanctions, the informal/parallel FX market premium, and the rial's overall depreciation trajectory are far larger drivers of both the tax base itself and of aggregate non-oil export volumes than this single ruling on how that base is taxed; the ruling reaffirms existing law rather than creating a new incentive.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی تسویه و تهاتر بدهی‌ها و مطالبات دولت از طریق انتشار اسناد (اوراق) تسویه خزانه (Implementing bylaw for treasury settlement-bond issuance to offset government debts/receivables, FY1404 budget)
justification: Authorizes the Treasury to issue up to 500 trillion rials of non-interest-bearing 'treasury settlement bonds' (type-2) to net out the government's overdue debts to banks, contractors, municipalities, pension funds and NIOC against those entities' debts/claims to the state — a direct, quantified debt-management/quasi-monetary instrument booked as government debt to the central bank.
caveats: This is debt netting/rescheduling of existing obligations rather than new net borrowing, so its effect on headline net-government-debt or GDP-ratio series may be smaller or differently timed than the face value suggests; Iran's WDI/WEO government-finance series are also frequently missing or lagged for recent fiscal years, limiting observability of this specific 1404 instrument in the data we hold.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده ۲۹ قانون اصلاح موادی از قانون برنامه چهارم توسعه و اجرای سیاستهای کلی اصل ۴۴ قانون اساسی (Implementing bylaw for use of Article-44 privatization sale proceeds)
justification: Sets out how revenue from state-enterprise divestiture is spent (poverty relief, cooperative subsidies, regional infrastructure, SOE debt/restructuring, incomplete-project completion) under the Article 44 privatization program, whose associated wave of state-company listings and share offerings materially expanded Tehran Stock Exchange listed-company market capitalization through the 2006-2013 period.
caveats: This particular bylaw governs downstream spending of proceeds, not the listing/divestiture mechanism itself, so its own causal weight on market cap is indirect; TSE market cap over this period was also driven heavily by inflation-driven equity flight, subsidized bank credit, and broader macro cycles, so attribution to this specific bylaw is weak.

law_title: آیین‌نامه نحوه واگذاری سهام ترجیحی (Bylaw on transferring 'preferred' discounted shares of privatized firms to workers/managers)
justification: Governs the mechanics (up to 5% of a privatized company's shares, at discounted/installment terms) by which workers and managers acquire equity when a state firm is divested — a structural feature of the Article 44 privatization wave that expanded the pool of listed shares and shareholders on the Tehran Stock Exchange in the late 2000s.
caveats: Preferred-share allocation is a small slice (up to 5%) of any given divestiture and primarily redistributes ownership rather than adding new capital; TSE market-cap growth in this era was dominated by the broader privatization/IPO wave and macro liquidity conditions, not this specific worker-share mechanism.

law_title: قانون نحوه واگذاری سهام دولتی و متعلق به دولت به ایثارگران و کارگران (Law on transferring state-owned shares to war veterans and workers)
justification: Establishes the foundational legal framework (concessional pricing, installment terms, veteran/worker cooperatives) requiring every state divestiture from 1994 onward to reserve a share for veterans and workers, underpinning decades of subsequent privatization-linked share issuance eventually reflected in Tehran Stock Exchange listed-company market capitalization.
caveats: Effect is structural and very long-lag (the law shapes decades of subsequent divestitures rather than moving the market in any one year); actual privatization volume was low through the 1990s and only accelerated after the 2006 Article 44 policy shift, so most of the observable market-cap effect is attributable to later instruments, not this 1994 law directly.

law_title: قانون اجازه مبادله قرارداد نفت با شرکت کنتینانتال (Law authorizing an exploration/production-sharing oil contract between NIOC and U.S.-based Continental Oil Company)
justification: Authorized a NIOC risk-service contract giving Continental Oil exploration and development rights in a ~12,860 km2 offshore block near Bandar Abbas, part of Iran's post-1957 strategy of diversifying oil development beyond the original Consortium to expand national production and revenue.
caveats: A single exploration concession in one block is a marginal addition to Iran's total oil output, which through this era was overwhelmingly driven by the Consortium's giant fields; exploration success/production start dates for this specific contract are uncertain from the text and may not have materially moved national output even if commercially successful.

law_title: قانون مربوط به موافقتنامه تفحص و اکتشاف و استخراج و بهره‌برداری نفت بین شرکت نفت ایران و شرکت ایتالیایی آجیپ مینراریا (Law authorizing the SIRIP exploration/production joint-venture agreement between NIOC and Italy's AGIP Mineraria)
justification: Created the landmark SIRIP 50/50 joint-venture (with Iran additionally receiving half of AGIP's profit share, i.e. a ~75% effective take), the first agreement to break the Anglo-Iranian/Consortium's exclusivity over Iranian oil development — a deliberate policy to expand exploration and boost national oil revenue, falling within the exact window covered by our Pahlavi-era oil revenue charts.
caveats: SIRIP's own fields (offshore Persian Gulf, Zagros, Gulf of Oman) were a small fraction of total Iranian output versus the Consortium's giant fields, so the revenue/production charts for 1955-1963 reflect mostly Consortium activity; any SIRIP contribution appears with a multi-year exploration-to-production lag and is not separately broken out in the charts we hold.

law_title: آئين‌نامه شماره ۳۴ درخصوص شرايط عمومي بيمه‌نامه اعتبار صادرات كالا و حداقل نرخ حق بيمه آن
justification: Sets standardized terms and a minimum premium floor for export credit insurance policies, a tool meant to de-risk export sales on credit and encourage exporters to sell to less-established foreign buyers.
caveats: Iran's aggregate merchandise export value is overwhelmingly driven by oil price, sanctions regimes, and exchange-rate policy; a niche insurance-product bylaw is a marginal, second-order factor at best and cannot be isolated in the aggregate series.

law_title: كاهش تعداد طبقات تعرفه‌اي كليه رديف‌هاي تعرفه جداول پيوست آيين‌نامه اجرايي قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات از ۱۴ طبقه به ۱۰ طبقه
justification: Cabinet decree directly restructures the customs tariff-rate bracket schedule (consolidating 14 duty bands into 10, e.g. merging the 6/8 and 12/15 bands), mechanically resetting the applied duty rate on many tariff lines simultaneously.
caveats: Net direction on the mean applied rate is ambiguous since bands were both merged upward and downward across different product lines; WDI's tariff-rate series may also lag or be estimated rather than reflecting the exact bracket change instantly.

law_title: آيين‌نامه معاملات در شركت بورس اوراق بهادار تهران و لواحق آن
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as market participants adapt to trading rules
justification: Establishes the operational trading-procedure bylaw (order types, settlement, member obligations) for the Tehran Stock Exchange, part of the regulatory infrastructure underpinning market liquidity and turnover.
caveats: TSE trading volume and market cap are driven far more by macro liquidity, inflation-hedging demand, privatization (Article 44) share offerings, and sanctions-driven capital flight/return than by trading-procedure bylaws; this is background market infrastructure rather than a demand shock.

law_title: قانون اداره امور شركتهاي بيمه
justification: Codifies the post-1979-nationalization governance structure of Iran's four state insurance companies (Iran, Asia, Alborz, Dana — merging several smaller nationalized insurers into Dana), setting the state-monopoly ownership and board-appointment framework that defined the sector until later private-insurer licensing reforms.
caveats: This is a structural/ownership law, not a market-growth instrument; premium volume, claims, and the shift toward private insurers seen in later charts are driven by subsequent liberalization laws, economic growth, and inflation (nominal premium growth), not by this 1988 administrative law itself.

law_title: ابقاء معافيتهاي قانوني حقوق گمركي و سود بازرگاني دارندگان پروانه مونتاژ ... تا پايان مدت اعتبار تصويبنامه هاي ياد شده
lag_description: gradual, several years (assembly-plant investment lead time)
justification: Extends existing customs-duty and commercial-profit-tax exemptions previously granted to holders of assembly (montage) licenses under Article 20(34) of the Customs Tariff Law, part of the early-1960s import-substitution policy environment that protected and subsidized domestic vehicle/appliance assembly plants (e.g. Iran National, founded 1962, ahead of Paykan production from 1967).
caveats: This decree only continues pre-existing exemptions rather than creating new incentives, and does not name the automotive sector specifically; national vehicle production from 1970 onward reflects many other factors (Paykan licensing deal, oil-boom domestic demand, later local-content mandates) that dominate over this narrow tariff-continuation decree.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي شناسه هاي كالا و رهگيري موضوع ماده ۱۳ قانون مبارزه با قاچاق كالا و ارز
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as tracking infrastructure rolls out
justification: Implementing bylaw creates a mandatory goods-identifier and supply-chain-tracking system under Article 13 of the Anti-Smuggling of Goods and Currency Law, intended to formalize goods flows and curb smuggling, which could shift some previously unrecorded trade into official statistics.
caveats: This is a domestic anti-smuggling enforcement/tracking mechanism, not a trade-policy instrument; officially recorded merchandise import value in this period is overwhelmingly driven by the 2018 sanctions snapback and exchange-rate collapse, which vastly outweigh any statistical formalization effect from goods-tracking codes.

law_title: رأي شماره ۶۴ هيئت عمومي ديوان عدالت اداري در ابطال بند ۶ مصوبه شوراي اقتصاد در خصوص تقليل نرخ خريد روغن نباتي
justification: Court ruling voids a retroactive cut (from 2,854 to 2,780 rials/kg) in the state's guaranteed factory-gate purchase price for vegetable oil, illustrating the guaranteed-purchase/price-control regime then governing edible-oil markets, and restoring the higher purchase price for the disputed period.
caveats: This is a narrow administrative/legal dispute over one company's retroactive pricing, not a market-wide price or output decision; Iran's vegetable-oil trade and consumption series are dominated by import dependence (Iran produces little edible oilseed), general subsidy policy, and currency/import-cost shocks, not by this single court ruling.

law_title: قانون چگونگی محاسبه و وصول حقوق گمرکی، سود بازرگانی و مالیات انواع خودرو و ماشین‌آلات راهسازی وارداتی و ساخت داخل و قطعات آنها (Law on Calculating/Collecting Customs Duties, Commercial Profit Tax and Tax on Imported/Domestic Vehicles and Road-Building Machinery and Their Parts)
lag_description: gradual over 1-5 years, cumulative through the 1990s-2000s
justification: The law sets customs duty at 25% of CIF value for fully imported cars vs. only 10% for imported CKD/knocked-down parts used in domestic assembly (and even lower on trucks/buses), a textbook tariff-escalation instrument that makes imported finished vehicles far more expensive than domestically assembled ones, directly protecting and incentivizing Iran Khodro/SAIPA-style domestic vehicle assembly.
caveats: Domestic auto production growth was also driven by import bans/currency-allocation restrictions imposed separately, rising domestic demand, joint-venture technology transfer deals, and (after 2012) sanctions-driven import substitution; the chart's data mostly ends in the mid-2000s with only milestone events after, so the tariff effect is one of several overlapping protectionist policies rather than the sole cause.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ورود موقت مواد اولیه موضوع ماده 12 قانون صادرات و واردات (Implementing Bylaw for Temporary Duty-Free Import of Raw Materials under Article 12 of the Export/Import Law)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as firms adopt the bonded-processing scheme
justification: The bylaw lets licensed manufacturing firms import raw materials duty-free on the condition the finished goods are re-exported (a bonded/inward-processing regime), lowering input costs for export-oriented manufacturers and thereby incentivizing manufactured-goods exports over raw commodity exports.
caveats: Manufactured export share is driven far more by exchange-rate policy, sanctions access to markets, and overall industrial competitiveness than by this single customs facilitation mechanism; take-up and enforcement of such schemes in Iran has historically been uneven.

law_title: آیین‌نامه بند (ه) ماده (14) قانون برنامه سوم توسعه موضوع برقراری نظام اقساطی فروش سهام (Bylaw Establishing the Installment Share-Sale System under the Third Development Plan Law)
lag_description: gradual over several years as privatized shares are listed and traded
justification: The bylaw operationalizes installment (deferred-payment) sale of state-owned company shares by the Privatization Organization, with shares held as collateral until paid off; by lowering the up-front cash barrier to buying privatized state enterprises, it plausibly widened the pool of buyers and fed newly privatized firms onto the Tehran Stock Exchange, supporting growth in listed companies and market capitalization.
caveats: Tehran Stock Exchange market cap and turnover in the 2000s-2010s were driven far more by macro liquidity (money supply growth), inflation-driven equity demand, and the broader Article 44 privatization wave than by this specific installment-payment mechanism, which is just one financing tool within it.

law_title: آئین‌نامه نحوه استفاده از زمین و منابع ملی در مناطق آزاد تجاری-صنعتی جمهوری اسلامی ایران (Bylaw on Use of Land and National Resources in Iran's Free Trade-Industrial Zones)
lag_description: gradual, multi-year effect as investors secure land/lease rights in FTZs
justification: This foundational bylaw governs how state-owned land inside Iran's free trade-industrial zones (Kish, Qeshm, Chabahar, Aras, etc.) is sold or leased to investors, giving Free Zone Organizations clear authority to allocate land and issue bankable lease/sale contracts - a basic precondition for domestic and foreign investors to build facilities in these zones.
caveats: Iran's FDI inflows are overwhelmingly shaped by sanctions regimes, oil-sector investment terms (buy-back contracts), and currency/repatriation risk rather than by FTZ land-allocation procedures; any effect of this bylaw is a small, necessary-but-not-sufficient facilitating condition rather than a primary driver.

law_title: قانون اختیارات بانک اعتبارات صنعتی (Law on the Powers of the Industrial Credit Bank)
lag_description: same fiscal year to a few years, as loans are drawn down
justification: The law authorizes the state-affiliated Industrial Credit Bank to directly borrow from foreign banks and institutions, with government guarantees capped at three times the bank's paid-in capital, in order to fund industrial lending - a direct, if narrow, addition to Iran's publicly-guaranteed external debt stock in the early 1970s.
caveats: Iran's external debt stock in this era was dominated by much larger sovereign and oil-revenue-backed borrowing for the Fourth/Fifth development plans; this single bank's foreign-borrowing authorization is a minor contributor whose guarantee ceiling limits its scale.

law_title: لایحه قانونی راجع به رفع مشکلات مربوط به تصفیه شرکتهای سهامی زراعی و تعاونیهای تولید روستائی (Bill on Resolving Problems in Liquidating Agricultural Joint-Stock Companies and Rural Production Cooperatives)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as dissolved farms are subdivided and reallocated
justification: This early revolutionary-era law dismantles the Pahlavi-era large-scale mechanized 'agricultural joint-stock companies' (used mainly for wheat, cotton, and sugarcane in regions like Khuzestan and Fars), transferring land, buildings, and forgiven debt back to individual farmers/shareholders - a land-fragmentation reform that could depress short-run mechanized output even as it changed ownership structure.
caveats: Agricultural output in 1979-1982 was overwhelmingly shaped by the revolution's general economic disruption, capital flight, loss of technical management, and the outbreak of the Iran-Iraq War in 1980, all of which dwarf the effect of this one institutional liquidation law; the sign and magnitude of any effect is genuinely uncertain.

law_title: قانون حفاظت و بهره‌برداری از جنگلها و مراتع (Law on Protection and Exploitation of Forests and Rangelands)
justification: This is Iran's foundational forest/rangeland law, building on the 1963 nationalization of forests: it establishes the licensing regime for logging, grazing permits, and protected zones, giving the state the core legal tools to regulate (and in principle limit) forest and rangeland exploitation nationwide.
caveats: Measured forest area over the following decades reflects population growth, agricultural land expansion, fuelwood demand, drought, and war-related deforestation at least as much as enforcement of this law; implementation capacity has varied greatly by era, so the law's net effect on the forest-area trend is hard to sign with confidence.

law_title: آیین نامه اجرایی ماده (33) اصلاحی قانون حفاظت و بهره‌برداری از جنگل‌ها و مراتع کشور (Implementing Bylaw for Amended Article 33 of the Forest and Rangeland Protection and Exploitation Law)
justification: This bylaw sets up provincial oversight committees and special judicial branches to police leases of national forest/rangeland land granted for agricultural and other projects, empowering the state to rescind leases and reclaim land where the approved project was not properly executed or land use was illegally converted - an enforcement mechanism aimed at curbing unauthorized deforestation/land-use change on leased state land.
caveats: This is a narrow procedural/enforcement bylaw layered onto a much older law; national forest-area trends are driven far more by drought, wildfires, agricultural encroachment, and broader economic pressure than by this specific oversight-committee mechanism, and enforcement consistency across provinces is uncertain.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی اجزای (1) و (2) بند (ع) تبصره (2) قانون بودجه 1404 موضوع تسویه و تهاتر بدهی‌ها و مطالبات دولت از طریق انتشار اسناد (اوراق) تسویه خزانه (Implementing Bylaw for Government Debt/Claims Netting via Issuance of Treasury Settlement Securities, under the FY1404 Budget Law)
justification: The bylaw operationalizes issuance of 'treasury settlement bonds' to net out the government's own unpaid arrears to contractors/banks against its own claims, converting off-book payment arrears into recognized tradable government securities - which mechanically shows up as an increase in measured government debt stock even though it does not represent new net spending.
caveats: This is a book-keeping/settlement instrument rather than a fiscal-expansion measure; any observed increase in net government debt in FY1404 also reflects the underlying budget deficit, currency devaluation effects on rial-denominated debt, and sanctions-constrained revenue, not this bylaw alone. Most of our budget/debt series do not yet have 2025 data, so this link is largely prospective.

law_title: اتخاذ تصمیماتی در خصوص تصویب‌نامه موضوع جداول پیوست آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (تعرفه ۱۳۸۷)
justification: Cabinet decree revises roughly 300 individual HS tariff-code duty rates (raising duties on many finished consumer/pharma/textile goods to protect domestic producers, cutting duties on raw materials/inputs) as part of the annual update to the executive bylaw of the Export & Import Regulations Law. This is one of the concrete instruments generating Iran's applied tariff schedule.
caveats: This is one incremental annual tariff-table update among many issued almost every year; changes are mixed direction (some lines up, some down) so aggregate simple/weighted-mean tariff impact is small and hard to isolate from the routine year-to-year tariff revision process. WDI's Iran tariff series may not even reflect fine-grained annual code-level revisions given data-reporting gaps.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی انتشار چهارصد میلیارد ریال اوراق مشارکت موضوع بند «د» تبصره (۴۰) قانون بودجه سال ۱۳۸۰ کل کشور
lag_description: 1-3 year lag (bond proceeds funded multi-year construction of processing plants and irrigation infrastructure)
justification: Authorizes the Central Bank/Ministry of Agriculture Jihad to issue 400 billion rials in participation bonds to finance expansion of sugarcane cultivation, processing facilities (sugar, molasses/alcohol, animal feed byproducts) and completion of unfinished projects in Khuzestan's sugarcane development scheme, plus price-differential subsidies to beet/cane sugar producers.
caveats: Sugarcane output in Khuzestan is also heavily driven by water availability/drought, international sugar prices, and the broader state sugarcane development program that spanned decades; this single bond issuance is only one financing tranche within that larger program, so attributing a discrete production bump to it specifically is speculative.

law_title: قانون تسهیلات مالی برای کارآیی بیشتر واحدهای صنعتی مشمول قانون حفاظت و توسعه صنایع ایران
justification: Post-revolution law directing banks to extend working-capital credit, raw-material/spare-parts import financing, and debt-rollover relief to industrial units placed under state-appointed management via the Industry Protection & Development Law, explicitly aimed at keeping nationalized/troubled industrial firms operating rather than shutting down.
caveats: 1980 sits at the start of the Iran-Iraq War and amid post-revolution capital flight and mass nationalization; manufacturing value-added trends in this era are dominated by the war, sanctions, and the broader nationalization wave rather than by this specific credit-facilitation law, which is a mitigating countermeasure rather than the primary driver.

law_title: لایحه قانونی راجع به انتقال سهام و دارائی و تعهدات شرکت سهامی تهیه و توزیع شیر ایران به شرکت سهامی صنایع شیر ایران
justification: Merges the state-owned regional dairy distribution companies (previously held by the Industrial & Mining Development Bank and Agricultural Development Bank) into a single nationalized entity, Iran Dairy Industries Co., under the Ministry of Agriculture — a corporate consolidation/nationalization of the dairy processing sector immediately after the revolution.
caveats: This is a corporate ownership/governance consolidation, not a production or pricing instrument; raw milk output is driven far more by herd size, feed and drought conditions than by which state company owns the processing/distribution arm, so any link to production levels is weak and largely coincidental with the broader post-revolution nationalization wave.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون جبران خسارات و پیشگیری عوارض ناشی از خشکسالی (شماره ۱۹۳۴۵/ت۲۴۸۸۰هـ)
justification: Implements a large drought-relief package (roughly 4,000+ billion rials): recapitalizes Agricultural Bank and the Crop & Livestock Insurance Fund, funds irrigation/well/qanat rehabilitation, provides working-capital loans and bank debt-rollovers to drought-hit farmers, ranchers and fish-farmers, and distributes cash grants by province — a direct farm-support/damage-mitigation instrument during Iran's severe 1999-2001 drought.
caveats: This bylaw is a mitigation/compensation response to an already-occurring drought, not an independent driver of production; actual output in these years is dominated by the severity of the drought itself and by rainfall/precipitation, which this instrument only partially offsets. Multiple similar annual drought-relief bylaws were issued in this period, making it hard to isolate this one's effect.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Executive Bylaw of the Export-Import Regulations Law)
lag_description: ongoing throughout the bylaw's decades-long applicability (amended repeatedly through the 1990s)
justification: This is the operational rulebook for Iran's post-revolution import/export licensing regime: it sets up the interministerial commission that approves/restricts import and export licenses, mandates exporters surrender foreign exchange (پیمان ارزی) to banks at bank-set rates, runs an export-goods pricing commission, and controls which goods can be imported against export-earned forex. As the governing machinery for most non-oil trade flows for years, it is background/contextual to virtually all Iran trade-volume and tariff series rather than a single discrete shock.
caveats: Effects are inseparable from the dominant drivers of the period: the Iran-Iraq War (1980-88) rationing, oil-price swings, and the much larger currency and subsidy reforms of the 1990s-2000s. The bylaw itself was amended over a dozen times, so no single before/after comparison is clean.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Executive Bylaw of the Export-Import Regulations Law)
justification: Articles 18-28 require exporters to surrender foreign-exchange earnings to the banking system at official/bank-set conversion terms (with partial discounts for prompt surrender). Mandating below-market official conversion for legitimate export proceeds is a classic driver of a parallel/black-market premium.
caveats: The official-vs-parallel exchange rate gap in Iran is overwhelmingly driven by later, larger forces: multiple currency-rationing regimes, international sanctions, and macro instability from the 1990s onward. This 1987 bylaw is one of many contributing regulatory layers, not the primary cause of the gap seen in later decades.

law_title: قانون خرید اراضی کشاورزی برای تأمین نیازمندی‌های صنعتی و معدنی (Law on Purchase of Agricultural Land for Industrial/Mining Needs)
