justification: The law lets industrial and mining units (state or private) compulsorily lease or purchase farmland (with administrative valuation if the owner refuses) to site plants and mines, removing a land-acquisition bottleneck for industrial/mining expansion during 1969-1980, the exact window covered by several of our mining-production series.
caveats: This is a case-by-case administrative mechanism, not a national program; mining and industrial output growth in the 1970s was overwhelmingly driven by the oil boom, Fifth Plan investment, and global commodity prices. The law's independent contribution to any national aggregate is essentially undetectable and this link is speculative.

law_title: قانون حمایت از تولید دام داخلی (Law Supporting Domestic Livestock Production)
justification: The law's single article directly instructs the government to announce and guarantee a minimum annual purchase price for domestically produced livestock/meat every year (Note 1), an explicit price-support instrument administered through the state meat organization.
caveats: Guaranteed-price levels were also shaped by inflation, subsidized-feed/forex availability, and later by broader agricultural subsidy reform (targeted subsidies law 2010+); the guaranteed price itself is a policy input, not a market-clearing price, so it may diverge from actual transaction prices.

law_title: قانون حمایت از تولید دام داخلی (Law Supporting Domestic Livestock Production)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag, with an explicit multi-year phase-out target for meat imports
justification: The law cuts meat-import volume by 10% of the 1990 quota, redirects the freed foreign exchange to domestic fodder/veterinary purchases and to buying domestic livestock, and sets a goal of ending meat imports entirely by the end of the First Development Plan (~1994) -- a direct import-substitution and domestic-production-support mechanism.
caveats: Iran's livestock sector and meat trade are also heavily affected by recurrent drought, animal disease outbreaks, feed-import costs tied to the exchange rate, and (after 2010) subsidy-reform-driven feed price shocks -- any of which could produce similar-looking movements in production or import volumes independent of this law.

law_title: قانون تسریع در برقی کردن موتور چاه‌های کشاورزی (Law Accelerating Electrification of Agricultural Well Pumps)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag (6-year loan repayment window specified in the law) through the 2000s
justification: The law channels a National Iranian Oil Company allocation through the Agricultural Bank as zero-interest loans to farmers who convert diesel-powered well pumps to electric ones, with the explicit stated purpose 'to reduce consumption of petroleum products' -- a direct fuel-substitution subsidy mechanism.
caveats: Gas-oil/diesel consumption is dominated by the transport sector and total agricultural demand growth, so the agricultural well-electrification effect is a small slice of the national total; rural electrification also proceeded independently under general Ministry of Energy grid-expansion programs.

law_title: قانون نوسازی و عمران شهری (Urban Renewal and Development Law)
justification: The law creates a nationwide annual municipal property levy (originally 5 per-thousand, later 1%) earmarked for urban renewal, redevelopment financing and municipal capacity-building across Iranian cities -- a foundational instrument of Iran's post-1968 urban land-value and municipal-finance framework.
caveats: Housing-stock and urban-development trends over this period were dominated by population growth, oil-boom urban migration, the 1979 revolution/war disruption, and later mass housing programs (e.g., Mehr Housing); we hold only sparse, census-year housing data, so any link to this specific municipal-finance law is background context at best, not a measurable driver.

law_title: قانون راجع به تأسیس شرکت شهرک‌های صنعتی ایران (Law Establishing the Iran Industrial Estates Company)
justification: The law creates the state company (and its provincial subsidiaries) that has since built the several hundred industrial estates/zones across Iran, providing subsidized serviced land and infrastructure to industrial investors -- the principal vehicle for site-level industrial-policy implementation from 1984 onward.
caveats: Industrial and manufacturing value-added growth is driven far more by oil-revenue cycles, sanctions, exchange-rate policy, and five-year development plans than by estate-level land provision alone; the estates are an enabling channel rather than the primary driver of sector output.

law_title: لایحه قانونی اداره و تملک سهام شرکت‌ها و مؤسسات پیمانکاری و مهندسی مشاور (Decree on State Administration/Takeover of Shares of Contracting and Consulting-Engineering Firms)
lag_description: immediate for affected firms, background thereafter
justification: This revolutionary-era decree nationalizes (fully or 51%) contracting and consulting-engineering firms whose owners were judged to have profited improperly under the former regime, folding them into state ownership -- part of the broader 1979-1981 nationalization wave affecting the construction/engineering sector.
caveats: It targets a narrow, named subset of firms rather than the construction sector as a whole; the Iran-Iraq War (1980-88), the far larger contemporaneous nationalizations (banking, industry), and general revolutionary capital flight dominate any construction/investment value-added movement in this period, making an isolated effect from this decree essentially unobservable in our aggregate charts.

law_title: قانون تشکیل شورای‌عالی اشتغال (Law Establishing the Supreme Employment Council)
lag_description: gradual, multi-year (coordinating body, not a direct job-creation program)
justification: Creates a cabinet-level council chaired by the President, with provincial coordination committees, tasked with balancing labor supply/demand, setting sectoral employment quotas, and monitoring employment-plan targets -- the standing institutional mechanism through which Iran's national employment policy has since been coordinated.
caveats: The council is a coordinating and monitoring body, not itself a job-creation instrument; Iran's unemployment and labor-force trends are driven far more by demographic youth bulges, sanctions-driven recessions, exchange-rate crises, and specific job-creation/credit programs than by this council's coordination function.

law_title: قانون بیمه‌های اجتماعی کارگران ساختمانی (Social Insurance Law for Construction Workers)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as enrollment ramped up
justification: The law makes building-permit issuance conditional on payment of a construction-worker social-insurance premium (funded by a percentage of permit fees, split between worker/employer/state shares) and mandates Social Security Organization enrollment for construction workers -- a direct, financed expansion of mandatory social-insurance coverage for a large previously under-covered informal labor segment.
caveats: Aggregate social-protection coverage and informal/vulnerable-employment shares are shaped by many concurrent programs (targeted subsidies cash transfers, general Social Security expansions, health-insurance reforms) and by construction-sector boom/bust cycles tied to housing demand and sanctions; isolating this law's specific contribution is not possible from national aggregates alone.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی نحوه تأسیس و عملیات شعب بانک‌های خارجی در ایران (Executive Bylaw on the Establishment and Operation of Foreign Bank Branches in Iran)
lag_description: intended immediate, but effectively blocked for most of the period since
justification: The bylaw creates a formal legal pathway (minimum EUR 5 million capital, profitability track record, central-bank supervision) for foreign banks to open branches in Iran, which in principle should support foreign direct investment inflows and expand banking-sector access.
caveats: This is a low-confidence link primarily because the intended effect was almost entirely nullified by international sanctions: very few foreign banks from major economies ever opened Iranian branches under this regime, and FDI/banking-access trends after 2009 are dominated by sanctions escalation/relief cycles (2012 sanctions, 2015 JCPOA, 2018 US withdrawal) rather than this bylaw's licensing framework.

law_title: قانون کمک به توسعه صدور بعضی از اقلام کالاهای صادراتی (Law on Assistance to Development of Exports of Certain Export Goods)
lag_description: same to 1-2 year lag (bonus paid on export completion, could shift extraction/export timing within a season)
justification: Article 1 pays exporters a cash export bonus (jayeze) of up to 20% of FOB value for iron ore, lead ore, manganese ore, chromite, and sinore/salambour, and up to 10% for zinc ore, funded via a dedicated treasury revolving account -- a direct, named financial incentive to raise export volumes of exactly these minerals. Higher export incentives plausibly pulled through higher mine output in the same commodities during the law's active years (mid-1960s-1970s).
caveats: Production of these minerals was also driven by domestic industrial demand (steel, construction), world commodity prices, discovery/investment cycles, and later by the law's own 1967 partial repeal (bond b of Article 1). Correlation between the bonus scheme and any given year's output cannot be cleanly separated from these other drivers, and the underlying charts report gross production, not export volume specifically.

law_title: لایحه قانونی معافیت حقوق گمرکی روغن نباتی (Legal Bill Exempting Vegetable Oil from Customs Duty)
lag_description: same fiscal year (immediate tariff removal on imports)
justification: The single-article bill exempts vegetable oil (tariff line 15.12) from customs duty entirely, with follow-up riders in the same file extending the exemption to specific hydrogenated-oil and soy-oil shipments -- a direct, named reduction in the landed cost of a staple food import.
caveats: The FAOSTAT vegetable oil trade/consumption series is annual and multi-country-sourced; 1979-80 also coincided with the revolution's disruption of trade financing and, from late 1980, the Iran-Iraq war and wartime rationing, any of which could dominate the same-period movement in imports/consumption independent of this tariff change.

law_title: آئین‌نامه اجرائی بند ۱۱ تبصره ۲۹ قانون بودجه ۱۳۶۸ (تنخواه‌گردان صادراتی) (Executive Bylaw for Export Revolving Fund, FY1368 Budget)
lag_description: same to 1 fiscal year (FX advanced against a signed export contract, repaid within a year)
justification: Establishes a foreign-currency revolving fund (تنخواه‌گردان ارزی) that non-oil exporters/producers could draw on solely to import raw materials, parts and packaging needed to produce goods for export, provided the resulting export earned at least 25% more FX than it consumed -- a working-capital instrument aimed at boosting non-oil export volume.
caveats: The scheme was a narrow, single-fiscal-year (1368) mechanism with a modest ceiling (e.g., a $6m cap on one sub-component); Iran's aggregate merchandise export series in this era is overwhelmingly dominated by oil, and non-oil trade in the late 1980s was also shaped by the end of the Iran-Iraq war, the collapsing official exchange rate, and broader post-war reconstruction policy, any of which would swamp this fund's marginal effect.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده (۱۰) قانون هدفمند کردن یارانه‌ها (Executive Bylaw for Article 10 of the Targeted Subsidies Law)
lag_description: gradual, 1-3 year lag as the broader subsidy reform program it belongs to phased in
justification: On reading the actual text, this specific bylaw does not set cash-transfer amounts or energy prices; it creates the central and provincial appeals commissions that adjudicate disputes when a household's subsidy/support payment (under Articles 7-8 of the Targeted Subsidies Law) is clawed back for inaccurate information. It is administrative machinery of the landmark 2010 Targeted Subsidies Reform -- the reform that sharply raised energy prices and introduced universal cash transfers, which is the actual driver of the price and welfare movements below.
caveats: This bylaw itself is a dispute-resolution/enforcement mechanism, not the price- or cash-amount-setting instrument (those are separate implementing regulations under Articles 1, 7 and 8 of the same law, not covered here). The 2010-2012 CPI spike and any social-protection coverage changes were also driven by the concurrent collapse of the rial, intensifying international sanctions, and the core subsidy/price reform itself -- this record should be read as loosely contextual, not as evidence this particular bylaw moved these charts.

law_title: لايحه قانوني ملي كردن جنگلها (Nationalization of Forests Act)
justification: Article 1 declared all forests, rangelands and natural woodlands state property regardless of prior private title, and handed exploitation rights to the state Forestry Organization (later replaced private harvest rights with state-issued logging licenses under Article 7). This is the foundational property-regime change behind Iran's subsequent forest-management/depletion statistics.
caveats: Deforestation and forest-rent trends are driven far more by population growth, fuelwood and agricultural encroachment, drought, and post-1979 weakening of enforcement than by this single property-law change; WDI series generally begin well after 1963 so no clean before/after comparison is possible, and nationalization could in principle either protect (centralized management) or accelerate (loss of local stewardship incentive) forest loss.

law_title: پيشنهاد مقررات مربوط به تعيين تكليف نسقه‌اي زراعي متروكه و املاك مسلوب‌المنفعه و باير (Disposition of abandoned tenant plots/idle estates seized under land reform)
justification: A narrow implementing regulation disposing of leftover abandoned farm plots and idle/unusable estates that the state had acquired under the 1962 Land Reform Law but never redistributed, assigning them to agricultural joint-stock companies, state farms, or rural cooperatives — a cleanup detail within the broader White Revolution land-tenure overhaul.
caveats: This covers only leftover, unallocated parcels, not the main body of land reform; Iran's agricultural land area and output over this period were driven overwhelmingly by irrigation investment, weather/drought, and oil-revenue-financed input and price subsidies rather than this specific settlement rule.

law_title: قانون تعيين سقف انتشار اوراق مشاركت توسط بانك مركزي (Law setting the ceiling for CBI participation-bond issuance)
justification: Authorized the Central Bank to issue up to 15,000 billion rials of 'musharakah' participation bonds by year-end 1384, with the coupon rate pegged to bank deposit rates and proceeds legally restricted to productive investment via Islamic contracts — a liquidity-mobilization/short-term sterilization tool.
caveats: 15,000 billion rials was a small fraction of Iran's total M2 by 2005; monetary base and broad money growth in this period were dominated by oil-revenue inflows and government deficit financing through the banking system, not this single bond ceiling.

law_title: آيين‌نامه اجرايي انتشار پانصد ميليارد ريال اوراق مشاركت (تبصره 40 بودجه 1380) (Bylaw for 500bn-rial bond issue, FY1380 budget)
justification: Authorized the Ministry of Energy to fund completion of water-supply and irrigation/drainage network projects using proceeds from a 500-billion-rial CBI-brokered participation-bond issue under FY1380 budget note 40(b), with bondholders given priority water allocation once projects were operational.
caveats: One modest single-year funding tranche within decades of much larger irrigation and dam-building investment; national irrigated-land share also reflects unrelated groundwater extraction trends and multi-year drought cycles, so isolating this bylaw's specific contribution is not possible.

law_title: آيين‌نامه اجرايي ماده 18 قانون رفع موانع توليد رقابت‌پذير (Bylaw implementing Article 18 — bank loan securitization)
justification: Created the legal machinery for banks to sell performing loan claims (up to 100,000 billion rials) to a special-purpose vehicle that issues tradable asset-backed securities, freeing bank balance-sheet capacity to originate new credit — an instrument aimed directly at expanding financing for production.
caveats: Actual uptake of loan securitization in Iran's thin bond market was limited relative to total bank credit; aggregate private-sector credit growth in this era was driven far more by inflation, sanctions-era dollarization/import financing needs, and government borrowing crowding out bank lending than by this single mechanism.

law_title: آيين‌نامه اجرايي ماده 46 قانون رفع موانع توليد رقابت‌پذير (Bylaw implementing Article 46 — settling banks' pre-2013 FX obligations at the official reference rate)
justification: Let the Central Bank settle commercial banks' backlog of 'definite' pre-2013 foreign-currency import/service obligations at the official reference exchange rate, funded from CBI's foreign-asset revaluation surplus — an operational cleanup mechanism inside Iran's existing post-sanctions multi-tier exchange-rate system rather than a rate-setting instrument.
caveats: Does not itself move the exchange rate; it presupposes and administers the official-vs-market rate gap created by sanctions-era currency controls, so any correlation with exchange-rate series reflects that pre-existing multi-tier regime rather than this bylaw's own causal effect. Actual gap dynamics are dominated by sanctions, oil revenue, and inflation.

law_title: آيين‌نامه ايجاد صندوق‌هاي سرمايه‌گذاري املاك و مستغلات (Bylaw creating state real-estate investment funds)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag (5-year divestment window specified in the bylaw)
justification: Establishes a Bank Maskan-sponsored REIT-like vehicle to receive up to 1,000 trillion rials of government real-estate assets and list/sell investment units on the stock exchange within five years — a state-asset-monetization and capital-market-deepening instrument.
caveats: Enacted April 2025, too recent for any observable effect on capital-market or housing charts yet; Iran's stock-market turnover is typically dominated by inflation-hedging flows and sanctions-related sentiment far more than any single new listed fund.

law_title: اصلاح آيين‌نامه اجرايي قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Amendment to Export/Import Regulations bylaw — border-trade cards/quotas)
justification: Revised eligibility for border-resident 'trade cards' and set/adjusted a roughly $200 million/year import ceiling and per-person export/import allowances for border provinces (excluding Gilan/Mazandaran, including Sistan-Baluchestan, Hormozgan, Bushehr, Khuzestan, Lamerd, Dashtestan) — a legal channel for small-scale formal cross-border trade.
caveats: The scale involved (roughly $200 million/year cap) is negligible next to Iran's tens-of-billions-of-dollars annual trade flows; national imports/exports aggregates are overwhelmingly driven by oil prices, sanctions regimes, and the exchange rate, not this border-trade administrative update, so any apparent co-movement is essentially coincidental.

law_title: اصلاح ماده (۵) آیین‌نامه بند «ه» ماده (۱۴) قانون برنامه سوم توسعه ... موضوع برقراری نظام اقساطی فروش سهام
justification: This 2005 amendment eases collateral-release conditions for buyers under the installment-sale system for state shares, a mechanism the Privatization Organization used to sell divested SOE shares (often via TSE listing) to a broader buyer base. Easier collateral terms plausibly supported continued uptake of the installment-sale privatization channel, one of several routes by which state enterprises were listed and traded, contributing marginally to growth in listed companies and market capitalization over the following years.
caveats: This is a narrow, technical amendment to an already-existing 2001 bylaw (collateral release procedure only), not the founding instrument of the installment-sale system itself. TSE listed-company count and market cap over 2005-2015 were overwhelmingly driven by the broader Article 44 privatization wave, inflation-driven equity demand, oil-revenue cycles, and macro/sanctions conditions -- this specific procedural tweak is at most one minor contributing thread among many and cannot be isolated in the data.

law_title: اصلاح ماده ۱۹ آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون نحوه واگذاری سهام دولتی و متعلق به دولت به ایثارگران و کارگران
justification: This 1997 amendment removes a coordination-approval requirement (deletes 'با هماهنگی ستاد') from the bylaw governing transfer of state-owned shares to veterans and workers, streamlining that share-transfer channel. As one of several SOE divestment/privatization mechanisms feeding shares into public ownership (and often onto the TSE), faster processing could modestly support growth in listed companies and traded equity over time.
caveats: This is a minor procedural streamlining of an existing distributive scheme, not a rate- or volume-setting policy; the veterans/workers transfer channel is a distributive rather than open-market privatization mechanism and its direct effect on TSE-listed company counts is uncertain. Iran's Capital Markets series (listed companies, market cap) is dominated by much larger drivers -- the 2000s Article 44 privatization program, inflation, sanctions cycles, and oil-revenue-driven liquidity -- so any contribution from this specific amendment is speculative and cannot be separated from those larger forces.

law_title: قانون جبران خسارات و پیشگیری عوارض ناشی از خشکسالی (Law on Compensating Damages and Preventing Adverse Effects of Drought)
lag_description: same fiscal year to 2-year lag (loan repayment extensions run through 1380-1381)
justification: The law authorizes roughly 1,500 billion rial in emergency credit through the Agricultural Bank for farmers, ranchers, aquaculturists and nomadic herders hit by the severe 1999-2001 drought, extends loan repayment by two years, waives water-well inspection fees for wells with reduced flow, and earmarks 0.3% of the state budget for drought relief and prevention -- a real fiscal/credit instrument aimed at cushioning farm output and livestock herds during the drought.
caveats: The 1999-2001 drought itself was an exceptionally severe multi-year natural shock and is the dominant driver of any dip in cereal yields, wheat production, or livestock numbers in this period; this law is a relief/credit mechanism that can at most soften the decline, not reverse it, and cannot be distinguished from the underlying weather shock in the data. Concurrent economic factors (oil revenue, exchange-rate policy) also affect agricultural investment.

law_title: قانون حمایت صنعتی و جلوگیری از تعطیل کارخانه‌های کشور (Law on Industrial Protection and Prevention of Factory Closures)
lag_description: gradual/ongoing -- a standing institutional mechanism used case-by-case over six decades
justification: Establishes a permanent 'Board for Industrial Protection' (هیئت حمایت از صنایع) with legal authority to place financially distressed factories (facing bankruptcy, seizure, or owner incapacity) under temporary state-appointed management rather than letting them shut down, prioritizing worker wages and keeping production lines running while creditor claims are resolved -- a direct industrial-policy lever aimed at manufacturing output and employment preservation.
caveats: This is a narrow, case-by-case rescue tool for individual distressed firms, not an economy-wide industrial stimulus; aggregate manufacturing/industry value-added over the 1964-present period is overwhelmingly driven by oil revenue cycles, war (1980s), sanctions, and exchange-rate shocks rather than by this backstop mechanism, so its measurable share of any movement in the value-added series is likely small.

law_title: رأی شماره 2620 هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری -- ابطال بخشنامه بانک مرکزی درباره تأمین ارز اعتبارات اسنادی (Court ruling annulling retroactive application of a CBI circular on FX financing for import letters of credit)
justification: The underlying dispute sits inside Iran's 2018 multi-tier exchange-rate regime (a fixed 42,000-rial official rate for priority imports versus a market-based NIMA/secondary rate) introduced amid the post-JCPOA-withdrawal currency crisis; the court voided retroactive application of a Central Bank circular that would have forced one importer's already-opened letters of credit to be settled at the costlier secondary rate.
caveats: This is a narrow procedural annulment affecting one company's dispute over one circular's retroactivity -- it did not alter the multi-tier exchange-rate system itself, which was set by separate Cabinet/CBI policy decisions driven by the 2018 sanctions-triggered rial collapse, not by this litigation. Any co-movement with the official/parallel FX gap chart reflects that broader crisis, not this ruling as a causal driver.

law_title: اصلاح ماده (۷) آیین‌نامه ضوابط فنی واردات خودرو (Amendment to Article 7 of the Technical Regulations Bylaw for Automobile Imports — ban on importing passenger cars more than one year old, new or used)
lag_description: same to next fiscal year (protective effect on domestic production is roughly immediate; full substitution takes 1-2 years)
justification: The cabinet decree bans registration/import of any passenger car (new or used) more than one year old from date of manufacture, in the customs territory and free zones alike. By closing off a used/near-new import channel, it removes a substitute for domestically assembled vehicles, plausibly shifting demand toward Iran Khodro/SAIPA output tracked in this chart, whose milestone annotations run through 2018.
caveats: Iran's auto production in this era was dominated by far larger forces: the 2015 JCPOA sanctions relief and 2018 US withdrawal/re-imposition of sanctions, rial depreciation, parts-import bottlenecks, and domestic price controls on automakers. A single technical-import ban is a minor supply-side lever compared to these; the chart's own production series stops at 2005 with only sparse milestone events after, limiting direct verification.

law_title: بخشنامه وزارت نیرو در خصوص تعرفه‌های برق و شرایط عمومی آنها (Ministry of Energy circular setting electricity tariffs and general conditions, effective Khordad 1404)
justification: This is the government's annual instrument fixing block-rate electricity tariffs (steeply progressive above a consumption 'pattern'/الگوی مصرف threshold, with multipliers up to 5x base cost) for households, agriculture, and industry. Steeper marginal pricing on above-quota use is a textbook demand-management lever that should modestly restrain metered electricity consumption at the margin.
caveats: Aggregate per-capita electricity consumption is driven far more by GDP growth, summer cooling-degree days, population and urbanization, and subsidized-tariff political constraints (most residential tariffs remain far below cost, per the circular's own 'هزینه تامین برق' baseline) than by any single year's tariff schedule; the WDI consumption series for Iran likely lags 2025 by several years, limiting near-term verifiability.

law_title: بخشنامه وزارت نیرو در خصوص تعرفه‌های برق و شرایط عمومی آنها (duplicate filing of the same Ministry of Energy electricity-tariff circular, effective Khordad 1404)
justification: Identical instrument to law_id 656e6ddd1cf5 (same circular text, filed in a different batch/collection): annual progressive electricity tariff schedule intended to price down above-quota consumption.
caveats: Same as above — GDP growth, weather/cooling demand, and continued heavy residential subsidization dominate consumption trends far more than one year's tariff notice; data-lag limits verification against 2025.

law_title: بخشنامه بانک مرکزی درخصوص ارسال فهرست سوم ثبت سفارش‌های تجهیزات و ملزومات پزشکی معاف از پرداخت مابه‌التفاوت وزارت بهداشت (CBI circular exempting a third batch of medical-equipment import registrations, financed at the preferential 285,000 and 42,000 rial/USD rates, from foreign-exchange price-differential clawback)
lag_description: concurrent (administrative clearance of already-financed import orders)
justification: The circular operationalizes Iran's multi-tier exchange-rate system by waiving the mabeh-al-tafavot (currency-differential) clawback for a specific list of medical-equipment import orders that had been financed at subsidized/preferential dollar rates (285,000 and legacy 42,000 rial), as long as customs clearance proof is provided. It is direct evidence of the operation of Iran's parallel official/preferential-rate regime that the exchange-rate chart tracks.
caveats: This is a narrow administrative housekeeping circular for one batch of import files, not a rate-setting or devaluation instrument — it does not itself move the market or official exchange rate; the rial's trajectory in the referenced FX chart is overwhelmingly driven by sanctions, oil-revenue access, and inflation rather than by this circular's exemption mechanics.

law_title: بخشنامه بانک مرکزی درخصوص ارسال فهرست سوم ثبت سفارش‌های تجهیزات و ملزومات پزشکی (duplicate filing, same CBI circular, filed in a court-ruling/Divan_edalat_edari batch)
lag_description: concurrent (administrative clearance of already-financed import orders)
justification: Identical instrument to law_id b4655d01599d: waives the FX price-differential clawback for a batch of medical-equipment import registrations financed at Iran's preferential exchange rates, illustrating the operation of the multi-tier currency system.
caveats: Same as above — a narrow one-batch administrative exemption, not a driver of the exchange-rate series itself; sanctions, oil revenue and inflation dominate.

law_title: قانون تشویق پس‌انداز مسکن (Law Encouraging Housing Savings — establishes a Housing Savings Fund (صندوق پس‌انداز مسکن) under Bank Rahni Iran, offering preferential interest and prize incentives on housing savings deposits, backed by Bank Markazi and Plan Organization funding)
lag_description: same year to 1-3 year lag (deposit growth as the savings scheme took up take-up)
justification: The law creates a dedicated savings-and-loan mechanism (with Bank Markazi back-funding and Plan Organization long-term deposits) specifically to mobilize private household savings toward mortgage lending. This chart's exact window (FY1963/64-1969/70) tracks private-sector bank deposits broken out by Sight/Saving/Time type, making the 'saving' deposit series a direct, contemporaneous observable of the law's intended mechanism.
caveats: 1960s Iran was in a broader oil-boom credit and income expansion, and overall deposit growth reflects general financial deepening, urbanization and rising incomes far more than this one housing-savings scheme; the chart cannot isolate deposits attributable specifically to the new housing fund from general saving-deposit growth.

law_title: لایحه قانونی راجع به معافیت کالاهای وارداتی مورد نیاز واحدهای تولیدی صنعتی مجاز از پرداخت حقوق گمرکی و سود بازرگانی در سال ۱۳۵۹ (Revolutionary Council decree exempting imported goods needed on the production line of licensed industrial units from customs duties/commercial profit, for year 1359)
justification: A one-year, revolution-era customs relief for imported line-production inputs of licensed domestic manufacturers (conditioned on no adequate domestic substitute existing), intended to keep industrial production running by cheapening capital/intermediate-goods imports.
caveats: 1980 is dominated by far larger shocks — the Iran-Iraq War erupted in September 1980, alongside post-revolution nationalizations, capital flight, and the US hostage-crisis sanctions/asset freeze — any of which would swamp the effect of a single-year, narrowly targeted customs exemption on aggregate manufacturing value added; a causal read from this decree alone is speculative.

law_title: قانون بیمه همگانی خدمات درمانی کشور (Universal Health Insurance Services Law)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag, then gradual over a decade (law mandated rollout to all groups within 5 years of enactment)
justification: Establishes a national Supreme Council of Health Insurance, mandates universal coverage rollout within five years, creates the government-owned Health Insurance Organization, sets per-capita premiums/tariffs by hiring category, and puts a floor under government/employer premium co-payment shares (Arts. 4-15) -- a direct, documented instrument shaping government and household health-financing shares.
caveats: Health expenditure and coverage metrics are also driven by GDP growth, oil-revenue-funded budget cycles, sanctions-era import costs for medicine, and later reforms (e.g., 2011 Health Transformation Plan, Social Security Organization changes) that are separate instruments layered on top of this 1994 law.

law_title: قانون اصلاح مواد سوم و چهارم قانون دخانیات (Law Amending Articles 3 and 4 of the Tobacco Law)
lag_description: same fiscal year (sets the monopoly purchase-price schedule for the 1932-1934 crop years directly in the statute)
justification: Sets the state tobacco monopoly's (Dokhaniyat) fixed purchase prices by tobacco type/region for 1932-1934 and mandates the state to directly buy/sell all domestic tobacco output starting with the 1933 crop -- a direct price-and-procurement instrument for the exact institution (Regie/Dokhaniyat conversion) the matched chart tracks.
caveats: The chart spans 1890-1995 and is described as sparse; many other statutes and decrees amended tobacco monopoly pricing and structure over that century, so this single 1932 amendment is one of several instruments shaping the series, not the sole driver of any observed level or workforce change.

law_title: لایحه قانونی اجازه تأمین سرمایه به منظور تداوم فعالیت بانکها و مؤسسات اعتباری ملی شده (Law Authorizing Capital Injection to Sustain Nationalized Banks)
lag_description: same fiscal year for the initial injection, with statutory repayment spread over the following 5 years
justification: Explicitly directs Bank Markazi to extend credit to recapitalize the newly nationalized banks for losses incurred at nationalization (Art. 2), with the government obligated to repay Bank Markazi over five years -- a documented, direct expansion of government debt to the central bank.
caveats: The 1978-2016 government-debt-to-central-bank series is also driven by war financing (1980-88), oil-revenue shortfalls, and later monetization of budget deficits; this single post-nationalization recapitalization is one identifiable but not sole contributor, especially given it coincides with the start of the Iran-Iraq War.

law_title: قانون تعیین حقوق گمرکی کالاهای وارداتی بر اساس سیستم هماهنگ شده توصیف و کدگذاری کالا (Law Setting Import Customs Duties per the Harmonized System)
lag_description: same fiscal year (the attached tariff schedule became the binding duty table upon enactment)
justification: Directly replaces Iran's import duty schedule with a new table keyed to the internationally harmonized HS coding system (single-article law adopting the annexed tariff schedule) -- a direct rewrite of the applied customs-duty structure for essentially all imported goods.
caveats: Because the law reset the entire tariff table rather than uniformly raising or lowering rates, net direction on average tariff level is ambiguous without the annex detail; it was itself superseded by the 2001 Customs Affairs Law amendment, and observed import-duty revenue/tariff-rate series are also shaped by import volumes, exchange-rate policy, and later WTO-accession-track reforms.

law_title: قانون تمدید مهلت اجرای قانون اجازه تعیین میزان حقوق گمرکی و سود بازرگانی کالاهای وارداتی توسط دولت (Law Extending the Deadline for Government-Set Import Customs Duties/Commercial Profit Rates)
lag_description: same fiscal year, bridging instrument until the HS-based tariff law took effect
justification: Purely procedural: it extends, without amending the rates themselves, the government's temporary authority (from a 1993 law) to set import customs duties/commercial-profit percentages, pending passage of the HS-based tariff law -- it kept an existing ad-hoc tariff-setting regime alive rather than changing duty levels.
caveats: Because it changes no rate itself, any movement in import-duty or tariff-rate charts around 1995-96 is better attributed to the substantive HS tariff law (982f83755dba) that this extension was bridging toward; this law's own effect is largely administrative continuity.

law_title: قانون اجازه تغییر درصدهای حقوق گمرکی بر اساس برخی از موافقتنامه‌های مصوب مجلس شورای اسلامی (Law Authorizing Changes to Customs Duty Percentages under Certain Trade Agreements)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as preferential-tariff schedules under GSTP/D-8-OIC/Izmir Protocol were implemented
justification: Authorizes the government to adjust the tariff/preference list and reduce non-tariff barriers to implement Iran's obligations under the Global System of Trade Preferences among Developing Countries, the Islamic Countries' preferential trade agreement, and the Izmir Protocol amendment -- a partial, agreement-scoped tariff-reduction instrument covering only goods/partners named in those pacts.
caveats: Effect is confined to trade with specific treaty partners and covered product lists, a small slice of total imports, so any change in the economy-wide applied tariff average is more plausibly driven by the broader HS tariff law (982f83755dba) and later trade-policy shifts than by this narrower authorization.

law_title: لایحه قانونی معافیت حقوق گمرکی و سود بازرگانی ماشین‌آلات و ادوات کشاورزی و لوازم یدکی مربوط (Law Exempting Agricultural Machinery/Parts from Customs Duty and Commercial Profit)
lag_description: gradual over a decade (lower import cost of machinery feeds into mechanization and yields over years, not immediately)
justification: Exempts agricultural machinery, equipment, and related spare parts not domestically manufactured from customs duty and commercial-profit charges (single-article law), lowering the landed cost of farm mechanization inputs and, over time, plausibly supporting mechanization-driven yield gains.
caveats: No agricultural-machinery-import or mechanization-specific chart exists in this dataset, so the link is inferred only through downstream crop/cereal yield series, which are dominated by weather, irrigation investment, guaranteed-purchase-price policy, fertilizer access, and land reform -- this customs exemption is at most a minor, hard-to-isolate contributor.

law_title: قانون معافیت صادرات کالا و خدمات از پرداخت عوارض (Law Exempting Exports of Goods and Services from Duties/Levies)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as exporters adjusted to the fee exemption
justification: Bars all ministries, agencies, municipalities, and local/provincial councils from levying any 'avarez' (local duties/fees) on exported goods and services (single-article law with implementing bylaw), removing a cost layer on exports as a trade-facilitation measure.
caveats: This is a minor fee exemption, not a tariff or exchange-rate change; Iran's goods and services export trends over this period are dominated by oil-price cycles, sanctions regimes, and exchange-rate policy, so this law's marginal contribution to aggregate export levels is likely small and hard to isolate.

law_title: قانون اصلاح سقف ارزی سال 1381 در جدول شماره (2) قانون برنامه سوم توسعه (Law Amending the 1381/2002 FX Ceiling Table under the Third Development Plan)
lag_description: same fiscal year (sets the FX allocation ceiling for the 1381/2002 budget year)
justification: Revises the statutory ceiling on foreign-exchange allocation for FY1381 (oil revenue plus a draw from the Foreign Exchange Reserve/Oil Stabilization Account) and, in its tabled proviso, explicitly authorizes the government to settle rial-cost differences arising 'during the unification of the exchange rate' -- placing it directly inside the 2002 official/parallel exchange-rate unification episode.
caveats: This law sets a budget-side FX ceiling and settlement mechanism, not the exchange-rate regime itself (the unification was implemented via separate central-bank/Majles decisions in 2002); the exchange-rate series is driven far more by oil prices, sanctions, and monetary policy than by this single ceiling-table amendment, so its own marginal effect on the official/parallel rate gap is modest and largely coincident with the broader unification reform.

law_title: رأی شماره ۱۰۹ دیوان عدالت اداری - ابطال بند مربوط به خسارت تأخیر تأدیه بهای برق به نرخ خاص
justification: This is a narrow administrative court ruling striking down one clause of a 1999 electricity-tariff circular on late-payment penalty calculation; it does not reset the underlying electricity price/tariff schedule.
caveats: No chart in the index tracks Iranian electricity tariffs or an energy CPI subcomponent, so there is no chart to map this to; excluded.

law_title: لغو مصوبه وزارت نیرو ۱۳۷۷ درباره افزایش بهای دیماند برق اشتراک‌های عمومی مسکونی
justification: Court ruling annulling a 1998 demand-charge tariff increase for public/residential electricity subscriptions on grounds it exceeded the statutory 20% cap under the Second Plan Law.
caveats: No electricity-tariff or energy-CPI chart exists in the index to receive this record; excluded rather than force-mapped.

law_title: رأی شماره ۳۴ دیوان عدالت اداری - ابطال واگذاری سهام هواپیمایی آسمان به صندوق بازنشستگی کشوری
justification: Ruling blocks a single share transfer of Aseman Airlines from the state to the Civil Servants Pension Fund on the grounds the recipient was not a qualifying cooperative/private entity under Third Plan Law Art.129.
caveats: The index has only aggregate air-transport passenger/freight charts and no privatization-transactions or airline-ownership chart; a single carrier's ownership dispute would not be visible in national passenger-traffic data, so no record mapped.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده ۵۲ قانون رفع موانع تولید رقابت‌پذیر و ارتقای نظام مالی کشور
justification: Implements Article 52 of the 2014 'Removing Obstacles to Competitive Production' law, easing conversion of agricultural land for industrial/mining unit construction and expansion — a supply-side bottleneck-removal measure for manufacturing capacity.
caveats: Highly multi-causal: manufacturing/industry value-added growth in this period is driven far more by the 2016 JCPOA sanctions relief, oil-price cycles, and exchange-rate shocks than by this one land-use permitting bylaw; treat as a minor contributing factor, not a primary driver.

law_title: آیین‌نامه نحوه اعطای اعتبارات به طرح‌های صادرات خدمات فنی و مهندسی
justification: Establishes a 5-year export-credit facility (via Export Guarantee Fund / operating banks) for Iranian firms undertaking technical and engineering services contracts abroad, intended to make such exports competitive against foreign-financed bids.
caveats: Iran's aggregate commercial service exports are dominated by travel and transport services, not engineering services; this credit facility covers a narrow set of project-based deals and its effect would be a small fraction of the total series, easily swamped by sanctions, exchange-rate and regional-demand swings.

law_title: دستورالعمل انتشار اوراق سفارش ساخت
justification: SEO directive creating 'construction-order sukuk' (istisna-like bonds) as a new tradable securities class for financing asset construction by any qualifying issuer (state or private), expanding the range of instruments available on Iran's capital market.
caveats: This is one of many Islamic-finance instrument types introduced to Iran's bond/sukuk market over the 2010s (also ijarah, murabaha, etc.); market capitalization and traded-value growth are overwhelmingly driven by equity market cycles (2019-2020 TSE boom/bust, inflation-driven repricing) rather than this specific instrument, so treat this as marginal.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی نحوه فروش خانه‌های ارزان‌قیمت موضوع تبصره ۳۷ قانون بودجه ۱۳۶۴
justification: One-off wartime-era bylaw allocating already-built low-price housing units (built from state development-budget funds) to eligible applicants via a points/lottery system; it is a distribution mechanism for an existing stock, not a housing-supply or price policy.
caveats: The only Housing-category chart in the index is a census-point series of housing units by household size (1966-2006) with no annual resolution around 1985, so this administrative allocation bylaw cannot be meaningfully linked to any chart we hold.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی تسهیلات اعتباری اشتغال مهاجرین جنگ تحمیلی (بند ۵ تبصره ۳ بودجه ۱۳۶۸)
justification: Narrow, one-fiscal-year (1368) credit facility (4 billion rials total) for employment/reconstruction projects specifically for war-displaced persons (Iran-Iraq war migrants), administered via the Bonyad-e Omoor-e Mohajerin.
caveats: Too small in scale and too narrowly targeted (a single displaced-persons cohort in one budget year) to register in any national labor, employment, or credit aggregate chart; no direct chart mapping is credible.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی توسعه مالکیت و افزایش ثروت خانوارهای ایرانی (سهام عدالت)
lag_description: gradual over a decade (distribution ran ~2006-2016, most shares unlocked/tradable from 2016)
justification: This is the implementing bylaw for the 'Justice Shares' (Saham-e Edalat) program: state-owned enterprise shares are transferred to intermediary holding companies and then distributed on installment to low-income-decile households, explicitly intended to broaden ownership and reduce wealth concentration; shares were later listed/tradable on the Tehran Stock Exchange, widening domestic share ownership.
caveats: Direction listed as 'decrease' for inequality/wealth-concentration measures (the stated policy goal), but real-world evidence on whether Justice Shares meaningfully reduced measured inequality is contested — implementation delays, elite capture, and the shares' volatile market value (especially post-2016 listing) complicate any clean read; broader inequality trends are also driven by inflation, sanctions-driven income shocks, and cash subsidy programs. Capital-market chart links reflect the mechanical effect of adding a large distributed shareholder base, not necessarily higher market efficiency.

law_title: قانون اصلاح قانون پیشگیری از عوارض ناشی از خشکسالی و جبران خسارات
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-2 year lag
justification: Waives interest/fees on farmers' and ranchers' overdue agricultural loans and grants well-monitoring-fee relief/exemptions for wells with reduced water yield in the 1379-1380 (2000-2001) drought years, intended to help farmers and livestock producers recover from drought losses.
caveats: This is a debt-relief and fee-waiver response to an already-occurring severe drought (1999-2001), not a driver of agricultural output itself; any dip and subsequent recovery visible in crop/cereal/livestock production charts around 2000-2002 is caused primarily by the drought (precipitation shock) and its end, with this law at most cushioning farmer solvency rather than moving physical output. Confidence and causal weight are low; label as background/context, not a direct driver.

law_title: ضوابط اعطای تسهیلات به منظور تسهیل و تسریع در اتمام پروژه‌های سازندگان مسکن و تخصیص آنها به متقاضیان مسکن مهر
justification: Directs Bank Maskan and other banks to expand credit ceilings (up to 250 million rials/unit) and participation financing to builders and Mehr Housing applicants; the Mehr Housing program's bank-credit-financed construction (much of it via low/no-cost Central Bank credit lines) is widely cited by Iranian economists as a major contributor to the 2010-2013 surge in base money and broad money growth.
caveats: This is one implementing circular within the much larger, multi-year Mehr Housing program (whose founding legislation is not in this batch); broad money and private-credit growth in 2010-2013 were also driven by the subsidy-reform cash-transfer program, government deficit monetization, and sanctions-driven exchange-rate pressure — Mehr Housing credit is one contributing channel among several, not the sole cause. No annual housing-price or housing-starts chart exists in the index to show the direct housing-market effect.

law_title: آیین‌نامه تسهیلات و نحوه تبدیل درآمد تولیدکنندگان غیردولتی برق به ارز
justification: Creates a preferential mechanism for independent (non-state) power producers to convert rial electricity-sale revenue into foreign currency at the deal-day rate via the Central Bank, in order to service their foreign project-finance obligations — an example of Iran's long-running practice of allocating preferential/administered exchange rates to specific sectors.
caveats: This is one narrow, small-scale sectoral FX-allocation channel among many such preferential-rate mechanisms Iran has used for decades; it is not a meaningful driver of the official-vs-parallel exchange rate gap, which is dominated by oil-revenue flows, sanctions, and monetary policy. Included only as low-confidence background context on Iran's multiple-exchange-rate regime, not as a causal driver of the FX gap series.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي قانون بازار اوراق بهادار جمهوري اسلامي ايران (Implementing bylaw for the Securities Market Law)
justification: Establishes the self-regulatory institutional architecture (SEO oversight of exchanges, OTC markets, clearing/depository companies, disclosure and listing rules) mandated by the 2005 Securities Market Law, which underpinned the subsequent expansion of listed companies, market capitalization and trading volume on the Tehran Stock Exchange.
caveats: This is an institutional/organizational bylaw, not a market-moving instrument itself; TSE growth over the following 15 years was driven far more by liquidity injections, subsidy-cash-to-stocks flows, sanctions-driven rial depreciation (nominal market cap in rial terms), and privatization listings than by this regulatory plumbing.

law_title: آيين نامه نحوه هزينه اعتبارات طرح يارانه سود تسهيلات براي كاهش شدت انرژي (Bylaw on spending energy-subsidy interest-rate-subsidy funds for reducing energy intensity)
justification: Sets up a small government-backed interest-subsidy/soft-loan fund (via a technical committee of Energy, Industries, Welfare ministries) to co-finance industrial energy-efficiency retrofit projects, directly targeting Iran's energy-intensity metric named in the program's own title.
caveats: Program scale relative to Iran's total industrial energy base is small; national energy intensity is dominated by heavily subsidized domestic fuel/electricity prices (making efficiency investment unattractive) far more than by this one credit facility, so any measured decline in intensity is more plausibly attributed to price reform (e.g. the 2010 targeted-subsidies law) than to this program.

law_title: آيين نامه اعطاي تسهيلات مسكن مهر (Bylaw granting Mehr Housing Scheme credit facilities)
justification: Sets the concessional 4%/7%/9% qarz-al-hasaneh loan terms and 15-year repayment schedule for the mass Mehr social-housing program (Bank Maskan credit line partly financed by central bank money creation, per Article 10 allowing the bank to carry the government as debtor pending subsidy appropriation), which both expanded homeownership housing stock nationally and is widely cited as a driver of 2010-2013 broad-money growth.
caveats: Housing tenure shifts also reflect ordinary urbanization and demographic trends unrelated to Mehr; broad-money growth in this period was multi-causal (budget deficits, sanctions-driven CBI financing of government, general credit expansion), so attributing M2 growth specifically to Mehr overstates a single channel among several.

law_title: آيين نامه اعطاي تسهيلات مسكن مهر (Bylaw granting Mehr Housing Scheme credit facilities)
justification: Mehr's central-bank-financed credit line is one of the commonly cited quasi-fiscal contributors to the liquidity growth that preceded Iran's 2012-2013 inflation surge.
caveats: The 2012-13 inflation spike is overwhelmingly attributed to the rial's collapse following intensified US/EU sanctions and the 2010 subsidy-reform price shock; Mehr financing is a secondary, hard-to-isolate contributor at best.

law_title: بخشنامه سازمان توسعه تجارت ايران درخصوص تغييرات نرخ ارز مبناي محاسبه حقوق ورودي (Trade Development Organization circular on changes to the FX rate used to calculate import customs duties)
justification: Implements a 1404/01/24 cabinet decree changing which official exchange rate is used as the customs-duty base for imports; since Iran runs a multi-tier FX regime, moving the customs-valuation rate directly changes the landed cost (and hence recorded import unit values) of imported goods.
caveats: The source text is a bare administrative circular with no rate figures preserved, so direction (duty burden up or down) cannot be determined from the text; import volumes/values are simultaneously driven by sanctions, oil-revenue-linked FX availability, and the broader official-vs-market exchange rate gap, making this circular one of many concurrent FX-policy moves in 2025.

law_title: تعيين سود بازرگاني كالاهاي مشمول رديفهاي تعرفه ... براي اجرا در سال 1389 (Setting commercial-profit/tariff rates on tariff-line goods for FY1389/2010)
justification: This cabinet decree is the actual legal instrument that annually sets the commercial-profit (effectively applied tariff) rate schedule for every HS tariff line under the Export-Import Regulations bylaw for the 1389 (2010/11) fiscal year, directly determining the applied-tariff-rate statistics for that year.
caveats: Because Iran resets this schedule annually as routine administration (not a one-off reform), the specific direction and magnitude of the rate change for any aggregate simple/weighted mean tariff statistic cannot be inferred from the decree text (the rate tables are in an unpreserved attachment); import volumes are also strongly affected by sanctions and FX availability in the same period.

law_title: تعيين سود بازرگاني كالاهاي مشمول رديف ها ... براي اجرا در سال 1388 (Setting commercial-profit/tariff rates on tariff-line goods for FY1388/2009)
justification: The predecessor annual decree to c783764058ee, setting the same category of commercial-profit/tariff-line schedule for the 1388 (2009/10) fiscal year under the Export-Import Regulations bylaw, directly determining that year's applied-tariff-rate statistics.
caveats: Routine annual rate-setting with unpreserved rate tables, so direction cannot be determined from text; import statistics for FY1388 were also heavily affected by the 2008-09 global financial crisis and oil-price collapse, which likely dominate any tariff-schedule effect.

law_title: اصلاح تبصره (۴) الحاقی ماده (۳) و ماده (۱۶) آیین‌نامه اجرایی گسترش بنگاه‌های کوچک اقتصادی زودبازده و کارآفرین (Amendment to the 'Quick-Return, Job-Creating' Small Enterprises credit bylaw)
lag_description: same-to-next fiscal year for credit stock; 1-3 year lag for any inflationary pass-through
justification: The amendment raises the ceiling on directed bank credit for the 'Zoud-Bazde' (quick-return) SME/job-creation lending program from 18,000 billion to 180,000 billion rial (a 10x jump) and lets provincial employment task forces earmark up to 20% of allocated facilities to working-capital shortfalls. This was a large, deliberate expansion of subsidized directed bank credit during 2005-2007, widely cited as a driver of double-digit private-sector credit and money-supply growth in that period.
caveats: Confidence limited to 'contributing' because domestic credit and private-sector credit-to-GDP series are driven by many simultaneous factors (oil-revenue-fueled government spending, general banking-sector expansion, interest-rate policy, later inflation from 2010-12 subsidy reform and sanctions). The program's disbursement was also often incomplete/politically contested, so its realized effect on aggregate credit stock is smaller than the nominal ceiling implies.

law_title: دستورالعمل ثبت و عرضۀ عمومی اوراق بهادار (Directive on Registration and Public Offering of Securities)
lag_description: gradual, over subsequent years as issuers used the new registration process
justification: This High Council of Securities and Exchange directive operationalizes the primary-market registration/prospectus (bianieh-e sabt) process required by the 2005 Securities Market Act, standardizing how companies register and publicly offer shares/bonds. As an implementing procedure it lowered administrative friction for new listings and share/bond issuance, one of several inputs into Tehran capital-market growth in the following decade.
caveats: This is a procedural/administrative instrument, not the primary enabling law (the 2005 Securities Market Act itself, not in this batch); market capitalization and listings were driven far more by privatization (Article 44) share transfers, oil-revenue liquidity, and retail investor waves than by this registration procedure alone. Confidence kept low because attribution to this specific directive versus the broader 2005 Act is not separable in the data.

law_title: دستورالعمل فعالیت بازارهای خارج از بورس (Directive on Over-the-Counter Securities Market Activity)
lag_description: gradual, over the following decade (OTC/Iran Fara Bourse market builds up post-2009)
justification: This directive is the founding regulatory framework for Iran's over-the-counter securities market (which became Iran Fara Bourse, launched 2009), setting capital requirements, licensing and supervisory rules for OTC market operators. It directly created a new, additional venue for listing and trading securities, expanding the pool of listed/traded companies beyond the main Tehran Stock Exchange.
caveats: Effect is institutional/structural rather than a one-time price shock; aggregate market-cap and turnover series also reflect the primary TSE, macro liquidity conditions, sanctions-driven capital flight into equities, and privatization share transfers, so isolating the OTC directive's specific contribution is not possible from chart data alone.

law_title: رأی شماره ۱۳۷ هیئت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری - ابطال آزادسازی نرخ حمل و نقل هوایی مسافر و بار (Administrative Court ruling annulling the Civil Aviation Council's liberalization of domestic air fares)
lag_description: same year, short-run effect on ticket pricing and possibly ridership
justification: The ruling struck down the Civil Aviation Supreme Council's 2002 decision to float domestic airfares (removing the price floor/ceiling and letting fares move with supply and demand), on the grounds it exceeded the Council's statutory authority and breached the Third Development Plan's 10%-per-year cap on state-enterprise price increases. The effect was to keep domestic air-ticket pricing under administrative constraint rather than market-set, which in principle would keep fares (and thus demand/ridership) different than under full liberalization.
caveats: This is a narrow, single-year regulatory/pricing dispute confined to domestic air travel; national air-passenger volume in the WDI series is far more strongly driven by GDP growth, fuel costs, fleet/sanctions-driven aircraft shortages, and general macroeconomic conditions than by this one ruling. Confidence is low because the practical enforcement and duration of the reinstated price cap after this ruling is not verifiable from the law text alone.

law_title: ابطال مصوبه شورای عالی هواپیمایی کشوری درخصوص تعیین نرخ حمل و نقل هوایی مسافر و بار
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-3 year lag
justification: The Administrative Justice Court annulled the Civil Aviation Council's 1381/2002 resolution that had floated domestic airfares (setting only a price floor tied to supply/demand) as exceeding its statutory authority and violating the Third Plan's 10% state-price-increase cap; the ruling forced a return to regulated fare-setting for domestic flights, which plausibly affects ticket affordability and hence passenger volumes.
caveats: This is a narrow court annulment of one resolution, not a general aviation pricing law; passenger volumes (wdi__IS.AIR) are driven far more by fleet capacity, sanctions-driven aircraft shortages, fuel costs, and overall demand than by this single ruling. No direct airfare or transport-CPI chart exists in our index to test the price channel directly.

law_title: رأی شماره 584-585 هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری: افزایش حقوق کارکنان و بازنشستگان دولت متناسب با نرخ تورم
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-3 year lag
justification: This Administrative Justice Court general ruling (under Article 150 of the Fourth Development Plan) compels annual pension/salary adjustments for government retirees and employees to be proportionate to the inflation rate, directly targeting the real value/adequacy of transfer income relative to beneficiaries' welfare.
caveats: WDI social-protection 'adequacy' indicators are sparse, survey-based, single-year snapshots for Iran, not an annual inflation-indexed series, so they may not register this ruling's effect at all; actual pension adequacy is also driven by pension-fund solvency, government budget constraints, and general inflation dynamics independent of this ruling.

law_title: رأی شماره 313 هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری: ابطال بند 1 مصوبه 263 هیات تنظیم بازار برق ایران
justification: Independent power producers (10 private generation companies) sued and won annulment of an Electricity Market Regulation Board decision that failed to adjust the base hourly generation-readiness capacity payment for inflation as required by Article 133 of the Fifth Development Plan; restoring inflation-indexed capacity payments improves the economics of private power generation investment.
caveats: This is a single dispute over one board resolution affecting a handful of IPPs, not an economy-wide tariff reform; private energy investment (PPI series) is far more strongly driven by sanctions, currency risk, and overall investment climate than by this ruling, and no direct electricity-price chart exists in our index.

law_title: رأی شماره 1022 هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری: ابطال بخشنامه مالیات بر ارزش افزوده بر روغن نباتی خام
justification: The court annulled a Tax Administration circular for improperly extending the period during which crude vegetable oil was subject to VAT, effectively restoring/enforcing the statutory VAT exemption date for this product; lowering the VAT burden on crude vegetable oil reduces its landed cost, which can affect import volumes and consumption.
caveats: This is a narrow, retroactive administrative-procedure ruling about the exact exemption cutoff date, not a change in the VAT rate itself; vegetable oil trade and consumption in Iran are dominated by import-dependency, FX availability, and subsidy policy, so any effect from this ruling alone is likely marginal and hard to isolate.

law_title: قانون تمدید مدت اجرای قانون اجازه تعیین میزان حقوق گمرکی و سود بازرگانی کالاهای وارداتی توسط دولت
justification: This law extends (through end of 1373/1994) the government's delegated authority to set customs duties and commercial-profit (tariff) rates on imported goods by decree rather than through new Majlis legislation each time, the general mechanism underpinning Iran's import tariff revenue.
caveats: It is a short procedural extension of pre-existing authority, not a substantive tariff-rate change itself, so any measurable movement in customs/import-duty revenue reflects the underlying tariff schedule set separately by the executive, not this extension law; broader trade volumes, exchange rate, and sanctions are much larger drivers of import duty revenue.

law_title: قانون اصلاح سقف ارزی سال 1382 در قانون برنامه سوم توسعه
justification: Enacted in the aftermath of Iran's March 2002 exchange-rate unification, this law raises the authorized 1382 (2003) FX ceiling to $15.438bn and permits drawing $2.5bn from the Oil Stabilization ('FX Reserve') Account to cover Central Bank debt arising from rate unification and to repay maturing FX loans, i.e. a budget-mechanics follow-on to the unification, relevant to the official/parallel exchange-rate gap.
caveats: This is a routine annual budget-ceiling adjustment, not the unification law itself; the official-vs-parallel FX gap in Iran is overwhelmingly driven by oil revenue, sanctions, and Central Bank FX policy rather than this specific ceiling amendment, and the exact quantitative effect of this one clause is not separable from those larger forces.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون استقلال شرکت‌های توزیع نیروی برق در استان‌ها
justification: This bylaw restructures provincial electricity distribution companies into independent entities, requires the Ministry of Energy to set an 'economic' (cost-reflective) electricity sale price each year to attract non-governmental capital, and caps any single private shareholder's stake at 5% while requiring capital-structure reform (debt/equity below 30%) — a direct, named mechanism for expanding private-sector participation in the power-distribution sector.
caveats: Private energy investment in Iran is also strongly shaped by sanctions, FX access, and general investment climate, all of which likely dominate over this sector-specific corporate-restructuring bylaw; the promised subsidized/cost-reflective pricing mechanism was, in practice, often not fully implemented in subsequent years.

law_title: قانون جبران و پیشگیری عوارض ناشی از خشکسالی
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-3 year lag
justification: This law adds credit facilities via Bank Keshavarzi for farmers/ranchers/nomads hit by the 1998-99 drought, extends loan repayment by a year, and earmarks up to 3% of the 1378 (1999) development budget for drought mitigation/compensation — a direct fiscal relief instrument aimed at sustaining agricultural production and preventing further output collapse after a drought shock.
caveats: The law is a mitigant responding to drought, not the primary driver of production trends; actual cereal/wheat output in the following years is overwhelmingly determined by rainfall and irrigation water availability, not by this relief package, and the direction of the relationship (recovery support vs. underlying weather shock) is easily confounded with the drought itself.

law_title: آيين نامه تشخيص قراردادهاي مهم نفتي و نحوه انعقاد آنها (Bylaw defining major oil contracts and their conclusion procedure)
lag_description: multi-year lag (upstream investment approvals take years to translate into production/export changes)
justification: Establishes the Economic Council approval process for long-term (>5yr) natural gas/LNG export contracts and major upstream oil & gas investment contracts (the framework used for post-JCPOA IPC deals with foreign partners), governing the pace at which new upstream investment and gas-export deals could be concluded.
caveats: This is a procedural approval mechanism, not a production or investment instrument itself; actual effect on production/FDI is swamped by the 2018 US withdrawal from the JCPOA and re-imposition of sanctions, which choked off most of the foreign investment this bylaw was meant to facilitate. Oil price swings, OPEC quotas, and sanctions are far larger drivers of the production series.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي تبصره 1 ماده 149 اصلاحي قانون ماليات هاي مستقيم (Executive bylaw, Note 1 of amended Article 149, Direct Taxes Law — asset revaluation tax exemption)
justification: Exempts the book-value surplus from corporate asset revaluation from income tax when the surplus is converted to paid-in capital; this created a strong incentive for Tehran Stock Exchange-listed firms to revalue fixed assets and issue bonus shares (افزایش سرمایه از محل تجدید ارزیابی), which mechanically inflates reported market capitalization.
caveats: Market capitalization in this period (esp. 2019-2020) was overwhelmingly driven by the TSE speculative boom fueled by rial depreciation, negative real deposit rates, and government promotion of retail stock investment — the tax bylaw is one contributing structural enabler among several, not the primary driver.

law_title: قانون مربوط باصلاح قانون افزایش اعتبارات کشاورزی (Amendment to Law Increasing Agricultural Credit)
justification: Directs Bank Melli to channel 3.5 billion rials to Bank Keshavarzi for agricultural loans (capped interest, 80% to provincial farmers), a direct farm-credit expansion instrument that plausibly supported irrigation, input purchases and cropped area over subsequent years.
caveats: Cereal/wheat output in this era is dominated by weather (rainfall variability), land reform-era tenure changes, and later the Green Revolution input package; a single credit-appropriation amendment is one of many contemporaneous drivers and cannot be isolated in aggregate production data.

law_title: قانون راجع به افزایش اعتبارات کشاورزی (Law on Increasing Agricultural Credit)
justification: Original 1958 law creating the 3.5 billion rial agricultural credit facility through Bank Keshavarzi (later amended by a3c1cbef6153 in 1960) — the founding instrument of state-directed farm credit expansion in this period.
caveats: Same caveats as the 1960 amendment: weather, land reform, and later Green Revolution inputs are more proximate drivers of measured cereal output than credit appropriations alone; FAOSTAT series begin around 1961, limiting direct pre/post comparison for this 1958 law.

law_title: لایحه قانونی الحاق یک تبصره ... معافیت حقوق گمرکی روغن نباتی (Customs Duty Exemption Expansion for Vegetable Oil)
justification: Extends an existing customs-duty exemption on vegetable oil (soybean oil imported by the state trading company) to a new supplier, directly lowering the landed cost of vegetable oil imports right after the 1979 revolution when import policy was highly unsettled.
caveats: This is a narrow one-company exemption during a period of broader trade disruption (revolution, war onset in 1980, exchange controls); aggregate FAOSTAT vegetable-oil trade volumes are also driven by these larger shocks, not this exemption alone.

law_title: لایحه قانونی الحاق یک تبصره ... معافیت حقوق گمرکی روغن نباتی (Customs Duty Exemption Expansion for Vegetable Oil)
justification: Cheaper imported vegetable oil (via customs exemption) is a plausible contributor to sustained per-capita vegetable oil consumption as a subsidized staple good.
caveats: Consumption is also shaped by rationing/coupon policy, subsidy budgets, and household income, all in flux during the early revolutionary and war years; this single tariff-exemption amendment is a minor, hard-to-isolate contributor.

law_title: قانون تامین تسهیلات اعتباری جدید ... افزایش ظرفیت پالایشگاه ولی‌عصر کنگان (Credit Facility Law incl. Vali-e Asr Kangan Refinery Capacity Expansion)
justification: Mandates Bank Markazi to supply 100 billion rials in new credit specifically to accelerate the Vali-e Asr Kangan refinery capacity-expansion project, a direct investment instrument for refinery output capacity that could show up in refinery-level production data from the early 2000s onward.
caveats: Refinery output also depends on feedstock crude availability, sanctions-driven equipment/investment constraints, and broader energy-sector investment cycles; the chart's coverage starts in 2001, several years after this credit was authorized, making direct attribution difficult.

law_title: قانون تامین تسهیلات اعتباری جدید ... خسارت کشاورزان و دامداران ناشی از خشکسالی (Drought-Relief Credit for Farmers and Livestock Herders)
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-2 year lag
justification: Authorizes 1,000 billion rials in emergency bank credit (via Bank Keshavarzi) to compensate farmers and herders for 1997 drought losses, a direct disaster-relief financing instrument intended to preserve livestock herds and restock capacity.
caveats: The credit mitigates but does not reverse drought damage — livestock production index movements around this period are driven primarily by the drought itself and subsequent rainfall recovery, not the relief credit; direction is ambiguous because the law responds to a decline rather than causing an increase outright.

law_title: قانون منع خرید و فروش کوپن‌های کالاهای اساسی (Law Banning Purchase/Sale of Essential-Goods Rationing Coupons)
justification: Criminalizes black-market trading of essential-goods and fuel ration coupons during the final year of the Iran-Iraq War, an enforcement instrument meant to prop up the official wartime rationing/pricing system that underlies part of the CPI basket.
caveats: This is background context, not a driver: 1988 inflation was overwhelmingly driven by war financing, monetary emission, and multiple exchange rates, not by anti-coupon-trafficking enforcement; the law's effect on measured price indices is speculative and likely negligible relative to these macro forces.

law_title: قانون نحوه انتشار اوراق مشارکت (Law on the Issuance of Participation Bonds)
lag_description: gradual over a decade or more
justification: Establishes the legal framework for government, state enterprises, and municipalities to issue Islamic participation bonds (اوراق مشارکت) to finance profitable projects, creating a new public/quasi-public debt-financing channel that was used with increasing frequency in subsequent decades.
caveats: Government net debt and liabilities are driven by many factors (budget deficits, oil-revenue swings, exchange-rate valuation effects, direct central-bank financing); participation-bond issuance is only one financing instrument among several, and its incremental contribution to aggregate debt series cannot be cleanly separated from these other channels.

law_title: تصویب‌نامه راجع به الحاق دو بند به ماده 43 آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (1367)
justification: Lets ministries with production companies use export FX earned via barter with socialist-bloc (COMECON) countries to import raw materials/machine parts directly, and mandates crediting export FX value at the bank-purchase rate; this barter/counter-trade channel shaped the composition and valuation of wartime (Iran-Iraq war era) non-oil trade flows.
caveats: War-economy trade was dominated by sanctions, shortages, and a fixed multi-tier exchange rate system; this is one narrow administrative channel among many controls, and its quantitative effect on aggregate merchandise trade values cannot be isolated from war disruption and rationing.

law_title: اصلاح آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (1381)
justification: Amends Article 11 of the import/export executive bylaw to set shipping-deadline windows (10-55 days depending on origin/mode) for goods imported without FX transfer under pre-existing permits, and removes Article 29; a procedural/customs-clearance timing rule for import legality, not a tariff or quota change.
caveats: Purely administrative/procedural amendment; any effect on measured import value is second-order relative to tariff policy, FX availability, and global commodity prices, which are the primary drivers of import totals.

law_title: نحوه استفاده از ارز حاصل از صادرات کالا توسط صادرکنندگان (1375)
justification: Directive from the Market Regulation Task Force lets exporters retain and use 30%/50% of their export FX proceeds to import goods on a Ministry of Commerce-approved list, directly governing the fraction of export earnings retained/repatriated to the formal banking system versus used for import substitution -- a lever that shapes export incentives and the FX supply channel underlying the official/parallel exchange-rate gap.
caveats: Iran ran a multi-tier, frequently revised FX allocation regime through the 1990s; the exchange-rate gap and trade values were also driven by oil-revenue swings, sanctions, and broader central-bank FX rationing, so this specific retention rule is one contributing mechanism among several, not the sole driver.

law_title: ضوابط کلی عرضه اولیه یا عمده سهام شرکت‌های دولتی مشمول واگذاری در بورس (1387)
justification: Supreme Securities & Exchange Council rules set the direct operating procedure for listing/IPO of state-owned company shares subject to Article 44 divestiture (minimum 5% initial float, disclosure, budget-based pricing) -- the concrete instrument by which state divestitures were converted into new listings, mechanically adding to Tehran Stock Exchange market capitalization.
caveats: Market capitalization is dominated by valuation swings (inflation, rial devaluation, investor sentiment) far more than by the pace of new listings; privatization IPOs are one contributing inflow among many, and some 'divestitures' under Article 44 transferred shares to quasi-state entities rather than genuinely widening free-float ownership.

law_title: اصلاح آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده 7 قانون اجرای سیاست‌های کلی اصل چهل و چهارم قانون اساسی (1388)
justification: Amendment to the business-licensing-reduction bylaw bars government agencies from issuing or requiring any permit without an explicit legal/cabinet basis and streamlines the licensing oversight board, intended to lower entry barriers for private business formation as part of the broader Article 44 business-environment-improvement push.
caveats: New business registration in Iran is much more strongly driven by macro conditions (inflation, sanctions, credit access, currency stability) than by administrative licensing simplification; this bylaw amendment is a minor procedural tweak within a much larger reform package, so any attributable effect on registration counts is weak and hard to isolate.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون جبران و پیشگیری عوارض ناشی از خشکسالی (1378)
justification: Implements the drought/flood-relief law by disbursing subsidized bank loans and development-project funds to Mazandaran flood victims and drought-affected farmers/herders (loan-repayment extensions, damage compensation funded via the agricultural-producer compensation fund); it is a compensatory fiscal transfer responding to an already-occurred production shock, not an input that would itself move production upward.
caveats: Wheat/cereal production in this period was driven primarily by the severe 1998-2001 multi-year Iranian drought itself, rainfall variability, and irrigation investment, not by this relief bylaw; the law is a small-scale, one-off disaster-response instrument whose macro effect on national output is negligible and mainly serves as background context for the drought episode.

law_title: ضوابط اجرایی قانون بودجه سال 1390 کل کشور (1390)
justification: Annual executive rules implementing the FY1390 budget impose government-wide austerity controls -- hiring freezes on hourly-paid teachers, caps on overtime pay and bonuses (max one month's salary), bans on new personnel-outsourcing contracts, office-closure mandates for Tehran-based agencies, and hard ceilings on agency commitments within allotted appropriations -- directly constraining public-sector compensation and consumption expenditure for that budget year.
caveats: These are one-year administrative rules re-issued (with variations) nearly every budget year, so isolating this specific instrument's effect on multi-year government compensation/consumption trends from the underlying budget law itself, inflation, and sanctions-driven fiscal stress is difficult; effects are likely modest and largely offset by base-year salary/price inflation.

law_title: قانون تعرفه‌گذاری خدمات پرستاری و تعدیل کارانه‌های پرستاری (1386)
