justification: Mandates the Health Ministry to set standardized tariffs for nursing diagnostic/treatment service bundles and to base nurses' incentive pay (karaneh) on those tariffs, financed through the national health-insurance system -- a direct wage/price-setting instrument in the healthcare labor market intended to improve nursing compensation and, by extension, retention/supply of nursing staff and public health spending.
caveats: Nurse workforce numbers and government health expenditure are driven far more by broader health-system budget growth, insurance-coverage expansion (e.g., the later Health Transformation Plan), and general wage inflation than by this single tariff-setting law; the implementing bylaw itself is not in the corpus, only the enabling statute, so actual tariff levels and their bite are unverified here.

law_title: قانون مربوط به حق باربری کالای صادراتی (Law on export-cargo loading duty)
justification: The law exempts export cargo loaded outside customs facilities (or transferred directly to vessels offshore) from loading (baraberi) fees, and caps the fee customs can charge exporters who partly self-handle loading — a small reduction in the transaction cost of exporting.
caveats: This is a narrow administrative fee waiver on a specific cargo-handling scenario, not a general tariff or trade-policy shift; observed movements in export value/volume over the following decades are overwhelmingly driven by oil exports, the 1979 revolution, the Iran-Iraq war, sanctions, and exchange-rate regime changes, not this fee rule. Effect on the aggregate export chart is likely undetectable.

law_title: آئین‌نامه اجرائی مرحله اول واگذاری سهام متعلق به بخش دولتی در واحدهای تولیدی به کارکنان واحدهای تولیدی و کارمندان دولت (Phase-1 bylaw for transferring state-owned shares in production units to workers/state employees)
justification: Implements the 1975 'Expansion of Ownership' law's first phase: state-held equity stakes in production units are priced by a state council and sold to plant workers and government employees (up to a per-person ceiling), with the state's Ownership-Expansion Finance Organization brokering/financing the sales — an early, narrow precursor to broader Iranian privatization that nudges some state equity into tradable/quasi-market ownership.
caveats: This is a worker/employee share-distribution scheme, not open-market listing or IPO activity, and predates or only marginally overlaps modern capital-market data; Tehran Stock Exchange capitalization and turnover over subsequent decades are driven far more by the much larger 2000s-era Article 44 privatization program, oil-revenue cycles, and sanctions/monetary conditions than by this 1988 bylaw.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی سازمان بازرسی و نظارت بر قیمت و توزیع کالا و خدمات (Executive bylaw establishing the Organization for Inspection & Oversight of Prices and Distribution of Goods and Services)
lag_description: same-to-following fiscal year, administrative capacity built up over subsequent years
justification: Creates a dedicated state body with statutory power to set/approve prices, license inspection agents (including Basij, local guilds, and government employees) to monitor 'profiteering,' undersupply, hoarding, and off-network sales, and refer violators to the Tazirat (price-enforcement) courts — a direct administrative price-control instrument aimed at suppressing measured inflation for controlled goods.
caveats: Iranian consumer-price inflation over the following decades is dominated by monetary financing of deficits, exchange-rate depreciation, and sanctions-driven supply shocks; administrative price-inspection regimes historically show limited and often only temporary effect on aggregate CPI/WPI, and enforcement intensity varied greatly by administration, so any effect on the aggregate index is not cleanly separable from these larger forces.

law_title: اصلاح آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Amendment to the executive bylaw of the export/import regulations law — tariff-code footnote updates)
law_date: 1381 SH text (batch metadata lists 1989; document itself dated 1381/2002-03)
lag_description: same fiscal year for the specific tariff lines affected
justification: Adds or revises technical footnotes to roughly a dozen specific HS tariff-code entries (glass products, machinery parts, chemicals, steel items, etc.) within the annual import/export tariff schedule — a routine technical maintenance update to the tariff book, not a rate or structural change.
caveats: Far too narrow (a handful of HS sub-headings) to move any national aggregate tariff or trade-value index; national average tariff rates and merchandise trade flows are driven by the periodic comprehensive tariff-schedule revisions, exchange-rate policy, and sanctions regimes rather than this kind of footnote-level amendment. Included only because the general instrument type (tariff schedule bylaw) mechanically touches the tariff-rate chart category.

law_title: اصلاح ماده ۱۶ آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون صادرات و واردات سال ۱۳۶۵ (Amendment to Article 16 of the 1986 import/export executive bylaw — export forex-balance requirement)
justification: Bars export of any good whose 'foreign-exchange balance' (export price minus its foreign-exchange import content) is not positive, tasking the Export Development Center with vetting export price lists against this rule before customs will clear them — a direct, binding control on which goods may legally be exported, shaping export composition and volume during the multiple-exchange-rate, war-economy period.
caveats: 1986 falls in the middle of the Iran-Iraq war under a heavily rationed, multi-tier exchange-rate system; observed export levels in this period are dominated by wartime oil-export constraints, sanctions, and the officially/black-market exchange-rate gap rather than this specific administrative screening rule, and the rule's real-world bite depended on enforcement capacity that is not separately observable.

law_title: آیین‌نامه استخراج و فروش مواد معدنی توسط بخش خصوصی (Bylaw on extraction and sale of mineral substances by the private sector)
lag_description: gradual, over subsequent years/decades as the tender-based operating regime took hold
justification: Establishes the tender (mezayedeh) mechanism, contract terms, output pricing, and reporting duties under which private operators may extract and sell from non-major state mines — an institutional/operating instrument intended to bring private capital into mining output that would otherwise sit idle under state control.
caveats: Iran's mineral-rent share of GDP and mining output are dominated by global commodity-price cycles and, for the 1980s specifically, by wartime disruption; this 1986 bylaw affects only smaller, non-major mines and its incremental contribution to aggregate mineral rents cannot be isolated from price and macro effects. Most of the era's own mining-production time series in this database stop in 1980, before this bylaw took effect, so only the longer-run mineral-rents series has any temporal overlap.

law_title: آیین‌نامه سرمایه‌گذاری خارجی در بورس‌ها و بازارهای خارج از بورس (Bylaw on foreign investment in stock exchanges and OTC markets)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as licensing and repatriation infrastructure was built out
justification: Directly authorizes foreign persons to obtain a trading license and buy/sell listed securities on Iranian exchanges (subject to 10%/20% ownership caps for non-strategic investors), and obliges the central bank to convert and permit repatriation of capital and gains — an explicit capital-account liberalization instrument aimed at growing foreign participation in, and thus liquidity/valuation of, Iranian capital markets.
caveats: This bylaw was adopted in the middle of an intensifying international sanctions regime (2010-2015) that heavily restricted foreign banks' and investors' ability to move money into Iran, which plausibly capped its real-world effect; Tehran Stock Exchange market cap and turnover over this period are driven far more by domestic liquidity, subsidy-reform-era inflation, and the 2016 sanctions-relief/2018 re-imposition cycle than by this foreign-investor access rule.

law_title: قانون تغییر جدول حقوق و ضوابط پرداخت مستخدمین مشمول قانون استخدام کشوری و قضات
lag_description: same fiscal year (FY1370/1991-92), with continuing effect via the law's built-in 3-5% annual seniority-increment mechanism in following years
justification: This law completely rewrote the civil-service base-pay grade table (replacing the Article 32 schedule of the Civil Servants Employment Law), introduced a new pay formula for judges, mandated annual seniority increments of 3-5%, and raised pensions for existing retirees by 20%, directly and intentionally increasing the government wage and pension bill starting FY1370.
caveats: WDI's compensation-of-employees series for Iran (if populated for this era) also reflects military/security payroll, later inflation-indexed adjustments, and headcount growth, so this single 1991 pay reform is one of several drivers of the long-run trend, not the sole cause of any given year's level.

law_title: قانون اصلاح قانون بودجه سال 1369 کل کشور
justification: This mid-year budget amendment added ~26 billion rials specifically earmarked for 'increase in state employees' salaries' plus targeted pay raises for judges, parliamentary staff, and teachers in deprived/rural areas, and a 10,000-rial/month increase for civil-service pensioners, directly augmenting the government wage and pension bill for FY1369.
caveats: This is one of several annual pay adjustments in the period (including the larger 1370/1991 pay-schedule overhaul); overall compensation trends were also driven by high inflation-linked cost-of-living pressures in the late-war/post-war years, not solely by this amendment.

law_title: قانون اصلاح قانون بودجه سال 1369 کل کشور
justification: The same amendment cut the budget line for the Consumer and Producer Protection Organization's price-differential subsidy on essential goods/foodstuffs by about 7.6 billion rials versus the amount originally approved for FY1369, a within-year reduction in the government's consumer-subsidy transfer.
caveats: A modest single line-item adjustment within one fiscal year; aggregate subsidy/transfer spending over time was driven far more by exchange-rate subsidy policy, world commodity prices, and broader price-control decisions than by this specific in-year cut.

law_title: قانون الحاق یک ماده به قانون نحوه انتشار اوراق مشارکت با اصلاحات و الحاقات بعدی
lag_description: gradual, 1-3 year lag as implementing regulations and bond issuances followed
justification: This amendment authorized all pension funds (armed-forces pension fund, Social Security Organization), government ministries/companies, municipalities, and (for a government-set share) cooperatives and private firms to convert a portion of their savings/reserve funds into government-issued 'participation bonds' (اوراق مشارکت), broadening a market-based domestic public-debt instrument used to help finance government deficits outside direct central-bank monetization.
caveats: The law only authorizes conversion of institutional reserves into bonds; it does not itself set issuance volumes, which depended on later implementing regulations and market demand. Broader public-debt and central-bank-financing trends over this period were driven mainly by oil-revenue swings and overall fiscal deficits, not this single enabling provision.

law_title: قانون متمم بودجه سال 1362 کل کشور
justification: This supplementary budget law added 50 billion rials to the FY1362 budget, with roughly 20 billion rials of the addition allocated directly to the 'expenses and obligations arising from the imposed war' line, plus additions for war-refugee affairs, roads, and ports -- a wartime increase to government spending.
caveats: A relatively small addition (50bn rials) against the much larger wartime state budget; overall government-finance trends in this period were dominated by the Iran-Iraq War's fiscal burden and oil-revenue volatility, not this specific corrective law. We hold no chart with granular FY1362 Iranian budget data, so this is a broad category-level, low-confidence flag.

law_title: اصلاحیه عبارتی قانون اصلاح برخی از ارقام و تبصره‌های قانون بودجه سال 1367 کل کشور
justification: A technical mid-year correction to the FY1367 (last year of the Iran-Iraq War) budget law, raising one spending ceiling from 160bn to 175bn rials, shifting about 38bn rials between current and development accounts, and setting a 140bn-rial minimum foreign-exchange revenue target from oil-related receipts -- routine budget bookkeeping rather than a new policy direction.
caveats: These are minor technical line-item amendments; FY1367 fiscal outcomes were dominated by the final year of the Iran-Iraq War, the 1986 oil-price collapse's lingering effects, and high wartime inflation, not by this corrective law. We hold no chart with granular FY1367 Iranian budget data, so this is a broad category-level, low-confidence flag.

law_title: قانون راجع به اصلاح ماده 15 قانون تقسیم و فروش املاک مورد اجاره به زارعین مستأجر مصوب دی‌ماه 1347
justification: This amendment only exempts rural cooperative land-transfer documents from registration fees, stamp duty and municipal levies — a narrow administrative/tax-procedure tweak to the land-reform tenant-purchase mechanism, not a change to land allocation, output, or prices.
caveats: No chart in the index tracks land-reform transaction volumes or tenant land titling; any effect would be invisible in aggregate agricultural land or output series and is swamped by other drivers. No chart mapped.

law_title: قانون تمدید مهلت مواعد مختلف مقرر در قانون تقسیم و فروش املاک مورد اجاره بزارعین مستاجر در موارد خاص
justification: Purely procedural deadline extension for land-reform tenant-purchase paperwork; does not change substantive land allocation, farm output, or prices.
caveats: No chart in the index measures land-reform titling timelines or tenant-farmer transaction counts. No chart mapped.

law_title: قانون اصلاح تبصره ماده 29 قانون آب و نحوه ملی شدن آن
justification: Reduces the engineering/appraisal fee rural cooperatives and agricultural joint-stock companies pay under the water nationalization law from 50% to 20% of cost — a narrow fee adjustment, not a water-allocation or irrigation-capacity change.
caveats: No chart isolates this fee mechanism; freshwater withdrawal and irrigated-land charts are driven by dam construction, drought, and much larger agricultural-water policy, not this fee schedule. No chart mapped.

law_title: آئین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مالیات تعاون ملی برای بازسازی مصوب 1367/12/9
justification: This is the implementing bylaw for the one-off post-Iran-Iraq-War 'National Reconstruction Cooperation Tax' — a levy on real estate, imported/luxury cars, and other assets collected via provincial tax offices; it operationalizes collection mechanics (forms, deadlines, installment terms) for a real, if temporary, tax instrument.
caveats: This is the collection bylaw, not the underlying revenue-setting law; WDI government-finance series for Iran are sparse for the late-1980s war-reconstruction period so the tax's contribution may not be separable from broader post-war fiscal reconstruction spending and inflation. Confidence kept low given data coverage uncertainty.

law_title: خروج شرکتهای توزیع نیروی برق از پیوست شماره (3) آیین‌نامه اجرایی ... اصل چهل و چهارم قانون اساسی
lag_description: same to following few years (2014 onward)
justification: Cabinet decree explicitly removes electricity distribution companies from the Article-44 privatization-eligible activity annex, meaning these utilities are reclassified as not subject to transfer to the private sector — a direct regulatory signal restricting private/PPP investment in the power-distribution segment of the energy sector.
caveats: These WDI series aggregate the whole energy sector (generation, transmission, distribution, renewables) across many projects, so the effect of excluding just electricity distribution may be diluted by opposite-direction moves elsewhere (e.g., renewable IPPs). Broader macro conditions (sanctions, subsidized tariffs deterring private entry) are also major drivers of low private energy investment in Iran independent of this decree.

law_title: تصویب‌نامه راجع به جداول حقوق ورودی پیوست بر اساس جدول نمانکلاتور جایگزین جداول پیوست آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات
justification: Replaces the customs import-duty schedule tables with a version re-coded to the updated Harmonized System (HS 2002) nomenclature. This is presented as a reclassification/recodification of tariff-line codes to match the new international nomenclature, not a stated change in duty rates.
caveats: The actual duty-rate tables are not present in the source text ('access to the appended tables is not possible'), so it cannot be confirmed whether average tariff levels rose, fell, or stayed flat through this recodification; any move in import-duty revenue or average tariff-rate charts around 2002-2003 is more plausibly explained by broader trade-liberalization steps and WTO-accession-track reforms of that era.

law_title: اتخاذ تصمیماتی در خصوص جداول پیوست آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات
justification: Cabinet decision phases in the effective date of certain already-approved rows (items 283-329) of the annual import tariff schedule, pending review by the customs working group, while items 1-282 take effect immediately.
caveats: This is purely a sequencing/administrative decision about when specific already-set tariff rows take effect; the underlying rate content is not visible in the text. Any tariff-rate or import-duty-revenue movement in 2008 is heavily confounded by that year's oil-price spike, subsidy politics, and sanctions-related trade shifts.

law_title: رأی شماره 411، 410، 409 هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری درخصوص ابطال ... بخشنامه شماره 35 مستمری‌های سازمان تأمین اجتماعی
justification: The Court of Administrative Justice struck down the cabinet decree and SSO circular No. 35 that had pro-rated annual pension cost-of-living increases by the number of months a pensioner had been on the rolls in 1380 (2001/02); the ruling enforces Article 96 of the Social Security Law, requiring the SSO to grant the full annual increase to all pensioners regardless of enrollment timing, which mechanically raises aggregate pension outlays relative to the voided partial-increase policy.
caveats: The chart series ends in 2006 so only a short post-ruling window is observable; total amounts paid are also driven by growth in the pensioner headcount and by separate annual cost-of-living adjustment decisions of the Supreme Labor Council, so the ruling's specific increment cannot be isolated from those larger trends.

law_title: رأی شماره 145 هیئت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری درخصوص اجرای مقررات اصلاحات ارضی
justification: Procedural unity-of-precedent ruling clarifying that land-reform case files closed under the 1975 law on finalizing land-reform disputes cannot be reopened via the error-correction clause (Article 38) of the 1964 land-reform bylaw; it settles a title-dispute jurisdiction question, not land allocation or output.
caveats: No chart in the index tracks land-reform title disputes or reallocated farmland; no plausible link to any held aggregate series. No chart mapped.

law_title: قانون الحاق دو تبصره به قانون تضمین خرید محصولات اساسی کشاورزی مصوب 1368
lag_description: same crop year onward (retroactive to 1383-84 crop year)
justification: Adds a binding rule that the government's guaranteed purchase price for basic agricultural crops (wheat, barley, rice, cotton, sugar beet, pulses, etc.) must rise each crop year by at least the Central Bank's announced inflation rate, applied retroactively to the 1383-84 (2004/05) crop year — a direct, documented price-floor indexation mechanism.
caveats: Guaranteed purchase prices are a floor and administrative benchmark, not necessarily identical to FAOSTAT's reported producer prices, and actual producer prices are also driven by market conditions, exchange-rate movements, and general inflation which this law's indexation formula itself references — so isolating this law's specific increment from broader inflation is difficult.

law_title: قانون مربوط به تبدیل وضع بانک سپه بصورت شرکت سهامی
lag_description: multi-year, institutional (effect visible only in later-decade aggregates)
justification: Converts the military-officers' cooperative Bank Sepah into a joint-stock company empowered to conduct full domestic and foreign banking operations, formalizing it as a full commercial/deposit bank under the Banking Law — part of the institutional buildup of Iran's mid-20th-century deposit banking sector.
caveats: This is a single institution's legal-form change nearly two years before the earliest chart snapshot (1957); the chart aggregates all deposit banks, so Bank Sepah's specific contribution cannot be isolated, and the broader growth in deposit banking through the 1950s reflects oil-revenue-driven economic expansion far more than this one bank's corporate reorganization.

law_title: قانون راجع باجازه تشکیل بانک تجارت خارجی ایران
justification: Authorizes Bank Melli Iran to convert the Export Development Bank into the Bank of Foreign Trade of Iran (Bank Tejarat Khareji), a trade-finance institution empowered to be up to 49% foreign-owned — an institutional building block of Iran's mid-century trade-finance and banking infrastructure.
caveats: Chart snapshots (June 1957-59) may predate or barely overlap this bank's actual founding (approved March 1959); as with Bank Sepah, this single institution's contribution to the aggregate deposit-bank statistics cannot be separated from overall 1950s banking-sector growth driven by oil income and general economic expansion.

law_title: قانون افزایش میزان صدور نفت خام موضوع تبصره (۲۶) قانون بودجه سال ۱۳۷۲ کل کشور (Law Raising Crude Oil Export Volume under Note 26 of the 1993 Budget Act)
justification: Single-article law explicitly raises the crude-oil export ceiling set in the 1993 budget's Note 26(a) from 190,000 to 285,000 barrels/day, a direct legislative instrument targeting oil export/production capacity.
caveats: Actual national oil output is overwhelmingly driven by OPEC production quotas, reservoir/field capacity, international sanctions, and world prices rather than a single domestic budget-note export ceiling; the ~95,000 bbl/day change is small relative to Iran's multi-million-bbl/day total production, so any effect is likely undetectable in the aggregate volume series. Correlation with any observed production change in 1997-98 is not evidence of causation.

law_title: قانون اجازه به شرکت ملی نفت ایران جهت اجرای طرحهای اکتشاف، توسعه و بهره‌برداری از میدانهای نفت و گاز دریای خزر (Law Authorizing NIOC to Explore/Develop/Produce Caspian Sea Oil and Gas Fields)
lag_description: multi-year lag (exploration and field development take years to reach production)
justification: Authorizes NIOC to contract with domestic and foreign firms for exploration, development and production in the Caspian Sea, an enabling instrument that could add to national oil/gas output if fields are commercialized.
caveats: Iran's Caspian Sea oil/gas activity has historically been constrained by unresolved maritime-boundary disputes among the five littoral states, and no major commercial Caspian field has been brought to significant production by Iran; national production/volume trends are dominated by onshore and Persian Gulf fields, OPEC quotas, and sanctions, not this enabling authorization alone.

law_title: قانون اجازه مبادله قرارداد الحاقی به قرارداد فروش نفت گاز مصوب هفتم آبان ماه ۱۳۳۳ (Law Ratifying the Supplemental Agreement to the 1954 Oil Consortium Sale Agreement)
lag_description: background/structural, effective from Jan 1964 onward, effects diffuse over following years
justification: Ratifies a supplemental agreement renegotiating discount, expensing and payment-comparison formulas within the 1954 Consortium Agreement between Iran/NIOC and the international oil consortium, adjusting the financial terms governing consortium operations and, indirectly, incentives around output levels.
caveats: This is primarily a revenue/royalty-formula amendment, not a production mandate; none of the oil-revenue or consortium-disbursement series we hold (which end in the early 1960s) cover the post-1964 period this agreement governs, so its effect cannot be directly verified against held data. Iranian oil output in this era was driven mainly by consortium offtake decisions tied to global demand, OPEC coordination, and geopolitics rather than this specific financial-terms amendment.

law_title: قانون اجازه واگذاری هفتصد هکتار اراضی منابع ملی شده بشرکت سهامی معادن مس سرچشمه کرمان (Law Authorizing the Grant of 700 Hectares of Nationalized Land to the Sarcheshmeh Copper Mines Company)
lag_description: multi-year lag (land grant enabled construction of smelter/plant facilities that came online later in the decade)
justification: Grants land at Sarcheshmeh, Rafsanjan, Bandar Abbas and Kerman free of charge specifically for constructing the factory and facilities of the Sarcheshmeh Copper Mines Company, a direct physical enabling instrument for expanding Iran's flagship copper complex (mine, concentrator and smelter).
caveats: Copper output also depended on capital investment, ore grade, technology transfer, global copper prices, and post-1979 revolutionary disruption to the mining sector; the land grant is a necessary infrastructure precondition but not the sole driver of the production trajectory shown in the chart.

law_title: قانون موافقتنامه تجارت ترجیحی بین دولت جمهوری اسلامی ایران و دولت جمهوری اسلامی پاکستان و پروتکل اصلاحی برخی مواد آن (Law Ratifying the Iran-Pakistan Preferential Trade Agreement)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag for tariff-preference effects to show up in trade flows
justification: Ratifies a bilateral preferential trade agreement with Pakistan granting reciprocal tariff concessions on a list of goods, a direct trade-policy instrument aimed at expanding Iran-Pakistan merchandise flows; we hold no Iran-Pakistan-specific trade chart, but Pakistan falls within the WDI 'South Asia' regional trade-share aggregates.
caveats: The WDI series aggregates all South Asian trading partners (India, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, etc.), not Pakistan alone, so any movement cannot be isolated to this agreement. Iran-Pakistan trade has historically been constrained by informal/border trade dominance, sanctions on formal banking channels, and security issues on the Baluchistan border, and implementation of this PTA's tariff schedule was itself partial and delayed; broader South-Asia trade-share trends are driven far more by relations with India and by exchange-rate/sanctions dynamics than by this agreement.

law_title: قانون اصلاح برخی از مواد قانون نحوه واگذاری سهام دولتی و متعلق به دولت به ایثارگران و کارگران (Law Amending Provisions of the Act on Transfer of State-Owned Shares to War Veterans and Workers)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as share transfers are processed and listed
justification: Amends the mechanics of a state-share-divestiture program (proceeds routing through Treasury, categories of eligible war-disabled/deprived-region workers, and reinvestment of proceeds into the divesting state enterprises), an incremental privatization/capital-market instrument that channels state-owned company shares toward veterans and workers, potentially adding listed shares and boosting Tehran Stock Exchange capitalization.
caveats: This is a narrow technical amendment to one of several parallel privatization channels of that era (alongside direct sales, IPOs, and later the much larger Justice Shares program from the mid-2000s); TSE listings and market capitalization are driven far more by macro conditions, inflation-driven nominal price gains, and the larger Article 44 privatization wave than by this specific amendment.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده (۵) قانون توسعه حمل و نقل عمومی و مدیریت مصرف سوخت (Executive Bylaw for Article 5 of the Law on Public Transport Development and Fuel-Consumption Management)
justification: Establishes a mandatory quarterly, cross-agency (traffic police, forensic medicine, health ministry, roads authority) road-accident data collection and reporting system under the Road Safety Commission, laying institutional groundwork that could inform later road-safety policy affecting traffic fatalities.
caveats: This bylaw, read in full, is exclusively a data-collection/reporting administrative mechanism -- it does not itself set speed limits, vehicle safety standards, enforcement levels, or road infrastructure spending. Iran's road traffic mortality trend is driven mainly by vehicle-fleet growth and age, road-infrastructure quality, enforcement intensity, fuel-price-driven driving behavior, and economic conditions, not by this reporting requirement alone; treat as background/context rather than a direct driver of the chart.

law_title: اصلاحیه ماده ۵۳ آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات سالجاری (۱۳۶۵)
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-2 year lag
justification: Raises the mandatory FX-repatriation ('پیمان ارزی') surrender rate on several export-goods categories from 75%->85% and 25%->35%, and removes a discount exemption, tightening the share of export proceeds exporters must surrender at the official rate during the Iran-Iraq war economy.
caveats: This is a wartime administrative FX-control tweak, not a price-setting law; export volumes and the official/parallel FX gap in this period were dominated by the Iran-Iraq war, oil-export disruption, and multiple-exchange-rate policy generally, so isolating this single measure's effect from those larger forces is not possible.

law_title: تصویب‌نامه راجع به تبدیل تبصره ماده ۲۸ آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (۱۳۶۸)
justification: Directs that non-oil export incentive awards ('جوایز صادراتی') be adjusted according to international prices and production costs, i.e. an explicit export-subsidy/incentive-calibration instrument meant to support non-oil export competitiveness just after the Iran-Iraq war ended.
caveats: Post-war reconstruction, exchange-rate liberalization steps of the early 1990s, and global commodity prices are much larger drivers of non-oil export value than this incentive-calibration clause; no non-oil-export-specific chart exists in the index, so this maps only to the aggregate merchandise-export series.

law_title: بخشنامه گمرک در خصوص نرخ حقوق ورودی خودرو بند (ر) تبصره (۱) قانون بودجه سال ۱۴۰۴
justification: Customs directorate circular directly instructing customs houses to keep applying the existing lower duty schedule (e.g. 4% for electric, 15% for hybrid vehicles) rather than the new 100% rate implied by budget-law clause 'ر', pending issuance of that clause's executive bylaw -- a direct tariff-administration instrument holding vehicle import duties down relative to the higher rate that would otherwise apply.
caveats: This is a narrow, short-lived administrative clarification limited to the vehicle-import tariff line and pending a not-yet-issued bylaw; it is not a general tariff reform, and Iran's aggregate applied-tariff and customs-revenue series are driven far more by sanctions-era import compression, exchange-rate swings, and broader budget-law tariff schedules than by this one circular.

law_title: دستورالعمل معاملات کالا و اوراق بهادار مبتنی بر کالا در بورس کالای ایران (۱۳۸۹)
justification: Comprehensive Securities & Exchange High Council directive establishing the auction mechanism, order types, price-discovery and clearing rules for the Iran Mercantile Exchange, formalizing a transparent price-discovery venue for commodities (agricultural goods, metals, petrochemicals) that previously traded mostly off-exchange.
caveats: This is market-microstructure plumbing, not a price-setting policy; it does not itself move commodity price levels, and any effect on measured price-index volatility/transparency is swamped by underlying supply, subsidy, and exchange-rate shocks. Mapped to the broad Capital Markets category only because IME turnover feeds into national stocks-traded/market-cap aggregates, and confidence is kept low given the indirect, structural nature of the mechanism.

law_title: تصویب‌نامه راجع به اصلاح ماده ۴۸ (احتمالاً ۴۱) آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (۱۳۷۰)
justification: Removes a requirement that a relevant ministry specifically approve imports made 'without FX transfer' (e.g. barter, self-financed, or in-kind imports), a minor procedural simplification of one import-licensing condition.
caveats: Extremely narrow procedural change to a single licensing condition within a much larger executive bylaw; post-war import liberalization, the 1991 multi-tier exchange-rate system, and broader trade-policy shifts of that period are the dominant drivers of any change in import value, not this clause.

law_title: قانون اجازه اخذ وام و اعتبارات بشرکت ملی ذوب‌آهن ایران
lag_description: 1-5 year lag (financing authorization to production ramp-up)
justification: The single-article law authorizes National Iranian Steel Company (NISCO) to obtain loans and credit facilities from domestic banks and credit institutions as approved by its supreme council, supplementing the financing for the Isfahan steel complex then under construction; the chart's production series begins in 1973, matching the plant's start-up.
caveats: The Isfahan mill's primary financing and technical construction came via a separate 1960s Soviet Union bilateral agreement, not this domestic-loan authorization; this law is a supplementary/working-capital financing instrument, so it is one contributing factor among several (state investment plans, Soviet technical assistance) rather than the sole driver of steel output growth.

law_title: قانون وصول بهای آب سازمانها و شرکت‌های تابع وزارت آب و برق
lag_description: gradual, over a decade or more
justification: The law makes water-fee contracts of state-majority irrigation companies under the Ministry of Water and Power legally enforceable like official deeds, improving fee-collection reliability; more reliable revenue for these companies plausibly supported sustained investment in and expansion of irrigation infrastructure over time.
caveats: This is a billing-enforcement/collections mechanism, not an irrigation-investment law itself; expansion of irrigated land share in this era was driven far more by dam construction, the White Revolution land reforms, and Fourth/Fifth Development Plan agricultural investment than by this collections statute, so the link is indirect and low-confidence.

law_title: قانون راجع به نحوه اداره بنیاد مسکن و معاملات واحدهای مسکونی
lag_description: immediate policy shock, effects visible over following census intervals
justification: The law dissolves the Housing Foundation's board (bringing it under ministerial control) and freezes/regulates housing-unit transfers in designated cities explicitly to balance housing supply and demand, curb speculation, moderate prices, and control unplanned urban growth -- a direct housing-market policy instrument from the immediate post-revolution period.
caveats: We hold no housing-price or transaction-volume series for this period; the only housing chart available is a low-frequency (5-year census) stock measure of housing units per household, which cannot isolate this law's short-run effect from the much larger disruptions of the 1980-88 Iran-Iraq war, revolution-era housing seizures, and displacement/refugee inflows that dominated housing outcomes in this decade. This record is included as background context rather than a demonstrated causal link.

law_title: تصویبنامه در اجرای ماده ۲۴ قانون اصلاحات ارضی مصوب دیماه ۱۳۴۰ (Cabinet decree implementing Article 24 of the Land Reform Law)
lag_description: multi-year to decade-long (land reform effects unfolded through the 1960s-70s)
justification: This 1962 cabinet decree implements Article 24 of the White Revolution Land Reform Law by setting the dispute-resolution mechanism for dividing harvests/crop shares between landlords and tenant farmers when they fail to cooperate. It is an operational/procedural instrument of the broader land-redistribution program (not the redistribution itself), which restructured Iranian agrarian relations through the 1960s and is widely studied for effects on cereal/wheat output and farm productivity.
caveats: This is a narrow implementing/procedural decree for harvest-division disputes, not the land-redistribution law itself, so any effect on aggregate output is indirect and diffuse. Iran's agricultural output in this era was also driven by weather/drought cycles, irrigation and Green Revolution input adoption (fertilizer, dam projects), rural-to-urban migration during the oil boom, and other land-reform articles/decrees not covered here. Historians dispute whether land reform raised or lowered short-run farm productivity, so direction is genuinely ambiguous.

law_title: آیین‌نامه استخراج و فروش معادن توسط بخش خصوصی (Bylaw on Extraction and Sale of Mines by the Private Sector)
lag_description: gradual, over decades (regulatory framework remained in force with amendments)
justification: This 1984 Council of Ministers bylaw (under Article 41 of the 1984 Mining Law) established the enduring auction-and-contract procedure by which the Ministry of Mines and Metals could allocate mines to private operators, with sale prices set by a ministry commission. It is a background regulatory framework that enabled private-sector participation in mining rather than a price- or output-setting instrument itself.
caveats: Almost all of our Mining & Minerals Production charts (barite, chromite, iron ore, copper, etc.) end in 1980, before this 1984 bylaw took effect, so no direct pre/post comparison is possible there. Mineral rents (% of GDP) and provincial mining-value shares are dominated by global commodity prices, exchange-rate swings, sanctions on the mining/metals sector, and state-owned enterprise dynamics far more than by this procedural auction bylaw; the link here is weak background context, not a demonstrated driver.

law_title: لايحه قانوني معافيت كارگاههاي ريسندگي و بافندگي از پرداخت هر گونه ماليات وعوارض (Exemption of spinning/weaving workshops from all taxes and duties)
lag_description: same-to-next-year, but the exemption itself was short-lived
justification: A 5-year full tax/duty exemption for small (under 50 looms) domestic spinning/weaving workshops, an industrial tax incentive meant to encourage textile manufacturing capacity.
caveats: This Mossadegh-era decree-law was annulled within about a year by the 1954 law abolishing all of Mossadegh's decree-laws, and the tax provision was later formally repealed in the 2017 tax-code consolidation, so its practical operative window was brief. The chart's 1954/55-1958/59 output growth is more plausibly driven by broader Plan Organization industrialization investment and postwar/post-coup economic stabilization than by this narrow, short-lived exemption; treat as a minor contributing factor at most.

law_title: آيين نامه نحوه تعيين و تشخيص معادن بزرگ و كوچك (Bylaw on criteria for classifying large vs. small mines)
lag_description: gradual, structural effect over subsequent years/decades
justification: Sets the criteria (reserve size, extraction capacity, investment thresholds) distinguishing 'large' from 'small' mines, which determines licensing procedure, royalty/duty regime, and regulatory burden applied to a given mining operation under the Mining Law.
caveats: This is a classification/administrative bylaw, not a price or investment instrument itself; actual mineral output and rents are driven far more by global commodity prices, sanctions on the mining/metals sector, state investment cycles, and privatization of mining firms. The provincial mining-production chart postdates this bylaw by nearly two decades, so any link is indirect and structural rather than a direct causal shock.

law_title: آيين نامه ماده 13 قانون توسعه ابزارها و نهادهاي مالي جديد (Bylaw on Article 13: professional-qualification requirements for boards/managers of securities issuers and financial institutions)
justification: Establishes minimum education/experience qualifications for board members and CEOs of securities issuers, financial institutions and self-regulatory bodies under the law facilitating Article 44 privatization policy implementation, intended to raise capital-market governance standards.
caveats: A governance/eligibility standard affects market quality and investor confidence only indirectly and slowly; Tehran Stock Exchange listing counts, capitalization and turnover in this period are dominated by sanctions cycles, currency depreciation, subsidized-share (privatization) distributions, and macro liquidity conditions, not board-qualification rules. Any observed movement in capital-market charts around this date is far more likely attributable to those larger forces.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي ماده 14 قانون توسعه ابزارها و نهادهاي مالي جديد (Executive bylaw on Article 14: violations and monetary penalties for regulated capital-market persons)
justification: Defines fines and penalty procedures for securities issuers, financial institutions and their managers who violate capital-market rules, strengthening enforcement under the Article 44 financial-instruments development law.
caveats: An enforcement/penalty schedule is a background institutional-quality measure with no direct, measurable link to aggregate market size or trading volume; capital market charts in this period are dominated by sanctions, inflation, and subsidized-share privatization waves. Confidence kept low/speculative.

law_title: اصلاحيه آيين نامه اجرايي قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Amendment to Export/Import Regulations bylaw, raising the Article 17 rial threshold)
justification: Raises a rial-denominated value threshold in Article 17 of the Export/Import Regulations bylaw from 1,000,000 to 4,400,000 rials, most likely a customs registration/exemption cutoff being adjusted for inflation.
caveats: This is a narrow, technical nominal-threshold update (roughly tracking rial depreciation/inflation over the preceding years) rather than substantive trade liberalization; it is very unlikely to have moved aggregate merchandise trade values, which are driven by oil revenue, exchange-rate policy, and global demand. Recorded at low confidence mainly for completeness.

law_title: اصلاحيه آيين نامه اجرايي قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Amendment to Export/Import Regulations bylaw, correcting Articles 15-17)
justification: A technical correction to a prior cabinet resolution amending Articles 15, 16 and 17 of the Export/Import Regulations implementing bylaw (customs procedural thresholds).
caveats: Purely administrative/procedural correction with no identifiable macro trade mechanism; aggregate import/export charts for this period are dominated by oil-price swings, exchange-rate policy and post-war reconstruction trade patterns. Recorded at low confidence mainly for completeness.

law_title: اصلاح جداول پيوست آيين نامه اجرايي قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Amendment to commodity-classification tables of the Export/Import Regulations bylaw)
justification: Amends tariff-schedule notes attached to the Export/Import Regulations bylaw, e.g. adding motorcycles to a pickup-truck classification note (chapter 87) and revising a footwear classification note (chapter 64), affecting how specific goods are tariff-classified at customs.
caveats: A narrow HS-classification correction affecting specific product lines; domestic vehicle production and import statistics in this period are driven far more by industrial-policy local-content rules, sanctions, and exchange-rate regimes than by a classification-note tweak. Confidence kept low/speculative.

law_title: اصلاح ارقام مندرج در ماده هاي 18 و 21 آيين نامه اجرايي قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Amendment raising rial figures in Articles 18 and 21 of the Export/Import Regulations bylaw)
justification: Raises rial-denominated value thresholds in Articles 18 (from 1,000,000 to 4,400,000 rials) and 21 (from 20,000,000 to 88,000,000 rials) of the Export/Import Regulations bylaw, likely customs guarantee/registration cutoffs being adjusted upward.
caveats: A nominal threshold indexation (roughly a 4.4x increase, consistent with rial depreciation/inflation over the period) rather than a substantive trade-policy change; unlikely to have a measurable, isolable effect on aggregate trade charts, which are dominated by oil revenue and exchange-rate shocks. Recorded at low confidence mainly for completeness.

law_title: دستورالعمل پذيرش اوراق بهادار در بورس اوراق بهادار تهران (Directive on admission of securities to the Tehran Stock Exchange)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag, cumulative over subsequent years
justification: Sets the formal listing-eligibility criteria (financial, disclosure and governance standards) for securities to be admitted to trading on the Tehran Stock Exchange, directly governing the pipeline of new listings and thus the exchange's breadth and depth.
caveats: Listing rules are one of several drivers of TSE size; the number of listed companies and market capitalization in Iran over this period were also heavily shaped by mass privatization share distributions (Article 44 policy), sanctions-driven capital flight/entry, and high domestic inflation pushing savers into equities as an inflation hedge. Treat as a contributing institutional factor, not the primary driver.

law_title: قانون چگونگي مصرف 15 ميليارد ريال براي هزينه‌هاي عمراني طرحهاي عشايري و 85 ميليارد ريال بقيه وام دريافتي دولت از بانك مركزي (Law on the use of a 100 billion rial loan from the Central Bank for nomadic development projects and other government spending)
justification: Directly legislates the government's drawdown of a 100 billion rial loan from the Central Bank (15 billion for nomadic/tribal development projects, 85 billion for other government uses), a documented instance of deficit financing via central-bank credit to government that directly increases government debt to the Central Bank.
caveats: This is one specific loan authorization among many similar wartime (Iran-Iraq war, which began September 1980) deficit-financing laws of the era; the broader upward trend in government debt to the Central Bank across 1978-2016 reflects cumulative decades of such borrowing, oil-revenue shortfalls, and post-revolution/war fiscal pressure, not this single law alone.

law_title: قانون اصلاح ماده (3) و جداول (4) و (8) قانون برنامه چهارم توسعه و متمم بودجه سال 1384 كل كشور
justification: Article 10 of the law explicitly resets the headline resources/expenditures totals of the FY1384 (2005/06) national budget law to a higher rial figure, and Articles 1-9 add specific supplementary allocations (upward revision of the crude-oil-revenue table, a gasoline-import subsidy top-up, funding for the wheat guaranteed-purchase-price shortfall, electricity/rural-road/education earmarks) financed from windfall oil revenue above the original budget's oil-price assumption.
caveats: This is a mid-year supplementary correction layered on an already-approved budget, not the primary annual budget law itself; the chart tracks official approved totals and may or may not distinctly incorporate such supplementary revisions depending on its data source. The underlying driver cited in the law text is a run-up in oil export revenue, not a domestic fiscal policy choice, so rising world oil prices are the more proximate cause of the upward revision.

law_title: قانون اصلاح ماده (60) و جدول شماره (2) قانون برنامه سوم توسعه و قانون بودجه سال 1382 كل كشور
justification: Note 1 added to Article 1 explicitly authorizes the government to draw on the Oil (Foreign Exchange) Reserve Account specifically 'to prevent an increase in government debt to the Central Bank' arising from the 2002/03 exchange-rate unification and to repay maturing FX loans from 1378-1381; Article 2 also revises FY1382 spending ceilings across agencies, directly touching the same debt-stock and budget-total series these charts track.
caveats: The dollar amounts authorized are modest relative to the full stock of government debt to the central bank shown in the chart; the 2002/03 exchange-rate unification itself -- not this technical budget-table amendment -- is the primary driver of the debt dynamics, and similar annual budget-amendment notes recur nearly every year, diluting the causal weight of any single instance.

law_title: قانون اصلاح قانون بودجه سال 1377 كل كشور
justification: The single-article law authorizes the government to reshuffle FY1377 (1998/99) current and development budget allocations across national/provincial agencies, issue up to 2,000 billion rial in participation (musharakah) bonds, and pre-sell oil or borrow up to US$1 billion abroad to plug a budget financing gap -- a direct fiscal-financing instrument for that year's budget.
caveats: This is an internal reallocation/financing-authorization mechanism layered on the original FY1377 budget law; the annual-budget-totals chart may not distinctly capture such a mid-year technical amendment. The 1997-98 collapse in oil prices is the actual proximate driver of the financing pressure this law responds to, not an independent policy choice to expand or contract the budget.

law_title: بخشنامه گمرک در خصوص ابلاغ احکام قانون بودجه سال 1404 کل کشور (رویه واردات)
justification: This customs circular directly implements the FY1404 (2025/26) budget law's tariff clauses: it cuts import duties to 1% on medicine, medical supplies, infant formula, basic/staple goods and livestock feed; cuts duties to 2% on production machinery, parts and industrial inputs; fixes mobile-phone import duty at 15-30% by price band; sets vehicle import duty at 100% for allocated-forex imports; and raises duties/fees on toys, cultural goods, tobacco, sugary drinks, cosmetics, and various imported agricultural commodities (grain, oilseed, cotton, tea, wood, etc.).
caveats: This is one of many annual implementing customs circulars rather than the budget law itself, so its incremental effect on any single aggregate trade or tariff-rate series is hard to isolate. Iran's aggregate merchandise-import value/volume and tariff indices (mostly WDI-sourced with a multi-year publication lag) are driven far more by sanctions exposure, exchange-rate volatility and forex-allocation policy than by this schedule of published statutory duty rates, and the direction of effect is mixed (some duties cut, others raised) depending on the good.

law_title: لایحه قانونی راجع به استفاده از مبلغ ۲۰ میلیارد ریال حاصل از قطع کمک دولت به قیمت گوشت مصرفی تهران و اصفهان جهت بهبود امر دامپروری و احیای دامپروری سنتی روستائی
lag_description: gradual, 2-5 years (funds redirected to livestock-development infrastructure/projects, not an instant output change)
justification: The decree withdraws the government's price subsidy on Tehran/Isfahan retail meat and redirects the freed ~10 billion rials from current to development (capital) budget for the Ministry of Agriculture's livestock-improvement and traditional rural husbandry programs, a real (if small) reallocation of public investment toward the livestock sector.
caveats: The sum was tiny relative to the national economy and coincided with the 1979 revolution's chaos and the September 1980 start of the Iran-Iraq war, both of which dominate any 1980s shift in livestock output far more than this single reallocation. FAOSTAT price series for Iran only start in 1991, so the retail price effect of removing the Tehran/Isfahan subsidy cannot be checked against any chart we hold.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده (۲۵) قانون رفع موانع تولید رقابت‌پذیر و ارتقای نظام مالی کشور
justification: Bylaw creates the legal basis for credit-rating agencies to rate participation bonds (اوراق مشارکت), lets the Securities & Exchange Organization set yield caps and permits below/above-par bond sales, and requires guarantors only for low-rated issues — a genuine capital-market-deepening instrument intended to widen the menu of tradable financial instruments and issuer base.
caveats: This is one bylaw within a much larger 2015 'Removing Production Obstacles' reform package; Iran's market-cap/GDP series is dominated by much larger forces (US sanctions re-imposition 2018, rial devaluation inflating nominal local-currency market cap, the 2020 retail-investor bubble and crash). Any contribution from this specific bond-rating bylaw is not separable from those larger swings and should be read as at most a minor, indirect contributor to overall capital-market development rather than to the market-cap swings themselves.

law_title: رد تصویب‌نامه شماره ۳۶۲۶ - ۱۳۴۲/۳/۶ راجع به وام شماره ۰۱۶/ه/۲۶۵ از سازمان عمران بین‌المللی آمریکا برای تأمین هزینه پروژه‌های اصلی برق ایران
lag_description: 3-7 year construction lag (loan approved 1965; generation data available 1968-72)
justification: Parliamentary ratification of a $2.4 million US foreign-aid loan explicitly earmarked to finance Iran's 'main electricity power projects' (پروژه‌های اصلی برق) — direct external financing of generation-capacity expansion whose output would show up in national electricity generation a few years after construction.
caveats: This was one loan among many financing Iran's rapid 1960s-70s electrification (also driven by oil-revenue-funded state investment, World Bank loans, and Plan Organization programs); $2.4M was a modest sum relative to total power-sector investment of the era, so its standalone contribution to the aggregate generation chart is minor and cannot be isolated from broader Pahlavi-era electrification investment.

law_title: قانون شمول قانون مجازات اخلال‌کنندگان در عرضه دام و توزیع گوشت درباره اخلال‌کنندگان در عرضه و توزیع گندم و آرد و قند و شکر و برنج و روغن
lag_description: same fiscal year to short-term (criminal-enforcement deterrent, not a production-side instrument)
justification: Extends the criminal penalties previously created for meat-supply hoarding/black-marketeering to wheat, flour, sugar, rice and cooking oil, aiming to keep staple-food distribution channels open and deter artificial shortages/price gouging during a period of high inflation from the 1974 oil boom.
caveats: This is a distribution/anti-hoarding enforcement instrument, not a producer-price or output-setting instrument, so its effect on national production quantities (the only Iran series we hold that spans 1975, since FAOSTAT price series only start in 1991) is indirect at best. The mid-1970s oil-boom inflation, land-reform aftermath, and weather were far larger determinants of wheat/rice/sugar output than this penal statute; recorded here as a weak, largely symbolic policy-context link.

law_title: قانون تشکیل سازمانهای عمران کشاورزی و توسعه دامپروری
lag_description: gradual, over a decade or more (institution-building takes years to translate into output)
justification: Authorizes the Ministry of Agriculture to establish dedicated agricultural-development and livestock-expansion organizations (with legal personality and the power to found or take stakes in companies), a genuine institution-building instrument meant to channel state capacity and investment into raising farm/livestock output.
caveats: Occurred amid the broader White Revolution land-reform program and 1960s-70s oil-revenue-financed agricultural investment; any subsequent movement in livestock production index or agricultural value added reflects that whole cluster of policies (land reform, irrigation dams, extension services, price supports) rather than this organizational law in isolation. Weather/drought cycles are also a major confound for annual agricultural output.

law_title: قانون اجازه منظور نمودن عوارض توسعه تلفن سالهای ۱۳۷۳ لغایت ۱۳۷۸ به حساب افزایش سرمایه دولت در شرکتهای مخابرات
lag_description: same-to-following fiscal years (converts already-collected 1994-1999 telecom-development fees into paid-in state capital, freeing funds for network build-out)
justification: Directs telecom-development-fee revenue collected 1994-1999 to be booked as an increase in the state's paid-in capital in the national and provincial telecom companies, a direct capital-injection mechanism intended to fund telephone-network expansion (mandated exemption from standard budget/accounting rules underscores this was a deliberate financing instrument for the sector).
caveats: Iran's fixed-line subscription growth through the 2000s was driven by many overlapping factors (broader telecom-sector liberalization, mobile-network buildout substituting for fixed lines later in the decade, general economic growth); this single capital-accounting law is one financing mechanism among several rather than the primary driver.

law_title: قانون مربوط بقرارداد فروش و خرید گاز طبیعی بین شرکت ملی گاز ایران و شرکت متالیمکس (METALIMEX) چکسلواکی
lag_description: same-to-next fiscal year, effect embedded in a multi-decade series
justification: Ratifies a specific gas sale/purchase contract between NIGC and Czechoslovakia's Metalimex, adding one export buyer to Iran's natural gas trade and marginally increasing gas-related rents.
caveats: This is one bilateral contract among many; Iran's late-1970s gas exports were dominated by the much larger IGAT-I pipeline to the USSR. The WDI 'natural gas rents % of GDP' series aggregates all domestic and export gas value and reflects world gas prices, production capacity, and the 1979 revolution/war disruptions far more than a single contract. No natural-gas-specific chart in the index covers the 1970s directly.

law_title: رد لایحه اضافه نمودن تبصره به بند 10 ماده اول قانون اصلاحی قانون اصلاحات ارضی
justification: This record is the parliamentary REJECTION of a proposed amendment to the Land Reform Law (which would have redefined small landholdings under 60 hectares as a single 'village' unit). Because the bill was voted down, it never took legal effect.
caveats: A rejected bill has no direct policy mechanism and no measurable effect on any held chart; recorded here only to document that no causal link should be inferred.

law_title: اصلاح ماده (4) آیین‌نامه اجرایی تبصره ماده (77) قانون مبارزه با قاچاق کالا و ارز
justification: Sets the commission rate (10%) paid to the state's seized-property disposal organization for selling confiscated smuggled goods — a narrow administrative fee-setting amendment.
caveats: Purely an internal administrative commission rate for asset disposal; it does not change smuggling enforcement intensity, trade volumes, or prices in any way likely to register in aggregate trade/customs charts.

law_title: اصلاح آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون نحوه اعمال تعزیرات حکومتی راجع به قاچاق کالاو ارز
justification: Amends procedural details of the seized-smuggled-goods handling bylaw (adds a footnote on perishable/hazardous goods handling and a notification requirement before sale).
caveats: A narrow procedural/administrative amendment with no rate-setting or volume-setting mechanism; not expected to move any aggregate trade, customs, or FX chart.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی تعیین قیمت و یارانه کالاها و خدمات اساسی در سال 1385
justification: Annual Economic Council bylaw fixing controlled prices and subsidy levels for essential goods and services (bread, staple foods, utilities) for FY1385, part of Iran's pre-2010 (pre-targeted-subsidy-reform) administered pricing system that directly set consumer prices for subsidized items and government subsidy outlays for that year.
caveats: The source text itself has not been digitized in the registry (only the title/metadata is available), so the exact goods and price levels set could not be verified from the primary text. CPI and wholesale price indices are driven by many other forces (global commodity prices, exchange-rate pass-through, monetary growth); subsidy transfers also reflect oil-revenue availability. Confidence limited accordingly.

law_title: رأی وحدت رویه شماره 519 دیوان عالی کشور - تجدیدنظر در دعاوی راجع به تعدیل اجاره‌بهای محل کسب و پیشه
justification: Purely a judicial-procedure ruling clarifying that first-instance court rulings on commercial-rent adjustment are appealable; it does not set rent levels, rent-adjustment formulas, or any economic rate.
caveats: No plausible mechanism for this jurisdictional/procedural ruling to move rent or housing-price data; excluded from chart mapping.

law_title: رأی وحدت رویه شماره 115 دیوان عالی کشور - دایره شمول مجازات تعطیل محل کسب فاقد پروانه در واحدهای عرضه ارزاق عمومی
justification: A criminal-procedure ruling limiting when courts (versus trade guilds) may order closure of unlicensed shops selling daily consumer goods; it does not set prices or regulate supply of consumer goods.
caveats: No economic rate-setting mechanism; not expected to move any price or consumption chart.

law_title: رأی وحدت رویه شماره 3067 دیوان عالی کشور - تلقی بزه قاچاق طلا برای هرگونه اقدام جهت خروج طلا از مملکت
justification: Clarifies that any act intended to move gold out of the country (not just its actual export) completes the crime of gold smuggling under the 1953 anti-smuggling law — a judicial interpretation broadening prosecutorial reach, not a price or capital-control rate-setting instrument.
caveats: No gold-price or capital-flight chart in the index covers 1960; any link to modern gold-coin price series (which start in 2010/2013) would be temporally and mechanistically implausible.

law_title: قانون راجع باجازه مبادله و اجرای پنج فقره قرارداد نفت
justification: Authorizes the government to exchange and execute five oil agreements (concession/service contracts with independent companies outside the Consortium), part of the mid-1960s expansion of Iran's oil-sector contracting that over time added production capacity.
caveats: Oil production and oil rents in this era were overwhelmingly driven by the Consortium's offtake decisions, OPEC dynamics, and global demand; a single authorizing law for five ancillary contracts is a minor contributing factor at best, and no chart isolates output from these specific agreements.

law_title: بخشنامه گمرک در خصوص ابلاغ احکام قانون بودجه سال 1404 کل کشور
lag_description: same fiscal year (1404/2025-26), though most held charts only run through ~2023-24 so the effect may not yet be visible in the data
justification: Customs circular directly implementing several FY1404 budget-law provisions: raises the VAT rate from 9% to 10%, sets the customs-valuation exchange rate for import duties off the Bourse/NIMA gold-and-currency-exchange rate, cuts tariffs to 1-2% on medicine, medical supplies, infant formula, essential goods and production inputs, raises duties on imported mobile phones (15%/30% tiers) and toys/cultural goods, sets a 100% duty on privileged-FX vehicle imports, and levies a new 1% fee on imported livestock feed/oilseed/sugar/rice/cotton inputs.
caveats: This is a circular for a single fiscal year within a much longer, highly volatile tariff and VAT history shaped by sanctions, currency crises, and repeated ad hoc budget-law changes; year-over-year swings in customs-duty revenue and applied tariff rates reflect many overlapping annual budget provisions, not just this circular. Most held series (WDI-based) lag several years behind 1404/2025 so the direct empirical link cannot yet be observed in the data.

law_title: قانون صندوق عمران مراتع [شرکت سهامی تهیه و تولید و توزیع علوفه]
lag_description: gradual over a decade or more
justification: Establishes a state joint-stock company (capitalized via development-budget funds, forest-land sale proceeds, and slaughter levies) tasked with producing and distributing animal fodder and improving rangelands to support livestock and poultry feed supply.
caveats: Livestock and meat production/prices are driven mainly by feed costs, drought/rangeland conditions, disease, import access, and broader agricultural policy; a single fodder-supply institution is one of many contributing inputs and its incremental effect cannot be isolated in aggregate production indices.

law_title: قانون بودجه کل سال 1322 کشور
justification: Historic WWII-era national budget law (revenue ~18.9bn rials, expenditure ~33bn rials, wartime allied-occupation context, state monopolies on grain/sugar/tea/opium/tobacco). No held chart provides Iranian government revenue/expenditure or price data reaching back to 1943.
caveats: No chart in the index covers the 1940s Iranian state budget or price data at this granularity; recorded as a no-match outcome.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي بندهاي (الف) و (ب) تبصره (18) قانون بودجه سال 1396 كل كشور
lag_description: 1-3 year lag (loan disbursement -> hiring -> registration in labor-force survey)
justification: Sets up a 200-trillion-rial subsidized-interest-rate loan facility (2 percentage-point subsidy, larger in border/deprived areas) run through the Supreme Employment Council specifically to finance job-creating projects that hire 'young workers' aged 20-35, with subsidy payment conditional on verified new Social Security registrations of young hires.
caveats: This is one of many annual active-labor-market subsidy schemes (repeated with variations almost every budget year); youth unemployment in Iran is driven far more by cohort size, overall GDP growth, sanctions cycles and university-graduate oversupply than by any single year's subsidized-credit program. Effect is impossible to isolate from macro noise and there is no guarantee the announced 200 trillion rial ceiling was fully disbursed or that jobs were sustained beyond the subsidy period.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي تبصره (35) و رديف (1) جدول شماره (3) تبصره (36) قانون اصلاح قانون بودجه سال 1395 كل كشور
lag_description: same to next fiscal year (accounting entries booked as the offsets are executed, 1395-1398)
justification: Uses the Central Bank's 'surplus account' from revaluing its net foreign-currency assets (i.e., the paper gain from rial depreciation) to write off up to ~331 trillion rials of state banks' debt to the Central Bank (overdrafts, credit lines, deposits) and to inject that same amount as fresh government capital into six state banks (Melli, Sepah, Keshavarzi, Sanat-o-Madan, Tose'e Saderat, Tose'e Ta'avon, later Maskan) -- a direct, documented instrument acting on the government/bank debt-to-central-bank stock and on the monetary base, since FX-revaluation gains being spent this way is a quasi-fiscal expansion of central bank liabilities.
caveats: State banks' debt to the central bank and the monetary base are driven by many other forces in the same years (repeated large-scale currency devaluations 1397-1401, sanctions-driven FX shortages, ordinary deficit monetization, other bank-recapitalization rounds). This single bylaw is one of several such capital-injection/write-off rounds in the period, so its isolated contribution to the aggregate series cannot be cleanly separated from the rest.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی بند (و) تبصره (5) قانون بودجه سال 1396 کل کشور
justification: Authorizes the Ministry of Economic Affairs and Finance to issue up to 50 trillion rials of 'treasury settlement bonds' (اوراق تسویه خزانه) that net the government's confirmed pre-1396 arrears to contractors/suppliers against those same parties' tax and other debts to the state, retiring recognized government arrears without a cash outlay.
caveats: This is a book-keeping netting operation of already-existing arrears rather than a change in the underlying fiscal stance; it converts one form of government liability into an offset rather than eliminating genuine fiscal deficits, and Iran's reported net government debt in this period is dominated by oil-revenue shocks, sanctions, and currency devaluation rather than this settlement mechanism.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی بندهای (الف)، (ب)، (ج) و (د) تبصره (5) قانون بودجه سال 1395 کل کشور
lag_description: same to next fiscal year (bond proceeds spent over the following 1-2 years)
justification: Authorizes issuance of up to 150 trillion rials of self-guaranteed participation bonds/sukuk by state-owned enterprises (oil, power, transport, telecom, defense, agriculture, atomic energy subsidiaries) plus government-guaranteed bonds for unfinished capital projects, adding to total public-sector debt outstanding.
caveats: This is one of several annual participation-bond authorizations repeated across many budget years at similar scale; the aggregate government/SOE debt series is shaped far more by oil-revenue swings, currency devaluation, and sanctions-driven deficits than by any single year's bond ceiling, and there is no guarantee the full authorized amount was actually issued and taken up by the market.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي بند (ز) تبصره (6) قانون بودجه سال 1394 كل كشور
justification: Same mechanism as the 1395 and 1394-series participation-bond bylaws: authorizes up to 100 trillion rials of self-guaranteed participation bonds/sukuk by state company subsidiaries (oil, power, transport, telecom, defense, agriculture, atomic energy) to fund capital projects, adding to public-sector debt outstanding.
caveats: Same as the 1395 counterpart: one of several recurring annual bond ceilings; aggregate net government debt is dominated by oil price swings, sanctions, and currency devaluation, not by any single year's bond authorization, and actual issuance/uptake versus the authorized ceiling is unverified.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي بند (ز) تبصره (5) قانون بودجه سال 1395 كل كشور
justification: Establishes the framework for issuing tax-exempt 'Islamic treasury notes' (اسناد خزانه اسلامی) that government agencies hand to contractors/suppliers in lieu of cash to settle confirmed (Article 19 Public Accounting Law) arrears, converting off-book payment obligations into a formal, tradable government security.
caveats: Converts an existing informal arrears stock into a recognized, tradable debt instrument -- this may raise measured government debt (formalization) or lower it (if it replaces higher-cost financing), and the net effect on reported aggregate debt in the WEO series is not clearly signed; other much larger drivers (oil revenue, currency devaluation, sanctions) dominate the same period.

law_title: آیین‌نامه نظام سنجش اعتبار (National Credit-Scoring System Bylaw)
lag_description: gradual over several years (system had to be built out after 2019)
justification: Establishes a formal national credit-bureau/credit-scoring regime (central data pool run by the central bank, licensed credit-scoring companies, mandatory credit reports before banks extend financing) intended to reduce information asymmetry in lending, which in principle supports expansion of private-sector credit over time.
caveats: Mechanism is indirect (better information, not more money); actual private-credit growth in Iran after 2019 was dominated by much larger forces -- double-digit inflation, sanctions-driven bank balance-sheet stress, and directed/subsidized lending policy -- so this bylaw is at most a minor contributing factor, not a primary driver. Confidence kept low.

law_title: ضوابط اجرایی بودجه سال ۱۳۸۱ کل کشور (FY1381/2002 Budget Execution Regulations)
justification: This cabinet bylaw is the executive-branch implementing regulation that operationalized disbursement, procurement and accounting procedures for the FY1381 (2002) budget already enacted by parliament -- it governs how that year's approved budget was executed, not its size or composition.
caveats: It is a procedural/administrative instrument, not a revenue or spending decision, so it does not itself explain the level or year-on-year change in the total budget figure; the underlying budget totals were set by the separate annual budget law, and any movement in that chart around FY1381 reflects oil-revenue and macro conditions of that year, not this execution bylaw.

law_title: رأی شماره ۷۲۹ الی ۷۳۷ دیوان عدالت اداری - ابطال بخشنامه‌های بانک مرکزی درباره مابه‌التفاوت نرخ ارز مرجع و مبادله‌ای بر واردکنندگان (Court ruling annulling CBI circulars charging importers the gap between reference and trading FX rates)
lag_description: same year as ruling, referencing 2012-2013 events
justification: The ruling adjudicates a dispute arising directly from Iran's 2012 multiple-exchange-rate system (official/reference vs. mobadele/trading rate) introduced after the sanctions-driven rial collapse; it annulled central bank circulars that retroactively forced importers to pay the rate differential on goods not yet cleared through customs, unless they had contractually agreed to it.
caveats: The ruling itself did not create or move the exchange-rate gap -- it is a narrow judicial remedy on retroactive application/contractual liability for a subset of importers. Any movement in the official-vs-parallel FX gap chart around 2012-2016 was driven by the sanctions shock, CBI's multi-tier currency policy, and oil-revenue loss, not by this court decision; recorded here only as background legal context for the multiple-exchange-rate episode.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مجازات استفاده‌کنندگان غیرمجاز از آب، برق، تلفن، فاضلاب و گاز
justification: This 1397 cabinet bylaw operationalizes the 1396 law penalizing unauthorized water/electricity/gas/telecom use, giving utilities concrete tools (service disconnection after unpaid bills, notarized-instrument debt collection, legal referral for illegal hookups) to curb non-technical losses from theft and illegal connections.
caveats: Technical losses (grid age, transmission distance, transformer inefficiency) dominate Iran's electricity T&D loss rate far more than illegal-connection theft; broader grid investment, urbanization, and utility enforcement capacity are stronger drivers. No isolated before/after data exists to verify this bylaw's specific contribution.

law_title: الحاق مواد (13)، (14)، (15)، (16) و (17) به آیین‌نامه ضوابط فنی واردات خودرو
justification: This 1396 cabinet decree imposed steep tiered import tariffs on passenger cars (55%-100% of value depending on engine size), banned import/registration of cars over $40,000 CFR value or with engines above 2500cc, and required official dealership/after-sales infrastructure -- a protectionist tariff and quota regime shielding domestic automakers (Iran Khodro, Saipa) from import competition.
caveats: The chart's quantitative production-count series ends in 2005; only qualitative 'milestone events' extend to 2018, so no direct production-volume verification is possible. Domestic output growth in this window was also driven by sanctions-era import scarcity, rial devaluation, and decades-old local-content mandates, making this one of several protectionist levers rather than the sole cause.

law_title: آیین‌نامه توزیع اعتبار جزء (6) ردیف (520000) قانون بودجه سال 1388 کل کشور
justification: Allocates roughly 907 billion rials of FY1388 budget credit to interest-rate subsidies and wage/loan support for job-creation programs (subsidized 'quick-return' micro-enterprise loans, cooperative-sector credit, Imam Khomeini Relief Committee employment schemes), administered by the Ministry of Labor for the express purpose of boosting employment.
caveats: This is one narrow implementing line within the much larger, widely-criticized Ahmadinejad-era 'zoodbazde' subsidized-credit expansion, which many economists argue fueled inflation more than durable job creation. Concurrent 2008-09 oil-revenue swings and other labor-market programs make isolating this single budget line's effect on employment/unemployment data speculative.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی بندهای (الف)، (ب)، (ج)، (د) و (ح) تبصره (5) و بند (و) تبصره (16) قانون بودجه سال 1396 کل کشور
justification: Authorizes issuance of up to 100 trillion rials (state-company-backed) plus 200 trillion rials (sovereign-backed) of Islamic participation bonds/sukuk to finance oil-gas, power, rail/transport, water, health and university capital projects across ministries in FY1396 -- a direct instrument expanding government market-based borrowing.
caveats: The WDI general-government liabilities series aggregates many financing channels (bank borrowing, bonds, arrears, central-bank credit); actual uptake of this specific bond ceiling versus other instruments cannot be isolated from the chart. The broader fiscal deficit, driven mainly by oil-price swings and subsidy costs, is a stronger determinant of debt trends than this one implementing bylaw.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی بند (الف) تبصره (27) قانون بودجه سال 1380 کل کشور
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-3 year lag
justification: Earmarks part of a roughly 1.38-trillion-rial FY1380 energy-conservation credit line specifically to subsidize converting public/private buses and vehicles to CNG ('gas-soz' conversion) and to electrify agricultural irrigation wells (displacing diesel pumping), directly funding a documented instrument of Iran's early-2000s natural-gas-substitution push.
caveats: The gas-consumption chart's flaring/injection/NIGC-delivery breakdown mainly reflects nationwide upstream dynamics (gas-injection-for-EOR ramp-up, petrochemical buildout) that vastly outweigh this one budget line's vehicle-conversion subsidies. Multiple successive annual budget laws, not just FY1380, continuously funded the CNG rollout through the 2000s, so attribution to this single bylaw is only partial.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی بند (الف) تبصره (27) قانون بودجه سال 1380 کل کشور
justification: The same bylaw funds bounty payments (up to 500,000-1,000,000 rials per vehicle) for converting gasoline/diesel vehicles to CNG and for retrofitting or retiring high-emission engines in seven major cities, mechanically displacing some gasoline and gas-oil demand.
caveats: National gasoline/gas-oil consumption in this period was dominated by rapidly rising vehicle ownership, population growth, and heavily subsidized retail fuel prices that pushed aggregate consumption upward despite conversion programs, so any substitution effect from this single, geographically limited budget line is likely swamped by those larger forces.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي قانون نحوه واگذاري سهام دولتي و متعلق به دولت به ايثارگران و كارگران
justification: This is the operative bylaw implementing the 1373 law that mandated transferring 33% (workers) + 10% (employees) of shares in state-owned enterprises undergoing privatization to veterans, war-disabled, and factory workers, and it sets the base-pricing and share-transfer procedures used in early privatization divestitures, which is one of several channels that grew the pool of listed/transferred companies over time.
caveats: Most of these transfers happened via direct cooperative allocation rather than public listing, so the link to aggregate stock-market-capitalization or listed-company counts is indirect; broader privatization waves (esp. post-2005 Article 44 policy), oil revenue cycles, and macro liquidity conditions are much larger drivers of these series.

law_title: ضوابط محاسبه سهام شناور آزاد موضوع ماده (6) قانون توسعه ابزارها و نهادهاي مالي جديد به منظور تسهيل اجراي سياست‌هاي كلي اصل 44 قانون اساسي
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-3 year lag
justification: Sets the formal methodology the Iranian stock exchange organization uses to compute free-float shares for listed companies, which is directly tied to a corporate tax exemption for free-float above certain thresholds; this created a documented incentive for issuers to raise their free-float ratio, which mechanically raises tradable share supply and turnover on the Tehran Stock Exchange.
