caveats: Turnover and traded value on TSE are dominated by broader factors -- inflation-driven equity demand, exchange-rate expectations, sanctions-era capital flight into equities, and IPO/privatization waves -- so this technical rule is at most a minor contributing factor, not a primary driver.

law_title: قانون بيمه بازنشستگي، فوت وازكارافتادگي بافندگان قالي، قاليچه گليم و زيلو
lag_description: 1-3 year lag (enrollment and eventual pension claims build gradually)
justification: Mandates the Social Security Organization to offer voluntary pension/disability/survivor insurance to home-based carpet weavers with no employer, funded partly by a 1.5% levy on carpet-export value; this is one of several occupational-group voluntary-insurance schemes that incrementally added pensioners/beneficiaries to SSO's rolls in the category breakdown.
caveats: Carpet weavers are a small occupational category relative to total SSO enrollment; the chart's overall trend in pensioners and amounts paid is driven mainly by the general mandatory scheme (formal-sector employment growth, other voluntary-insurance laws, inflation-driven benefit indexation), not this narrow program.

law_title: قانون پیشگیری از عوارض ناشی از خشکسالی و جبران خسارات (Law on Preventing Drought Damages and Compensation)
justification: Directs ~4,000 billion rials (5% of national development credits + levies on state enterprise/bank operating income) to the Drought Headquarters, with 2,000bn earmarked as Agricultural Bank capital injection for subsidized loans for water/soil recovery projects, livestock/poultry restocking, seed and greenhouse cultivation, and 2,000bn for crop insurance fund recapitalization, livestock loss compensation and emergency drinking water — a direct fiscal instrument aimed at propping up agricultural output and livestock herds after the severe 1999-2001 drought.
caveats: Actual cereal/livestock output and yield are dominated by rainfall variability itself, irrigation investment, guaranteed procurement prices, input costs and sanctions-era input access; this one-off relief package is a small, temporary offset against the drought's own depressive effect, not a standalone driver — the WDI drought-exposure series only reports a single cross-sectional average (1990-2009) so it cannot show the law's effect over time.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی بند (15) تبصره (2) قانون بودجه سال 1385 کل کشور (Implementing Bylaw for Budget Clause 1385 — Livestock Fattening & Fish-meal Working Capital)
justification: Channels up to 3,741 billion rials through Bank Keshavarzi as subsidized working-capital loans (2.5 points below the policy rate) for cattle/livestock fattening units and fishing vessels/plants supplying fish-meal and fish-oil producers, explicitly to raise red-meat and fish-meal output.
caveats: This is a single fiscal-year (1385) credit line; livestock and meat production series are far more shaped by feed-grain import costs, exchange-rate pass-through, disease outbreaks and demand trends than by one year's subsidized loan program, and no chart in the index isolates fish-meal output specifically.

law_title: آیین‌نامه شماره 81: مقررات تعیین حق بیمه انواع رشته‌های بیمه‌ای (Regulation No. 81 — Rules for Setting Premiums Across All Insurance Lines)
lag_description: same year onward, effects visible over subsequent years
justification: Supreme Insurance Council directive requiring every insurer to set premiums via an actuarial committee, mandating loss-ratio bands (health 50-85%, other lines 40-75%) and technical rating factors per line (fire, engineering, marine, aviation, cargo, auto, medical malpractice) — this is the binding framework governing how premium levels and loss ratios are formed across the whole Iranian insurance market from 2013 onward, replacing the 2009-2011 tariff-reform decisions it explicitly repeals.
caveats: Premium levels and claims ratios are also driven by inflation/exchange-rate pass-through on repair and medical costs, market competition, and macro insurance penetration; direction of premium change (up or down) depends on which line and which technical factor moved, so no single directional effect can be claimed market-wide.

law_title: الحاق تبصره به بند (2) ماده (10) آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Addendum to Import/Export Regulations Bylaw — Customs Registration + Tariff Classification Consolidation)
justification: Beyond the narrow customs-registration fix for goods of licensed production units stuck in clearance, the same instrument obliges the Ministry of Industry, Mine and Trade to cut the number of tariff-schedule classification tiers from 14 to 8 over 1393-1396, a genuine tariff-structure simplification with potential to compress or reshuffle effective tariff rates.
caveats: 1393-1396 (2014-2017) is dominated by the JCPOA sanctions-relief shock (Jan 2016) which swamps any tariff-classification effect on import volumes or tariff-rate series; cannot isolate this bylaw's contribution from that far larger driver.

law_title: اصلاح ماده (11) آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون مقررات صادرات و واردات (Amendment to Article 11 — Import Declaration Clearance Deadlines by Shipping Mode)
justification: Sets/updates maximum days allowed to finalize an import declaration (ثبت سفارش) after bill-of-lading issuance by transport mode (air 10 days; sea from Persian Gulf/neighboring countries 20 days; other countries by land 40 / by sea 55 days) — a procedural streamlining of customs clearance timing.
caveats: Purely a processing-deadline rule, not a tariff or quota change; any effect on import volumes or customs efficiency indices is second-order and is overwhelmed by the concurrent 2015-16 JCPOA sanctions-relief trade surge, exchange-rate movements, and global oil-price collapse affecting Iran's import capacity in the same window.

law_title: قانون اجازه تحصیل وام از بانک صادرات و واردات واشنگتن جهت رفع نیازمندیهای شرکت هواپیمائی ملی ایران (Law Authorizing an Exim Bank Washington Loan for Iran Air's Needs — 2 Boeing 727s)
lag_description: gradual, over subsequent years as jets entered service (from 1967)
justification: Authorizes a $9.76 million US Exim Bank credit (80% of a $12.2m purchase) financing two Boeing 727 jets plus spares for Iran Air, directly expanding national airline capacity.
caveats: A two-aircraft addition is a negligible share of Iran's aggregate air-transport growth, which in available charts is driven far more by decades of broader fleet expansion, tourism/business travel growth, and infrastructure investment; WDI air-transport series typically do not extend back to the mid-1960s, so no usable data overlap likely exists to test this link.

law_title: Supplementary Budget Law for FY1361/1982
justification: Note 6 adds 240 billion rials to budget line 710100 (use of banking-system credit or bond issuance) to close the FY1361 deficit, and Note 7 authorizes pre-selling oil-export receivables to the Central Bank for early rial financing -- direct deficit-monetization instruments during the Iran-Iraq War.
caveats: One of several wartime supplementary budgets (1360-1367); the Iran-Iraq War itself, not this specific law, is the underlying driver of both the deficit and the debt monetization, and oil-price/export swings over the war years are a major confound.

law_title: Supplementary Budget Law for FY1361/1982
justification: Note 1 adds 145 billion rials to budget line 503041 (war-imposed expenses) and reallocates funds within it toward reconstruction of war-affected areas, formally appropriating wartime military/defense-related spending for FY1361.
caveats: The law is a budgetary bookkeeping instrument ratifying spending already driven by active war operations against Iraq; it does not independently cause higher military expenditure, and WDI's cross-country series may not cleanly capture Iranian rial figures amid the era's exchange-rate distortions.

law_title: CNG/dual-fuel vehicle conversion program bylaw, FY1384 (band th, tabsare 11)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag (station construction and fleet conversion take time to materialize)
justification: Mandates NIOC's Fuel Consumption Optimization Organization to build 400 CNG stations by end-1384, subsidizes private CNG-station construction, funds conversion of at least 50,000 existing vehicles and targets 200,000 new dual-fuel vehicles/year, and cuts import tariffs on dual-fuel vehicles and CNG equipment -- a concrete, named instrument to shift transport fuel demand toward natural gas.
caveats: The bulk of Iran's CNG-vehicle boom actually took off after 2007 alongside the Targeted Subsidies Reform's steep gasoline price hikes, a separate and larger driver; the chart's window (1370-1385) only captures the program's first year or two, and gas consumption growth also reflects unrelated industrial/residential network expansion.

law_title: CNG/dual-fuel vehicle conversion program bylaw, FY1384 (band th, tabsare 11)
justification: Note 1 explicitly ties the program to reducing gasoline (motor-spirit) import volumes, paying automakers per converted vehicle in proportion to the resulting import savings -- a stated policy goal of substituting CNG for imported gasoline.
caveats: Gasoline-import levels in this period were driven at least as much by refining capacity constraints and subsidized domestic pump prices encouraging consumption; the CNG program's import-reducing effect was only beginning to show within this chart's 1370-1385 window.

law_title: Job-creation subsidized-credit program bylaw, FY1398 (band alef, tabsare 18)
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-2 year lag
justification: Channels roughly 250 trillion rials (National Development Fund loans, subsidized 'administered funds', interest-rate subsidies, and grants) through 13 ministries/agencies to fund job-creation and job-retention projects across industry, agriculture, tourism, ICT and welfare-target groups for FY1398.
caveats: One of many overlapping annual subsidized-credit employment schemes; FY1398-1399 also saw a severe sanctions-driven recession and the onset of COVID-19 the following year, both of which dominate labor-market outcomes far more than this single credit facility.

law_title: Oil/gas revenue-sharing & domestic fuel price schedule bylaw, FY1392 (band 3)
justification: Sets the official ex-refinery/pump price schedule (both subsidized-tier and 'free'-tier prices) for gasoline, gasoil, kerosene, LPG and aviation fuel for FY1392, and fixes NIOC's revenue-share/treasury transfer formula -- domestic fuel prices are a direct input to petroleum-product demand and the sectoral energy balance.
caveats: One of many near-identical annual budget-clause bylaws issued every fiscal year in this series; consumption in 1392 was also shaped by the 2012-13 sanctions shock, rial depreciation, and the ongoing multi-year Targeted Subsidies Reform trajectory.

law_title: Oil/gas revenue-sharing & power-plant fuel pricing bylaw, FY1387 (band 7)
justification: Fixes the 'free-market' price of natural gas and liquid fuel consumed by power plants for FY1387 and the associated NIOC oil/gas revenue-accounting formula -- a direct input to power-sector fuel choice and reported domestic petroleum-product consumption for that year.
caveats: One of a recurring annual series of near-identical implementing bylaws; power-plant fuel mix and overall consumption in 1387 were also driven by electricity demand growth, refinery constraints, and macro conditions well beyond this single pricing clause.

law_title: Oil-windfall reallocation amending Fourth Plan tables 4 & 8 / FY1385 budget
justification: Item 15 earmarks $80 million from the oil Foreign Exchange Reserve Account specifically for rural natural-gas distribution, and item 2 earmarks $150 million toward replacing aging buses/taxis with CNG dual-fuel vehicles, both feeding directly into natural gas consumption.
caveats: One line item within a large, diffuse multi-sector oil-windfall spending package (urban rail, roads, hospitals, universities, water, industrial zones, mining, border security, etc.) enabled chiefly by the mid-2000s oil-price boom itself; the chart's window ends the same year (1385) the funds were appropriated, before most build-out could show up in consumption data.

law_title: قانون دریافت عوارض خروج از کشور (Law on Collection of Country-Exit Fees)
lag_description: same fiscal year, ongoing while fee remains in force
justification: Imposes a mandatory per-passenger exit fee (50,000 rial first trip, 100,000 rial subsequent trips per year in 1985 terms, later indexed upward) on every Iranian passport holder leaving the country, collected as general government revenue. As a direct marginal cost added to every outbound trip, it is a plausible small contributing factor to Iranian outbound travel volume and spending, which feeds into WDI's international tourism expenditure series (expenditure by outbound residents).
caveats: Effect is likely marginal: many traveler categories are exempted (pilgrims at 50% discount, medical patients, students, workers, children under 7, border residents), and the dominant drivers of outbound Iranian travel/expenditure are exchange-rate movements, foreign-currency access restrictions, sanctions on international payment/card networks, and income levels, not this fixed fee. The fee has also been repeatedly amended and its real value eroded by inflation, further diluting any lasting behavioral effect.

law_title: آیین‌نامه ساماندهی خوشه‌ها و شبکه‌های صادرات‌گرای کسب و کار (Bylaw Organizing Export-Oriented Business Clusters and Networks)
justification: Establishes an institutional framework (definitions, government-agency roles, support mechanisms) to organize SME export clusters and networks with the stated aim of boosting non-oil export capacity. As an export-promotion support program, it is a plausible minor contributing input to aggregate merchandise export performance over time.
caveats: This is a soft institutional/organizational bylaw with no direct subsidy amounts, tariff changes, or export targets attached, so its measurable effect on national export totals is diffuse and hard to isolate. Iran's merchandise export trajectory over this period is overwhelmingly driven by oil prices, international sanctions regimes, and the exchange rate, which dwarf any cluster-support program's contribution.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی قانون الحاق یک ماده به قانون تنظیم بخشی از مقررات مالی دولت (1384) — Executive Bylaw on Well-Drilling Permits and Compensatory Fees in Banned/Restricted Aquifer Plains
justification: Sets procedures and mandatory 'compensatory fees' for regional water companies to issue well-drilling and water-extraction permits in officially banned/restricted groundwater plains (dasht-e mamnoe), requires closure of unauthorized wells, and directs revenue toward aquifer-recharge and water-balancing projects. As a regulatory instrument aimed at managing/limiting new groundwater extraction, it is a plausible small contributing factor toward moderating agricultural freshwater withdrawal and water stress trends.
caveats: Iran's groundwater depletion crisis over this period is driven mainly by decades of unregulated/illegal well drilling, drought, and weak enforcement capacity of regional water companies — this bylaw is a narrow administrative permitting mechanism, not a binding extraction cap, and its real-world enforcement has been inconsistent, so any measurable dampening effect on national freshwater-withdrawal or irrigation-land aggregates is speculative.

law_title: دستورالعمل اجرايي ماده (5) آيين‌نامه اجرايي تبصره (13) قانون بودجه سال 1385 كل كشور — Old-Vehicle Scrappage/Replacement Program
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-3 year lag (scheme ran 2006-2010)
justification: This 2006 implementing directive sets up a nationwide scrappage scheme: owners of vehicles older than fixed age thresholds surrender them to be scrapped in exchange for a new dual-fuel/CNG vehicle, financed via bank-loan interest subsidies and customs-duty discounts channeled through automaker 'کارگزار' representatives (Iran Khodro, Saipa) — a direct demand-stimulus instrument aimed at domestic vehicle output.
caveats: Vehicle production is also driven by general consumer demand, credit conditions, import-substitution pressure from sanctions, and currency depreciation; the scrappage program was one of several concurrent demand levers on the industry, and the correlation with any single year of the production series cannot be cleanly isolated from these other factors.

law_title: آيين‌نامه اجرايي بند (هـ) تبصره (42) قانون بودجه سال 1380 كل كشور — Import Surcharge on Electronic Components to Fund Electronics Industry Support
lag_description: same fiscal year (1380/2001-02); effect concentrated in the surcharge's active period
justification: This 2001 bylaw imposes a 3-12% customs surcharge on a defined list of imported electronic components and parts (TV/radio/telecom parts, capacitors, semiconductors, printed circuits, etc.), with proceeds earmarked to capitalize an 'Electronics Industry Support Fund' at the Ministry of Industries — a direct tariff-raising instrument protecting/funding domestic electronics assembly.
caveats: WDI tariff-rate and import-duty series average across all tariff lines and all years, so one narrow product-list surcharge is only a small contributor to the aggregate; electronics import volumes are also driven far more by exchange-rate swings, sanctions, and broader trade policy than by this single measure.

law_title: آيين‌نامه اجرايي تبصره 4 قانون بودجه سال 1375 كل كشور — Subsidized Agricultural Working-Capital Credit via Bank Keshavarzi
lag_description: same-year disbursement; output effects typically lag about one growing season (roughly 1 year)
justification: This 1996 bylaw channels up to 1,300 billion rials in subsidized (interest-free qarz-al-hasaneh plus below-market bank) working-capital credit through Bank Keshavarzi to farm cooperatives, rural producers, carpet weavers, and agri-input distributors, earmarked specifically for purchasing seed, fertilizer, animal feed, veterinary supplies, and farm machinery — a direct agricultural-input-subsidy instrument intended to raise output.
caveats: This is a routine annual re-authorization of a recurring credit facility (the 'tabsare 4' program repeated nearly every budget year in this era) rather than a unique policy shift, so its incremental effect on any single crop/livestock series is hard to isolate from weather, irrigation water availability, guaranteed-purchase prices, and world commodity prices, which are larger drivers across the many production series in this category.

law_title: آيين‌نامه اجرايي تبصره 4 قانون بودجه سال 1374كل كشور — Subsidized Agricultural Working-Capital Credit via Bank Keshavarzi (prior-year version)
lag_description: same-year disbursement; output effects typically lag about one growing season (roughly 1 year)
justification: The direct predecessor of the 1375 bylaw: this 1995 implementing regulation channels up to 1,300 billion rials in subsidized qarz-al-hasaneh and other bank credit through Bank Keshavarzi to agricultural, rural, pastoral, and fishery cooperatives specifically for buying seed, fertilizer, pesticides, feed, veterinary drugs, and farm/irrigation equipment — a direct input-subsidy instrument intended to raise farm output.
caveats: Like its 1375 successor, this is a routine annual re-authorization of a recurring credit line rather than a distinct new policy, so its marginal effect on any single crop/livestock series cannot be cleanly separated from weather, water availability, guaranteed-purchase prices, and global commodity prices.

law_title: آيين‌نامه اجرايي بند (ب) تبصره (12) قانون بودجه سال 1394 كل كشور — Subsidized/Venture Credit Fund for ICT Knowledge-Based and Startup Firms
lag_description: gradual, multi-year (venture investments take years to mature into registered/growing firms)
justification: This 2015 bylaw creates an 'administered funds' facility, financed from ICT-sector state-company profits and disbursed via a designated bank, offering subsidized loans and risk (venture) capital to cooperative- and private-sector ICT/knowledge-based and startup companies for software development, R&D-to-prototype support, and export capacity — a targeted instrument to expand business formation and investment in the sector.
caveats: The fund was small relative to the whole economy and its budget authorization applied only to FY1394 (briefly extended into FY1395); WDI new-business-registration trends are driven far more by macro conditions (inflation, sanctions, general ease-of-doing-business reforms) than by this single sector-specific credit line, and no ICT-specific business-formation chart exists in the index to link more precisely.

law_title: ضوابط اجرايي بودجه سال 1377 كل كشور و لغو چند تصويبنامه‌ي مربوط به اين موضوع — FY1998 Government Austerity/Administrative Rules
justification: This 1998 decree freezes government hiring and new administrative posts, caps overtime pay at prior-year (1376) levels and 120 hours, and caps agency fuel/water/electricity/telephone consumption at 1376 levels — a direct instrument aimed at holding down government compensation and operating expenditure.
caveats: This is one of many near-identical annual budget-implementation austerity circulars issued almost every fiscal year in this period, so it is not a distinctive one-off policy; WDI government-finance series for Iran have sparse/inconsistent coverage in the late 1990s, and aggregate compensation and goods-and-services expense move far more with inflation, oil-revenue swings, and broader fiscal policy than with this single administrative circular.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اکتشاف معادن و جایزه کشف (Mineral Exploration Procedure and Discovery-Reward Bylaw)
lag_description: multi-decade (framework persists in background; visible effects, if any, only in recent data)
justification: Established the standard administrative procedure (exploration permits, principle agreements, discovery-reward payments under the 1983 Mining Law) that has underpinned Iran's mineral-exploration incentive system since 1984; this basic framework, though repeatedly amended, still shapes how new mineral deposits are found and brought to production.
caveats: 34+ year gap to the only post-1984 mining chart we hold (a 2018-2023 provincial value snapshot); the exploration bylaw itself was substantially superseded by later mining-law revisions (1998, 2013), so any link to modern production value is far more plausibly driven by commodity prices, sanctions-driven import substitution, and provincial industrial policy than by this specific 1984 procedural bylaw. No chart in our index actually overlaps this law's own era (all 1961-1980 mining production series end before the law existed).

law_title: آیین‌نامه ایجاد بازار بهینه‌سازی انرژی و محیط‌زیست (Energy Optimization & Environment Market Bylaw)
lag_description: gradual, multi-year (certificate market took years to become active; effects if any would show mid-2020s onward)
justification: Creates a tradable 'energy-saving certificate' market (گواهی صرفه‌جویی انرژی) under the 2011 Energy Consumption Pattern Reform Law, letting energy suppliers and large consumers monetize verified efficiency gains — a market-based instrument explicitly aimed at lowering the energy intensity of the economy.
caveats: The certificate market has historically had very low trading volume relative to the scale of the economy, so its measurable effect on aggregate energy intensity is likely small; energy intensity in Iran is dominated by heavily subsidized domestic energy prices, industrial structure, and sanctions-driven inefficiencies, which swamp this niche instrument. Confidence kept low because we cannot confirm the market achieved meaningful trading activity.

law_title: آیین‌نامه ایجاد بازار بهینه‌سازی انرژی و محیط‌زیست (Energy Optimization & Environment Market Bylaw)
justification: Same energy-efficiency certificate market instrument; if effective, it should raise GDP output per unit of energy consumed by rewarding verified energy savings by industrial and commercial consumers.
caveats: Same as above: thin market participation, and GDP-per-energy-unit trends are far more strongly driven by currency devaluation/PPP conversion effects, sanctions-induced industrial contraction, and subsidized fuel pricing than by this niche certificate scheme.

law_title: قانون موافقتنامه تشویق و حمایت متقابل از سرمایه‌گذاری بین ایران و قطر (Iran-Qatar Reciprocal Investment Promotion & Protection Agreement)
lag_description: 1-5 year lag, cumulative with other BITs
justification: A bilateral investment treaty guaranteeing expropriation protection, free capital repatriation, and dispute arbitration for Qatari investors in Iran (and vice versa) — a standard instrument used to lower the perceived risk of inbound FDI and thereby support net FDI inflows.
caveats: This is one of dozens of similar BITs Iran signed in the 2000s as part of a broader legal push (alongside the 2002 Foreign Investment Promotion and Protection Act); isolating this single treaty's marginal contribution to aggregate FDI inflows is not credible. Iran's aggregate FDI series is dominated by oil-sector buyback contracts, sanctions cycles, and currency risk, not bilateral treaty coverage with a single Gulf partner.

law_title: قانون موافقتنامه تشویق و حمایت متقابل از سرمایه‌گذاری بین ایران و تونس (Iran-Tunisia Reciprocal Investment Promotion & Protection Agreement)
lag_description: 1-5 year lag, cumulative with other BITs
justification: Same type of bilateral investment-protection instrument (expropriation guarantees, capital-transfer rights, arbitration) as the Qatar agreement, this time with Tunisia, intended to encourage two-way investment flows.
caveats: Tunisia is a minor investment partner for Iran with negligible bilateral capital flows historically; this treaty's traceable effect on Iran's aggregate FDI series is speculative at best and confidence is kept lower than the Qatar treaty for that reason. Aggregate FDI trends are overwhelmingly driven by oil policy and sanctions, not this treaty.

law_title: قانون موافقتنامه اجتناب از اخذ مالیات مضاعف بین ایران و ارمنستان (Iran-Armenia Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement)
justification: Removes double taxation of cross-border income between Iran and Armenia, which in principle lowers the effective tax cost of bilateral investment and can support small increases in cross-border capital flows.
caveats: Armenia is a very small trading/investment partner for Iran; there is no country-specific FDI chart in our index to isolate this effect, and any traceable movement in Iran's aggregate FDI series is far more plausibly explained by oil-sector cycles, sanctions, and larger investment partners. This is a background/context link, not a demonstrable driver.

law_title: قانون موافقتنامه همکاری‌های تجاری اکو (ECO Trade Cooperation Agreement)
lag_description: gradual, multi-year (regional tariff preferences phased in over subsequent years)
justification: A regional trade-liberalization framework among Economic Cooperation Organization members (Iran, Turkey, Pakistan, Afghanistan, and Central Asian states) intended to reduce tariff and non-tariff barriers and expand intra-regional trade, which should support Iran's overall merchandise export/import volumes with these neighbors over time.
caveats: Intra-ECO trade has historically remained a small share of Iran's total trade due to persistent non-tariff barriers, weak transport/logistics links, and slow ratification of tariff schedules by member states; Iran's aggregate merchandise trade series is dominated by oil exports, sanctions cycles, and exchange-rate shocks, so this agreement's standalone contribution is modest and hard to isolate from other trade-policy and macro shocks over the same period.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي بند (الف) تبصره (20) قانون بودجه سال 1383 كل كشور
lag_description: 1-3 year lag (loan disbursement to harvest/processing upgrade)
chart_ids: faostat__Green tea (not fermented), black tea (fermented) and partly fermented tea, in immediate packings of a content not exceeding 3 kg__production
justification: This bylaw operationalizes a FY1383 budget earmark creating subsidized 'managed funds' loans (7% grower equity, 93% subsidized bank credit, with interest/fee subsidy) through the Agriculture Bank specifically to expand canola/safflower cultivation, modernize tea-processing factories to improve green-leaf quality, and standardize/upgrade export-grade horticultural (dried fruit/nut) products.
caveats: This is one year's earmarked credit tranche among many recurring agricultural support programs; oilseed and tea output are driven mainly by rainfall/irrigation, world commodity prices, land-use decisions, and concurrent guaranteed-price policies, so isolating this single bylaw's contribution from the production trend is not possible.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي ماده (11) قانون تشكيل وزارت جهاد كشاورزي - مصوب 1379
justification: Establishes standing national and provincial 'Agricultural Water' coordination task forces (Energy + Agriculture Ministries) to prioritize, allocate and move toward volumetric delivery of irrigation water and to modernize irrigation/drainage networks — the institutional machinery behind Iran's irrigated-area and agricultural water-use figures.
caveats: This is a coordination/institutional bylaw, not a resource-allocation or investment instrument in itself; the share of land under irrigation and agriculture's share of freshwater withdrawal are dominated by multi-decade dam/canal investment cycles, drought years, and groundwater over-extraction, not by this administrative coordination structure.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي بند (د) ماده (32) قانون برنامه پنجساله پنجم توسعه جمهوري اسلامي ايران
lag_description: 1-3 year lag, reinforced by the 2014 Health Transformation Plan that followed
justification: This bylaw implementing the Fifth Development Plan's health-system article explicitly mandates (Article 4, tabsare) that by the end of the plan period, patients' direct out-of-pocket payment share of total health spending must fall to a maximum of 30%, via consolidating basic health-insurance funds into one purchaser and rolling out the family-physician/referral system.
caveats: The larger, better-known driver of Iran's out-of-pocket decline in this period was the 2014 Health Transformation Plan (طرح تحول نظام سلامت), a separate and much bigger initiative that came right after this bylaw; several overlapping insurance reforms share credit, and out-of-pocket share also moves with subsidy-reform-driven inflation and household income shocks unrelated to this instrument.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي بند (د) ماده (32) قانون برنامه پنجساله پنجم توسعه جمهوري اسلامي ايران
justification: The same bylaw directs general-budget health line items, government-subsidized insurance premiums for low-income households, and new health-harming-goods duties (Article 7) into the basic health-insurance pool, which would tend to raise government/insurance funding's share of current health expenditure and total health spending as a share of GDP.
caveats: Government health-expenditure share and its GDP share are also strongly affected by oil-revenue cycles, rial depreciation (which moves the GDP denominator), and the much larger 2014 Health Transformation Plan that followed; attributing movement in these aggregates to this specific bylaw alone is weak.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي بند (ث) تبصره (12) قانون بودجه سال 1383 كل كشور
lag_description: gradual over 1-2 decades (CNG rollout continued for years after this bylaw)
justification: This bylaw launched the first phase of Iran's CNG vehicle-conversion program: it mandated CNG refueling-station construction across ~30 cities/corridors in 1383, required a combined 135,000 new dual-fuel (gasoline/CNG) taxis and private cars to be produced that year, required all newly purchased government (including military) vehicles and new urban public-transit vehicles in CNG-served cities to be dual-fuel or gas-capable, and mandated annual technical inspection for gas-converted vehicles — the opening instrument behind Iran later becoming the world's largest CNG-vehicle fleet, substituting gasoline demand with natural gas.
caveats: The available series only partly overlap the multi-year rollout (the natural-gas series ends 1385/2006, just two years after the bylaw), and the CNG program continued expanding for a decade afterward via many follow-on decrees; gasoline and gas consumption are also driven by heavily subsidized pump prices, population and vehicle-fleet growth, and later gasoline-rationing/price-reform laws, so isolating this single bylaw's share of the substitution effect is not possible.

law_title: ضوابط اجرايي بودجه جاري سال 1378 كل كشور
justification: This annual budget-execution circular freezes new administrative posts and hiring, caps overtime and business-travel days at the prior year's level, caps fuel/water/electricity/telephone consumption at FY1377 levels, and bans new spending on recreational/welfare facilities for FY1378 — administrative cost-control measures that would nominally bear on compensation-of-employees and goods-and-services government expenditure.
caveats: This is a routine annual budget-discipline circular reissued with near-identical language almost every fiscal year; national compensation-of-employees and goods-and-services expenditure aggregates are dominated by inflation, exchange-rate shifts, and multi-year wage-law changes far larger than one year's administrative austerity circular, so no distinguishable effect from this specific bylaw should be expected in the annual data.

law_title: آئين نامه اجرايي تبصره (4) قانون بودجه سال 1370 كل كشور
lag_description: same-year to 1-year lag (working-capital credit for a single crop year)
justification: This bylaw channels a 400-billion-rial pool of subsidized (Qarz-ol-Hasaneh plus other bank) working-capital credit through the Agriculture Bank to farmers, fishing/livestock/poultry/beekeeping cooperatives and their unions, explicitly earmarked for wheat, feed grains, animal protein (fisheries/livestock/poultry/dairy), rice, legumes, cotton, sugar, potatoes, oilseeds, sugar beet and silk production, with the stated aim (Article 1) of raising sector output under the First Five-Year Development Plan.
caveats: This is one year's subsidized-credit tranche among many concurrent and recurring agricultural support instruments (guaranteed prices, input subsidies, irrigation investment); output for these commodities is also heavily driven by weather/rainfall, water availability, and world price movements, so the working-capital credit's separate contribution to any single crop's production trend cannot be isolated.

law_title: آیین نامه اجرایی بند (خ) تبصره (19) قانون بودجه سال 1382 کل کشور
justification: The bylaw created a specific wheat-import-for-flour-export mechanism: private flour mills could import wheat outside the state grain monopoly on condition they export an equivalent volume of flour within the fiscal year, administered via the state Grain Organization with sourcing, quality-certificate, and customs-delivery rules spelled out in the text.
caveats: FAOSTAT's aggregate wheat-and-products trade figures are dominated by the much larger state wheat import/subsidy system, domestic harvest size (drought years), and world wheat prices; this bylaw only affects a specific private mill-based sub-channel, simultaneously pushing up both wheat imports and flour exports, so a clean net-direction read is not possible from this instrument alone.

law_title: لایحه قانونی اصلاح قانون اخذ مالیات از اتومبیلهای سواری و صفحات گرامافون
justification: The law set a materially lower excise-tax schedule on domestically manufactured/assembled cars, trucks, buses and motorcycles versus a flat 10% tax on imported passenger cars (5% on imported trucks/buses, plus customs duties on all imports), creating a tax/tariff wedge that protected and incentivized domestic vehicle assembly.
caveats: Domestic vehicle production over 1970-2018 was driven far more by Iran's broader import-substitution strategy, post-revolution forex/import controls, wartime necessity during the Iran-Iraq War, and later joint-venture deals (Peugeot, Kia, etc.); this excise-tax schedule is one incremental protectionist tool among a much larger policy suite, not the primary driver.

law_title: قانون معافیت کالاها و تجهیزات صنعت آب و برق از پرداخت حقوق و عوارض گمرکی و سود بازرگانی
lag_description: gradual, multi-year infrastructure buildout; the exemption itself only ran through end of 1365 (1986)
justification: The law exempted the wholly state-owned Ministry of Energy (Niroo) and its subsidiaries from customs duties, tariffs, and commercial-profit tax on imported water- and power-industry equipment through the end of 1365, lowering the landed cost of imported capital goods for power-sector build-out.
caveats: Electricity access expansion in Iran is driven mainly by decades of state-led Five-Year Plan investment, oil-revenue-financed infrastructure spending, and rural electrification programs; this narrow, temporary (single-year) customs exemption is at most a minor cost-reducing input and cannot be isolated as a cause in the aggregate WDI series.

law_title: قانون اصلاح قانون صندوق عمران مراتع مصوب 1349
justification: The law converted the Rangeland Development Fund into a state joint-stock company under the Ministry of Agriculture explicitly tasked with procuring, producing and distributing forage/fodder to supply livestock feed and support animal-protein output.
caveats: Livestock production reflects herd sizes, rangeland/drought conditions, disease, feed-import policy and price supports; this is a narrow institutional restructuring of one state fodder-supply entity amid the end of the Iran-Iraq War and reconstruction period, so its marginal effect on the national livestock index is not separable from these larger forces.

law_title: قانون وظایف وزارت کشاورزی
lag_description: long-run, institutional, effects unfold over decades
justification: This foundational statute created the legal mandate and administrative apparatus of the Ministry of Agriculture, explicitly authorizing seed/fertilizer/machinery/pesticide distribution programs, agricultural extension, and livestock-breed improvement aimed at raising farm output and farmer incomes.
caveats: This is an institution-building law, not a specific price, subsidy, or quota instrument; actual production outcomes for any given crop or livestock series depend on many later specific programs, land reform, weather, war, and sanctions, so it should be read as background institutional context rather than a direct driver of any single chart.

law_title: قانون قرارداد بین ایران و جمهوری فدرال آلمان راجع به اجتناب از اخذ مالیات مضاعف از درآمد و ثروت
justification: The double-taxation treaty with West Germany removed a barrier to cross-border investment and commercial income by ensuring income/capital was not taxed twice, a mechanism generally understood to encourage bilateral trade and investment flows.
caveats: Iran's aggregate FDI net inflows (as tracked in WDI, largely post-1970 data) are dominated by oil-sector dynamics, nationalization, the 1979 revolution, war, and sanctions; a single bilateral tax treaty's marginal effect on economy-wide FDI decades later is not separable from these dominant forces and is highly speculative.

law_title: قانون ترمیم اعتبار هزینه‌های ناشی از جنگ تحمیلی و منابع تأمین اعتبار قانون بودجه سال ۱۳۶۰ (Law on Restoring Credit for Imposed-War Costs and Financing Sources for the FY1360 Budget Law)
lag_description: same fiscal year (1360/1981-82), with knock-on effects into subsequent years as war financing needs persisted
justification: The single-article law authorizes the government to add war-related budget appropriations (125bn+5bn rials) and, critically, to (a) pre-sell to Bank Markazi the proceeds of oil export shipping documents for Bahman-Esfand 1360 before their normal maturity, and (b) draw up to 180bn rials in additional financing from the banking system or bond issuance to cover an oil-revenue shortfall — both direct instruments that increase government indebtedness to the central bank / banking system.
caveats: This is one of many similar ad hoc wartime financing laws passed during the 1980-88 Iran-Iraq War; the government-debt-to-central-bank series reflects the cumulative effect of dozens of such measures, oil price collapses, and general war-economy deficit financing, not this law alone. Confidence is capped at 3 because the chart cannot be decomposed to isolate this specific FY1360 authorization from the broader war-era pattern.

law_title: قانون انتقال شرکت سهامی پخش کود شیمیائی بوزارت کشاورزی و منابع طبیعی
justification: The law transfers the chemical fertilizer distribution company (previously under National Iranian Oil Co.) to the Agriculture Ministry, consolidating fertilizer procurement/distribution under one national body with a mandate (Note 4) to organize nationwide input distribution — a structural change that could support wider farmer access to chemical fertilizer over the following years.
caveats: This is purely an institutional/administrative transfer with no pricing or subsidy provision attached, so the mechanism is indirect. Iran's fertilizer consumption trend in the 1970s-80s was driven far more by Green Revolution-era input subsidies, credit expansion, irrigation investment, and later by war disruption and sanctions on input imports; this single transfer law cannot be isolated as a driver.

law_title: آئين‌نامه شماره 25 راجع به تعرفه بيمه آتش‌سوزي و خطرات اضافي (Fire & Additional Hazards Insurance Tariff Regulation No. 25)
lag_description: gradual over the two decades the tariff was in force (1991-2011)
justification: The Supreme Insurance Council set detailed mandatory minimum premium rates (per-mille) for fire, explosion, lightning and add-on peril (earthquake, flood, riot, etc.) coverage across hundreds of industrial and commercial risk classes, and barred insurers from underwriting below these floors except with Bimeh Markazi approval. As a binding price floor for one major non-life insurance class, it is a plausible contributing factor to the level and growth of earned/written insurance premiums recorded in the sector during the years it was enforced.
caveats: This was one line of insurance among several (life, motor, marine, etc.) driving total sector premium; the much larger forces behind the observed premium trends in these charts are macro growth, inflation/rial depreciation raising insured asset values, market entry of new insurers, and financial-sector liberalization. The tariff regime itself was abolished by a 2010/2011 insurance-tariff-reform decree, after which fire premiums were market-set, so this specific instrument cannot be isolated from other simultaneous insurance-market reforms in the data.

law_title: دستورالعمل انضباطی ناشران پذیرفته‌شده در بورس اوراق بهادار تهران (مصوب هیئت‌مدیره سازمان بورس)
justification: Establishes a graduated ladder of disciplinary sanctions (written notices, suspension, delisting) for TSE-listed issuers that fail disclosure, AGM, and dividend-payment obligations, intended to strengthen investor protection and market governance, which over time can support market depth and issuer retention.
caveats: Aggregate market capitalization and listed-company counts in Iran are overwhelmingly driven by macro conditions -- inflation-driven equity rallies, sanctions, oil-price swings, and privatization waves -- not by disciplinary enforcement rules; no direct quantitative channel links this compliance directive to the index-level aggregates.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی بند (ب) ماده (2) قانون رفع موانع تولید رقابت‌پذیر و ارتقای نظام مالی کشور (تنفیذ اوراق صکوک اجاره برای تسویه بدهی دولت)
justification: Creates the executive mechanism for the government (via the Ministry of Economic Affairs and Finance) to issue Islamic lease-bond (sukuk ijarah) instruments backed by state assets to settle confirmed arrears owed to contractors and creditors, formally converting off-book payment arrears into recognized public debt.
caveats: This is one of several debt-management tools used across many budget-year annexes; the scale of sukuk issued under this specific bylaw is modest relative to total government net debt, which is driven far more by oil-revenue shortfalls, sanctions, and overall fiscal deficits.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی بند (ب) ماده (2) قانون رفع موانع تولید رقابت‌پذیر و ارتقای نظام مالی کشور (تنفیذ اوراق صکوک اجاره برای تسویه بدهی دولت)
justification: By channeling government arrears settlement through market-sold sukuk ijarah bonds rather than direct central-bank overdraft/financing, this bylaw offers an alternative to monetizing deficits through the Central Bank, which could modestly reduce reliance on direct government debt to the CBI.
caveats: Government debt to the central bank over this period was shaped mainly by oil-sanction revenue shocks and recurring budget deficits; the substitution effect from this narrow sukuk mechanism is likely small and hard to isolate from other financing-law changes in the same budget years.

law_title: رأی شماره 2895 هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری (اعمال ماده 91 نسبت به رأی 432-1396 و عدم ابطال بخشنامه 9253 سازمان امور مالیاتی درباره مالیات بر ارزش افزوده بیمه درمان تکمیلی)
justification: This ruling reinstates VAT liability on supplementary health-insurance and drug/therapy-management services (overturning a prior 2017 exemption), raising the cost insurers face and are likely to pass through in premiums, feeding into private and out-of-pocket health spending.
caveats: General health-cost inflation and the rial's collapse against the dollar over 2018-2020 dominate movements in private and out-of-pocket health expenditure; VAT on supplementary insurance premiums is a marginal share of total private health spending and cannot be isolated from these larger forces.

law_title: رأی شماره 67 هیأت عمومی دیوان عدالت اداری (ابطال بخشنامه 7304 سازمان امور مالیاتی درباره مالیات بر سود و کارمزد وام‌های خارجی نشده‌اند)
justification: Annuls a tax circular that had imposed Iranian withholding tax on interest/fees Iranian firms pay foreign banks for loans used entirely abroad (e.g., to pay foreign suppliers directly) without the funds ever transferring into Iran, removing a tax-cost barrier to that form of offshore trade financing.
caveats: Issued in 2019 amid intensified US sanctions, when foreign banks' willingness to extend credit to Iranian private firms was already severely curtailed regardless of Iranian tax treatment, so the practical behavioral effect on external private non-guaranteed debt is likely minimal and hard to distinguish from sanctions-driven trends.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي ماده 10 قانون نظام جامع دامپروري كشور (Executive Bylaw, Art. 10, Comprehensive Livestock System Law)
justification: The bylaw creates a national livestock genetic-resource registry, breed-certification system, and import/export licensing regime for animal genetic material, intended to improve breeding quality and productivity over time.
caveats: Livestock production is dominated by feed costs, drought/water availability, disease outbreaks, sanctions-driven input costs, and demand shifts; a niche breeding-registry institution is a marginal, slow-acting contributor at best, not a primary driver.

law_title: آئيننامه نحوه دريافت عوارض فرودگاهي براي سفرهاي خارج از كشور (Bylaw on Airport Departure Fee for Outbound International Travel)
justification: Imposed a mandatory 200,000-rial departure levy (paid into a Central Bank account) on every Iranian buying an international air ticket, raising the direct cost of outbound travel and functioning partly as a foreign-exchange control instrument during the Iran-Iraq war.
caveats: 1988 outbound travel and tourism-expenditure levels were far more strongly shaped by the ongoing Iran-Iraq war, strict multi-tier exchange controls, and general economic hardship than by this single fee; WDI series coverage for Iran in this period is also thin, limiting the ability to isolate the fee's effect.

law_title: قانون الحاق يك ماده به قانون وصول برخي از درآمدهاي دولت (Law Adding an Article to the Law on Collection of Certain State Revenues — groundwater-well permit fees in restricted zones)
justification: Authorized the Ministry of Energy to issue new small-capacity (max 25 m3/day) well permits in officially banned/restricted groundwater plains for essential industrial, poultry/livestock, and greenhouse use, while directing the resulting permit-fee revenue toward artificial recharge and irrigation-efficiency projects meant to offset the additional extraction.
caveats: National freshwater withdrawal and water-stress trends are driven overwhelmingly by large-scale agricultural irrigation, multi-year drought cycles, dam operations, and population growth; this narrowly-scoped permit provision for small users in restricted zones is a marginal factor whose net effect (new extraction vs. funded recharge) is not clearly signed.

law_title: قانون پرداخت چهار ميليون دلار وام بشركت ملي نفت ايران براي سرمايه‌گذاري در شركت نفت ايران و ايتاليا (سيريپ) (Law Authorizing a $4 Million Loan to NIOC for Investment in the Iran-Italy Oil Company/SIRIP)
justification: Provided NIOC financing (via a state loan repaid from taxes/royalties collected on SIRIP) to help fund Iran's equity stake in SIRIP, the joint venture with Italy's ENI exploring offshore Persian Gulf fields, part of the broader post-1957 push to develop new Iranian crude output outside the old Consortium structure.
caveats: Iran's national crude oil production trajectory in the 1960s-70s was overwhelmingly driven by the Consortium agreement, OPEC production/pricing arrangements, and much larger-scale field development than this single $4 million loan; SIRIP's own output was a small fraction of national production, so this financing detail is at most a marginal contributor.

law_title: آیین‌نامه شماره ۳۵ درخصوص حداقل حق بیمه و میزان تعهدات بیمه اجباری شخص ثالث و صندوق تأمین خسارت‌های بدنی
lag_description: immediate re-pricing, effects persist across the regulation's 1995-2008 life
justification: Supreme Insurance Council set binding minimum premiums/tariffs for compulsory third-party motor liability insurance and raised the bodily-injury compensation-fund ceiling, a direct price-floor instrument on the motor insurance line that stayed in force until a 2008 law replaced it.
caveats: Aggregate insurance-market premium and loss series are driven far more by vehicle-fleet growth, general inflation, and market liberalization (new private insurers entering after ~2000) than by this single tariff regulation; cannot isolate its specific contribution in annual chart data.

law_title: قانون مربوط به نحوه استفاده از بقیه موجودی وجوه پذیره نفت
justification: The maddeh vahedeh directly earmarks the remaining ~$9.3M oil-concession signing-bonus (pazireh) receipts from Pan American Oil across specific budget lines (border-area irrigation, a Gachsaran-Shiraz pipeline/plan-org loan fund, unfinished health/culture/justice buildings, provincial development, embassy purchases, border development) for FY1958 — exactly the kind of allocation decision the Pahlavi-era oil-revenue-by-recipient/by-allocation charts are built from.
caveats: This is a one-off supplementary appropriation of a specific signing-bonus windfall, not the main NIOC/Consortium royalty flow that dominates total oil revenue in this period; it shapes the composition of oil-revenue allocation rather than its overall level, and the charts' broader trends reflect the 1954 Consortium Agreement and world oil prices far more than this single law.

law_title: قانون معافیت سیمان‌های صادر از کشور از حقوق و عوارض گمرکی
lag_description: same fiscal year to a few years, effect compounds while the exemption is in force
justification: Exempts exported cement from customs duties, port fees, and a historic-monuments levy, an explicit export-incentive instrument that lowers the effective cost of shipping cement abroad and should modestly favor export-oriented production volumes.
caveats: National cement production in this era was overwhelmingly driven by the domestic construction and infrastructure boom of the late Pahlavi development plans, not by the (likely small) export share; cannot separate the export-exemption effect from broader industrial expansion in the annual production series.

law_title: قانون استرداد حقوق گمرکی مواد اولیه مصنوعات ماشینی کارخانجات داخلی
lag_description: gradual, cumulative over decades the scheme has remained in force
justification: A duty-drawback scheme refunding customs duty/commercial-profit tax on imported inputs used in domestically manufactured exports, a standard instrument for lowering effective input costs and encouraging manufactured (vs. raw-material) exports.
caveats: The manufactured-exports share of merchandise exports is dominated by oil-price swings (denominator effect), sanctions, exchange-rate policy, and broad industrial-policy shifts across six decades; this narrow, long-standing drawback scheme is very unlikely to be identifiable as a driver in the aggregate WDI series and is included only as a plausible low-confidence contributing mechanism.

law_title: ضوابط زیست‌محیطی فعالیت‌های معدنی
lag_description: long-run structural constraint, effects if any would show up over subsequent decades
justification: Cabinet-approved environmental standards barring mining licenses/permits in national parks, protected wetlands, wildlife refuges, protected rivers, forests, and urban zones; a background regulatory constraint on where and how much mining activity can legally occur.
caveats: Provincial mining-production-value data (2018-2023) is far removed in time from this 2005 rule and is driven overwhelmingly by commodity prices, sanctions-affected trade, and currency devaluation; the environmental permitting constraint cannot be isolated as a cause in this series and is recorded only as background context.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی بند (ب) ماده ۱۲ قانون افزایش بهره‌وری بخش کشاورزی و منابع طبیعی
lag_description: gradual, funding mechanism whose effects would only show cumulatively over years
justification: Establishes a methodology for assessing damages when government projects degrade forests/rangelands and directs the resulting compensation revenue into a dedicated budget line for reforestation/rehabilitation of natural resources, an indirect fiscal mechanism touching forest-area and depletion outcomes.
caveats: Forest-area and forest-depletion trends are dominated by drought, wildfire, agricultural encroachment, and population pressure; this is a narrow administrative damage-assessment and compensation-funding bylaw, not a primary driver, and its effect size relative to those forces is likely negligible and unmeasurable in the WDI series.

law_title: قانون ایجاد تسهیلات برای توسعه طرحهای فاضلاب و بازسازی شبکه‌های آب شهری
justification: Law authorizes urban water/sewage utility companies to levy connection fees and a surcharge (up to 10%) on water tariffs, earmarked exclusively for building out sewage networks and rehabilitating urban water infrastructure in that city — a self-funding mechanism for network expansion that has been amended and kept in force through 2024.
caveats: Access-to-water/sanitation gains over decades are driven mainly by broader Five-Year Plan infrastructure investment, population/urbanization trends, and World Bank/state budget capital spending; this tariff-based instrument is one of several funding channels, not the primary driver, and its incremental effect cannot be isolated in the aggregate indicator.

law_title: دستورالعمل اجرایی آیین‌نامۀ سرمایه‌گذاری خارجی در بورس‌ها و بازارهای خارج از بورس
justification: Executive instructions operationalizing the 2010 foreign-investment-in-exchanges bylaw: establishes the trading-code application process, fund-repatriation formula, and disclosure duties that formally opened the Tehran Stock Exchange and OTC markets to foreign portfolio investors.
caveats: Issued in 2011 as international sanctions on Iran's financial sector were intensifying, which severely limited real foreign portfolio inflows regardless of the legal mechanism; TSE market capitalization and turnover are dominated by domestic drivers (rial depreciation, liquidity conditions, IPOs, retail investor waves), so any effect of this instrument is likely marginal and hard to disentangle.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی ماده (۱۵) قانون نظام جامع دامپروری کشور
lag_description: gradual over a decade or more, if any
justification: The bylaw sets licensing/certification standards for private-sector livestock extension, consulting, research, and service centers, intended to raise professional quality of husbandry practices feeding into national herd productivity.
caveats: This is a narrow institutional/licensing regulation, not a subsidy, price support, or production quota — it creates certification infrastructure for private service providers rather than directly incentivizing output. Any livestock production index movement is far more plausibly driven by feed costs, drought/water availability, sanctions-driven input costs, and general agricultural policy than by this bylaw. Confidence kept minimal because the causal chain (licensing standards -> measurable national output) is long and unverified.

law_title: قانون قرارداد الحاقی بقرارداد بین شرکت ملی نفت ایران و شرکت پان‌آمریکن پترولیوم کورپوریشن (شرکت نفت اموکو ایران)
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-2 year lag
justification: The supplementary contract raises the income-tax rate on the NIOC/Amoco (Pan American) joint-venture's oil revenue to 55% and sets a minimum per-barrel exploration-cost deduction floor, mirroring the early-1970s OPEC-wide renegotiation of host-government take (Tehran Agreement era) that increased Iran's per-barrel oil revenue share.
caveats: This is a bilateral tax-term adjustment for one relatively small foreign joint-venture operator (Pan American/Amoco), not the main Iranian Oil Consortium agreement that dominated national oil revenue. Any rise in oil rents % of GDP around 1972-73 is overwhelmingly driven by the broader OPEC tax renegotiation wave and the 1973 oil price shock, not this specific minor contract; this law is at most one small instance within that larger trend.

law_title: (no record found in law index)
justification: Law ID does not exist in the law batch TSVs (data/processed/laws/batches/*.tsv) or anywhere in the project; no title, date, or text could be retrieved to evaluate.
caveats: Likely a placeholder/duplicate ID with no underlying record.

law_title: (no record found in law index)
justification: Law ID does not exist in the law batch TSVs (data/processed/laws/batches/*.tsv) or anywhere in the project; no title, date, or text could be retrieved to evaluate.
caveats: Likely a placeholder/duplicate ID with no underlying record.

law_title: Law setting the ceiling on participation-bond issuance by the Central Bank of Iran
lag_description: same fiscal year to following year
justification: Authorizes the Central Bank (under the 4th Development Plan, Art.10h) to issue up to 20 trillion rials of participation bonds explicitly 'to control liquidity' - a sterilization tool intended to slow M2/monetary-base growth.
caveats: The authorized ceiling was tiny relative to total M2 by 2006; broad money growth in this period was driven far more by oil-revenue-fueled bank credit expansion and government deficit financing. One of several minor sterilization instruments, not the primary driver of the aggregate.

law_title: Law on participation-bond issuance by the Central Bank of Iran
lag_description: same fiscal year to following year
justification: Authorizes CBI to issue up to 40 trillion rials of participation bonds under the interest-free banking framework 'to implement monetary policy and control liquidity.'
caveats: Same-year oil windfalls, expansionary government spending, and rapid bank credit growth were much larger drivers of the 2007 monetary aggregates than this single bond tranche; part of a recurring annual series of similar bond-ceiling laws.

law_title: Law on participation-bond issuance by the Central Bank of Iran (related/duplicate ceiling law)
lag_description: same fiscal year to following year
justification: Same instrument type: authorizes CBI to issue 10 trillion rials of participation bonds under Art.10(h) of the 4th Plan to implement monetary policy and control liquidity.
caveats: Small in scale relative to total M2; part of a repeated annual bond-ceiling authorization pattern rather than a distinct one-off shock, overshadowed by oil-revenue-driven liquidity growth.

law_title: Law on the independence of provincial electricity distribution companies
justification: Mandates unbundling of regional electricity monopolies into autonomous, non-governmental provincial distribution companies with independent management/finance, intended to improve operational efficiency and accountability in electricity distribution.
caveats: Transmission/distribution losses are also driven by grid infrastructure investment, theft/non-payment, subsidized tariffs discouraging efficiency upgrades, and sanctions-limited access to equipment; institutional restructuring alone is a weak, slow-acting lever whose effect is hard to isolate.

law_title: Law on loans for livestock-sector development and improvement
lag_description: gradual, over multiple years to a decade
justification: Directs proceeds from imported feed-corn/forage sales into a revolving loan fund (administered by the Ministry of Agriculture) for livestock and dairy development, breed improvement, dairy-factory construction, and pasture/forage improvement - a direct agricultural-credit instrument aimed at expanding livestock and dairy output.
caveats: Long-run livestock/dairy production trends in Iran were also shaped by land reform (concurrent White Revolution), population growth, drought cycles, and later by war and subsidy policy; this 1967 loan-fund law is one of many contributing inputs and its incremental effect cannot be isolated from these broader forces.

law_title: Amendment to customs-tariff Article 20 item 19 - duty exemption for hospital construction materials
justification: Adds a narrow customs-duty exemption for imported building materials used in hospital/health-facility construction by charities; too small and institution-specific to register in any held aggregate trade or price series.
caveats: No aggregate customs, trade, or price chart in the index isolates hospital-construction-material imports; also predates most available time series (pre-1960).

law_title: Amendment to Article 6 of the customs duty-drawback law for raw materials of domestic machine-made goods
justification: Technical amendment expanding a duty-refund mechanism for domestic factories selling machine-made goods to embassies/international organizations; a narrow administrative fix, not a broad trade or industry policy shift.
caveats: No held chart tracks this specific duty-drawback channel; any effect on aggregate industrial output or trade would be far too small to distinguish from 1970s oil-boom industrial growth.

law_title: Customs/commercial-profit duty exemption for medical and orthopedic goods imported by the Bonyad-e Shahid foundation
justification: Grants duty exemption to a single charitable foundation's medical-supply imports; scale is far too small relative to national trade/customs-revenue aggregates.
caveats: No chart in the index tracks single-institution import exemptions; wartime (Iran-Iraq War) medical needs are the actual driver of this law, unrelated to broader economic trends.

law_title: Decree exempting a pharmaceutical charity's drug and equipment imports from customs duty
justification: Narrow duty exemption for one charitable pharmaceutical-import organization affiliated with the Health Ministry; not a broad trade or price policy instrument.
caveats: No held chart isolates this institution's import volumes; occurs amid the 1979 revolutionary transition when many other confounding shocks (nationalization, capital flight, sanctions onset) dominate.

law_title: Law on customs clearance of vehicles imported by residents of Persian Gulf islands, Sistan-Baluchestan, and Larestan
justification: A narrow, one-time amnesty allowing residents of specific border regions to legalize previously informally-imported cars/pickups by paying duties, capped at one vehicle per person and geographically restricted.
caveats: Scale (a bounded population of border/island residents regularizing existing vehicles) is far too small to move the held national vehicle-production chart, which tracks domestic manufacturing output, not informal cross-border vehicle imports; no customs-revenue or import-volume chart in the index is granular enough to capture this episode.

law_title: قانون اجازه اجرای موافقت‌نامه ایران با شرکت‌های خریدار نفت خام (Tehran Agreement 1971)
lag_description: same year to 1-4 year lag (agreement phased posted-price increases through 1975)
justification: Authorizes Iran's implementation of the February 1971 Tehran Agreement between Gulf oil-producing states and the major buyer companies, which raised posted crude oil prices (Iranian heavy/light crude) by an initial ~33 cents/barrel plus scheduled annual escalations through 1975 and raised the income-tax rate on oil companies toward 55%. This directly increased Iran's per-barrel government take from crude exports in the early-to-mid 1970s.
caveats: Oil rents (% of GDP) is a composite measure driven by many factors beyond this one pricing agreement: global oil demand, OPEC-wide production quotas, and the much larger 1973-74 oil price shock (embargo/OPEC unilateral pricing) that followed just two years later and dwarfs this agreement's effect. The 1971 Tehran Agreement is a real but comparatively modest precursor step; most of the 1970s oil-rent spike is attributable to the 1973 shock, not this law.

law_title: اصلاح آیین‌نامه فروش مواد معدنی و آیین‌نامه استخراج و فروش معادن توسط بخش خصوصی
lag_description: gradual, multi-year (pricing-formula change, not a one-time shock)
justification: Cabinet decree rewrites the pricing formula (Article 4 of the mineral-sales bylaw and Article 5 of the private-sector mine extraction/sale bylaw) that the Ministry of Mines and Metals uses to set at-mine sale prices for extracted minerals, now requiring the price to reflect pre-extraction ore value, extraction/exploration costs, transport, and comparable mineral prices, subject to consumer/producer-protection agency approval. A more cost-reflective administered price plausibly affects private operators' incentive to extract and sell through official channels.
caveats: This is an internal administrative pricing-formula tweak, not a price or subsidy announcement itself; it does not set any specific mineral's price, so its effect on measured production volumes (barite, chromite, iron ore, etc. in the index) is indirect and hard to isolate from concurrent drivers such as the Iran-Iraq War (still ongoing in 1985), sanctions, and general 1980s economic disruption. Confidence is kept low because the mechanism is diffuse and applies to many minerals at once rather than a single traceable series.

law_title: تصویب‌نامه راجع به شیوه‌های قیمت‌گذاری سهام، تخفیف‌ها و چگونگی پرداخت قیمت توسط خریداران
lag_description: gradual over the following decade (2000s-2010s privatization wave)
justification: Cabinet decree sets the methodology for base-value pricing of state-owned company shares, buyer discounts, and payment terms for privatization sales, providing an alternative pricing route (Article 4, clauses a/b) when the standard six pricing methods fail to yield a realistic share value. This is part of the administrative machinery that enabled Iran's 2000s-2010s privatization program (culminating in the Article 44 general policies from 2006), which grew the number of listed companies and market capitalization on the Tehran Stock Exchange.
caveats: This 2003 decree is one narrow technical piece (share-pricing methodology) of a much larger, multi-year privatization program; TSE listings and market cap growth in the 2000s-2010s were driven far more by the 2006 Article 44 general policy shift, oil-revenue-fueled liquidity, and macro conditions than by this specific pricing-method decree. Attribution to this single instrument is speculative and should be read as one contributing administrative input, not a primary cause.

law_title: اجازه اعتبار سنوات آتی شاه لوله دوم گاز ایران (Authorization of multi-year credit for Iran's second gas trunk pipeline)
lag_description: gradual, multi-decade (pipeline capacity built out over years, effects on gas rents visible only over following decades)
justification: This is a parliamentary budget-commission authorization of multi-year credit (current-year plus post-Fifth-Plan years) to the National Iranian Oil Company for construction of the second Iran gas trunk pipeline (IGAT-2), a major piece of national gas transmission infrastructure. Expanded trunk-pipeline capacity is a plausible long-run enabling condition for higher natural gas production/rents captured in GDP.
caveats: This is one financing authorization among many gas-sector infrastructure investments across decades; the chart (natural gas rents % of GDP) is driven far more by global gas/oil prices, domestic consumption growth, sanctions, and the 1979 revolution's disruption of the pipeline's original export purpose (gas swap with the USSR was largely abandoned post-revolution). No pre/post-1976 gas production or rents chart exists in our index to test this directly; the link is inferred from general infrastructure-to-output reasoning, not from a matched time series.

law_title: قانون راجع به نحوه تقسیم مبلغ چهارده میلیون و هفتصد هزار دلار از محل ۲۵ میلیون دلار پذیره نفت دریافتی از شرکت پان آمریکن
justification: This law is the specific legislative instrument allocating the $14.7M Pan American Oil signing-bonus receipt (out of a $25M total) across the state budget, unfinished-building completion, Ministry of Agriculture land reclamation/pest control, Fars settlement fund, and NIOC investment. It falls squarely within the time window and subject matter of the 'Oil Revenue by Allocation (Budget/NIOC/Plan Org/B.P.C.)' series, which tracks exactly these recipient categories for 1955/56-1962/63, and the 'Oil Revenue Distribution by Recipient' chart for 1957-1959.
caveats: This is one specific signing-bonus disbursement among many oil-revenue flows in the period; the charts aggregate multiple such allocations from ordinary royalty/tax revenue as well as one-off bonuses, so this single law is only a partial (though clearly identifiable) contributor to the recorded totals, not the sole driver.

law_title: لایحه قانونی مجازات اخلال در امر کشاورزی و دامداری (Criminal penalties for disruption of agriculture and animal husbandry)
lag_description: immediate legal effect; any measurable production effect would only be visible over the following 1-5 years given the dominance of other post-revolution shocks
justification: Enacted by the Revolutionary Council two months after the revolution, this law criminalizes unauthorized land seizure and disruption of planting/harvesting/livestock activity (3 months-3 years imprisonment plus internal exile), explicitly aimed at preventing the halt of farming amid the post-1979 land-tenure chaos. It is a protective/deterrent instrument meant to sustain agricultural output during a period of widespread rural land seizures.
caveats: This is a background legal-order instrument, not a subsidy, price, or input-support policy, so any link to production levels is indirect and unverifiable from the law text alone. Iranian agricultural/cereal output in 1979-1983 was simultaneously shaped by the revolution's administrative disruption, the outbreak of the Iran-Iraq war (1980), drought cycles, the separate (and much larger) land-reform and land-redistribution upheavals of the same period, and emigration of large landowners/managers - any of which likely dominates over this narrow criminal-deterrence statute. Treat as a weak contextual factor only, not a driver.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي قانون اصلاح جداول شماره (4) و (8) قانون برنامه چهارم توسعه ... تخصیص 2% درآمد صادرات نفت و گاز به استانهای نفت‌خیز و گازخیز و مناطق محروم
lag_description: gradual over a decade; this specific bylaw covered only FY1385 budget execution but represents a recurring institutional pattern (earmarked oil/gas revenue shares to producing provinces) repeated in later annual budget laws
justification: Establishes the mechanism by which 2% of crude oil and natural gas export revenue is credited to oil/gas-producing provinces and deprived counties for capital projects (rural gas networks, infrastructure), administered via provincial development councils.
caveats: This particular bylaw was explicitly temporary (tied to the 4th Development Plan and the 1385/2006 budget law) and was superseded by later annual budget-law versions of the same earmarking idea, so it is only one instance of a recurring policy family, not a single durable instrument. The chart's 1397-1401 window postdates this specific bylaw by a decade; provincial capital-expenditure shares are also driven by population, general budget allocation formulas, and ad hoc political bargaining, none of which this law touches. Confidence kept low because the direct causal chain from this specific 2007 bylaw to the observed 2018-2022 provincial data is speculative.

law_title: آيين نامه اجرايي ماده 12 اصلاحي قانون نحوه انتشار اوراق مشاركت مصوب 1379 (بازنشستگی/تعاونی/بخش خصوصی و اوراق مشارکت دولتی)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag, cumulative effect over several years as institutions gradually convert reserves into bonds
justification: Permits pension funds, cooperative/social-security funds, and private-sector firms to convert a share of their deposits/reserves into government participation (musharakah) bonds, giving the government a market-based financing channel as an alternative to borrowing directly from the central bank.
caveats: Participation is voluntary and the bylaw sets no mandatory quota, so real-world uptake and its scale relative to total government financing needs is unknown and plausibly small. Government debt to the central bank over 1978-2016 is dominated by much larger factors -- oil-revenue swings, war/reconstruction financing, sanctions-driven budget deficits, and monetization of fiscal shortfalls -- so this bylaw is at most a minor contributing channel, not a primary driver. Confidence kept low given the indirect and voluntary nature of the mechanism.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اعطای مجوز و فعالیت کارگزار در قالب شخصیت حقوقی (Bylaw on licensing corporate-entity brokers)
justification: Part of the mid-2000s capital-market modernization push (implementing the 2005 Securities Market Law) that let brokerage firms operate as corporate legal entities rather than only individuals, expanding intermediary capacity for the Tehran Stock Exchange.
caveats: Market capitalization and trading-value aggregates are dominated by inflation-driven nominal price gains, oil-revenue cycles, sanctions/JCPOA swings, and mass retail entry (e.g. 2020 boom), so this single licensing bylaw's contribution to the WDI capital-market series is at most a small structural enabler, not a measurable driver on its own.

law_title: مصوبه ابزار مالی قراردادهای آتی و مقررات مربوط به معاملات قرارداد آتی (Approval of futures-contract instrument and related trading rules)
justification: The Supreme Council of the Stock Exchange formally introduced futures contracts as a new tradable instrument in 2008, adding a derivatives layer to Iran's capital market that could incrementally add to reported trading turnover over subsequent years (futures were rolled out initially on commodities like gold coin, later equities).
caveats: Reported stocks-traded/turnover aggregates are driven overwhelmingly by spot-equity trading and macro conditions (inflation, sanctions, liquidity growth); the incremental volume attributable to nascent futures markets is small and hard to isolate from the overall series.

law_title: دستورالعمل نحوه تعیین قیمت خرید و فروش اوراق بهادار در صندوق‌های سرمایه‌گذاری (Directive on pricing method for securities purchase/sale in investment funds)
justification: A back-office pricing/valuation procedure for mutual-fund securities transactions issued by the exchange organization's board; it standardizes fund operations rather than changing market size, prices, or turnover.
caveats: This is purely a procedural/technical directive with no direct mechanism for moving aggregate market capitalization or trading-value figures; any association with capital-market chart movements would be coincidental at most, driven instead by macro and sanctions factors.

law_title: اصلاح برخی از مواد آئین نامه ارز (Amendment to Foreign Exchange Bylaw)
lag_description: same fiscal year to a few years
justification: This 1950 cabinet-level amendment revised articles of Iran's foreign-exchange implementing bylaw (آیین‌نامه ارز), which governed how the state allocated and rationed scarce hard currency in the early post-war/pre-oil-nationalization period, a mechanism that structurally shapes the gap between official and parallel exchange rates captured in this chart's long time series.
caveats: The scraped record contains only the amendment's title and date, not the substantive text of the revised articles, so the specific mechanism and magnitude are unverified. The official/parallel FX gap chart spans 1937-2026 and is driven far more by oil-revenue swings, the 1951-53 oil nationalization crisis, and later sanctions/devaluations than by a single narrow bylaw amendment; treat this as low-confidence background context, not a demonstrated driver.

law_title: تصویب‌نامه راجع به تنفیذ آیین‌نامه‌های مدیریت تولید، انتقال و توزیع نیروی برق ایران (Ratification of bylaws for Iran's electricity generation, transmission & distribution management company)
justification: This 2006 cabinet decree ratifies (تنفیذ) the operating bylaws of the state company responsible for managing electricity generation, transmission and distribution across Iran, an internal governance instrument that could plausibly influence grid efficiency (transmission/distribution losses) and electrification reach over time.
caveats: The scraped text preserves only the decree's title/date and signature line, not its substantive provisions, so the actual operational changes are unknown. Transmission losses and electrification rates are driven mainly by infrastructure investment, subsidized tariffs, sanctions-era equipment access, and demand growth rather than by ratification of an internal management bylaw; this link is speculative and should be read as weak background context only.

law_title: قانون اجازه اجرای اساسنامه شرکت ملی نفت ایران (Law authorizing execution of the National Iranian Oil Company statute)
justification: This 1974 law authorizes the government to implement a new corporate statute for the National Iranian Oil Company, part of the mid-1970s consolidation of state control over oil operations following the 1973 renegotiation of the Consortium Agreement; NIOC's governance structure directly determines how Iran's oil sector is operated and reported.
caveats: This is an internal corporate-governance instrument (a two-year trial statute per its own text), not a production quota, pricing, or investment decision.
