Iran's oil production and revenue in the mid-1970s were overwhelmingly driven by the 1973-74 OPEC price shock, global demand, and the broader nationalization wave rather than by this statute itself; the link is speculative and low-confidence.

law_title: لایحه قانونی بخشودگی زیان دیرکرد وامهای پرداختی به زارعین چغندرکار (Forgiveness of late-payment penalties on loans to sugar-beet farmers)
justification: Authorizes the Ministry of Agriculture to waive late-payment penalties on sugar-beet development loans for farmers hit by adverse weather, provided principal and interest are repaid within 18 months; this relief was meant to keep beet farmers solvent and in production rather than defaulting and exiting the crop.
caveats: Only a debt-relief/forbearance measure, not a price or output support; national sugar-beet production is driven far more by weather, guaranteed purchase prices, irrigation and area planted than by this narrow loan-penalty waiver. Effect on aggregate output, if any, would be small and hard to isolate from other factors in the immediate post-revolution disruption of 1979.

law_title: اختصاص مبلغ پنجاه میلیون ریال اعتبار فوق‌العاده جهت احداث انبارهای فلزی برای تمرکز گندم‌های خریداری... در اختیار سازمان غله کشور (Emergency credit for metal grain silos for the Grain Organization)
justification: A one-time extraordinary credit (50 million rials) from wheat sale/export proceeds to build metal silos for the state Grain Organization to store purchased wheat, intended to expand post-harvest storage capacity for the government wheat-procurement system.
caveats: This is storage infrastructure, not a price or production incentive; it may reduce post-harvest loss and support marketed volume but has no direct mechanism to raise sown area or yields. Wheat production series are dominated by rainfall, guaranteed purchase price policy, and area planted, not a single silo-construction credit. Speculative link.

law_title: تعهد اعتبار سنوات آتی طرح (لوله‌کشی نفت) (Multi-year credit commitment for an oil pipeline project)
lag_description: multi-year (project construction, effects felt years later)
justification: The Majlis Budget Commission approved a multi-year credit commitment for an oil pipeline project under the Plan and Budget Law, part of the Fifth Development Plan's oil-sector infrastructure investment during Iran's 1970s oil-boom capital spending; pipeline capacity expansion supports oil production/export throughput.
caveats: The underlying table of amounts is not preserved in the extracted text, and Iran's oil production/export volumes in this era were overwhelmingly set by OPEC quotas, global oil prices, and geopolitics (e.g., 1973 oil shock, later the 1979 revolution and Iran-Iraq war), not by a single pipeline credit line among many concurrent Plan Organization infrastructure projects.

law_title: قانون استفساریه ماده (۲۹) قانون معادن (Interpretive law clarifying Article 29 of the Mining Law)
lag_description: gradual over a decade or more, if any
justification: Clarifies that Article 29 of the 1998 Mining Law repealed all prior laws/decrees/circulars imposing charges other than tax and statutory government dues on mineral extractors (while non-cost-affecting fees remain valid), reinforcing a lighter, more predictable fiscal burden on mining operators.
caveats: Purely an interpretive resolution of an existing 1998 provision, not a new substantive change; the originating Mining Law itself is not in our law set. Iran-held production charts for individual minerals in our index stop at 1980, long before this 2000 law; the only post-2000 mining aggregate (mineral rents % of GDP) is dominated by global commodity prices and sanctions, making any link highly speculative and long-lagged.

law_title: قانون اجازه الحاق دولت شاهنشاهی ایران بمقاوله‌نامه بین‌المللی شماره ۱۲۲ مربوط به سیاست اشتغال (Iran's accession to ILO Convention 122 on Employment Policy)
lag_description: gradual over a decade, background policy orientation
justification: Ratifies ILO Convention 122, committing the state in principle to pursue an active policy of full, productive, and freely chosen employment; this is a framework/aspirational international commitment coinciding with Iran's Fifth Development Plan-era push toward full employment during the oil-boom industrialization drive.
caveats: The Convention creates no binding domestic mechanism or funded program by itself; observed shifts in employment by sector/gender over subsequent decades are driven overwhelmingly by oil-revenue-funded industrialization, urbanization, the 1979 revolution, war, and later demographic transition, not by treaty ratification. Best treated as background context, not a causal driver.

law_title: اعتبار طرحهای عمرانی (سرمایه‌گذاری ثابت) در سال ۲۵۳۷ [FY1357]
justification: This joint Majlis/Senate Plan Commission act fixed the development-project (fixed capital investment) credit table under Note 6 of the original FY1357 (2537 imperial) national budget law, appropriating roughly 122.9 billion rials across regional and national development projects. It is the base-year capital-investment appropriation for the exact fiscal year covered by the 'Mid-Year Supplementary Budget Additions' chart, whose own dataset notes the original FY1357 main budget law could not be located — this law is a surviving fragment of that missing baseline.
caveats: This is a different legal instrument from the mid-year supplementary addition the chart actually tracks (salary/pension reform, foreign loan repayments, etc.) — it does not itself explain the magnitude or composition of the supplementary items. It only supplies background context on that year's original capital-budget scale, enacted under the outgoing Pahlavi government just before the 1979 revolution disrupted normal budget continuity.

law_title: قانون اصلاح ماده ۲۰ اصلاحی قانون برق ایران مصوب ۱۳۶۲ (تمدید معافیت مالیاتی)
lag_description: gradual over several years (First Five-Year Development Plan, 1989-1994)
justification: This 1991 law extended a corporate income-tax exemption (originally granted in 1983) for Iran's regional water-and-electricity companies and Tavanir through the end of the First Five-Year Development Plan. By reducing the state utilities' tax burden it plausibly freed retained earnings for grid expansion and generation-capacity investment during the post-war reconstruction period, contributing at the margin to growth in electricity access/consumption.
caveats: These are wholly state-owned utilities, so a tax exemption is largely an intra-government fiscal transfer rather than a market incentive, making the investment-stimulus mechanism weak and indirect. Electricity access and consumption growth in this period were driven far more by the post-war reconstruction program and rural electrification plans than by this tax provision; this is a minor contributing factor at most, not a primary driver.

law_title: قانون اصلاح ماده ۲۰ قانون برق ایران مصوب سال ۱۳۴۶ (معافیت مالیاتی شرکت‌های آب و برق منطقه‌ای)
justification: This 1983 law granted a 100% income-tax exemption on the taxable income of regional water-and-electricity companies affiliated with the Ministry of Energy through the end of 1988 (retroactive to expiry of an earlier 1967 exemption). As with its 1991 extension, the intent was to preserve utility cash flow for wartime/post-war grid maintenance and expansion, a plausible marginal contributor to electricity access and consumption growth.
caveats: Same caveat as the related 1991 extension: the beneficiaries are state-owned utilities, so the fiscal mechanism is weak and indirect, and the dominant drivers of electricity-sector outcomes in this Iran-Iraq War period were wartime infrastructure damage/repair, oil-revenue swings, and central development planning rather than this narrow tax provision.

law_title: آیین‌نامه اجرایی جزء (۷) بند (د) ماده (۴۸) قانون برنامه هفتم توسعه (تخصیص محصولات پیش‌فروش‌شده پتروشیمی به واحدهای پایین‌دستی)
lag_description: 1-3 year lag as downstream allocation and investment materialize
justification: This 2025 bylaw directs pre-sold petrochemical company output that carries no export commitment to be channeled to downstream/value-chain processing units instead of export, an industrial-policy lever aimed at deepening domestic value-added processing of petrochemical feedstock.
caveats: Effective only from 2025, so any measurable effect on manufacturing value-added composition is not yet observable in current data and the chart's data vintage may lag well behind the law's effective date. Chemical value-added share is also driven by global petrochemical prices, sanctions-driven export constraints, feedstock/gas-price policy, and capacity utilization, all of which likely dominate this narrow allocation rule.

law_title: اصلاح آیین‌نامه تاسیس صندوق‌های غیردولتی توسعه صادرات و نحوه حمایت از آنها
lag_description: gradual, within the 5-year window (Third Development Plan, ~2000-2005) the exemption covered
justification: The amendment revises Article 6 of the 2001 bylaw establishing non-governmental export-development funds, granting a 5-year corporate tax exemption on activities strictly limited to export development of goods and services (per Article 5) during the Third Five-Year Plan period. A tax incentive for private-sector export-promotion vehicles is a plausible small contributor to non-oil merchandise export growth.
caveats: Iran's aggregate merchandise export value is dominated by crude oil/petroleum products, whose trends are driven by global oil prices, OPEC quotas and sanctions rather than this bylaw. Non-oil export performance in this period was also shaped by exchange-rate policy, the broader Third Plan trade-liberalization package, and regional demand -- the incremental effect of a narrow tax break for one class of export-support funds is likely marginal and not separately observable in an aggregate exports series.

law_title: تصویب نامه در خصوص الحاق یک تبصره به تصویب نامه موضوع پرداخت جوایز و مشوق های صادراتی (Decree adding a note to the export prizes/incentives payment decision)
lag_description: same fiscal year to 1-3 year lag
justification: The decree amends the export-incentive scheme by setting semi-private companies' (50-100% non-state-owned) export prize/bonus share at 80% of the standard rate, a narrow eligibility tweak to an existing national export subsidy mechanism intended to encourage export activity.
caveats: This is a marginal administrative adjustment to one clause of a subsidy program, not a new subsidy or tariff regime; aggregate merchandise export values are dominated by oil prices, sanctions regimes, and exchange-rate swings, so any effect from this specific tweak would be undetectable in national export data. Confidence kept at minimum.

law_title: تصویب‌نامه در خصوص افزایش گروه شغلی کارکنان دولت (Decree raising the job-grade classification of state employees)
justification: The cabinet decree raises the pay-scale job-grade of civil servants under the Unified Civil Servants Compensation Law (1991) — by two grades for employees with education up to associate-degree level and one grade for bachelor's-degree holders — which directly increases base salaries across the public-sector workforce and thus government wage-bill spending.
caveats: This is one of many periodic public-sector pay/grade adjustments; WDI 'Compensation of employees' reflects the cumulative effect of numerous laws, inflation indexing, headcount changes, and broader budget policy, so this single decree's contribution cannot be isolated. Iran's WDI government-finance series also has significant year gaps.
